snapshot.stmt: For a pair block \(b\), \(s_b(v_{b,r},a)=(v_{b,1-r},a)\) for \(r,a\in\{0,1\}\), with \(s_b\) fixing all outside members and relabelling complete records as in \(	exttt{def:group-action}\). Its arm-specific orbits are \(O_{b,0}=\{(v_{b,0},0),(v_{b,1},0)\}\) and \(O_{b,1}=\{(v_{b,0},1),(v_{b,1},1)\}\). If a target-arm selector is supported on \(O_{b,1}\) while an exclusive proposed calibration rule for that target is supported on the realized mate's control state in \(O_{b,0}\), their orbit-mass vectors differ, exact joint balance is infeasible for that rule, and arm \(1\) satisfies the disjoint-orbit premise. The zero-coverage conclusion applies only when \(k\le M\); for \(k=M+1\), coverage is one by \(	exttt{prop:rank-boundary}\).

defs.def:pair-action: For pair block \(b\), \(s_b(v_{b,r},a)=(v_{b,1-r},a)\) for \(r,a\in\{0,1\}\); \(s_b\) fixes members outside block \(b\) and relabels \(D_i\) by \(	exttt{def:group-action}\). Thus \(O_{b,0}=\{(v_{b,0},0),(v_{b,1},0)\}\) and \(O_{b,1}=\{(v_{b,0},1),(v_{b,1},1)\}\).

snapshot.stmt: Let \(\mathcal V=\{v_{1,0},v_{1,1},v_{2,0},v_{2,1}\}\) and \(\mathcal G=S_2\times S_2\), with the generators in \(	exttt{def:pair-action}\). The four arm-specific orbits are \(O_{1,0},O_{1,1},O_{2,0},O_{2,1}\). If \(q_{\mathrm{tar}}\) concentrates on \(v_{1,0}\), its arm-\(1\) orbit vector has mass \(1\) on \(O_{1,1}\). An exclusive mate-control selector concentrated on \((v_{1,1},0)\) has mass \(1\) on \(O_{1,0}\), so the relevant stacked LP equations cannot both hold. For \(k\le M\), the bounded witness has \(\mathcal C_w=\{0\}\) and coverage \(0\); for \(k=M+1\), it has \(\mathcal C_w=\mathbb R\) and coverage \(1\).

defs.def:pair-action: For pair block \(b\), \(s_b(v_{b,r},a)=(v_{b,1-r},a)\) for \(r,a\in\{0,1\}\); \(s_b\) fixes members outside block \(b\) and relabels \(D_i\) by \(	exttt{def:group-action}\). Thus \(O_{b,0}=\{(v_{b,0},0),(v_{b,1},0)\}\) and \(O_{b,1}=\{(v_{b,0},1),(v_{b,1},1)\}\).

snapshot.stmt: The finite maximum \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)\) in \(	exttt{def:coverage-frontier}\) is attained by binary orbit-constant scores \(s_a(O)\in\{0,K\}\). Thus the sharp common-target frontier reduces to a finite \(0\)-\(1\) optimization over adverse orbit subsets with exact binomial or multinomial rank probabilities. Its boundary cases are zero coverage under disjoint support when \(k\le M\), and coverage one for every score configuration when \(k=M+1\).

defs.def:coverage-frontier: \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)=\max_{\substack{s_a:\mathcal O\to[0,K]\\a\in\{0,1\}}}\Big[(1-\alpha)-\Pr\{s_1(O_{T,1})\le Q_k(s_1(O_{1,1}),\ldots,s_1(O_{M,1}))\quad\text{and}\quad s_0(O_{T,0})\le Q_k(s_0(O_{1,0}),\ldots,s_0(O_{M,0}))\}\Big]_+\), where \(O_{i,a}\sim q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,a}\), \(O_{T,a}\sim q_{\mathrm{tar},a}\), the target orbits are the two lifts of one \(T\), and the calibration draws use \(\texttt{ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization}\).

snapshot.stmt: Conditional on \(\mathcal T\), joint orbit balance is equivalent to equality in law of \(S_{i,a}\) and \(S_{0,a}\), for each \(a\in\{0,1\}\), uniformly over \(P\in\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\) and arbitrary fixed measurable trained predictor maps \(\widehat m_a:\mathsf X\to\mathbb R\). Under balance, conditional on \(\mathcal T\), \(S_{1,a},\ldots,S_{M,a},S_{0,a}\) are iid for each arm. The assertion is armwise and makes no joint-exchangeability claim across arms. If an orbit mass differs, \(	exttt{def:orbit-separating-law}\) gives a bounded invariant law and fixed predictor maps with unequal score laws.

defs.def:observable-replicated-experiment: For each \(i\in\{0,\ldots,M\}\), draw \(L_i\), draw \(Z_i\sim\nu\), set \(Y^{\mathrm{obs}}_{iv}=Y_{iv}(Z_{iv})\), and form \(\mathsf O_i=\{(X_{iv},Z_{iv},Y^{\mathrm{obs}}_{iv}):v\in\mathcal V\}\). Draw the one common \(T\sim q_{\mathrm{tar}}\), observe \(X_{0T}\) after selection, and use neither \(Y_{0T}(0)\) nor \(Y_{0T}(1)\) in fitting or calibration.
defs.def:observable-selector-mixture: For every \(i\in\{1,\ldots,M\}\) and \(a\in\{0,1\}\), set \(\kappa_{w,a}=\sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_h\kappa_{h,a}\), draw \(J_{i,a}\sim\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\), set \(U_{i,a}=(J_{i,a},a)\), and set \(q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,a}=\sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_hq^{\mathrm{obs}}_{h,a}\).
defs.def:invariant-laws: \(\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}=\{P:P\circ(gL_i)^{-1}=P\quad\forall g\in\mathcal G,\quad\max_{v\in\mathcal V,\ a\in\{0,1\}}|Y_v(a)|\le K\}\).
defs.def:orbit-separating-law: For \(a\in\{0,1\}\) and \(O\in\mathcal O\), set \(\widehat m_0=\widehat m_1=0\), set \(Y_v(a)=K\mathbf 1\{\iota_a(v)\in O\}\), set \(Y_v(1-a)=0\), and randomize the orbit-constant prototype by a uniform \(g\in\mathcal G\) before drawing \(Z_i\sim\nu\).

assumptions.ass:observable-selector-kernel: \(\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\ge0,\quad\sum_{v\in\mathcal V}\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=1,\quad z_v\ne a\Rightarrow\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=0\)
assumptions.ass:selector-equivariance: \(\kappa_{h,a}(\pi_gv\mid gz)=\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\quad\forall g\in\mathcal G,\ h\in\mathcal H,\ a\in\{0,1\}\)
assumptions.ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization: \(\mathcal L((J_{i,a})_{1\le i\le M,\ a\in\{0,1\}}\mid(L_i)_{0\le i\le M},(Z_i)_{0\le i\le M},T,\mathcal T)=\prod_{i=1}^M\prod_{a=0}^1\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\)

snapshot.stmt: There exists \(w\in\Delta(\mathcal H)\) jointly orbit-balancing both arms if and only if \(b_{\mathrm{LP}}\in\operatorname{conv}\{A_{\cdot h}:h\in\mathcal H\}\). Equivalently, the stacked LP in \(	exttt{def:common-selector-feasibility-lp}\) is feasible. If the condition fails, a Farkas separating hyperplane separates \(b_{\mathrm{LP}}\) from that convex hull. Conditional on \(\mathcal T\), a fixed feasible \(w\), iid arrays, independent target selection, and selector auxiliary draws imply \(\Pr(S_{0,a}>r_{a,(k)}\mid\mathcal T)\le(M+1-k)/(M+1)\le\alpha/2\) for each arm; Bonferroni gives \(\Pr(\tau_T\in\mathcal C_w\mid\mathcal T)\ge1-\alpha\) without arm independence.

defs.def:rank-inversion: For each \(a\in\{0,1\}\), compute \(S_{i,a}=|Y^{\mathrm{obs}}_{iJ_{i,a}}-\widehat m_a(X_{iJ_{i,a}})|\) for \(i\in\{1,\ldots,M\}\), take \(r_{a,(k)}\), set \(I_{a,w}=[\widehat m_a(X_{0T})-r_{a,(k)},\widehat m_a(X_{0T})+r_{a,(k)}]\), and return \(\mathcal C_w=I_{1,w}-I_{0,w}\). For proof ranks only, append iid continuous uniforms and use lexicographic ranks; the reported closed intervals use weak inclusion.

assumptions.ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization: \(\mathcal L((J_{i,a})_{1\le i\le M,\ a\in\{0,1\}}\mid(L_i)_{0\le i\le M},(Z_i)_{0\le i\le M},T,\mathcal T)=\prod_{i=1}^M\prod_{a=0}^1\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\)

snapshot.stmt: Conditional on \(\mathcal T\), orbit overlap implies \(\Pr_P(\tau_T\in\mathcal C^{\mathrm W}_w\mid\mathcal T)\ge1-\alpha\) for every fixed or prespecified \(w\), every \(P\in\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\), and arbitrary fixed measurable trained predictor maps. The proof obligation is a finite-support weighted-exchangeability lemma for calibration orbit-score pairs drawn under \(q_{a,O}\) and the target pair drawn under \(p_{a,O}\), followed by the infinity-augmented quantile armwise and Bonferroni. Invariance makes score laws coincide within an orbit, so orbit likelihood ratios suffice even with member-level selector singularity. Under joint balance every relevant \(\lambda_{a,O}=1\), recovering ordinary rank inversion.

defs.def:weighted-rank-inversion: For each \(a\in\{0,1\}\), place mass \(\lambda_{a,O_{i,a}}\) at every \(S_{i,a}\), place mass \(\lambda_{a,O_{T,a}}\) at \(+\infty\), normalize the total mass, take its left-continuous \((1-\alpha/2)\)-quantile \(r^{\mathrm W}_{a,w}\), and return the closed intervals \(I^{\mathrm W}_{a,w}\) and \(\mathcal C^{\mathrm W}_w=I^{\mathrm W}_{1,w}-I^{\mathrm W}_{0,w}\).
defs.def:rank-inversion: For each \(a\in\{0,1\}\), compute \(S_{i,a}=|Y^{\mathrm{obs}}_{iJ_{i,a}}-\widehat m_a(X_{iJ_{i,a}})|\) for \(i\in\{1,\ldots,M\}\), take \(r_{a,(k)}\), set \(I_{a,w}=[\widehat m_a(X_{0T})-r_{a,(k)},\widehat m_a(X_{0T})+r_{a,(k)}]\), and return \(\mathcal C_w=I_{1,w}-I_{0,w}\). For proof ranks only, append iid continuous uniforms and use lexicographic ranks; the reported closed intervals use weak inclusion.

assumptions.ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization: \(\mathcal L((J_{i,a})_{1\le i\le M,\ a\in\{0,1\}}\mid(L_i)_{0\le i\le M},(Z_i)_{0\le i\le M},T,\mathcal T)=\prod_{i=1}^M\prod_{a=0}^1\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\)

snapshot.stmt: For every fixed or prespecified \(w\), conditional on \(\mathcal T\), \(\Pr_P(\tau_T\in\mathcal C_w\mid\mathcal T)\ge1-\alpha-\Delta_0(w)-\Delta_1(w)\) for every \(P\in\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\) and arbitrary fixed measurable trained predictor maps. Consequently \(|\delta_{a,O}(w)|\le\Delta_a(w)\) for every \(a,O\).

defs.def:rank-inversion: For each \(a\in\{0,1\}\), compute \(S_{i,a}=|Y^{\mathrm{obs}}_{iJ_{i,a}}-\widehat m_a(X_{iJ_{i,a}})|\) for \(i\in\{1,\ldots,M\}\), take \(r_{a,(k)}\), set \(I_{a,w}=[\widehat m_a(X_{0T})-r_{a,(k)},\widehat m_a(X_{0T})+r_{a,(k)}]\), and return \(\mathcal C_w=I_{1,w}-I_{0,w}\). For proof ranks only, append iid continuous uniforms and use lexicographic ranks; the reported closed intervals use weak inclusion.

assumptions.ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization: \(\mathcal L((J_{i,a})_{1\le i\le M,\ a\in\{0,1\}}\mid(L_i)_{0\le i\le M},(Z_i)_{0\le i\le M},T,\mathcal T)=\prod_{i=1}^M\prod_{a=0}^1\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\)

snapshot.stmt: Suppose \(k\le M\), equivalently \(\alpha\ge2/(M+1)\), and, for one arm \(a\), every target-supported orbit has zero \(q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,a}\) mass. Then \(	exttt{def:disjoint-orbit-law}\) belongs to \(\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\), all adverse-arm calibration scores equal \(0\), and \(r_{a,(k)}=0\). The other arm's scores and threshold equal \(0\), so \(\mathcal C_w=\{0\}\). The common target effect equals \(K\) for \(a=1\) and \(-K\) for \(a=0\), hence \(\Pr_P(\tau_T\in\mathcal C_w\mid\mathcal T)=0\).

defs.def:disjoint-orbit-law: Fix \(a\in\{0,1\}\). Set \(\widehat m_0=\widehat m_1=0\), set \(Y_v(a)=K\) on target-supported arm-\(a\) orbits, set \(Y_v(a)=0\) on every state with positive calibration mass and on remaining states, set \(Y_v(1-a)=0\), and randomize the orbit-constant prototype by a uniform \(g\in\mathcal G\) before drawing \(Z_i\sim\nu\).
defs.def:invariant-laws: \(\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}=\{P:P\circ(gL_i)^{-1}=P\quad\forall g\in\mathcal G,\quad\max_{v\in\mathcal V,\ a\in\{0,1\}}|Y_v(a)|\le K\}\).

For proof ranks only, append iid continuous uniforms and use lexicographic ranks; the reported closed intervals use weak inclusion.

defs.def:pair-action: For pair block \(b\), \(s_b(v_{b,r},a)=(v_{b,1-r},a)\) for \(r,a\in\{0,1\}\); \(s_b\) fixes members outside block \(b\) and relabels \(D_i\) by \(\texttt{def:group-action}\). Thus \(O_{b,0}=\{(v_{b,0},0),(v_{b,1},0)\}\) and \(O_{b,1}=\{(v_{b,0},1),(v_{b,1},1)\}\).

snapshot.stmt: Let \(\mathcal V=\{v_{1,0},v_{1,1},v_{2,0},v_{2,1}\}\) and \(\mathcal G=S_2\times S_2\), with the generators in \(\texttt{def:pair-action}\). The four arm-specific orbits are \(O_{1,0},O_{1,1},O_{2,0},O_{2,1}\). If \(q_{\mathrm{tar}}\) concentrates on \(v_{1,0}\), its arm \(1\) orbit vector has mass \(1\) on \(O_{1,1}\). An exclusive mate-control analogue concentrated on \((v_{1,1},0)\) has mass \(1\) on \(O_{1,0}\), so the relevant stacked LP equations cannot both hold and the analogue is not a legal arm \(1\) selector. If a legal implemented selector has \(q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,1}(O_{1,1})=0\), then for \(k\le M\) the bounded witness has \(\mathcal C_w=\{0\}\) and coverage \(0\); for \(k=M+1\), it has \(\mathcal C_w=\mathbb R\) and coverage \(1\).

defs.def:pair-action: For pair block \(b\), \(s_b(v_{b,r},a)=(v_{b,1-r},a)\) for \(r,a\in\{0,1\}\); \(s_b\) fixes members outside block \(b\) and relabels \(D_i\) by \(\texttt{def:group-action}\). Thus \(O_{b,0}=\{(v_{b,0},0),(v_{b,1},0)\}\) and \(O_{b,1}=\{(v_{b,0},1),(v_{b,1},1)\}\).

snapshot.stmt: The binary-attainment assertion is false in general. Writing \(\mathcal O_a=\{O\in\mathcal O:O\subseteq\mathcal U_a\}\) and \(m_a=|\mathcal O_a|\), the finite maximum \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)\) is instead attained by enumerating pairs of total preorders on \(\mathcal O_0\) and \(\mathcal O_1\); every preorder has a representative in \([0,K]\) with at most \(m_a\) levels, and the exact objective is a finite sum over target members and calibration orbit-pair configurations. A brute-force algorithm takes \(O\!\left(F_{m_0}F_{m_1}|\mathcal V|(m_0m_1)^M\right)\) arithmetic operations after the joint calibration orbit law is tabulated, where \(F_m\) is the number of total preorders on an \(m\)-element set. In an admissible four-pair instance with \(M=3\) and \(\alpha=1/2\), a four-level ordering has frontier objective \(24957/160000\), whereas every binary score pair has objective at most \(759109/5000000\), a strict gap of \(83189/20000000\). Under disjoint target and calibration orbit support in one arm and \(k\le M\), \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)=1-\alpha\), corresponding to zero coverage; when \(k=M+1\), \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)=0\), corresponding to coverage one for every score configuration.

defs.def:coverage-frontier: \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)=\max_{\substack{s_a:\mathcal O\to[0,K]\\a\in\{0,1\}}}\Big[(1-\alpha)-\Pr\{s_1(O_{T,1})\le Q_k(s_1(O_{1,1}),\ldots,s_1(O_{M,1}))\quad\text{and}\quad s_0(O_{T,0})\le Q_k(s_0(O_{1,0}),\ldots,s_0(O_{M,0}))\}\Big]_+\), where \(O_{i,a}\sim q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,a}\), \(O_{T,a}\sim q_{\mathrm{tar},a}\), the target orbits are the two lifts of one \(T\), and the calibration draws use \(\texttt{ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization}\).

snapshot.stmt: Conditional on \(\mathcal T\), for every arm \(a\) and every fixed measurable predictor \(\widehat m_a\), there are probability laws \(F_{a,O}\) on \([0,\infty)\), indexed by \(O\in\mathcal O\) with \(O\subseteq\mathcal U_a\), such that for every Borel set \(C\), \[\Pr(O_{i,a}=O,S_{i,a}\in C\mid\mathcal T)=q_{a,O}F_{a,O}(C),\qquad\Pr(O_{T,a}=O,S_{0,a}\in C\mid\mathcal T)=p_{a,O}F_{a,O}(C).\] The \(M\) calibration orbit-score pairs are iid and independent of the target pair, armwise.

defs.def:observable-selector-mixture: For every \(i\in\{1,\ldots,M\}\) and \(a\in\{0,1\}\), set \(\kappa_{w,a}=\sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_h\kappa_{h,a}\), draw \(J_{i,a}\sim\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\), set \(U_{i,a}=(J_{i,a},a)\), and set \(q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,a}=\sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_hq^{\mathrm{obs}}_{h,a}\).

assumptions.ass:observable-selector-kernel: \(\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\ge0,\quad\sum_{v\in\mathcal V}\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=1,\quad z_v\ne a\Rightarrow\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=0\)
assumptions.ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization: \(\mathcal L((J_{i,a})_{1\le i\le M,\ a\in\{0,1\}}\mid(L_i)_{0\le i\le M},(Z_i)_{0\le i\le M},T,\mathcal T)=\prod_{i=1}^M\prod_{a=0}^1\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\)

snapshot.stmt: If \(k=M+1\), then \(r_{0,(k)}=r_{1,(k)}=+\infty\), \(I_{0,w}=I_{1,w}=\mathbb R\), \(\mathcal C_w=\mathbb R\), and \(\Pr_P(\tau_T\in\mathcal C_w\mid\mathcal T)=1\) for every law and trained predictor maps.

defs.def:rank-inversion: For each \(a\in\{0,1\}\), compute \(S_{i,a}=|Y^{\mathrm{obs}}_{iJ_{i,a}}-\widehat m_a(X_{iJ_{i,a}})|\) for \(i\in\{1,\ldots,M\}\), take \(r_{a,(k)}\), set \(I_{a,w}=[\widehat m_a(X_{0T})-r_{a,(k)},\widehat m_a(X_{0T})+r_{a,(k)}]\), and return \(\mathcal C_w=I_{1,w}-I_{0,w}\). For proof ranks only, append iid continuous uniforms and use lexicographic ranks; the reported closed intervals use weak inclusion.

snapshot.stmt: There exists \(w\in\Delta(\mathcal H)\) jointly orbit-balancing both arms if and only if \(b_{\mathrm{LP}}\in\operatorname{conv}\{A_{\cdot h}:h\in\mathcal H\}\). Equivalently, the stacked LP in \(\texttt{def:common-selector-feasibility-lp}\) is feasible. If the condition fails, a Farkas separating hyperplane separates \(b_{\mathrm{LP}}\) from that convex hull. Conditional on \(\mathcal T\), a fixed feasible \(w\), iid arrays, independent target selection, and selector auxiliary draws imply \(\Pr(S_{0,a}>r_{a,(k)}\mid\mathcal T)\le(M+1-k)/(M+1)\le\alpha/2\) for each arm; Bonferroni gives \(\Pr(\tau_T\in\mathcal C_w\mid\mathcal T)\ge1-\alpha\) without arm independence.

defs.def:rank-inversion: For each \(a\in\{0,1\}\), compute \(S_{i,a}=|Y^{\mathrm{obs}}_{iJ_{i,a}}-\widehat m_a(X_{iJ_{i,a}})|\) for \(i\in\{1,\ldots,M\}\), take \(r_{a,(k)}\), set \(I_{a,w}=[\widehat m_a(X_{0T})-r_{a,(k)},\widehat m_a(X_{0T})+r_{a,(k)}]\), and return \(\mathcal C_w=I_{1,w}-I_{0,w}\). For proof ranks only, append iid continuous uniforms and use lexicographic ranks; the reported closed intervals use weak inclusion.

assumptions.ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization: \(\mathcal L((J_{i,a})_{1\le i\le M,\ a\in\{0,1\}}\mid(L_i)_{0\le i\le M},(Z_i)_{0\le i\le M},T,\mathcal T)=\prod_{i=1}^M\prod_{a=0}^1\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\)

snapshot.stmt: For every fixed or prespecified \(w\), conditional on \(\mathcal T\), \(\Pr_P(\tau_T\in\mathcal C_w\mid\mathcal T)\ge1-\alpha-\Delta_0(w)-\Delta_1(w)\) for every \(P\in\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\) and arbitrary fixed measurable trained predictor maps. Consequently \(|\delta_{a,O}(w)|\le\Delta_a(w)\) for every \(a,O\).

defs.def:rank-inversion: For each \(a\in\{0,1\}\), compute \(S_{i,a}=|Y^{\mathrm{obs}}_{iJ_{i,a}}-\widehat m_a(X_{iJ_{i,a}})|\) for \(i\in\{1,\ldots,M\}\), take \(r_{a,(k)}\), set \(I_{a,w}=[\widehat m_a(X_{0T})-r_{a,(k)},\widehat m_a(X_{0T})+r_{a,(k)}]\), and return \(\mathcal C_w=I_{1,w}-I_{0,w}\). For proof ranks only, append iid continuous uniforms and use lexicographic ranks; the reported closed intervals use weak inclusion.

assumptions.ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization: \(\mathcal L((J_{i,a})_{1\le i\le M,\ a\in\{0,1\}}\mid(L_i)_{0\le i\le M},(Z_i)_{0\le i\le M},T,\mathcal T)=\prod_{i=1}^M\prod_{a=0}^1\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\)

assumptions.ass:selector-equivariance: \(\kappa_{h,a}(\pi_gv\mid gz)=\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\quad\forall g\in\mathcal G,\ h\in\mathcal H,\ a\in\{0,1\}\)
assumptions.ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization: \(\mathcal L((J_{i,a})_{1\le i\le M,\ a\in\{0,1\}}\mid(L_i)_{0\le i\le M},(Z_i)_{0\le i\le M},T,\mathcal T)=\prod_{i=1}^M\prod_{a=0}^1\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\)

Equivalently, the stacked LP in \(\texttt{def:common-selector-feasibility-lp}\) is feasible. For proof ranks only, append iid continuous uniforms and use lexicographic ranks; the reported closed intervals use weak inclusion.

assumptions.ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization: \(\mathcal L((J_{i,a})_{1\le i\le M,\ a\in\{0,1\}}\mid(L_i)_{0\le i\le M},(Z_i)_{0\le i\le M},T,\mathcal T)=\prod_{i=1}^M\prod_{a=0}^1\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\)

snapshot.stmt: Conditional on \(\mathcal T\), orbit overlap implies \(\Pr_P(\tau_T\in\mathcal C^{\mathrm W}_w\mid\mathcal T)\ge1-\alpha\) for every fixed or prespecified \(w\), every \(P\in\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\), and arbitrary fixed measurable trained predictor maps. The proof obligation is a finite-support weighted-exchangeability lemma for calibration orbit-score pairs drawn under \(q_{a,O}\) and the target pair drawn under \(p_{a,O}\), followed by the infinity-augmented quantile armwise and Bonferroni. Invariance makes score laws coincide within an orbit, so orbit likelihood ratios suffice even with member-level selector singularity. Under joint balance every relevant \(\lambda_{a,O}=1\), recovering ordinary rank inversion.

defs.def:weighted-rank-inversion: For each \(a\in\{0,1\}\), place mass \(\lambda_{a,O_{i,a}}\) at every \(S_{i,a}\), place mass \(\lambda_{a,O_{T,a}}\) at \(+\infty\), normalize the total mass, take its left-continuous \((1-\alpha/2)\)-quantile \(r^{\mathrm W}_{a,w}\), and return the closed intervals \(I^{\mathrm W}_{a,w}\) and \(\mathcal C^{\mathrm W}_w=I^{\mathrm W}_{1,w}-I^{\mathrm W}_{0,w}\).
defs.def:rank-inversion: For each \(a\in\{0,1\}\), compute \(S_{i,a}=|Y^{\mathrm{obs}}_{iJ_{i,a}}-\widehat m_a(X_{iJ_{i,a}})|\) for \(i\in\{1,\ldots,M\}\), take \(r_{a,(k)}\), set \(I_{a,w}=[\widehat m_a(X_{0T})-r_{a,(k)},\widehat m_a(X_{0T})+r_{a,(k)}]\), and return \(\mathcal C_w=I_{1,w}-I_{0,w}\). For proof ranks only, append iid continuous uniforms and use lexicographic ranks; the reported closed intervals use weak inclusion.

assumptions.ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization: \(\mathcal L((J_{i,a})_{1\le i\le M,\ a\in\{0,1\}}\mid(L_i)_{0\le i\le M},(Z_i)_{0\le i\le M},T,\mathcal T)=\prod_{i=1}^M\prod_{a=0}^1\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\)

snapshot.stmt: If \(k=M+1\), then \(r_{0,(k)}=r_{1,(k)}=+\infty\), \(I_{0,w}=I_{1,w}=\mathbb R\), \(\mathcal C_w=\mathbb R\), and \(\Pr_P(\tau_T\in\mathcal C_w\mid\mathcal T)=1\) for every law and trained predictor maps.

defs.def:rank-inversion: For each \(a\in\{0,1\}\), compute \(S_{i,a}=|Y^{\mathrm{obs}}_{iJ_{i,a}}-\widehat m_a(X_{iJ_{i,a}})|\) for \(i\in\{1,\ldots,M\}\), take \(r_{a,(k)}\), set \(I_{a,w}=[\widehat m_a(X_{0T})-r_{a,(k)},\widehat m_a(X_{0T})+r_{a,(k)}]\), and return \(\mathcal C_w=I_{1,w}-I_{0,w}\). For proof ranks only, append iid continuous uniforms and use lexicographic ranks; the reported closed intervals use weak inclusion.

snapshot.stmt: Suppose \(k\le M\), equivalently \(\alpha\ge2/(M+1)\), and, for one arm \(a\), every target-supported orbit has zero \(q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,a}\) mass. Then \(\texttt{def:disjoint-orbit-law}\) belongs to \(\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\), all adverse-arm calibration scores equal \(0\), and \(r_{a,(k)}=0\). The other arm's scores and threshold equal \(0\), so \(\mathcal C_w=\{0\}\). The common target effect equals \(K\) for \(a=1\) and \(-K\) for \(a=0\), hence \(\Pr_P(\tau_T\in\mathcal C_w\mid\mathcal T)=0\).

defs.def:disjoint-orbit-law: Fix \(a\in\{0,1\}\). Set \(\widehat m_0=\widehat m_1=0\), set \(Y_v(a)=K\) on target-supported arm-\(a\) orbits, set \(Y_v(a)=0\) on every state with positive calibration mass and on remaining states, set \(Y_v(1-a)=0\), and randomize the orbit-constant prototype by a uniform \(g\in\mathcal G\) before drawing \(Z_i\sim\nu\).
defs.def:invariant-laws: \(\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}=\{P:P\circ(gL_i)^{-1}=P\quad\forall g\in\mathcal G,\quad\max_{v\in\mathcal V,\ a\in\{0,1\}}|Y_v(a)|\le K\}\).
defs.def:rank-inversion: For each \(a\in\{0,1\}\), compute \(S_{i,a}=|Y^{\mathrm{obs}}_{iJ_{i,a}}-\widehat m_a(X_{iJ_{i,a}})|\) for \(i\in\{1,\ldots,M\}\), take \(r_{a,(k)}\), set \(I_{a,w}=[\widehat m_a(X_{0T})-r_{a,(k)},\widehat m_a(X_{0T})+r_{a,(k)}]\), and return \(\mathcal C_w=I_{1,w}-I_{0,w}\). For proof ranks only, append iid continuous uniforms and use lexicographic ranks; the reported closed intervals use weak inclusion.

assumptions.ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization: \(\mathcal L((J_{i,a})_{1\le i\le M,\ a\in\{0,1\}}\mid(L_i)_{0\le i\le M},(Z_i)_{0\le i\le M},T,\mathcal T)=\prod_{i=1}^M\prod_{a=0}^1\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\)

snapshot.stmt: For a pair block \(b\), \(s_b(v_{b,r},a)=(v_{b,1-r},a)\) for \(r,a\in\{0,1\}\), with \(s_b\) fixing all outside members and relabelling complete records as in \(\texttt{def:group-action}\). Its arm-specific orbits are \(O_{b,0}=\{(v_{b,0},0),(v_{b,1},0)\}\) and \(O_{b,1}=\{(v_{b,0},1),(v_{b,1},1)\}\). If the target arm-\(1\) lifted law is supported on \(O_{b,1}\), an exclusive mate-control analogue supported on the realized mate's control state in \(O_{b,0}\) has a different orbit-mass vector, cannot satisfy exact joint balance, and is not a legal arm-\(1\) selector. Separately, if a legal implemented arm-\(1\) selector satisfies \(q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,1}(O_{b,1})=0\), then arm \(1\) satisfies the disjoint-orbit premise. Its zero-coverage conclusion applies when \(k\le M\); for \(k=M+1\), coverage is one by \(\texttt{prop:rank-boundary}\).

defs.def:pair-action: For pair block \(b\), \(s_b(v_{b,r},a)=(v_{b,1-r},a)\) for \(r,a\in\{0,1\}\); \(s_b\) fixes members outside block \(b\) and relabels \(D_i\) by \(\texttt{def:group-action}\). Thus \(O_{b,0}=\{(v_{b,0},0),(v_{b,1},0)\}\) and \(O_{b,1}=\{(v_{b,0},1),(v_{b,1},1)\}\).

snapshot.stmt: Let \(\mathcal V=\{v_{1,0},v_{1,1},v_{2,0},v_{2,1}\}\) and \(\mathcal G=S_2\times S_2\), with the generators in \(\texttt{def:pair-action}\). The four arm-specific orbits are \(O_{1,0},O_{1,1},O_{2,0},O_{2,1}\). If \(q_{\mathrm{tar}}\) concentrates on \(v_{1,0}\), its arm \(1\) orbit vector has mass \(1\) on \(O_{1,1}\). An exclusive mate-control analogue concentrated on \((v_{1,1},0)\) has mass \(1\) on \(O_{1,0}\), so the relevant stacked LP equations cannot both hold and the analogue is not a legal arm \(1\) selector. If a legal implemented selector has \(q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,1}(O_{1,1})=0\), then for \(k\le M\) the bounded witness has \(\mathcal C_w=\{0\}\) and coverage \(0\); for \(k=M+1\), it has \(\mathcal C_w=\mathbb R\) and coverage \(1\).

defs.def:pair-action: For pair block \(b\), \(s_b(v_{b,r},a)=(v_{b,1-r},a)\) for \(r,a\in\{0,1\}\); \(s_b\) fixes members outside block \(b\) and relabels \(D_i\) by \(\texttt{def:group-action}\). Thus \(O_{b,0}=\{(v_{b,0},0),(v_{b,1},0)\}\) and \(O_{b,1}=\{(v_{b,0},1),(v_{b,1},1)\}\).

assumptions.ass:observable-selector-kernel: \(\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\ge0,\quad\sum_{v\in\mathcal V}\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=1,\quad z_v\ne a\Rightarrow\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=0\)

snapshot.stmt: Let \(N=M+1\), \(k=\lceil N(1-\alpha/2)\rceil\), and let \(\rho_{M,\alpha}\) be defined by \(\texttt{def:finite-rank-error}\). Then \[\rho_{M,\alpha}=\frac{N-k}{N}=\frac{\lfloor N\alpha/2\rfloor}{N}\le\frac{\alpha}{2}.\] For every integer \(j\ge0\) whose displayed interval intersects \((0,1)\), \(\rho_{M,\alpha}=j/N\) exactly when \(\alpha\in[2j/N,2(j+1)/N)\). In particular, \(k=M+1\) if and only if \(\rho_{M,\alpha}=0\), equivalently \(0<\alpha<2/(M+1)\).

defs.def:rank-inversion: For each \(a\in\{0,1\}\), compute \(S_{i,a}=|Y^{\mathrm{obs}}_{iJ_{i,a}}-\widehat m_a(X_{iJ_{i,a}})|\) for \(i\in\{1,\ldots,M\}\), take \(r_{a,(k)}\), set \(I_{a,w}=[\widehat m_a(X_{0T})-r_{a,(k)},\widehat m_a(X_{0T})+r_{a,(k)}]\), and return \(\mathcal C_w=I_{1,w}-I_{0,w}\). For proof ranks only, append iid continuous uniforms and use lexicographic ranks; the reported closed intervals use weak inclusion.

snapshot.stmt: For every fixed or prespecified \(w\), conditional on \(\mathcal T\), \[\Pr_P(\tau_T\in\mathcal C_w\mid\mathcal T)\ge\max\{0,1-2\rho_{M,\alpha}-\Delta_0(w)-\Delta_1(w)\}\] for every \(P\in\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\) and arbitrary fixed measurable trained predictor maps. Here \(\rho_{M,\alpha}=j/(M+1)\) on each staircase interval \(\alpha\in[2j/(M+1),2(j+1)/(M+1))\), so the elbows occur at \(\alpha=2j/(M+1)\). Consequently \(|\delta_{a,O}(w)|\le\Delta_a(w)\) for every \(a\in\{0,1\}\) and \(O\in\mathcal O\).

defs.def:rank-inversion: For each \(a\in\{0,1\}\), compute \(S_{i,a}=|Y^{\mathrm{obs}}_{iJ_{i,a}}-\widehat m_a(X_{iJ_{i,a}})|\) for \(i\in\{1,\ldots,M\}\), take \(r_{a,(k)}\), set \(I_{a,w}=[\widehat m_a(X_{0T})-r_{a,(k)},\widehat m_a(X_{0T})+r_{a,(k)}]\), and return \(\mathcal C_w=I_{1,w}-I_{0,w}\). For proof ranks only, append iid continuous uniforms and use lexicographic ranks; the reported closed intervals use weak inclusion.

assumptions.ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization: \(\mathcal L((J_{i,a})_{1\le i\le M,\ a\in\{0,1\}}\mid(L_i)_{0\le i\le M},(Z_i)_{0\le i\le M},T,\mathcal T)=\prod_{i=1}^M\prod_{a=0}^1\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\)

snapshot.stmt: The functional \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)\) in \(\texttt{def:coverage-frontier}\) is the exact finite worst-coverage deficit over the stated class of bounded orbit-constant score maps; no equality with a supremum over all \(P\in\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\) is asserted. Writing \(\mathcal O_a=\{O\in\mathcal O:O\subseteq\mathcal U_a\}\) and \(m_a=|\mathcal O_a|\), its maximum is attained by enumerating pairs of total preorders on \(\mathcal O_0\) and \(\mathcal O_1\). Every preorder has a representative in \([0,K]\) with at most \(m_a\) levels, and the exact objective is a finite sum over coherent target members and calibration orbit-pair configurations. After the joint calibration orbit law is tabulated, a brute-force algorithm uses \(O\!\left(F_{m_0}F_{m_1}|\mathcal V|(m_0m_1)^M\right)\) arithmetic operations, where \(F_m\) is the number of total preorders on an \(m\)-element labelled set. Binary attainment is false: in an admissible four-pair instance with \(M=3\) and \(\alpha=1/2\), a four-level ordering has frontier objective \(24957/160000\), whereas every binary score pair has objective at most \(759109/5000000\), a strict gap of \(83189/20000000\). Under disjoint target and calibration orbit support in one arm and \(k\le M\), \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)=1-\alpha\); when \(k=M+1\), \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)=0\).

defs.def:pair-action: For pair block \(b\), \(s_b(v_{b,r},a)=(v_{b,1-r},a)\) for \(r,a\in\{0,1\}\); \(s_b\) fixes members outside block \(b\) and relabels \(D_i\) by \(\texttt{def:group-action}\). Thus \(O_{b,0}=\{(v_{b,0},0),(v_{b,1},0)\}\) and \(O_{b,1}=\{(v_{b,0},1),(v_{b,1},1)\}\).
defs.def:observable-selector-mixture: For every \(i\in\{1,\ldots,M\}\) and \(a\in\{0,1\}\), set \(\kappa_{w,a}=\sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_h\kappa_{h,a}\), draw \(J_{i,a}\sim\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\), set \(U_{i,a}=(J_{i,a},a)\), and set \(q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,a}=\sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_hq^{\mathrm{obs}}_{h,a}\).
defs.def:coverage-frontier: \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)=\max_{\substack{s_a:\mathcal O\to[0,K]\\a\in\{0,1\}}}\Big[(1-\alpha)-\Pr\{s_1(O_{T,1})\le Q_k(s_1(O_{1,1}),\ldots,s_1(O_{M,1}))\quad\text{and}\quad s_0(O_{T,0})\le Q_k(s_0(O_{1,0}),\ldots,s_0(O_{M,0}))\}\Big]_+\), where \(O_{i,a}\sim q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,a}\), \(O_{T,a}\sim q_{\mathrm{tar},a}\), the target orbits are the two lifts of one \(T\), and the calibration draws use \(\texttt{ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization}\).

assumptions.ass:observable-selector-kernel: \(\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\ge0,\quad\sum_{v\in\mathcal V}\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=1,\quad z_v\ne a\Rightarrow\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=0\)
assumptions.ass:selector-equivariance: \(\kappa_{h,a}(\pi_gv\mid gz)=\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\quad\forall g\in\mathcal G,\ h\in\mathcal H,\ a\in\{0,1\}\)
assumptions.ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization: \(\mathcal L((J_{i,a})_{1\le i\le M,\ a\in\{0,1\}}\mid(L_i)_{0\le i\le M},(Z_i)_{0\le i\le M},T,\mathcal T)=\prod_{i=1}^M\prod_{a=0}^1\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\)

snapshot.stmt: For every fixed or prespecified \(w\), conditional on \(\mathcal T\), \[\Pr_P(\tau_T\in\mathcal C_w\mid\mathcal T)\ge\max\{0,1-2\rho_{M,\alpha}-\Delta_0(w)-\Delta_1(w)\}\] for every \(P\in\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\) and arbitrary fixed measurable trained predictor maps. Here \(\rho_{M,\alpha}=j/(M+1)\) on each staircase interval \(\alpha\in[2j/(M+1),2(j+1)/(M+1))\), so the elbows occur at \(\alpha=2j/(M+1)\). Consequently \(|\delta_{a,O}(w)|\le\Delta_a(w)\) for every \(a\in\{0,1\}\) and \(O\in\mathcal O\).

defs.def:finite-rank-error: For \(k=\lceil(M+1)(1-\alpha/2)\rceil\), define \[\rho_{M,\alpha}=\frac{M+1-k}{M+1}=\frac{\lfloor(M+1)\alpha/2\rfloor}{M+1}.\]
defs.def:rank-inversion: Set \(k=\lceil(M+1)(1-\alpha/2)\rceil\). For each \(a\in\{0,1\}\), compute \(S_{i,a}=|Y^{\mathrm{obs}}_{iJ_{i,a}}-\widehat m_a(X_{iJ_{i,a}})|\) for \(i\in\{1,\ldots,M\}\), take \(r_{a,(k)}\), set \(I_{a,w}=[\widehat m_a(X_{0T})-r_{a,(k)},\widehat m_a(X_{0T})+r_{a,(k)}]\), and return \(\mathcal C_w=I_{1,w}-I_{0,w}\). If \(k=M+1\), set \(r_{a,(k)}=+\infty\). For proof ranks only, append iid continuous uniforms and use lexicographic ranks; the reported closed intervals use weak inclusion. The associated exact one-arm rank error is \(\rho_{M,\alpha}\) from \(\texttt{def:finite-rank-error}\).

assumptions.ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization: \(\mathcal L((J_{i,a})_{1\le i\le M,\ a\in\{0,1\}}\mid(L_i)_{0\le i\le M},(Z_i)_{0\le i\le M},T,\mathcal T)=\prod_{i=1}^M\prod_{a=0}^1\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\)

snapshot.stmt: There exists \(w\in\Delta(\mathcal H)\) jointly orbit-balancing both arms if and only if \(b_{\mathrm{LP}}\in\operatorname{conv}\{A_{\cdot h}:h\in\mathcal H\}\). Equivalently, the stacked LP in \(\texttt{def:common-selector-feasibility-lp}\) is feasible. If the condition fails, a Farkas separating hyperplane separates \(b_{\mathrm{LP}}\) from that convex hull. Conditional on \(\mathcal T\), a fixed feasible \(w\), iid arrays, independent target selection, and selector auxiliary draws imply \(\Pr(S_{0,a}>r_{a,(k)}\mid\mathcal T)\le(M+1-k)/(M+1)\le\alpha/2\) for each arm; Bonferroni gives \(\Pr(\tau_T\in\mathcal C_w\mid\mathcal T)\ge1-\alpha\) without arm independence.

For each \(a\in\{0,1\}\), compute \(S_{i,a}=|Y^{\mathrm{obs}}_{iJ_{i,a}}-\widehat m_a(X_{iJ_{i,a}})|\) for \(i\in\{1,\ldots,M\}\), take \(r_{a,(k)}\), set \(I_{a,w}=[\widehat m_a(X_{0T})-r_{a,(k)},\widehat m_a(X_{0T})+r_{a,(k)}]\), and return \(\mathcal C_w=I_{1,w}-I_{0,w}\). If \(k=M+1\), set \(r_{a,(k)}=+\infty\). For proof ranks only, append iid continuous uniforms and use lexicographic ranks; the reported closed intervals use weak inclusion. The associated exact one-arm rank error is \(\rho_{M,\alpha}\) from \(\texttt{def:finite-rank-error}\).
defs.def:finite-rank-error: For \(k=\lceil(M+1)(1-\alpha/2)\rceil\), define \[\rho_{M,\alpha}=\frac{M+1-k}{M+1}=\frac{\lfloor(M+1)\alpha/2\rfloor}{M+1}.\]

assumptions.ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization: \(\mathcal L((J_{i,a})_{1\le i\le M,\ a\in\{0,1\}}\mid(L_i)_{0\le i\le M},(Z_i)_{0\le i\le M},T,\mathcal T)=\prod_{i=1}^M\prod_{a=0}^1\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\)

snapshot.stmt: Conditional on \(\mathcal T\), orbit overlap implies \(\Pr_P(\tau_T\in\mathcal C^{\mathrm W}_w\mid\mathcal T)\ge1-\alpha\) for every fixed or prespecified \(w\), every \(P\in\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\), and arbitrary fixed measurable trained predictor maps. The proof obligation is a finite-support weighted-exchangeability lemma for calibration orbit-score pairs drawn under \(q_{a,O}\) and the target pair drawn under \(p_{a,O}\), followed by the infinity-augmented quantile armwise and Bonferroni. Invariance makes score laws coincide within an orbit, so orbit likelihood ratios suffice even with member-level selector singularity. Under joint balance every relevant \(\lambda_{a,O}=1\), recovering ordinary rank inversion.

defs.def:weighted-rank-inversion: For each \(a\in\{0,1\}\), place mass \(\lambda_{a,O_{i,a}}\) at every \(S_{i,a}\), place mass \(\lambda_{a,O_{T,a}}\) at \(+\infty\), normalize the total mass, take its left-continuous \((1-\alpha/2)\)-quantile \(r^{\mathrm W}_{a,w}\), and return the closed intervals \(I^{\mathrm W}_{a,w}\) and \(\mathcal C^{\mathrm W}_w=I^{\mathrm W}_{1,w}-I^{\mathrm W}_{0,w}\).
defs.def:rank-inversion: Set \(k=\lceil(M+1)(1-\alpha/2)\rceil\). For each \(a\in\{0,1\}\), compute \(S_{i,a}=|Y^{\mathrm{obs}}_{iJ_{i,a}}-\widehat m_a(X_{iJ_{i,a}})|\) for \(i\in\{1,\ldots,M\}\), take \(r_{a,(k)}\), set \(I_{a,w}=[\widehat m_a(X_{0T})-r_{a,(k)},\widehat m_a(X_{0T})+r_{a,(k)}]\), and return \(\mathcal C_w=I_{1,w}-I_{0,w}\). snapshot.stmt: If \(k=M+1\), then \(r_{0,(k)}=r_{1,(k)}=+\infty\), \(I_{0,w}=I_{1,w}=\mathbb R\), \(\mathcal C_w=\mathbb R\), and \(\Pr_P(\tau_T\in\mathcal C_w\mid\mathcal T)=1\) for every law and trained predictor maps.

defs.def:finite-rank-error: For \(k=\lceil(M+1)(1-\alpha/2)\rceil\), define \[\rho_{M,\alpha}=\frac{M+1-k}{M+1}=\frac{\lfloor(M+1)\alpha/2\rfloor}{M+1}.\]
defs.def:rank-inversion: Set \(k=\lceil(M+1)(1-\alpha/2)\rceil\). For each \(a\in\{0,1\}\), compute \(S_{i,a}=|Y^{\mathrm{obs}}_{iJ_{i,a}}-\widehat m_a(X_{iJ_{i,a}})|\) for \(i\in\{1,\ldots,M\}\), take \(r_{a,(k)}\), set \(I_{a,w}=[\widehat m_a(X_{0T})-r_{a,(k)},\widehat m_a(X_{0T})+r_{a,(k)}]\), and return \(\mathcal C_w=I_{1,w}-I_{0,w}\). snapshot.stmt: Suppose \(k\le M\), equivalently \(\alpha\ge2/(M+1)\), and, for one arm \(a\), every target-supported orbit has zero \(q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,a}\) mass. Then \(\texttt{def:disjoint-orbit-law}\) belongs to \(\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\), all adverse-arm calibration scores equal \(0\), and \(r_{a,(k)}=0\). The other arm's scores and threshold equal \(0\), so \(\mathcal C_w=\{0\}\). The common target effect equals \(K\) for \(a=1\) and \(-K\) for \(a=0\), hence \(\Pr_P(\tau_T\in\mathcal C_w\mid\mathcal T)=0\).

defs.def:disjoint-orbit-law: Fix \(a\in\{0,1\}\). Set \(\widehat m_0=\widehat m_1=0\), set \(Y_v(a)=K\) on target-supported arm-\(a\) orbits, set \(Y_v(a)=0\) on every state with positive calibration mass and on remaining states, set \(Y_v(1-a)=0\), and randomize the orbit-constant prototype by a uniform \(g\in\mathcal G\) before drawing \(Z_i\sim\nu\).
defs.def:invariant-laws: \(\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}=\{P:P\circ(gL_i)^{-1}=P\quad\forall g\in\mathcal G,\quad\max_{v\in\mathcal V,\ a\in\{0,1\}}|Y_v(a)|\le K\}\).

For each \(a\in\{0,1\}\), compute \(S_{i,a}=|Y^{\mathrm{obs}}_{iJ_{i,a}}-\widehat m_a(X_{iJ_{i,a}})|\) for \(i\in\{1,\ldots,M\}\), take \(r_{a,(k)}\), set \(I_{a,w}=[\widehat m_a(X_{0T})-r_{a,(k)},\widehat m_a(X_{0T})+r_{a,(k)}]\), and return \(\mathcal C_w=I_{1,w}-I_{0,w}\). If \(k=M+1\), set \(r_{a,(k)}=+\infty\). For proof ranks only, append iid continuous uniforms and use lexicographic ranks; the reported closed intervals use weak inclusion. The associated exact one-arm rank error is \(\rho_{M,\alpha}\) from \(\texttt{def:finite-rank-error}\).

assumptions.ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization: \(\mathcal L((J_{i,a})_{1\le i\le M,\ a\in\{0,1\}}\mid(L_i)_{0\le i\le M},(Z_i)_{0\le i\le M},T,\mathcal T)=\prod_{i=1}^M\prod_{a=0}^1\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\)

snapshot.stmt: For a pair block \(b\), \(s_b(v_{b,r},a)=(v_{b,1-r},a)\) for \(r,a\in\{0,1\}\), with \(s_b\) fixing all outside members and relabelling complete records as in \(\texttt{def:group-action}\). Its arm-specific orbits are \(O_{b,0}=\{(v_{b,0},0),(v_{b,1},0)\}\) and \(O_{b,1}=\{(v_{b,0},1),(v_{b,1},1)\}\). If the target arm-\(1\) lifted law is supported on \(O_{b,1}\), an exclusive mate-control analogue supported on the realized mate's control state in \(O_{b,0}\) has a different orbit-mass vector, cannot satisfy exact joint balance, and is not a legal arm-\(1\) selector. Separately, if a legal implemented arm-\(1\) selector satisfies \(q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,1}(O_{b,1})=0\), then arm \(1\) satisfies the disjoint-orbit premise. Its zero-coverage conclusion applies when \(k\le M\); for \(k=M+1\), coverage is one by \(\texttt{prop:rank-boundary}\).

defs.def:pair-action: For pair block \(b\), \(s_b(v_{b,r},a)=(v_{b,1-r},a)\) for \(r,a\in\{0,1\}\); \(s_b\) fixes members outside block \(b\) and relabels \(D_i\) by \(\texttt{def:group-action}\). Thus \(O_{b,0}=\{(v_{b,0},0),(v_{b,1},0)\}\) and \(O_{b,1}=\{(v_{b,0},1),(v_{b,1},1)\}\).

assumptions.ass:observable-selector-kernel: \(\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\ge0,\quad\sum_{v\in\mathcal V}\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=1,\quad z_v\ne a\Rightarrow\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=0\)

snapshot.stmt: Let \(\mathcal V=\{v_{1,0},v_{1,1},v_{2,0},v_{2,1}\}\) and \(\mathcal G=S_2\times S_2\), with the generators in \(\texttt{def:pair-action}\). The four arm-specific orbits are \(O_{1,0},O_{1,1},O_{2,0},O_{2,1}\). If \(q_{\mathrm{tar}}\) concentrates on \(v_{1,0}\), its arm \(1\) orbit vector has mass \(1\) on \(O_{1,1}\). An exclusive mate-control analogue concentrated on \((v_{1,1},0)\) has mass \(1\) on \(O_{1,0}\), so the relevant stacked LP equations cannot both hold and the analogue is not a legal arm \(1\) selector. If a legal implemented selector has \(q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,1}(O_{1,1})=0\), then for \(k\le M\) the bounded witness has \(\mathcal C_w=\{0\}\) and coverage \(0\); for \(k=M+1\), it has \(\mathcal C_w=\mathbb R\) and coverage \(1\).

snapshot.stmt: The functional \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)\) in \(\texttt{def:coverage-frontier}\) is the exact finite worst-coverage deficit over the stated class of bounded orbit-constant score maps; no equality with a supremum over all \(P\in\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\) is asserted. Writing \(\mathcal O_a=\{O\in\mathcal O:O\subseteq\mathcal U_a\}\) and \(m_a=|\mathcal O_a|\), its maximum is attained by enumerating pairs of total preorders on \(\mathcal O_0\) and \(\mathcal O_1\). defs.def:weighted-rank-inversion: For each \(a\in\{0,1\}\), place mass \(\lambda_{a,O_{i,a}}\) at every \(S_{i,a}\), place mass \(\lambda_{a,O_{T,a}}\) at \(+\infty\), normalize the total mass, take its left-continuous \((1-\alpha/2)\)-quantile \(r^{\mathrm W}_{a,w}\), and return the closed intervals \(I^{\mathrm W}_{a,w}\) and \(\mathcal C^{\mathrm W}_w=I^{\mathrm W}_{1,w}-I^{\mathrm W}_{0,w}\).
defs.def:rank-inversion: Set \(k=\lceil(M+1)(1-\alpha/2)\rceil\). For each \(a\in\{0,1\}\), compute \(S_{i,a}=|Y^{\mathrm{obs}}_{iJ_{i,a}}-\widehat m_a(X_{iJ_{i,a}})|\) for \(i\in\{1,\ldots,M\}\), take \(r_{a,(k)}\), set \(I_{a,w}=[\widehat m_a(X_{0T})-r_{a,(k)},\widehat m_a(X_{0T})+r_{a,(k)}]\), and return \(\mathcal C_w=I_{1,w}-I_{0,w}\). If \(k=M+1\), set \(r_{a,(k)}=+\infty\). For proof ranks only, append iid continuous uniforms and use lexicographic ranks; the reported closed intervals use weak inclusion. The associated exact one-arm rank error is \(\rho_{M,\alpha}\) from \(\texttt{def:finite-rank-error}\).

assumptions.ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization: \(\mathcal L((J_{i,a})_{1\le i\le M,\ a\in\{0,1\}}\mid(L_i)_{0\le i\le M},(Z_i)_{0\le i\le M},T,\mathcal T)=\prod_{i=1}^M\prod_{a=0}^1\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\)

snapshot.stmt: If \(k=M+1\), then \(r_{0,(k)}=r_{1,(k)}=+\infty\), \(I_{0,w}=I_{1,w}=\mathbb R\), \(\mathcal C_w=\mathbb R\), and \(\Pr_P(\tau_T\in\mathcal C_w\mid\mathcal T)=1\) for every law and trained predictor maps.

defs.def:finite-rank-error: For \(k=\lceil(M+1)(1-\alpha/2)\rceil\), define \[\rho_{M,\alpha}=\frac{M+1-k}{M+1}=\frac{\lfloor(M+1)\alpha/2\rfloor}{M+1}.\]
defs.def:rank-inversion: Set \(k=\lceil(M+1)(1-\alpha/2)\rceil\). For each \(a\in\{0,1\}\), compute \(S_{i,a}=|Y^{\mathrm{obs}}_{iJ_{i,a}}-\widehat m_a(X_{iJ_{i,a}})|\) for \(i\in\{1,\ldots,M\}\), take \(r_{a,(k)}\), set \(I_{a,w}=[\widehat m_a(X_{0T})-r_{a,(k)},\widehat m_a(X_{0T})+r_{a,(k)}]\), and return \(\mathcal C_w=I_{1,w}-I_{0,w}\). If \(k=M+1\), set \(r_{a,(k)}=+\infty\). For proof ranks only, append iid continuous uniforms and use lexicographic ranks; the reported closed intervals use weak inclusion. The associated exact one-arm rank error is \(\rho_{M,\alpha}\) from \(\texttt{def:finite-rank-error}\).

snapshot.stmt: Suppose \(k\le M\), equivalently \(\alpha\ge2/(M+1)\), and, for one arm \(a\), every target-supported orbit has zero \(q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,a}\) mass. Then \(\texttt{def:disjoint-orbit-law}\) belongs to \(\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\), all adverse-arm calibration scores equal \(0\), and \(r_{a,(k)}=0\). The other arm's scores and threshold equal \(0\), so \(\mathcal C_w=\{0\}\). The common target effect equals \(K\) for \(a=1\) and \(-K\) for \(a=0\), hence \(\Pr_P(\tau_T\in\mathcal C_w\mid\mathcal T)=0\).

defs.def:disjoint-orbit-law: Fix \(a\in\{0,1\}\). Set \(\widehat m_0=\widehat m_1=0\), set \(Y_v(a)=K\) on target-supported arm-\(a\) orbits, set \(Y_v(a)=0\) on every state with positive calibration mass and on remaining states, set \(Y_v(1-a)=0\), and randomize the orbit-constant prototype by a uniform \(g\in\mathcal G\) before drawing \(Z_i\sim\nu\).
defs.def:invariant-laws: \(\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}=\{P:P\circ(gL_i)^{-1}=P\quad\forall g\in\mathcal G,\quad\max_{v\in\mathcal V,\ a\in\{0,1\}}|Y_v(a)|\le K\}\).
defs.def:observable-selector-mixture: For every \(i\in\{1,\ldots,M\}\) and \(a\in\{0,1\}\), set \(\kappa_{w,a}=\sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_h\kappa_{h,a}\), draw \(J_{i,a}\sim\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\), set \(U_{i,a}=(J_{i,a},a)\), and set \(q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,a}=\sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_hq^{\mathrm{obs}}_{h,a}\).
defs.def:rank-inversion: Set \(k=\lceil(M+1)(1-\alpha/2)\rceil\). For each \(a\in\{0,1\}\), compute \(S_{i,a}=|Y^{\mathrm{obs}}_{iJ_{i,a}}-\widehat m_a(X_{iJ_{i,a}})|\) for \(i\in\{1,\ldots,M\}\), take \(r_{a,(k)}\), set \(I_{a,w}=[\widehat m_a(X_{0T})-r_{a,(k)},\widehat m_a(X_{0T})+r_{a,(k)}]\), and return \(\mathcal C_w=I_{1,w}-I_{0,w}\). If \(k=M+1\), set \(r_{a,(k)}=+\infty\). For proof ranks only, append iid continuous uniforms and use lexicographic ranks; the reported closed intervals use weak inclusion. The associated exact one-arm rank error is \(\rho_{M,\alpha}\) from \(\texttt{def:finite-rank-error}\).

assumptions.ass:observable-selector-kernel: \(\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\ge0,\quad\sum_{v\in\mathcal V}\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=1,\quad z_v\ne a\Rightarrow\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=0\)
assumptions.ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization: \(\mathcal L((J_{i,a})_{1\le i\le M,\ a\in\{0,1\}}\mid(L_i)_{0\le i\le M},(Z_i)_{0\le i\le M},T,\mathcal T)=\prod_{i=1}^M\prod_{a=0}^1\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\)

snapshot.stmt: For a pair block \(b\), \(s_b(v_{b,r},a)=(v_{b,1-r},a)\) for \(r,a\in\{0,1\}\), with \(s_b\) fixing all outside members and relabelling complete records as in \(\texttt{def:group-action}\). Its arm-specific orbits are \(O_{b,0}=\{(v_{b,0},0),(v_{b,1},0)\}\) and \(O_{b,1}=\{(v_{b,0},1),(v_{b,1},1)\}\). If the target arm-\(1\) lifted law is supported on \(O_{b,1}\), an exclusive mate-control analogue supported on the realized mate's control state in \(O_{b,0}\) has a different orbit-mass vector, cannot satisfy exact joint balance, and is not a legal arm-\(1\) selector. Separately, if a legal implemented arm-\(1\) selector satisfies \(q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,1}(O_{b,1})=0\), then arm \(1\) satisfies the disjoint-orbit premise. Its zero-coverage conclusion applies when \(k\le M\); for \(k=M+1\), coverage is one by \(\texttt{prop:rank-boundary}\).

defs.def:pair-action: For pair block \(b\), \(s_b(v_{b,r},a)=(v_{b,1-r},a)\) for \(r,a\in\{0,1\}\); \(s_b\) fixes members outside block \(b\) and relabels \(D_i\) by \(\texttt{def:group-action}\). Thus \(O_{b,0}=\{(v_{b,0},0),(v_{b,1},0)\}\) and \(O_{b,1}=\{(v_{b,0},1),(v_{b,1},1)\}\).

assumptions.ass:observable-selector-kernel: \(\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\ge0,\quad\sum_{v\in\mathcal V}\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=1,\quad z_v\ne a\Rightarrow\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=0\)

snapshot.stmt: Let \(\mathcal V=\{v_{1,0},v_{1,1},v_{2,0},v_{2,1}\}\) and \(\mathcal G=S_2\times S_2\), with the generators in \(\texttt{def:pair-action}\). The four arm-specific orbits are \(O_{1,0},O_{1,1},O_{2,0},O_{2,1}\). If \(q_{\mathrm{tar}}\) concentrates on \(v_{1,0}\), its arm \(1\) orbit vector has mass \(1\) on \(O_{1,1}\). An exclusive mate-control analogue concentrated on \((v_{1,1},0)\) has mass \(1\) on \(O_{1,0}\), so the relevant stacked LP equations cannot both hold and the analogue is not a legal arm \(1\) selector. If a legal implemented selector has \(q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,1}(O_{1,1})=0\), then for \(k\le M\) the bounded witness has \(\mathcal C_w=\{0\}\) and coverage \(0\); for \(k=M+1\), it has \(\mathcal C_w=\mathbb R\) and coverage \(1\).

defs.def:pair-action: For pair block \(b\), \(s_b(v_{b,r},a)=(v_{b,1-r},a)\) for \(r,a\in\{0,1\}\); \(s_b\) fixes members outside block \(b\) and relabels \(D_i\) by \(\texttt{def:group-action}\). Thus \(O_{b,0}=\{(v_{b,0},0),(v_{b,1},0)\}\) and \(O_{b,1}=\{(v_{b,0},1),(v_{b,1},1)\}\).

assumptions.ass:observable-selector-kernel: \(\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\ge0,\quad\sum_{v\in\mathcal V}\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=1,\quad z_v\ne a\Rightarrow\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=0\)

snapshot.stmt: Let \(N=M+1\), \(k=\lceil N(1-\alpha/2)\rceil\), and let \(\rho_{M,\alpha}\) be defined by \(\texttt{def:finite-rank-error}\). Then \[\rho_{M,\alpha}=\frac{N-k}{N}=\frac{\lfloor N\alpha/2\rfloor}{N}\le\frac{\alpha}{2}.\] For every integer \(j\ge0\) whose displayed interval intersects \((0,1)\), \(\rho_{M,\alpha}=j/N\) exactly when \(\alpha\in[2j/N,2(j+1)/N)\). In particular, \(k=M+1\) if and only if \(\rho_{M,\alpha}=0\), equivalently \(0<\alpha<2/(M+1)\).

defs.def:finite-rank-error: For \(k=\lceil(M+1)(1-\alpha/2)\rceil\), define \[\rho_{M,\alpha}=\frac{M+1-k}{M+1}=\frac{\lfloor(M+1)\alpha/2\rfloor}{M+1}.\]
defs.def:rank-inversion: Set \(k=\lceil(M+1)(1-\alpha/2)\rceil\). For each \(a\in\{0,1\}\), compute \(S_{i,a}=|Y^{\mathrm{obs}}_{iJ_{i,a}}-\widehat m_a(X_{iJ_{i,a}})|\) for \(i\in\{1,\ldots,M\}\), take \(r_{a,(k)}\), set \(I_{a,w}=[\widehat m_a(X_{0T})-r_{a,(k)},\widehat m_a(X_{0T})+r_{a,(k)}]\), and return \(\mathcal C_w=I_{1,w}-I_{0,w}\). If \(k=M+1\), set \(r_{a,(k)}=+\infty\). For proof ranks only, append iid continuous uniforms and use lexicographic ranks; the reported closed intervals use weak inclusion. The associated exact one-arm rank error is \(\rho_{M,\alpha}\) from \(\texttt{def:finite-rank-error}\).

snapshot.stmt: For every fixed or prespecified \(w\), conditional on \(\mathcal T\), \[\Pr_P(\tau_T\in\mathcal C_w\mid\mathcal T)\ge\max\{0,1-2\rho_{M,\alpha}-\Delta_0(w)-\Delta_1(w)\}\] for every \(P\in\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\) and arbitrary fixed measurable trained predictor maps. Here \(\rho_{M,\alpha}=j/(M+1)\) on each staircase interval \(\alpha\in[2j/(M+1),2(j+1)/(M+1))\), so the elbows occur at \(\alpha=2j/(M+1)\). Consequently \(|\delta_{a,O}(w)|\le\Delta_a(w)\) for every \(a\in\{0,1\}\) and \(O\in\mathcal O\).

defs.def:finite-rank-error: For \(k=\lceil(M+1)(1-\alpha/2)\rceil\), define \[\rho_{M,\alpha}=\frac{M+1-k}{M+1}=\frac{\lfloor(M+1)\alpha/2\rfloor}{M+1}.\]
defs.def:rank-inversion: Set \(k=\lceil(M+1)(1-\alpha/2)\rceil\). For each \(a\in\{0,1\}\), compute \(S_{i,a}=|Y^{\mathrm{obs}}_{iJ_{i,a}}-\widehat m_a(X_{iJ_{i,a}})|\) for \(i\in\{1,\ldots,M\}\), take \(r_{a,(k)}\), set \(I_{a,w}=[\widehat m_a(X_{0T})-r_{a,(k)},\widehat m_a(X_{0T})+r_{a,(k)}]\), and return \(\mathcal C_w=I_{1,w}-I_{0,w}\). If \(k=M+1\), set \(r_{a,(k)}=+\infty\). For proof ranks only, append iid continuous uniforms and use lexicographic ranks; the reported closed intervals use weak inclusion. The associated exact one-arm rank error is \(\rho_{M,\alpha}\) from \(\texttt{def:finite-rank-error}\).

assumptions.ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization: \(\mathcal L((J_{i,a})_{1\le i\le M,\ a\in\{0,1\}}\mid(L_i)_{0\le i\le M},(Z_i)_{0\le i\le M},T,\mathcal T)=\prod_{i=1}^M\prod_{a=0}^1\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\)

snapshot.stmt: The functional \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)\) in \(\texttt{def:coverage-frontier}\) is the exact finite worst-coverage deficit over the stated class of bounded orbit-constant score maps; no equality with a supremum over all \(P\in\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\) is asserted. Writing \(\mathcal O_a=\{O\in\mathcal O:O\subseteq\mathcal U_a\}\) and \(m_a=|\mathcal O_a|\), its maximum is attained by enumerating pairs of total preorders on \(\mathcal O_0\) and \(\mathcal O_1\). Every preorder has a representative in \([0,K]\) with at most \(m_a\) levels, and the exact objective is a finite sum over coherent target members and calibration orbit-pair configurations. After the joint calibration orbit law is tabulated, a brute-force algorithm uses \(O\!\left(F_{m_0}F_{m_1}|\mathcal V|(m_0m_1)^M\right)\) arithmetic operations, where \(F_m\) is the number of total preorders on an \(m\)-element labelled set. Binary attainment is false: in an admissible four-pair instance with \(M=3\) and \(\alpha=1/2\), a four-level ordering has frontier objective \(24957/160000\), whereas every binary score pair has objective at most \(759109/5000000\), a strict gap of \(83189/20000000\). Under disjoint target and calibration orbit support in one arm and \(k\le M\), \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)=1-\alpha\); when \(k=M+1\), \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)=0\).

defs.def:pair-action: For pair block \(b\), \(s_b(v_{b,r},a)=(v_{b,1-r},a)\) for \(r,a\in\{0,1\}\); \(s_b\) fixes members outside block \(b\) and relabels \(D_i\) by \(\texttt{def:group-action}\). Thus \(O_{b,0}=\{(v_{b,0},0),(v_{b,1},0)\}\) and \(O_{b,1}=\{(v_{b,0},1),(v_{b,1},1)\}\).
defs.def:observable-selector-mixture: For every \(i\in\{1,\ldots,M\}\) and \(a\in\{0,1\}\), set \(\kappa_{w,a}=\sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_h\kappa_{h,a}\), draw \(J_{i,a}\sim\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\), set \(U_{i,a}=(J_{i,a},a)\), and set \(q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,a}=\sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_hq^{\mathrm{obs}}_{h,a}\).
defs.def:coverage-frontier: \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)=\max_{\substack{s_a:\mathcal O\to[0,K]\\a\in\{0,1\}}}\Big[(1-\alpha)-\Pr\{s_1(O_{T,1})\le Q_k(s_1(O_{1,1}),\ldots,s_1(O_{M,1}))\quad\text{and}\quad s_0(O_{T,0})\le Q_k(s_0(O_{1,0}),\ldots,s_0(O_{M,0}))\}\Big]_+\), where \(O_{i,a}\sim q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,a}\), \(O_{T,a}\sim q_{\mathrm{tar},a}\), the target orbits are the two lifts of one \(T\), and the calibration draws use \(\texttt{ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization}\). This functional is restricted to bounded orbit-constant score maps \(s_a\); it does not by definition equal a supremum over all laws in \(\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\).

assumptions.ass:observable-selector-kernel: \(\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\ge0,\quad\sum_{v\in\mathcal V}\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=1,\quad z_v\ne a\Rightarrow\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=0\)
assumptions.ass:selector-equivariance: \(\kappa_{h,a}(\pi_gv\mid gz)=\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\quad\forall g\in\mathcal G,\ h\in\mathcal H,\ a\in\{0,1\}\)
assumptions.ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization: \(\mathcal L((J_{i,a})_{1\le i\le M,\ a\in\{0,1\}}\mid(L_i)_{0\le i\le M},(Z_i)_{0\le i\le M},T,\mathcal T)=\prod_{i=1}^M\prod_{a=0}^1\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\)

snapshot.stmt: There exists \(w\in\Delta(\mathcal H)\) jointly orbit-balancing both arms if and only if \(b_{\mathrm{LP}}\in\operatorname{conv}\{A_{\cdot h}:h\in\mathcal H\}\). Equivalently, the stacked LP in \(\texttt{def:common-selector-feasibility-lp}\) is feasible. If the condition fails, a Farkas separating hyperplane separates \(b_{\mathrm{LP}}\) from that convex hull. Conditional on \(\mathcal T\), a fixed feasible \(w\), iid arrays, independent target selection, and selector auxiliary draws imply \(\Pr(S_{0,a}>r_{a,(k)}\mid\mathcal T)\le(M+1-k)/(M+1)\le\alpha/2\) for each arm; Bonferroni gives \(\Pr(\tau_T\in\mathcal C_w\mid\mathcal T)\ge1-\alpha\) without arm independence.

defs.def:rank-inversion: Set \(k=\lceil(M+1)(1-\alpha/2)\rceil\). For each \(a\in\{0,1\}\), compute \(S_{i,a}=|Y^{\mathrm{obs}}_{iJ_{i,a}}-\widehat m_a(X_{iJ_{i,a}})|\) for \(i\in\{1,\ldots,M\}\), take \(r_{a,(k)}\), set \(I_{a,w}=[\widehat m_a(X_{0T})-r_{a,(k)},\widehat m_a(X_{0T})+r_{a,(k)}]\), and return \(\mathcal C_w=I_{1,w}-I_{0,w}\). If \(k=M+1\), set \(r_{a,(k)}=+\infty\). For proof ranks only, append iid continuous uniforms and use lexicographic ranks; the reported closed intervals use weak inclusion. The associated exact one-arm rank error is \(\rho_{M,\alpha}\) from \(\texttt{def:finite-rank-error}\).

assumptions.ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization: \(\mathcal L((J_{i,a})_{1\le i\le M,\ a\in\{0,1\}}\mid(L_i)_{0\le i\le M},(Z_i)_{0\le i\le M},T,\mathcal T)=\prod_{i=1}^M\prod_{a=0}^1\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\)

snapshot.stmt: Conditional on \(\mathcal T\), orbit overlap implies \(\Pr_P(\tau_T\in\mathcal C^{\mathrm W}_w\mid\mathcal T)\ge1-\alpha\) for every fixed or prespecified \(w\), every \(P\in\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\), and arbitrary fixed measurable trained predictor maps. The proof obligation is a finite-support weighted-exchangeability lemma for calibration orbit-score pairs drawn under \(q_{a,O}\) and the target pair drawn under \(p_{a,O}\), followed by the infinity-augmented quantile armwise and Bonferroni. Invariance makes score laws coincide within an orbit, so orbit likelihood ratios suffice even with member-level selector singularity. Under joint balance every relevant \(\lambda_{a,O}=1\), recovering ordinary rank inversion.

defs.def:weighted-rank-inversion: For each \(a\in\{0,1\}\), place mass \(\lambda_{a,O_{i,a}}\) at every \(S_{i,a}\), place mass \(\lambda_{a,O_{T,a}}\) at \(+\infty\), normalize the total mass, and take its left-continuous \((1-\alpha/2)\)-quantile \(r^{\mathrm W}_{a,w}\). assumptions.ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization: \(\mathcal L((J_{i,a})_{1\le i\le M,\ a\in\{0,1\}}\mid(L_i)_{0\le i\le M},(Z_i)_{0\le i\le M},T,\mathcal T)=\prod_{i=1}^M\prod_{a=0}^1\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\)

snapshot.stmt: For a pair block \(b\), \(s_b(v_{b,r},a)=(v_{b,1-r},a)\) for \(r,a\in\{0,1\}\), with \(s_b\) fixing all outside members and relabelling complete records as in \(\texttt{def:group-action}\). Its arm-specific orbits are \(O_{b,0}=\{(v_{b,0},0),(v_{b,1},0)\}\) and \(O_{b,1}=\{(v_{b,0},1),(v_{b,1},1)\}\). If the target arm-\(1\) lifted law is supported on \(O_{b,1}\), an exclusive mate-control analogue supported on the realized mate's control state in \(O_{b,0}\) has a different orbit-mass vector, cannot satisfy exact joint balance, and is not a legal arm-\(1\) selector. Separately, if a legal implemented arm-\(1\) selector satisfies \(q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,1}(O_{b,1})=0\), then arm \(1\) satisfies the disjoint-orbit premise. Its zero-coverage conclusion applies when \(k\le M\); for \(k=M+1\), coverage is one by \(\texttt{prop:rank-boundary}\).

defs.def:pair-action: For pair block \(b\), \(s_b(v_{b,r},a)=(v_{b,1-r},a)\) for \(r,a\in\{0,1\}\); \(s_b\) fixes members outside block \(b\) and relabels \(D_i\) by \(\texttt{def:group-action}\). Thus \(O_{b,0}=\{(v_{b,0},0),(v_{b,1},0)\}\) and \(O_{b,1}=\{(v_{b,0},1),(v_{b,1},1)\}\).

assumptions.ass:observable-selector-kernel: \(\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\ge0,\quad\sum_{v\in\mathcal V}\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=1,\quad z_v\ne a\Rightarrow\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=0\)

snapshot.stmt: Let \(\mathcal V=\{v_{1,0},v_{1,1},v_{2,0},v_{2,1}\}\) and \(\mathcal G=S_2\times S_2\), with the generators in \(\texttt{def:pair-action}\). The four arm-specific orbits are \(O_{1,0},O_{1,1},O_{2,0},O_{2,1}\). If \(q_{\mathrm{tar}}\) concentrates on \(v_{1,0}\), its arm \(1\) orbit vector has mass \(1\) on \(O_{1,1}\). An exclusive mate-control analogue concentrated on \((v_{1,1},0)\) has mass \(1\) on \(O_{1,0}\), so the relevant stacked LP equations cannot both hold and the analogue is not a legal arm \(1\) selector. If a legal implemented selector has \(q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,1}(O_{1,1})=0\), then for \(k\le M\) the bounded witness has \(\mathcal C_w=\{0\}\) and coverage \(0\); for \(k=M+1\), it has \(\mathcal C_w=\mathbb R\) and coverage \(1\).

defs.def:pair-action: For pair block \(b\), \(s_b(v_{b,r},a)=(v_{b,1-r},a)\) for \(r,a\in\{0,1\}\); \(s_b\) fixes members outside block \(b\) and relabels \(D_i\) by \(\texttt{def:group-action}\). Thus \(O_{b,0}=\{(v_{b,0},0),(v_{b,1},0)\}\) and \(O_{b,1}=\{(v_{b,0},1),(v_{b,1},1)\}\).

assumptions.ass:observable-selector-kernel: \(\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\ge0,\quad\sum_{v\in\mathcal V}\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=1,\quad z_v\ne a\Rightarrow\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=0\)

snapshot.stmt: Let \(N=M+1\), \(k=\lceil N(1-\alpha/2)\rceil\), and let \(\rho_{M,\alpha}\) be defined by \(\texttt{def:finite-rank-error}\). Then \[\rho_{M,\alpha}=\frac{N-k}{N}=\frac{\lfloor N\alpha/2\rfloor}{N}\le\frac{\alpha}{2}.\] For every integer \(j\ge0\) whose displayed interval intersects \((0,1)\), \(\rho_{M,\alpha}=j/N\) exactly when \(\alpha\in[2j/N,2(j+1)/N)\). In particular, \(k=M+1\) if and only if \(\rho_{M,\alpha}=0\), equivalently \(0<\alpha<2/(M+1)\).

defs.def:finite-rank-error: For \(k=\lceil(M+1)(1-\alpha/2)\rceil\), define \[\rho_{M,\alpha}=\frac{M+1-k}{M+1}=\frac{\lfloor(M+1)\alpha/2\rfloor}{M+1}.\]
defs.def:rank-inversion: Set \(k=\lceil(M+1)(1-\alpha/2)\rceil\). For each \(a\in\{0,1\}\), compute \(S_{i,a}=|Y^{\mathrm{obs}}_{iJ_{i,a}}-\widehat m_a(X_{iJ_{i,a}})|\) for \(i\in\{1,\ldots,M\}\), take \(r_{a,(k)}\), set \(I_{a,w}=[\widehat m_a(X_{0T})-r_{a,(k)},\widehat m_a(X_{0T})+r_{a,(k)}]\cap[-K,K]\), and return \(\mathcal C_w=I_{1,w}-I_{0,w}\). If \(k=M+1\), set \(r_{a,(k)}=+\infty\), so the reported interval is \(I_{a,w}=[-K,K]\). For proof ranks only, append iid continuous uniforms and use lexicographic ranks; the reported closed intervals use weak inclusion. The associated exact one-arm rank error is \(\rho_{M,\alpha}\) from \(\texttt{def:finite-rank-error}\).

snapshot.stmt: For every fixed or prespecified \(w\), conditional on \(\mathcal T\), \[\Pr_P(\tau_T\in\mathcal C_w\mid\mathcal T)\ge\max\{0,1-2\rho_{M,\alpha}-\Delta_0(w)-\Delta_1(w)\}\] for every \(P\in\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\) and arbitrary fixed measurable trained predictor maps. Here \(\rho_{M,\alpha}=j/(M+1)\) on each staircase interval \(\alpha\in[2j/(M+1),2(j+1)/(M+1))\), so the elbows occur at \(\alpha=2j/(M+1)\). Consequently \(|\delta_{a,O}(w)|\le\Delta_a(w)\) for every \(a\in\{0,1\}\) and \(O\in\mathcal O\).

defs.def:finite-rank-error: For \(k=\lceil(M+1)(1-\alpha/2)\rceil\), define \[\rho_{M,\alpha}=\frac{M+1-k}{M+1}=\frac{\lfloor(M+1)\alpha/2\rfloor}{M+1}.\]
defs.def:rank-inversion: Set \(k=\lceil(M+1)(1-\alpha/2)\rceil\). For each \(a\in\{0,1\}\), compute \(S_{i,a}=|Y^{\mathrm{obs}}_{iJ_{i,a}}-\widehat m_a(X_{iJ_{i,a}})|\) for \(i\in\{1,\ldots,M\}\), take \(r_{a,(k)}\), set \(I_{a,w}=[\widehat m_a(X_{0T})-r_{a,(k)},\widehat m_a(X_{0T})+r_{a,(k)}]\cap[-K,K]\), and return \(\mathcal C_w=I_{1,w}-I_{0,w}\). If \(k=M+1\), set \(r_{a,(k)}=+\infty\), so the reported interval is \(I_{a,w}=[-K,K]\). For proof ranks only, append iid continuous uniforms and use lexicographic ranks; the reported closed intervals use weak inclusion. The associated exact one-arm rank error is \(\rho_{M,\alpha}\) from \(\texttt{def:finite-rank-error}\).

assumptions.ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization: \(\mathcal L((J_{i,a})_{1\le i\le M,\ a\in\{0,1\}}\mid(L_i)_{0\le i\le M},(Z_i)_{0\le i\le M},T,\mathcal T)=\prod_{i=1}^M\prod_{a=0}^1\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\)

snapshot.stmt: The functional \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)\) in \(\texttt{def:coverage-frontier}\) is the exact finite worst-coverage deficit over the stated class of bounded orbit-constant score maps; no equality with a supremum over all \(P\in\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\) is asserted. Writing \(\mathcal O_a=\{O\in\mathcal O:O\subseteq\mathcal U_a\}\) and \(m_a=|\mathcal O_a|\), its maximum is attained by enumerating pairs of total preorders on \(\mathcal O_0\) and \(\mathcal O_1\). Every preorder has a representative in \([0,K]\) with at most \(m_a\) levels, and the exact objective is a finite sum over coherent target members and calibration orbit-pair configurations. After the joint calibration orbit law is tabulated, a brute-force algorithm uses \(O\!\left(F_{m_0}F_{m_1}|\mathcal V|(m_0m_1)^M\right)\) arithmetic operations, where \(F_m\) is the number of total preorders on an \(m\)-element labelled set. Binary attainment is false: in an admissible four-pair instance with \(M=3\) and \(\alpha=1/2\), a four-level ordering has frontier objective \(24957/160000\), whereas every binary score pair has objective at most \(759109/5000000\), a strict gap of \(83189/20000000\). Under disjoint target and calibration orbit support in one arm and \(k\le M\), \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)=1-\alpha\); when \(k=M+1\), \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)=0\).

defs.def:pair-action: For pair block \(b\), \(s_b(v_{b,r},a)=(v_{b,1-r},a)\) for \(r,a\in\{0,1\}\); \(s_b\) fixes members outside block \(b\) and relabels \(D_i\) by \(\texttt{def:group-action}\). Thus \(O_{b,0}=\{(v_{b,0},0),(v_{b,1},0)\}\) and \(O_{b,1}=\{(v_{b,0},1),(v_{b,1},1)\}\).
defs.def:observable-selector-mixture: For every \(i\in\{1,\ldots,M\}\) and \(a\in\{0,1\}\), set \(\kappa_{w,a}=\sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_h\kappa_{h,a}\), draw \(J_{i,a}\sim\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\), set \(U_{i,a}=(J_{i,a},a)\), and set \(q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,a}=\sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_hq^{\mathrm{obs}}_{h,a}\).
defs.def:coverage-frontier: \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)=\max_{\substack{s_a:\mathcal O\to[0,K]\\a\in\{0,1\}}}\Big[(1-\alpha)-\Pr\{s_1(O_{T,1})\le Q_k(s_1(O_{1,1}),\ldots,s_1(O_{M,1}))\quad\text{and}\quad s_0(O_{T,0})\le Q_k(s_0(O_{1,0}),\ldots,s_0(O_{M,0}))\}\Big]_+\), where \(O_{i,a}\sim q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,a}\), \(O_{T,a}\sim q_{\mathrm{tar},a}\), the target orbits are the two lifts of one \(T\), and the calibration draws use \(\texttt{ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization}\). This functional is restricted to bounded orbit-constant score maps \(s_a\); it does not by definition equal a supremum over all laws in \(\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\).

assumptions.ass:observable-selector-kernel: \(\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\ge0,\quad\sum_{v\in\mathcal V}\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=1,\quad z_v\ne a\Rightarrow\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=0\)
assumptions.ass:selector-equivariance: \(\kappa_{h,a}(\pi_gv\mid gz)=\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\quad\forall g\in\mathcal G,\ h\in\mathcal H,\ a\in\{0,1\}\)
assumptions.ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization: \(\mathcal L((J_{i,a})_{1\le i\le M,\ a\in\{0,1\}}\mid(L_i)_{0\le i\le M},(Z_i)_{0\le i\le M},T,\mathcal T)=\prod_{i=1}^M\prod_{a=0}^1\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\)

snapshot.stmt: There exists \(w\in\Delta(\mathcal H)\) jointly orbit-balancing both arms if and only if \(b_{\mathrm{LP}}\in\operatorname{conv}\{A_{\cdot h}:h\in\mathcal H\}\). Equivalently, the stacked LP in \(\texttt{def:common-selector-feasibility-lp}\) is feasible. If the condition fails, a Farkas separating hyperplane separates \(b_{\mathrm{LP}}\) from that convex hull. Conditional on \(\mathcal T\), a fixed feasible \(w\), iid arrays, independent target selection, and selector auxiliary draws imply \(\Pr(S_{0,a}>r_{a,(k)}\mid\mathcal T)\le(M+1-k)/(M+1)\le\alpha/2\) for each arm; Bonferroni gives \(\Pr(\tau_T\in\mathcal C_w\mid\mathcal T)\ge1-\alpha\) without arm independence.

defs.def:rank-inversion: Set \(k=\lceil(M+1)(1-\alpha/2)\rceil\). For each \(a\in\{0,1\}\), compute \(S_{i,a}=|Y^{\mathrm{obs}}_{iJ_{i,a}}-\widehat m_a(X_{iJ_{i,a}})|\) for \(i\in\{1,\ldots,M\}\), take \(r_{a,(k)}\), set \(I_{a,w}=[\widehat m_a(X_{0T})-r_{a,(k)},\widehat m_a(X_{0T})+r_{a,(k)}]\cap[-K,K]\), and return \(\mathcal C_w=I_{1,w}-I_{0,w}\). If \(k=M+1\), set \(r_{a,(k)}=+\infty\), so the reported interval is \(I_{a,w}=[-K,K]\). For proof ranks only, append iid continuous uniforms and use lexicographic ranks; the reported closed intervals use weak inclusion. The associated exact one-arm rank error is \(\rho_{M,\alpha}\) from \(\texttt{def:finite-rank-error}\).
defs.def:finite-rank-error: For \(k=\lceil(M+1)(1-\alpha/2)\rceil\), define \[\rho_{M,\alpha}=\frac{M+1-k}{M+1}=\frac{\lfloor(M+1)\alpha/2\rfloor}{M+1}.\]

assumptions.ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization: \(\mathcal L((J_{i,a})_{1\le i\le M,\ a\in\{0,1\}}\mid(L_i)_{0\le i\le M},(Z_i)_{0\le i\le M},T,\mathcal T)=\prod_{i=1}^M\prod_{a=0}^1\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\)

snapshot.stmt: Conditional on \(\mathcal T\), orbit overlap implies \(\Pr_P(\tau_T\in\mathcal C^{\mathrm W}_w\mid\mathcal T)\ge1-\alpha\) for every fixed or prespecified \(w\), every \(P\in\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\), and arbitrary fixed measurable trained predictor maps. The proof obligation is a finite-support weighted-exchangeability lemma for calibration orbit-score pairs drawn under \(q_{a,O}\) and the target pair drawn under \(p_{a,O}\), followed by the infinity-augmented quantile armwise and Bonferroni. Invariance makes score laws coincide within an orbit, so orbit likelihood ratios suffice even with member-level selector singularity. Under joint balance every relevant \(\lambda_{a,O}=1\), recovering ordinary rank inversion.

defs.def:weighted-rank-inversion: For each \(a\in\{0,1\}\), place mass \(\lambda_{a,O_{i,a}}\) at every \(S_{i,a}\), place mass \(\lambda_{a,O_{T,a}}\) at \(+\infty\), normalize the total mass, and take its left-continuous \((1-\alpha/2)\)-quantile \(r^{\mathrm W}_{a,w}\). Set \(I^{\mathrm W}_{a,w}=[\widehat m_a(X_{0T})-r^{\mathrm W}_{a,w},\widehat m_a(X_{0T})+r^{\mathrm W}_{a,w}]\cap[-K,K]\), and return \(\mathcal C^{\mathrm W}_w=I^{\mathrm W}_{1,w}-I^{\mathrm W}_{0,w}\).
defs.def:rank-inversion: Set \(k=\lceil(M+1)(1-\alpha/2)\rceil\). For each \(a\in\{0,1\}\), compute \(S_{i,a}=|Y^{\mathrm{obs}}_{iJ_{i,a}}-\widehat m_a(X_{iJ_{i,a}})|\) for \(i\in\{1,\ldots,M\}\), take \(r_{a,(k)}\), set \(I_{a,w}=[\widehat m_a(X_{0T})-r_{a,(k)},\widehat m_a(X_{0T})+r_{a,(k)}]\cap[-K,K]\), and return \(\mathcal C_w=I_{1,w}-I_{0,w}\). The associated exact one-arm rank error is \(\rho_{M,\alpha}\) from \(\texttt{def:finite-rank-error}\).

assumptions.ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization: \(\mathcal L((J_{i,a})_{1\le i\le M,\ a\in\{0,1\}}\mid(L_i)_{0\le i\le M},(Z_i)_{0\le i\le M},T,\mathcal T)=\prod_{i=1}^M\prod_{a=0}^1\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\)

snapshot.stmt: If \(k=M+1\), then \(r_{0,(k)}=r_{1,(k)}=+\infty\), \(I_{0,w}=I_{1,w}=[-K,K]\), \(\mathcal C_w=[-2K,2K]\), and \(\Pr_P(\tau_T\in\mathcal C_w\mid\mathcal T)=1\) for every law and trained predictor maps.

defs.def:finite-rank-error: For \(k=\lceil(M+1)(1-\alpha/2)\rceil\), define \[\rho_{M,\alpha}=\frac{M+1-k}{M+1}=\frac{\lfloor(M+1)\alpha/2\rfloor}{M+1}.\]
defs.def:rank-inversion: Set \(k=\lceil(M+1)(1-\alpha/2)\rceil\). For each \(a\in\{0,1\}\), compute \(S_{i,a}=|Y^{\mathrm{obs}}_{iJ_{i,a}}-\widehat m_a(X_{iJ_{i,a}})|\) for \(i\in\{1,\ldots,M\}\), take \(r_{a,(k)}\), set \(I_{a,w}=[\widehat m_a(X_{0T})-r_{a,(k)},\widehat m_a(X_{0T})+r_{a,(k)}]\cap[-K,K]\), and return \(\mathcal C_w=I_{1,w}-I_{0,w}\). snapshot.stmt: Suppose \(k\le M\), equivalently \(\alpha\ge2/(M+1)\), and, for one arm \(a\), every target-supported orbit has zero \(q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,a}\) mass. Then \(\texttt{def:disjoint-orbit-law}\) belongs to \(\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\), all adverse-arm calibration scores equal \(0\), and \(r_{a,(k)}=0\). The other arm's scores and threshold equal \(0\), so \(\mathcal C_w=\{0\}\). The common target effect equals \(K\) for \(a=1\) and \(-K\) for \(a=0\), hence \(\Pr_P(\tau_T\in\mathcal C_w\mid\mathcal T)=0\).

defs.def:disjoint-orbit-law: Fix \(a\in\{0,1\}\). Set \(\widehat m_0=\widehat m_1=0\), set \(Y_v(a)=K\) on target-supported arm-\(a\) orbits, set \(Y_v(a)=0\) on every state with positive calibration mass and on remaining states, set \(Y_v(1-a)=0\), and randomize the orbit-constant prototype by a uniform \(g\in\mathcal G\) before drawing \(Z_i\sim\nu\).
defs.def:invariant-laws: \(\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}=\{P:P\circ(gL_i)^{-1}=P\quad\forall g\in\mathcal G,\quad\max_{v\in\mathcal V,\ a\in\{0,1\}}|Y_v(a)|\le K\}\).
defs.def:observable-selector-mixture: For every \(i\in\{1,\ldots,M\}\) and \(a\in\{0,1\}\), set \(\kappa_{w,a}=\sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_h\kappa_{h,a}\), draw \(J_{i,a}\sim\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\), set \(U_{i,a}=(J_{i,a},a)\), and set \(q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,a}=\sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_hq^{\mathrm{obs}}_{h,a}\).

snapshot.stmt: For every fixed or prespecified \(w\), conditional on \(\mathcal T\), \[\Pr_P(\tau_T\in\mathcal C_w\mid\mathcal T)\ge\max\{0,1-2\rho_{M,\alpha}-\Delta_0(w)-\Delta_1(w)\}\] for every \(P\in\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\) and arbitrary fixed measurable trained predictor maps. Here \(\rho_{M,\alpha}=j/(M+1)\) on each staircase interval \(\alpha\in[2j/(M+1),2(j+1)/(M+1))\), so the elbows occur at \(\alpha=2j/(M+1)\). Consequently \(|\delta_{a,O}(w)|\le\Delta_a(w)\) for every \(a\in\{0,1\}\) and \(O\in\mathcal O\).

defs.def:finite-rank-error: For \(k=\lceil(M+1)(1-\alpha/2)\rceil\), define \[\rho_{M,\alpha}=\frac{M+1-k}{M+1}=\frac{\lfloor(M+1)\alpha/2\rfloor}{M+1}.\]
defs.def:rank-inversion: Set \(k=\lceil(M+1)(1-\alpha/2)\rceil\). For each \(a\in\{0,1\}\), compute \(S_{i,a}=|Y^{\mathrm{obs}}_{iJ_{i,a}}-\widehat m_a(X_{iJ_{i,a}})|\) for \(i\in\{1,\ldots,M\}\), take \(r_{a,(k)}\), set \(I_{a,w}=[\widehat m_a(X_{0T})-r_{a,(k)},\widehat m_a(X_{0T})+r_{a,(k)}]\cap[-K,K]\), and return \(\mathcal C_w=I_{1,w}-I_{0,w}\). If \(k=M+1\), set \(r_{a,(k)}=+\infty\), so the reported interval is \(I_{a,w}=[-K,K]\). For proof ranks only, append iid continuous uniforms and use lexicographic ranks; the reported closed intervals use weak inclusion. The associated exact one-arm rank error is \(\rho_{M,\alpha}\) from \(\texttt{def:finite-rank-error}\).

assumptions.ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization: \(\mathcal L((J_{i,a})_{1\le i\le M,\ a\in\{0,1\}}\mid(L_i)_{0\le i\le M},(Z_i)_{0\le i\le M},T,\mathcal T)=\prod_{i=1}^M\prod_{a=0}^1\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\)

snapshot.stmt: The functional \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)\) in \(\texttt{def:coverage-frontier}\) is the exact finite worst-coverage deficit over the stated class of bounded orbit-constant score maps; no equality with a supremum over all \(P\in\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\) is asserted. Writing \(\mathcal O_a=\{O\in\mathcal O:O\subseteq\mathcal U_a\}\) and \(m_a=|\mathcal O_a|\), its maximum is attained by enumerating pairs of total preorders on \(\mathcal O_0\) and \(\mathcal O_1\). Every preorder has a representative in \([0,K]\) with at most \(m_a\) levels, and the exact objective is a finite sum over coherent target members and calibration orbit-pair configurations. After the joint calibration orbit law is tabulated, a brute-force algorithm uses \(O\!\left(F_{m_0}F_{m_1}|\mathcal V|(m_0m_1)^M\right)\) arithmetic operations, where \(F_m\) is the number of total preorders on an \(m\)-element labelled set. Binary attainment is false: in an admissible four-pair instance with \(M=3\) and \(\alpha=1/2\), a four-level ordering has frontier objective \(24957/160000\), whereas every binary score pair has objective at most \(759109/5000000\), a strict gap of \(83189/20000000\). Under disjoint target and calibration orbit support in one arm and \(k\le M\), \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)=1-\alpha\); when \(k=M+1\), \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)=0\).

defs.def:pair-action: For pair block \(b\), \(s_b(v_{b,r},a)=(v_{b,1-r},a)\) for \(r,a\in\{0,1\}\); \(s_b\) fixes members outside block \(b\) and relabels \(D_i\) by \(\texttt{def:group-action}\). Thus \(O_{b,0}=\{(v_{b,0},0),(v_{b,1},0)\}\) and \(O_{b,1}=\{(v_{b,0},1),(v_{b,1},1)\}\).
defs.def:observable-selector-mixture: For every \(i\in\{1,\ldots,M\}\) and \(a\in\{0,1\}\), set \(\kappa_{w,a}=\sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_h\kappa_{h,a}\), draw \(J_{i,a}\sim\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\), set \(U_{i,a}=(J_{i,a},a)\), and set \(q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,a}=\sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_hq^{\mathrm{obs}}_{h,a}\).
defs.def:coverage-frontier: \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)=\max_{\substack{s_a:\mathcal O\to[0,K]\\a\in\{0,1\}}}\Big[(1-\alpha)-\Pr\{s_1(O_{T,1})\le Q_k(s_1(O_{1,1}),\ldots,s_1(O_{M,1}))\quad\text{and}\quad s_0(O_{T,0})\le Q_k(s_0(O_{1,0}),\ldots,s_0(O_{M,0}))\}\Big]_+\), where \(O_{i,a}\sim q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,a}\), \(O_{T,a}\sim q_{\mathrm{tar},a}\), the target orbits are the two lifts of one \(T\), and the calibration draws use \(\texttt{ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization}\). This functional is restricted to bounded orbit-constant score maps \(s_a\); it does not by definition equal a supremum over all laws in \(\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\).

assumptions.ass:observable-selector-kernel: \(\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\ge0,\quad\sum_{v\in\mathcal V}\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=1,\quad z_v\ne a\Rightarrow\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=0\)
assumptions.ass:selector-equivariance: \(\kappa_{h,a}(\pi_gv\mid gz)=\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\quad\forall g\in\mathcal G,\ h\in\mathcal H,\ a\in\{0,1\}\)
assumptions.ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization: \(\mathcal L((J_{i,a})_{1\le i\le M,\ a\in\{0,1\}}\mid(L_i)_{0\le i\le M},(Z_i)_{0\le i\le M},T,\mathcal T)=\prod_{i=1}^M\prod_{a=0}^1\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\)

snapshot.stmt: There exists \(w\in\Delta(\mathcal H)\) jointly orbit-balancing both arms if and only if \(b_{\mathrm{LP}}\in\operatorname{conv}\{A_{\cdot h}:h\in\mathcal H\}\). Equivalently, the stacked LP in \(\texttt{def:common-selector-feasibility-lp}\) is feasible. If the condition fails, a Farkas separating hyperplane separates \(b_{\mathrm{LP}}\) from that convex hull. Conditional on \(\mathcal T\), a fixed feasible \(w\), iid arrays, independent target selection, and selector auxiliary draws imply \(\Pr(S_{0,a}>r_{a,(k)}\mid\mathcal T)\le(M+1-k)/(M+1)\le\alpha/2\) for each arm; Bonferroni gives \(\Pr(\tau_T\in\mathcal C_w\mid\mathcal T)\ge1-\alpha\) without arm independence.

defs.def:rank-inversion: Set \(k=\lceil(M+1)(1-\alpha/2)\rceil\). For each \(a\in\{0,1\}\), compute \(S_{i,a}=|Y^{\mathrm{obs}}_{iJ_{i,a}}-\widehat m_a(X_{iJ_{i,a}})|\) for \(i\in\{1,\ldots,M\}\), take \(r_{a,(k)}\), set \(I_{a,w}=[\widehat m_a(X_{0T})-r_{a,(k)},\widehat m_a(X_{0T})+r_{a,(k)}]\cap[-K,K]\), and return \(\mathcal C_w=I_{1,w}-I_{0,w}\). If \(k=M+1\), set \(r_{a,(k)}=+\infty\), so the reported interval is \(I_{a,w}=[-K,K]\). For proof ranks only, append iid continuous uniforms and use lexicographic ranks; the reported closed intervals use weak inclusion. The associated exact one-arm rank error is \(\rho_{M,\alpha}\) from \(\texttt{def:finite-rank-error}\).

snapshot.stmt: If \(k=M+1\), then \(r_{0,(k)}=r_{1,(k)}=+\infty\), \(I_{0,w}=I_{1,w}=[-K,K]\), \(\mathcal C_w=[-2K,2K]\), and \(\Pr_P(\tau_T\in\mathcal C_w\mid\mathcal T)=1\) for every law and trained predictor maps.

defs.def:finite-rank-error: For \(k=\lceil(M+1)(1-\alpha/2)\rceil\), define \[\rho_{M,\alpha}=\frac{M+1-k}{M+1}=\frac{\lfloor(M+1)\alpha/2\rfloor}{M+1}.\]

For each \(a\in\{0,1\}\), compute \(S_{i,a}=|Y^{\mathrm{obs}}_{iJ_{i,a}}-\widehat m_a(X_{iJ_{i,a}})|\) for \(i\in\{1,\ldots,M\}\), take \(r_{a,(k)}\), set \(I_{a,w}=[\widehat m_a(X_{0T})-r_{a,(k)},\widehat m_a(X_{0T})+r_{a,(k)}]\cap[-K,K]\), and return \(\mathcal C_w=I_{1,w}-I_{0,w}\). If \(k=M+1\), set \(r_{a,(k)}=+\infty\), so the reported interval is \(I_{a,w}=[-K,K]\). For proof ranks only, append iid continuous uniforms and use lexicographic ranks; the reported closed intervals use weak inclusion. The associated exact one-arm rank error is \(\rho_{M,\alpha}\) from \(\texttt{def:finite-rank-error}\).

snapshot.stmt: Suppose \(k\le M\), equivalently \(\alpha\ge2/(M+1)\), and, for one arm \(a\), every target-supported orbit has zero \(q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,a}\) mass. Then \(\texttt{def:disjoint-orbit-law}\) belongs to \(\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\), all adverse-arm calibration scores equal \(0\), and \(r_{a,(k)}=0\). The other arm's scores and threshold equal \(0\), so \(\mathcal C_w=\{0\}\). The common target effect equals \(K\) for \(a=1\) and \(-K\) for \(a=0\), hence \(\Pr_P(\tau_T\in\mathcal C_w\mid\mathcal T)=0\).

defs.def:disjoint-orbit-law: Fix \(a\in\{0,1\}\). Set \(\widehat m_0=\widehat m_1=0\), set \(Y_v(a)=K\) on target-supported arm-\(a\) orbits, set \(Y_v(a)=0\) on every state with positive calibration mass and on remaining states, set \(Y_v(1-a)=0\), and randomize the orbit-constant prototype by a uniform \(g\in\mathcal G\) before drawing \(Z_i\sim\nu\).
defs.def:invariant-laws: \(\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}=\{P:P\circ(gL_i)^{-1}=P\quad\forall g\in\mathcal G,\quad\max_{v\in\mathcal V,\ a\in\{0,1\}}|Y_v(a)|\le K\}\).
defs.def:observable-selector-mixture: For every \(i\in\{1,\ldots,M\}\) and \(a\in\{0,1\}\), set \(\kappa_{w,a}=\sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_h\kappa_{h,a}\), draw \(J_{i,a}\sim\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\), set \(U_{i,a}=(J_{i,a},a)\), and set \(q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,a}=\sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_hq^{\mathrm{obs}}_{h,a}\).
