assumptions.ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization: \(\mathcal L((J_{i,a})_{1\le i\le M,\ a\in\{0,1\}}\mid(L_i)_{0\le i\le M},(Z_i)_{0\le i\le M},T,\mathcal T)=\prod_{i=1}^M\prod_{a=0}^1\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\)

snapshot.stmt: For a pair block \(b\), \(s_b(v_{b,r},a)=(v_{b,1-r},a)\) for \(r,a\in\{0,1\}\), with \(s_b\) fixing all outside members and relabelling complete records as in \(\texttt{def:group-action}\). Its arm-specific orbits are \(O_{b,0}=\{(v_{b,0},0),(v_{b,1},0)\}\) and \(O_{b,1}=\{(v_{b,0},1),(v_{b,1},1)\}\). If the target arm-\(1\) lifted law is supported on \(O_{b,1}\), an exclusive mate-control analogue supported on the realized mate's control state in \(O_{b,0}\) has a different orbit-mass vector, cannot satisfy exact joint balance, and is not a legal arm-\(1\) selector. Separately, if a legal implemented arm-\(1\) selector satisfies \(q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,1}(O_{b,1})=0\), then arm \(1\) satisfies the disjoint-orbit premise. Its zero-coverage conclusion applies when \(k\le M\); for \(k=M+1\), coverage is one by \(\texttt{prop:rank-boundary}\).

defs.def:pair-action: For pair block \(b\), \(s_b(v_{b,r},a)=(v_{b,1-r},a)\) for \(r,a\in\{0,1\}\); \(s_b\) fixes members outside block \(b\) and relabels \(D_i\) by \(\texttt{def:group-action}\). Thus \(O_{b,0}=\{(v_{b,0},0),(v_{b,1},0)\}\) and \(O_{b,1}=\{(v_{b,0},1),(v_{b,1},1)\}\).

assumptions.ass:observable-selector-kernel: \(\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\ge0,\quad\sum_{v\in\mathcal V}\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=1,\quad z_v\ne a\Rightarrow\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=0\)

snapshot.stmt: Let \(\mathcal V=\{v_{1,0},v_{1,1},v_{2,0},v_{2,1}\}\) and \(\mathcal G=S_2\times S_2\), with the generators in \(\texttt{def:pair-action}\). The four arm-specific orbits are \(O_{1,0},O_{1,1},O_{2,0},O_{2,1}\). If \(q_{\mathrm{tar}}\) concentrates on \(v_{1,0}\), its arm \(1\) orbit vector has mass \(1\) on \(O_{1,1}\). An exclusive mate-control analogue concentrated on \((v_{1,1},0)\) has mass \(1\) on \(O_{1,0}\), so the relevant stacked LP equations cannot both hold and the analogue is not a legal arm \(1\) selector. If a legal implemented selector has \(q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,1}(O_{1,1})=0\), then for \(k\le M\) the bounded witness has \(\mathcal C_w=\{0\}\) and coverage \(0\); for \(k=M+1\), it has \(I_{0,w}=I_{1,w}=[-K,K]\), \(\mathcal C_w=[-2K,2K]\), and coverage \(1\).

defs.def:pair-action: For pair block \(b\), \(s_b(v_{b,r},a)=(v_{b,1-r},a)\) for \(r,a\in\{0,1\}\); \(s_b\) fixes members outside block \(b\) and relabels \(D_i\) by \(\texttt{def:group-action}\). Thus \(O_{b,0}=\{(v_{b,0},0),(v_{b,1},0)\}\) and \(O_{b,1}=\{(v_{b,0},1),(v_{b,1},1)\}\).

snapshot.stmt:
Let \(\boldsymbol\varepsilon=(\varepsilon_0,\varepsilon_1)\) be prespecified with \(\varepsilon_a>0\) and \(\varepsilon_0+\varepsilon_1\le\alpha\), put \(N=M+1\), and define \(k_a=\lceil N(1-\varepsilon_a)\rceil\). Then, for each \(a\in\{0,1\}\),
\[
\rho_{M,\varepsilon_a}=\frac{N-k_a}{N}=\frac{\lfloor N\varepsilon_a\rfloor}{N}\le\varepsilon_a.
\]
For every integer \(j\ge0\) whose displayed interval intersects \((0,1)\), \(\rho_{M,\varepsilon_a}=j/N\) exactly when \(\varepsilon_a\in[j/N,(j+1)/N)\). Thus the two-dimensional staircase has coordinate elbows at \(\varepsilon_a=j/(M+1)\), and \(k_a=M+1\) exactly when \(0<\varepsilon_a<1/(M+1)\). Under \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), one has \(k_0=k_1=k\) and \(\rho_{M,\varepsilon_0}=\rho_{M,\varepsilon_1}=\rho_{M,\alpha}\).

defs.def:finite-rank-error: For \(k=\lceil(M+1)(1-\alpha/2)\rceil\), define \[\rho_{M,\alpha}=\frac{M+1-k}{M+1}=\frac{\lfloor(M+1)\alpha/2\rfloor}{M+1}.\]

For each \(a\in\{0,1\}\), compute \(S_{i,a}=|Y^{\mathrm{obs}}_{iJ_{i,a}}-\widehat m_a(X_{iJ_{i,a}})|\) for \(i\in\{1,\ldots,M\}\), take \(r_{a,(k)}\), set \(I_{a,w}=[\widehat m_a(X_{0T})-r_{a,(k)},\widehat m_a(X_{0T})+r_{a,(k)}]\cap[-K,K]\), and return \(\mathcal C_w=I_{1,w}-I_{0,w}\). If \(k=M+1\), set \(r_{a,(k)}=+\infty\), so the reported interval is \(I_{a,w}=[-K,K]\). For proof ranks only, append iid continuous uniforms and use lexicographic ranks; the reported closed intervals use weak inclusion. The associated exact one-arm rank error is \(\rho_{M,\alpha}\) from \(\texttt{def:finite-rank-error}\).

snapshot.stmt:
For every fixed or prespecified \(w\), conditional on \(\mathcal T\), the asymmetric ordinary rank construction satisfies
\[
\Pr_P\!\left\{\tau_T\in\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w\mid\mathcal T\right\}
\ge
\max\!\left\{0,1-\rho_{M,\varepsilon_0}-\rho_{M,\varepsilon_1}-\Delta_0(w)-\Delta_1(w)\right\}
\]
for every \(P\in\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\) and arbitrary fixed measurable trained predictor maps. The coordinate elbows are \(\varepsilon_a=j/(M+1)\), and \(|\delta_{a,O}(w)|\le\Delta_a(w)\) for every arm and orbit. On the diagonal \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), this becomes the symmetric bound \(\max\{0,1-2\rho_{M,\alpha}-\Delta_0(w)-\Delta_1(w)\}\).

defs.def:finite-rank-error: For \(k=\lceil(M+1)(1-\alpha/2)\rceil\), define \[\rho_{M,\alpha}=\frac{M+1-k}{M+1}=\frac{\lfloor(M+1)\alpha/2\rfloor}{M+1}.\]

snapshot.stmt: For each arm \(a\), if \(k_a=M+1\), then \(r_{a,(k_a)}=+\infty\) and \(I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}=[-K,K]\). If \(k_0=M+1\) and \(k_1\le M\), then \(\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w=I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{1,w}-[-K,K]\); if \(k_1=M+1\) and \(k_0\le M\), then \(\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w=[-K,K]-I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{0,w}\). If both \(k_0=k_1=M+1\), then \(\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w=[-2K,2K]\) and its conditional coverage is one for every law and trained predictor maps. On the symmetric diagonal, either both arms are at the boundary or neither is, and this reduces to \(\texttt{prop:rank-boundary}\).

snapshot.stmt: Fix an arm \(a\) and suppose every target-supported arm-\(a\) orbit has zero \(q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,a}\) mass. Under \(\texttt{def:disjoint-orbit-law}\), the latent law belongs to \(\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\), every selected calibration score in both arms is zero, and the common target effect is \(K\) if \(a=1\) and \(-K\) if \(a=0\). If \(k_0\le M\) and \(k_1\le M\), then \(I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{0,w}=I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{1,w}=\{0\}\), \(\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w=\{0\}\), and coverage is zero. If exactly one \(k_c=M+1\), then the boundary arm interval is \([-K,K]\), the other is \(\{0\}\), \(\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w=[-K,K]\), and this witness has coverage one. If both \(k_0=k_1=M+1\), then \(\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w=[-2K,2K]\) and this witness again has coverage one. Moreover \(k_c\le M\) is equivalent to \(\varepsilon_c\ge1/(M+1)\). The symmetric choice recovers \(\texttt{prop:disjoint-orbit-witness}\).

The associated exact one-arm rank error is \(\rho_{M,\alpha}\) from \(\texttt{def:finite-rank-error}\).

assumptions.ass:observable-selector-kernel: \(\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\ge0,\quad\sum_{v\in\mathcal V}\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=1,\quad z_v\ne a\Rightarrow\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=0\)
assumptions.ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization: \(\mathcal L((J_{i,a})_{1\le i\le M,\ a\in\{0,1\}}\mid(L_i)_{0\le i\le M},(Z_i)_{0\le i\le M},T,\mathcal T)=\prod_{i=1}^M\prod_{a=0}^1\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\)

snapshot.stmt: The pair-swap and mate-control legality conclusions of \(\texttt{prop:s2-reduction}\) remain unchanged under the asymmetric budget. If the target arm-\(1\) lift is supported on \(O_{b,1}\) and a legal implemented arm-\(1\) selector has \(q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,1}(O_{b,1})=0\), the asymmetric disjoint-orbit witness has zero coverage when both \(k_0,k_1\le M\), has effect set \([-K,K]\) and coverage one when exactly one rank equals \(M+1\), and has effect set \([-2K,2K]\) and coverage one when both ranks equal \(M+1\). The symmetric choice recovers \(\texttt{prop:s2-reduction}\).

defs.def:pair-action: For pair block \(b\), \(s_b(v_{b,r},a)=(v_{b,1-r},a)\) for \(r,a\in\{0,1\}\); \(s_b\) fixes members outside block \(b\) and relabels \(D_i\) by \(\texttt{def:group-action}\). Thus \(O_{b,0}=\{(v_{b,0},0),(v_{b,1},0)\}\) and \(O_{b,1}=\{(v_{b,0},1),(v_{b,1},1)\}\).

assumptions.ass:observable-selector-kernel: \(\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\ge0,\quad\sum_{v\in\mathcal V}\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=1,\quad z_v\ne a\Rightarrow\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=0\)

snapshot.stmt: In the two-pair instance of \(\texttt{prop:two-pair-sanity-check}\), the four arm-specific orbits and the mate-control LP and legality failures are unchanged under the asymmetric budget. If a legal selector has \(q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,1}(O_{1,1})=0\), the bounded witness has \(\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w=\{0\}\) and coverage zero when both ranks are finite, \(\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w=[-K,K]\) and coverage one when exactly one rank is at the boundary, and \(\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w=[-2K,2K]\) and coverage one when both ranks are at the boundary. The symmetric choice recovers the frozen two-pair calculation.

defs.def:pair-action: For pair block \(b\), \(s_b(v_{b,r},a)=(v_{b,1-r},a)\) for \(r,a\in\{0,1\}\); \(s_b\) fixes members outside block \(b\) and relabels \(D_i\) by \(\texttt{def:group-action}\). Thus \(O_{b,0}=\{(v_{b,0},0),(v_{b,1},0)\}\) and \(O_{b,1}=\{(v_{b,0},1),(v_{b,1},1)\}\).

assumptions.ass:observable-selector-kernel: \(\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\ge0,\quad\sum_{v\in\mathcal V}\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=1,\quad z_v\ne a\Rightarrow\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=0\)

snapshot.stmt: The functional \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)\) in \(\texttt{def:coverage-frontier}\) is the exact finite worst-coverage deficit over the stated class of bounded orbit-constant score maps for armwise ranks \(k_0,k_1\); no equality with a supremum over all \(P\in\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\) is asserted. Writing \(\mathcal O_a=\{O\in\mathcal O:O\subseteq\mathcal U_a\}\) and \(m_a=|\mathcal O_a|\), its maximum is attained by enumerating pairs of total preorders. Every preorder has a representative in \([0,K]\) with at most \(m_a\) levels. After tabulating the joint calibration orbit law, exact evaluation takes \(O\!\left(F_{m_0}F_{m_1}|\mathcal V|(m_0m_1)^M\right)\) arithmetic operations. Binary attainment remains false because the symmetric admissible instance \(M=3\), \(\alpha=1/2\), \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=1/4\) has a four-level objective \(24957/160000\), while every binary pair has objective at most \(759109/5000000\), a gap \(83189/20000000\). If target and calibration orbit supports are disjoint in an arm \(a\) with \(k_a\le M\), then \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)=1-\alpha\). If \(k_a=M+1\), the frontier reduces exactly to the other arm's rank event; if both ranks equal \(M+1\), the frontier is zero. On the diagonal \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), this is \(\texttt{thm:sharp-orbit-coverage-frontier}\).

defs.def:pair-action: For pair block \(b\), \(s_b(v_{b,r},a)=(v_{b,1-r},a)\) for \(r,a\in\{0,1\}\); \(s_b\) fixes members outside block \(b\) and relabels \(D_i\) by \(\texttt{def:group-action}\). Thus \(O_{b,0}=\{(v_{b,0},0),(v_{b,1},0)\}\) and \(O_{b,1}=\{(v_{b,0},1),(v_{b,1},1)\}\).
defs.def:observable-selector-mixture: For every \(i\in\{1,\ldots,M\}\) and \(a\in\{0,1\}\), set \(\kappa_{w,a}=\sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_h\kappa_{h,a}\), draw \(J_{i,a}\sim\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\), set \(U_{i,a}=(J_{i,a},a)\), and set \(q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,a}=\sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_hq^{\mathrm{obs}}_{h,a}\).
defs.def:coverage-frontier: \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)=\max_{\substack{s_a:\mathcal O\to[0,K]\\a\in\{0,1\}}}\Big[(1-\alpha)-\Pr\{s_1(O_{T,1})\le Q_k(s_1(O_{1,1}),\ldots,s_1(O_{M,1}))\quad\text{and}\quad s_0(O_{T,0})\le Q_k(s_0(O_{1,0}),\ldots,s_0(O_{M,0}))\}\Big]_+\), where \(O_{i,a}\sim q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,a}\), \(O_{T,a}\sim q_{\mathrm{tar},a}\), the target orbits are the two lifts of one \(T\), and the calibration draws use \(\texttt{ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization}\). This functional is restricted to bounded orbit-constant score maps \(s_a\); it does not by definition equal a supremum over all laws in \(\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\).

assumptions.ass:observable-selector-kernel: \(\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\ge0,\quad\sum_{v\in\mathcal V}\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=1,\quad z_v\ne a\Rightarrow\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=0\)
assumptions.ass:selector-equivariance: \(\kappa_{h,a}(\pi_gv\mid gz)=\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\quad\forall g\in\mathcal G,\ h\in\mathcal H,\ a\in\{0,1\}\)
assumptions.ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization: \(\mathcal L((J_{i,a})_{1\le i\le M,\ a\in\{0,1\}}\mid(L_i)_{0\le i\le M},(Z_i)_{0\le i\le M},T,\mathcal T)=\prod_{i=1}^M\prod_{a=0}^1\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\)

snapshot.stmt: Let \(\epsilon_0,\epsilon_1>0\) be prespecified and satisfy \(\epsilon_0+\epsilon_1\le\alpha\), and let arm \(a\)'s weighted threshold in \(\texttt{def:weighted-rank-inversion}\) use its left-continuous \((1-\epsilon_a)\)-quantile. Conditional on \(\mathcal T\), orbit overlap implies \[\Pr_P(\tau_T\in\mathcal C^{\mathrm W}_w\mid\mathcal T)\ge1-\epsilon_0-\epsilon_1\ge1-\alpha\] for every fixed or prespecified \(w\), every \(P\in\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\), and arbitrary fixed measurable trained predictor maps. Invariance makes score laws coincide within an orbit, so the orbit likelihood ratios suffice even with member-level selector singularity. Under joint balance, every relevant \(\lambda_{a,O}=1\), and \(r^{\mathrm W}_{a,w}=r_{a,(k_a)}\) for \(k_a=\lceil(M+1)(1-\epsilon_a)\rceil\), including the \(+\infty\) clause when \(k_a=M+1\); hence weighted inversion recovers ordinary arm-specific rank inversion. Taking \(\epsilon_0=\epsilon_1=\alpha/2\) gives \(k_0=k_1=\lceil(M+1)(1-\alpha/2)\rceil\) and recovers the original symmetric statement and formulas.

defs.def:weighted-rank-inversion: Prespecify \(\boldsymbol\varepsilon=(\varepsilon_0,\varepsilon_1)\) with \(\varepsilon_a>0\) and \(\varepsilon_0+\varepsilon_1\le\alpha\). For each arm \(a\), place mass \(\lambda_{a,O_{i,a}}\) at each \(S_{i,a}\), place mass \(\lambda_{a,O_{T,a}}\) at \(+\infty\), normalize the total mass, and take its left-continuous \((1-\varepsilon_a)\)-quantile \(r^{\mathrm W}_{a,w}\). Set \(I^{\mathrm W}_{a,w}=[\widehat m_a(X_{0T})-r^{\mathrm W}_{a,w},\widehat m_a(X_{0T})+r^{\mathrm W}_{a,w}]\cap[-K,K]\), and return \(\mathcal C^{\mathrm W}_w=I^{\mathrm W}_{1,w}-I^{\mathrm W}_{0,w}\). Under \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), this is the previous symmetric weighted construction. Thus the declared estimand functional \(\mathcal C^{\mathrm W}_w\) now denotes the arm-specific weighted procedure while retaining its equal-split specialization.
defs.def:rank-inversion: Prespecify \(\boldsymbol\varepsilon=(\varepsilon_0,\varepsilon_1)\) with \(\varepsilon_a>0\) and \(\varepsilon_0+\varepsilon_1\le\alpha\), and set \(k_a=\lceil(M+1)(1-\varepsilon_a)\rceil\). For each \(a\in\{0,1\}\), compute \(S_{i,a}=|Y^{\mathrm{obs}}_{iJ_{i,a}}-\widehat m_a(X_{iJ_{i,a}})|\), take \(r_{a,(k_a)}\), set \(I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}=[\widehat m_a(X_{0T})-r_{a,(k_a)},\widehat m_a(X_{0T})+r_{a,(k_a)}]\cap[-K,K]\), and return \(\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w=I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{1,w}-I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{0,w}\). If \(k_a=M+1\), set \(r_{a,(k_a)}=+\infty\), so \(I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}=[-K,K]\). For proof ranks only, append independent continuous uniforms and use lexicographic ranks; reported closed intervals use weak inclusion. Under \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), define \(k=k_0=k_1\), \(r_{a,(k)}=r_{a,(k_a)}\), \(I_{a,w}=I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}\), and \(\mathcal C_w=\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w\), recovering the symmetric construction.

assumptions.ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization: \(\mathcal L((J_{i,a})_{1\le i\le M,\ a\in\{0,1\}}\mid(L_i)_{0\le i\le M},(Z_i)_{0\le i\le M},T,\mathcal T)=\prod_{i=1}^M\prod_{a=0}^1\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\)

snapshot.stmt:
Assume that \(\mathcal H\) is finite and nonempty. The minimum \(D_*\) in \(\texttt{def:minimum-imbalance-common-selector}\) is attained, so \(\mathcal W_*\) is nonempty, and the displayed absolute-value linear program has value \(D_*\). Its quantitative dual is attained and satisfies the strong-duality identity
\[
D_*=\max_{\lVert y\rVert_\infty\le1/2}\left\{\min_{h\in\mathcal H}y^\top A_{\cdot h}-y^\top b_{\mathrm{LP}}\right\}.
\]
If \(D_*>0\), the negative of any maximizing dual vector is a Farkas separating direction with margin \(D_*\). Every \(w_*\in\mathcal W_*\) is computable solely from the known randomization law, target rule, and selector library before scores are observed, and hence is prespecified. Moreover, \(D_*=0\) if and only if the exact joint-balance linear program in \(\texttt{def:common-selector-feasibility-lp}\) is feasible. For every prespecified \(\boldsymbol\varepsilon=(\varepsilon_0,\varepsilon_1)\) with \(\varepsilon_a>0\) and \(\varepsilon_0+\varepsilon_1\le\alpha\), every \(P\in\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\), and arbitrary fixed measurable trained predictor maps, conditional on \(\mathcal T\),
\[
\Pr_P\!\left(\tau_T\in\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{w_*}\mid\mathcal T\right)\ge\max\!\left\{0,1-\rho_{M,\varepsilon_0}-\rho_{M,\varepsilon_1}-D_*\right\}.
\]
This numerical certificate weakly dominates the corresponding certificate for every fixed common \(w\in\Delta(\mathcal H)\), and the dominance is strict whenever \(D_*<\Delta_0(w)+\Delta_1(w)\) and \(1-\rho_{M,\varepsilon_0}-\rho_{M,\varepsilon_1}-D_*>0\). On the equal-split diagonal \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\),
\[
\Pr_P(\tau_T\in\mathcal C_{w_*}\mid\mathcal T)\ge\max\{0,1-2\rho_{M,\alpha}-D_*\}.
\]

defs.def:observable-selector-mixture: For every \(i\in\{1,\ldots,M\}\) and \(a\in\{0,1\}\), set \(\kappa_{w,a}=\sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_h\kappa_{h,a}\), draw \(J_{i,a}\sim\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\), set \(U_{i,a}=(J_{i,a},a)\), and set \(q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,a}=\sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_hq^{\mathrm{obs}}_{h,a}\).
defs.def:invariant-laws: \(\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}=\{P:P\circ(gL_i)^{-1}=P\quad\forall g\in\mathcal G,\quad\max_{v\in\mathcal V,\ a\in\{0,1\}}|Y_v(a)|\le K\}\).
defs.def:rank-inversion: Prespecify \(\boldsymbol\varepsilon=(\varepsilon_0,\varepsilon_1)\) with \(\varepsilon_a>0\) and \(\varepsilon_0+\varepsilon_1\le\alpha\), and set \(k_a=\lceil(M+1)(1-\varepsilon_a)\rceil\). For each \(a\in\{0,1\}\), compute \(S_{i,a}=|Y^{\mathrm{obs}}_{iJ_{i,a}}-\widehat m_a(X_{iJ_{i,a}})|\), take \(r_{a,(k_a)}\), set \(I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}=[\widehat m_a(X_{0T})-r_{a,(k_a)},\widehat m_a(X_{0T})+r_{a,(k_a)}]\cap[-K,K]\), and return \(\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w=I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{1,w}-I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{0,w}\). If \(k_a=M+1\), set \(r_{a,(k_a)}=+\infty\), so \(I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}=[-K,K]\). For proof ranks only, append independent continuous uniforms and use lexicographic ranks; reported closed intervals use weak inclusion. Under \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), define \(k=k_0=k_1\), \(r_{a,(k)}=r_{a,(k_a)}\), \(I_{a,w}=I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}\), and \(\mathcal C_w=\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w\), recovering the symmetric construction.
defs.def:finite-rank-error:
For \(N=M+1\), prespecified \(\boldsymbol\varepsilon=(\varepsilon_0,\varepsilon_1)\), and \(k_a=\lceil N(1-\varepsilon_a)\rceil\), define
\[
\rho_{M,\varepsilon_a}=\frac{N-k_a}{N}=\frac{\lfloor N\varepsilon_a\rfloor}{N},\qquad a\in\{0,1\}.
\]
Under \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), write their common value as
\[
\rho_{M,\alpha}=\frac{M+1-k}{M+1}=\frac{\lfloor(M+1)\alpha/2\rfloor}{M+1}.
\]

assumptions.ass:observable-selector-kernel: \(\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\ge0,\quad\sum_{v\in\mathcal V}\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=1,\quad z_v\ne a\Rightarrow\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=0\)

snapshot.stmt: The functional \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)\) in \(\texttt{def:coverage-frontier}\) is the exact finite worst-coverage deficit over the stated class of bounded orbit-constant score maps; no equality with a supremum over all \(P\in\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\) is asserted. Writing \(\mathcal O_a=\{O\in\mathcal O:O\subseteq\mathcal U_a\}\) and \(m_a=|\mathcal O_a|\), its maximum is attained by enumerating pairs of total preorders on \(\mathcal O_0\) and \(\mathcal O_1\). Every preorder has a representative in \([0,K]\) with at most \(m_a\) levels, and the exact objective is a finite sum over coherent target members and calibration orbit-pair configurations. After the joint calibration orbit law is tabulated, a brute-force algorithm uses \(O\!\left(F_{m_0}F_{m_1}|\mathcal V|(m_0m_1)^M\right)\) arithmetic operations, where \(F_m\) is the number of total preorders on an \(m\)-element labelled set. Binary attainment is false: in an admissible four-pair instance with \(M=3\) and \(\alpha=1/2\), a four-level ordering has frontier objective \(24957/160000\), whereas every binary score pair has objective at most \(759109/5000000\), a strict gap of \(83189/20000000\). Under disjoint target and calibration orbit support in one arm and \(k\le M\), \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)=1-\alpha\); when \(k=M+1\), \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)=0\).

defs.def:pair-action: For pair block \(b\), \(s_b(v_{b,r},a)=(v_{b,1-r},a)\) for \(r,a\in\{0,1\}\); \(s_b\) fixes members outside block \(b\) and relabels \(D_i\) by \(\texttt{def:group-action}\). Thus \(O_{b,0}=\{(v_{b,0},0),(v_{b,1},0)\}\) and \(O_{b,1}=\{(v_{b,0},1),(v_{b,1},1)\}\).
defs.def:observable-selector-mixture: For every \(i\in\{1,\ldots,M\}\) and \(a\in\{0,1\}\), set \(\kappa_{w,a}=\sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_h\kappa_{h,a}\), draw \(J_{i,a}\sim\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\), set \(U_{i,a}=(J_{i,a},a)\), and set \(q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,a}=\sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_hq^{\mathrm{obs}}_{h,a}\).
defs.def:coverage-frontier:
For prespecified \(\boldsymbol\varepsilon=(\varepsilon_0,\varepsilon_1)\), define
\[
\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)=\max_{\substack{s_a:\mathcal O\to[0,K]\\a\in\{0,1\}}}\left[(1-\alpha)-\Pr\left\{s_1(O_{T,1})\le Q_{k_1}(s_1(O_{1,1}),\ldots,s_1(O_{M,1}))\ \text{and}\ s_0(O_{T,0})\le Q_{k_0}(s_0(O_{1,0}),\ldots,s_0(O_{M,0}))\right\}\right]_+.
\]
Here \(O_{i,a}\sim q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,a}\), the target orbits are the two lifts of the one common \(T\sim q_{\mathrm{tar}}\), and the calibration draws use \(\texttt{ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization}\), including their within-replicate cross-arm coupling. The functional is restricted to bounded orbit-constant score maps. Under \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), set \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)=\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)\), recovering the symmetric frontier.

assumptions.ass:observable-selector-kernel: \(\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\ge0,\quad\sum_{v\in\mathcal V}\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=1,\quad z_v\ne a\Rightarrow\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=0\)
assumptions.ass:selector-equivariance: \(\kappa_{h,a}(\pi_gv\mid gz)=\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\quad\forall g\in\mathcal G,\ h\in\mathcal H,\ a\in\{0,1\}\)
assumptions.ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization: \(\mathcal L((J_{i,a})_{1\le i\le M,\ a\in\{0,1\}}\mid(L_i)_{0\le i\le M},(Z_i)_{0\le i\le M},T,\mathcal T)=\prod_{i=1}^M\prod_{a=0}^1\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\)

Writing \(\mathcal O_a=\{O\in\mathcal O:O\subseteq\mathcal U_a\}\) and \(m_a=|\mathcal O_a|\), its maximum is attained by enumerating pairs of total preorders on \(\mathcal O_0\) and \(\mathcal O_1\). After the joint calibration orbit law is tabulated, a brute-force algorithm uses \(O\!\left(F_{m_0}F_{m_1}|\mathcal V|(m_0m_1)^M\right)\) arithmetic operations, where \(F_m\) is the number of total preorders on an \(m\)-element labelled set. Binary attainment is false: in an admissible four-pair instance with \(M=3\) and \(\alpha=1/2\), a four-level ordering has frontier objective \(24957/160000\), whereas every binary score pair has objective at most \(759109/5000000\), a strict gap of \(83189/20000000\). Under disjoint target and calibration orbit support in one arm and \(k\le M\), \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)=1-\alpha\); when \(k=M+1\), \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)=0\).

defs.def:pair-action: For pair block \(b\), \(s_b(v_{b,r},a)=(v_{b,1-r},a)\) for \(r,a\in\{0,1\}\); \(s_b\) fixes members outside block \(b\) and relabels \(D_i\) by \(\texttt{def:group-action}\). Thus \(O_{b,0}=\{(v_{b,0},0),(v_{b,1},0)\}\) and \(O_{b,1}=\{(v_{b,0},1),(v_{b,1},1)\}\).
defs.def:observable-selector-mixture: For every \(i\in\{1,\ldots,M\}\) and \(a\in\{0,1\}\), set \(\kappa_{w,a}=\sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_h\kappa_{h,a}\), draw \(J_{i,a}\sim\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\), set \(U_{i,a}=(J_{i,a},a)\), and set \(q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,a}=\sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_hq^{\mathrm{obs}}_{h,a}\).
defs.def:coverage-frontier:
For prespecified \(\boldsymbol\varepsilon=(\varepsilon_0,\varepsilon_1)\), define
\[
\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)=\max_{\substack{s_a:\mathcal O\to[0,K]\\a\in\{0,1\}}}\left[(1-\alpha)-\Pr\left\{s_1(O_{T,1})\le Q_{k_1}(s_1(O_{1,1}),\ldots,s_1(O_{M,1}))\ \text{and}\ s_0(O_{T,0})\le Q_{k_0}(s_0(O_{1,0}),\ldots,s_0(O_{M,0}))\right\}\right]_+.
\]
Here \(O_{i,a}\sim q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,a}\), the target orbits are the two lifts of the one common \(T\sim q_{\mathrm{tar}}\), and the calibration draws use \(\texttt{ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization}\), including their within-replicate cross-arm coupling. The functional is restricted to bounded orbit-constant score maps. Under \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), set \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)=\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)\), recovering the symmetric frontier.
defs.def:finite-rank-error:
For \(N=M+1\), prespecified \(\boldsymbol\varepsilon=(\varepsilon_0,\varepsilon_1)\), and \(k_a=\lceil N(1-\varepsilon_a)\rceil\), define
\[
\rho_{M,\varepsilon_a}=\frac{N-k_a}{N}=\frac{\lfloor N\varepsilon_a\rfloor}{N},\qquad a\in\{0,1\}.
\]
Under \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), write their common value as
\[
\rho_{M,\alpha}=\frac{M+1-k}{M+1}=\frac{\lfloor(M+1)\alpha/2\rfloor}{M+1}.
\]
defs.def:rank-inversion: Prespecify \(\boldsymbol\varepsilon=(\varepsilon_0,\varepsilon_1)\) with \(\varepsilon_a>0\) and \(\varepsilon_0+\varepsilon_1\le\alpha\), and set \(k_a=\lceil(M+1)(1-\varepsilon_a)\rceil\). For each \(a\in\{0,1\}\), compute \(S_{i,a}=|Y^{\mathrm{obs}}_{iJ_{i,a}}-\widehat m_a(X_{iJ_{i,a}})|\), take \(r_{a,(k_a)}\), set \(I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}=[\widehat m_a(X_{0T})-r_{a,(k_a)},\widehat m_a(X_{0T})+r_{a,(k_a)}]\cap[-K,K]\), and return \(\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w=I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{1,w}-I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{0,w}\). If \(k_a=M+1\), set \(r_{a,(k_a)}=+\infty\), so \(I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}=[-K,K]\). For proof ranks only, append independent continuous uniforms and use lexicographic ranks; reported closed intervals use weak inclusion. Under \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), define \(k=k_0=k_1\), \(r_{a,(k)}=r_{a,(k_a)}\), \(I_{a,w}=I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}\), and \(\mathcal C_w=\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w\), recovering the symmetric construction.

assumptions.ass:observable-selector-kernel: \(\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\ge0,\quad\sum_{v\in\mathcal V}\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=1,\quad z_v\ne a\Rightarrow\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=0\)
assumptions.ass:selector-equivariance: \(\kappa_{h,a}(\pi_gv\mid gz)=\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\quad\forall g\in\mathcal G,\ h\in\mathcal H,\ a\in\{0,1\}\)
assumptions.ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization: \(\mathcal L((J_{i,a})_{1\le i\le M,\ a\in\{0,1\}}\mid(L_i)_{0\le i\le M},(Z_i)_{0\le i\le M},T,\mathcal T)=\prod_{i=1}^M\prod_{a=0}^1\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\)

snapshot.stmt: The functional \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)\) in \(\texttt{def:coverage-frontier}\) is the exact finite worst-coverage deficit over the stated class of bounded orbit-constant score maps for armwise ranks \(k_0,k_1\); no equality with a supremum over all \(P\in\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\) is asserted. Writing \(\mathcal O_a=\{O\in\mathcal O:O\subseteq\mathcal U_a\}\) and \(m_a=|\mathcal O_a|\), its maximum is attained by enumerating pairs of total preorders. After tabulating the joint calibration orbit law, exact evaluation takes \(O\!\left(F_{m_0}F_{m_1}|\mathcal V|(m_0m_1)^M\right)\) arithmetic operations. snapshot.stmt: The functional \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)\) in \(\texttt{def:coverage-frontier}\) is the exact finite worst-coverage deficit over the stated class of bounded orbit-constant score maps for armwise ranks \(k_0,k_1\); no equality with a supremum over all \(P\in\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\) is asserted. Writing \(\mathcal O_a=\{O\in\mathcal O:O\subseteq\mathcal U_a\}\), \(m_a=|\mathcal O_a|\), and letting \(F_m\) denote the number of total preorders on an \(m\)-element labelled set, its maximum is attained by enumerating pairs of total preorders. Every preorder has a representative in \([0,K]\) with at most \(m_a\) levels. After tabulating the joint calibration orbit law, if \(k_0,k_1\le M\), full enumeration uses \(O\!\left(F_{m_0}F_{m_1}|\mathcal V|\{m_0m_1+Mk_0k_1\}\right)\) arithmetic operations and \(O(k_0k_1)\) working memory. If exactly one rank \(k_b\) equals \(M+1\), put \(c=1-b\), so \(k_c\le M\). For a fixed nonboundary score map \(s_c\), define \(\theta(O_t)=\sum_{O\in\mathcal O_c:s_c(O)<s_c(O_t)}q_{c,O}\). The exact rank-event probability is \[\sum_{O_t\in\mathcal O_c}p_{c,O_t}\Pr\!\left\{\operatorname{Bin}\bigl(M,\theta(O_t)\bigr)<k_c\right\}.\] After the marginal \(q_c\) is tabulated, exact maximization enumerates only the \(F_{m_c}\) nonboundary total preorders and uses \(O\!\left(F_{m_c}m_c\{m_c+Mk_c\}\right)\) arithmetic operations and \(O(k_c)\) working memory, with no factor \(F_{m_{1-c}}\), \(|\mathcal V|\), or per-case \(m_0m_1\) scan. If both ranks equal \(M+1\), evaluation is constant and the frontier is zero. Binary attainment remains false because the symmetric admissible instance \(M=3\), \(\alpha=1/2\), \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=1/4\) has a four-level objective \(24957/160000\), while every binary pair has objective at most \(759109/5000000\), a gap \(83189/20000000\). If target and calibration orbit supports are disjoint in an arm \(a\) with \(k_a\le M\), then \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)=1-\alpha\). If \(k_a=M+1\), the frontier reduces exactly to the other arm's rank event through the preceding one-arm formula. On the diagonal \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), this is \(\texttt{thm:sharp-orbit-coverage-frontier}\).

defs.def:pair-action: For pair block \(b\), \(s_b(v_{b,r},a)=(v_{b,1-r},a)\) for \(r,a\in\{0,1\}\); \(s_b\) fixes members outside block \(b\) and relabels \(D_i\) by \(\texttt{def:group-action}\). Thus \(O_{b,0}=\{(v_{b,0},0),(v_{b,1},0)\}\) and \(O_{b,1}=\{(v_{b,0},1),(v_{b,1},1)\}\).
defs.def:observable-selector-mixture: For every \(i\in\{1,\ldots,M\}\) and \(a\in\{0,1\}\), set \(\kappa_{w,a}=\sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_h\kappa_{h,a}\), draw \(J_{i,a}\sim\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\), set \(U_{i,a}=(J_{i,a},a)\), and set \(q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,a}=\sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_hq^{\mathrm{obs}}_{h,a}\).
defs.def:coverage-frontier:
For prespecified \(\boldsymbol\varepsilon=(\varepsilon_0,\varepsilon_1)\), define
\[
\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)=\max_{\substack{s_a:\mathcal O\to[0,K]\\a\in\{0,1\}}}\left[(1-\alpha)-\Pr\left\{s_1(O_{T,1})\le Q_{k_1}(s_1(O_{1,1}),\ldots,s_1(O_{M,1}))\ \text{and}\ s_0(O_{T,0})\le Q_{k_0}(s_0(O_{1,0}),\ldots,s_0(O_{M,0}))\right\}\right]_+.
\]
Here \(O_{i,a}\sim q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,a}\), the target orbits are the two lifts of the one common \(T\sim q_{\mathrm{tar}}\), and the calibration draws use \(\texttt{ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization}\), including their within-replicate cross-arm coupling. The functional is restricted to bounded orbit-constant score maps. Under \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), set \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)=\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)\), recovering the symmetric frontier.
defs.def:finite-rank-error:
For \(N=M+1\), prespecified \(\boldsymbol\varepsilon=(\varepsilon_0,\varepsilon_1)\), and \(k_a=\lceil N(1-\varepsilon_a)\rceil\), define
\[
\rho_{M,\varepsilon_a}=\frac{N-k_a}{N}=\frac{\lfloor N\varepsilon_a\rfloor}{N},\qquad a\in\{0,1\}.
\]
Under \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), write their common value as
\[
\rho_{M,\alpha}=\frac{M+1-k}{M+1}=\frac{\lfloor(M+1)\alpha/2\rfloor}{M+1}.
\]
defs.def:rank-inversion: Prespecify \(\boldsymbol\varepsilon=(\varepsilon_0,\varepsilon_1)\) with \(\varepsilon_a>0\) and \(\varepsilon_0+\varepsilon_1\le\alpha\), and set \(k_a=\lceil(M+1)(1-\varepsilon_a)\rceil\). For each \(a\in\{0,1\}\), compute \(S_{i,a}=|Y^{\mathrm{obs}}_{iJ_{i,a}}-\widehat m_a(X_{iJ_{i,a}})|\), take \(r_{a,(k_a)}\), set \(I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}=[\widehat m_a(X_{0T})-r_{a,(k_a)},\widehat m_a(X_{0T})+r_{a,(k_a)}]\cap[-K,K]\), and return \(\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w=I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{1,w}-I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{0,w}\). If \(k_a=M+1\), set \(r_{a,(k_a)}=+\infty\), so \(I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}=[-K,K]\). For proof ranks only, append independent continuous uniforms and use lexicographic ranks; reported closed intervals use weak inclusion. Under \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), define \(k=k_0=k_1\), \(r_{a,(k)}=r_{a,(k_a)}\), \(I_{a,w}=I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}\), and \(\mathcal C_w=\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w\), recovering the symmetric construction.

assumptions.ass:observable-selector-kernel: \(\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\ge0,\quad\sum_{v\in\mathcal V}\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=1,\quad z_v\ne a\Rightarrow\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=0\)
assumptions.ass:selector-equivariance: \(\kappa_{h,a}(\pi_gv\mid gz)=\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\quad\forall g\in\mathcal G,\ h\in\mathcal H,\ a\in\{0,1\}\)
assumptions.ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization: \(\mathcal L((J_{i,a})_{1\le i\le M,\ a\in\{0,1\}}\mid(L_i)_{0\le i\le M},(Z_i)_{0\le i\le M},T,\mathcal T)=\prod_{i=1}^M\prod_{a=0}^1\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\)

snapshot.stmt: The functional \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)\) in \(\texttt{def:coverage-frontier}\) is the exact finite worst-coverage deficit over the stated class of bounded orbit-constant score maps for armwise ranks \(k_0,k_1\); no equality with a supremum over all \(P\in\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\) is asserted. \]
Here \(O_{i,a}\sim q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,a}\), the target orbits are the two lifts of the one common \(T\sim q_{\mathrm{tar}}\), and the calibration draws use \(\texttt{ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization}\), including their within-replicate cross-arm coupling. The functional is restricted to bounded orbit-constant score maps. Under \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), set \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)=\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)\), recovering the symmetric frontier.
defs.def:rank-inversion: Prespecify \(\boldsymbol\varepsilon=(\varepsilon_0,\varepsilon_1)\) with \(\varepsilon_a>0\) and \(\varepsilon_0+\varepsilon_1\le\alpha\), and set \(k_a=\lceil(M+1)(1-\varepsilon_a)\rceil\). For each \(a\in\{0,1\}\), compute \(S_{i,a}=|Y^{\mathrm{obs}}_{iJ_{i,a}}-\widehat m_a(X_{iJ_{i,a}})|\), take \(r_{a,(k_a)}\), set \(I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}=[\widehat m_a(X_{0T})-r_{a,(k_a)},\widehat m_a(X_{0T})+r_{a,(k_a)}]\cap[-K,K]\), and return \(\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w=I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{1,w}-I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{0,w}\). If \(k_a=M+1\), set \(r_{a,(k_a)}=+\infty\), so \(I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}=[-K,K]\). For proof ranks only, append independent continuous uniforms and use lexicographic ranks; reported closed intervals use weak inclusion. Under \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), define \(k=k_0=k_1\), \(r_{a,(k)}=r_{a,(k_a)}\), \(I_{a,w}=I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}\), and \(\mathcal C_w=\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w\), recovering the symmetric construction.

assumptions.ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization: \(\mathcal L((J_{i,a})_{1\le i\le M,\ a\in\{0,1\}}\mid(L_i)_{0\le i\le M},(Z_i)_{0\le i\le M},T,\mathcal T)=\prod_{i=1}^M\prod_{a=0}^1\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\)

snapshot.stmt:
The functional \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)\) in \(\texttt{def:coverage-frontier}\) is the exact finite worst-coverage deficit over the stated class of bounded orbit-constant score maps for armwise ranks \(k_0,k_1\); no equality with a supremum over all \(P\in\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\) is asserted. Writing \(\mathcal O_a=\{O\in\mathcal O:O\subseteq\mathcal U_a\}\), \(m_a=|\mathcal O_a|\), and letting \(F_m\) denote the number of total preorders on an \(m\)-element labelled set, if \(k_0,k_1\le M\), its maximum is attained by enumerating pairs of total preorders. Every such preorder has a representative in \([0,K]\) with at most \(m_a\) levels. After tabulating the joint calibration orbit law, in this two-finite-rank regime full enumeration uses \(O\!\left(F_{m_0}F_{m_1}|\mathcal V|\{m_0m_1+Mk_0k_1\}\right)\) arithmetic operations and \(O(k_0k_1)\) working memory.

If exactly one rank \(k_b\) equals \(M+1\), put \(c=1-b\), so \(k_c\le M\). For a fixed nonboundary score map \(s_c\), define \(\theta(O_t)=\sum_{O\in\mathcal O_c:s_c(O)<s_c(O_t)}q_{c,O}\). The exact rank-event probability is
\[
\sum_{O_t\in\mathcal O_c}p_{c,O_t}\Pr\!\left\{\operatorname{Bin}\bigl(M,\theta(O_t)\bigr)<k_c\right\}.
\]
For ordered nonempty tie classes \(C_1\prec\cdots\prec C_d\), define \(q_j=q_c(C_j)\), \(p_j=p_c(C_j)\), and \(\theta_j=\sum_{\ell<j}q_\ell\); then the preceding probability is \(\sum_{j=1}^d p_j\Pr\{\operatorname{Bin}(M,\theta_j)<k_c\}\), and these masses are formed by one cumulative ordered-class scan. For \(0<\theta<1\), exact lower-tail terms start at \(t_0=(1-\theta)^M\) and obey
\[
t_{r+1}=t_r\frac{M-r}{r+1}\frac{\theta}{1-\theta},\qquad 0\le r<k_c-1,
\]
while exact upper-tail terms start at \(u_M=\theta^M\) and obey
\[
u_{r-1}=u_r\frac{r}{M-r+1}\frac{1-\theta}{\theta},\qquad k_c<r\le M.
\]
Exponentiation by squaring initializes either endpoint term in \(O(\log M)\) arithmetic operations; summing the shorter lower tail or complementary upper tail uses \(O(\min\{k_c,M-k_c+1\})\) further operations. At \(\theta=0\) and \(\theta=1\), the probabilities are \(1\) and \(0\), respectively, without division. Hence, after both \(q_c\) and \(p_c\) are tabulated, exact maximization in the unit-cost arithmetic model enumerates only the \(F_{m_c}\) nonboundary total preorders and uses
\[
O\!\left(F_{m_c}m_c\left[1+\log M+\min\{k_c,M-k_c+1\}\right]\right)
\]
arithmetic operations and \(O(1)\) auxiliary CDF memory, with no factor \(F_{m_{1-c}}\), \(|\mathcal V|\), or per-case \(m_0m_1\) scan.

If both ranks equal \(M+1\), evaluation is constant and the frontier is zero. Binary attainment remains false because the symmetric admissible instance \(M=3\), \(\alpha=1/2\), \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=1/4\) has a four-level objective \(24957/160000\), while every binary pair has objective at most \(759109/5000000\), a gap \(83189/20000000\). If target and calibration orbit supports are disjoint in an arm \(a\) with \(k_a\le M\), then \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)=1-\alpha\). If \(k_a=M+1\), the frontier reduces exactly to the other arm's rank event through the preceding one-arm formula. On the diagonal \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), this is \(\texttt{thm:sharp-orbit-coverage-frontier}\).

defs.def:pair-action: For pair block \(b\), \(s_b(v_{b,r},a)=(v_{b,1-r},a)\) for \(r,a\in\{0,1\}\); \(s_b\) fixes members outside block \(b\) and relabels \(D_i\) by \(\texttt{def:group-action}\). Thus \(O_{b,0}=\{(v_{b,0},0),(v_{b,1},0)\}\) and \(O_{b,1}=\{(v_{b,0},1),(v_{b,1},1)\}\).
defs.def:observable-selector-mixture: For every \(i\in\{1,\ldots,M\}\) and \(a\in\{0,1\}\), set \(\kappa_{w,a}=\sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_h\kappa_{h,a}\), draw \(J_{i,a}\sim\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\), set \(U_{i,a}=(J_{i,a},a)\), and set \(q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,a}=\sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_hq^{\mathrm{obs}}_{h,a}\).

For prespecified \(\boldsymbol\varepsilon=(\varepsilon_0,\varepsilon_1)\), define
\[

defs.def:finite-rank-error:
For \(N=M+1\), prespecified \(\boldsymbol\varepsilon=(\varepsilon_0,\varepsilon_1)\), and \(k_a=\lceil N(1-\varepsilon_a)\rceil\), define
\[
\rho_{M,\varepsilon_a}=\frac{N-k_a}{N}=\frac{\lfloor N\varepsilon_a\rfloor}{N},\qquad a\in\{0,1\}.
\]
Under \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), write their common value as
\[
\rho_{M,\alpha}=\frac{M+1-k}{M+1}=\frac{\lfloor(M+1)\alpha/2\rfloor}{M+1}.
\]

assumptions.ass:observable-selector-kernel: \(\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\ge0,\quad\sum_{v\in\mathcal V}\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=1,\quad z_v\ne a\Rightarrow\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=0\)

assumptions.ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization: \(\mathcal L((J_{i,a})_{1\le i\le M,\ a\in\{0,1\}}\mid(L_i)_{0\le i\le M},(Z_i)_{0\le i\le M},T,\mathcal T)=\prod_{i=1}^M\prod_{a=0}^1\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\)

snapshot.stmt:
Suppose \(k_0,k_1\le M\). Write \(\mathcal O_a=\{O\in\mathcal O:O\subseteq\mathcal U_a\}\), \(m_a=|\mathcal O_a|\), and let \(F_m\) be the number of total preorders on an \(m\)-element labelled set. Define
\[
\kappa_{w,a}(O\mid z)=\sum_{v:(v,a)\in O}\kappa_{w,a}(v\mid z),\qquad R_w(O_0,O_1)=\sum_z\nu(z)\kappa_{w,0}(O_0\mid z)\kappa_{w,1}(O_1\mid z).
\]
For \(v\in\mathcal V\) and \(a\in\{0,1\}\), let \(O_a(v)\) be the orbit containing \((v,a)\), and define the coherent target-orbit-pair law and its support size by
\[
P_T(O_0,O_1)=\sum_{v\in\mathcal V}q_{\mathrm{tar}}(v)\mathbf 1\{O_0(v)=O_0,\ O_1(v)=O_1\},\qquad L=|\operatorname{supp}(P_T)|.
\]
Then \(L\le\min\{|\mathcal V|,m_0m_1\}\). For every pair of total preorders on \(\mathcal O_0\) and \(\mathcal O_1\), one two-dimensional prefix table of the full joint calibration-orbit law \(R_w\) yields in constant time the four Bernoulli-cell probabilities associated with each supported coherent target pair. Averaging the exact two-count dynamic-program probabilities against \(P_T\) gives the joint rank-event probability while preserving the one common target and the full within-replicate cross-arm calibration dependence. After \(R_w\) is tabulated, \(P_T\) is tabulated in \(O(|\mathcal V|)\) operations, and exact enumeration over all preorder pairs uses
\[
O\!\left(|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}\left[m_0m_1+L\{1+Mk_0k_1\}\right]\right)
\]
unit-cost arithmetic operations and \(O(m_0m_1+L+k_0k_1)\) working memory.

snapshot.stmt:
The functional \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)\) in \(\texttt{def:coverage-frontier}\) is the exact finite worst-coverage deficit over the stated class of bounded orbit-constant score maps for armwise ranks \(k_0,k_1\); no equality with a supremum over all \(P\in\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\) is asserted. Writing \(\mathcal O_a=\{O\in\mathcal O:O\subseteq\mathcal U_a\}\), \(m_a=|\mathcal O_a|\), and letting \(F_m\) denote the number of total preorders on an \(m\)-element labelled set, if \(k_0,k_1\le M\), its maximum is attained by enumerating pairs of total preorders. Every such preorder has a representative in \([0,K]\) with at most \(m_a\) levels. Let \(P_T\), \(L\), and the full joint calibration-orbit law \(R_w\) be as in \(\texttt{lem:coherent-two-finite-rank-frontier-evaluator}\). For each preorder pair with ordered tie classes \(C_{a,1}\prec_a\cdots\prec_a C_{a,d_a}\) and class maps \(c_a\), push \(P_T\) forward to \(P_T^C=(c_0,c_1)_\#P_T\) and put \(L_C(c_0,c_1)=|\operatorname{supp}(P_T^C)|\le\min\{L,d_0d_1\}\). All supported coherent orbit pairs in one class pair have the same four prefix-table probabilities and the same coupled two-count dynamic-program value, so one run weighted by their aggregated target mass suffices. If \(\mathfrak C\) is the set of all \(F_{m_0}F_{m_1}\) preorder pairs and \(L_C=\max_{(c_0,c_1)\in\mathfrak C}L_C(c_0,c_1)\), exact full enumeration has the instance-adaptive bound
\[
O\!\left(
|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}(m_0m_1+L)
+\{1+Mk_0k_1\}\sum_{(c_0,c_1)\in\mathfrak C}L_C(c_0,c_1)
\right),
\]
and hence
\[
O\!\left(|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}\left[m_0m_1+L+L_C\{1+Mk_0k_1\}\right]\right)
\]
unit-cost arithmetic operations and \(O(m_0m_1+L+k_0k_1)\) working memory.

If exactly one rank \(k_b\) equals \(M+1\), put \(c=1-b\), so \(k_c\le M\). For a fixed nonboundary score map \(s_c\), define \(\theta(O_t)=\sum_{O\in\mathcal O_c:s_c(O)<s_c(O_t)}q_{c,O}\). The exact rank-event probability is
\[
\sum_{O_t\in\mathcal O_c}p_{c,O_t}\Pr\!\left\{\operatorname{Bin}\bigl(M,\theta(O_t)\bigr)<k_c\right\}.
\]
For ordered nonempty tie classes \(C_1\prec\cdots\prec C_d\), define \(q_j=q_c(C_j)\), \(p_j=p_c(C_j)\), and \(\theta_j=\sum_{\ell<j}q_\ell\); then the preceding probability is \(\sum_{j=1}^d p_j\Pr\{\operatorname{Bin}(M,\theta_j)<k_c\}\), and these masses are formed by one cumulative ordered-class scan. For \(0<\theta<1\), exact lower-tail terms start at \(t_0=(1-\theta)^M\) and obey
\[
t_{r+1}=t_r\frac{M-r}{r+1}\frac{\theta}{1-\theta},\qquad 0\le r<k_c-1,
\]
while exact upper-tail terms start at \(u_M=\theta^M\) and obey
\[
u_{r-1}=u_r\frac{r}{M-r+1}\frac{1-\theta}{\theta},\qquad k_c<r\le M.
\]
Exponentiation by squaring initializes either endpoint term in \(O(\log M)\) arithmetic operations; summing the shorter lower tail or complementary upper tail uses \(O(\min\{k_c,M-k_c+1\})\) further operations. At \(\theta=0\) and \(\theta=1\), the probabilities are \(1\) and \(0\), respectively, without division. Hence, after both \(q_c\) and \(p_c\) are tabulated, exact maximization in the unit-cost arithmetic model enumerates only the \(F_{m_c}\) nonboundary total preorders and uses
\[
O\!\left(F_{m_c}m_c\left[1+\log M+\min\{k_c,M-k_c+1\}\right]\right)
\]
arithmetic operations and \(O(1)\) auxiliary CDF memory, with no factor \(F_{m_{1-c}}\), \(|\mathcal V|\), or per-case \(m_0m_1\) scan.

If both ranks equal \(M+1\), evaluation is constant and the frontier is zero. Binary attainment remains false because the symmetric admissible instance \(M=3\), \(\alpha=1/2\), \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=1/4\) has a four-level objective \(24957/160000\), while every binary pair has objective at most \(759109/5000000\), a gap \(83189/20000000\). If target and calibration orbit supports are disjoint in an arm \(a\) with \(k_a\le M\), then \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)=1-\alpha\). If \(k_a=M+1\), the frontier reduces exactly to the other arm's rank event through the preceding one-arm formula. On the diagonal \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), this is \(\texttt{thm:sharp-orbit-coverage-frontier}\).

defs.def:pair-action: For pair block \(b\), \(s_b(v_{b,r},a)=(v_{b,1-r},a)\) for \(r,a\in\{0,1\}\); \(s_b\) fixes members outside block \(b\) and relabels \(D_i\) by \(\texttt{def:group-action}\). Thus \(O_{b,0}=\{(v_{b,0},0),(v_{b,1},0)\}\) and \(O_{b,1}=\{(v_{b,0},1),(v_{b,1},1)\}\).
defs.def:observable-selector-mixture: For every \(i\in\{1,\ldots,M\}\) and \(a\in\{0,1\}\), set \(\kappa_{w,a}=\sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_h\kappa_{h,a}\), draw \(J_{i,a}\sim\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\), set \(U_{i,a}=(J_{i,a},a)\), and set \(q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,a}=\sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_hq^{\mathrm{obs}}_{h,a}\).
defs.def:coverage-frontier:
For prespecified \(\boldsymbol\varepsilon=(\varepsilon_0,\varepsilon_1)\), define
\[
\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)=\max_{\substack{s_a:\mathcal O\to[0,K]\\a\in\{0,1\}}}\left[(1-\alpha)-\Pr\left\{s_1(O_{T,1})\le Q_{k_1}(s_1(O_{1,1}),\ldots,s_1(O_{M,1}))\ \text{and}\ s_0(O_{T,0})\le Q_{k_0}(s_0(O_{1,0}),\ldots,s_0(O_{M,0}))\right\}\right]_+.
\]
Here \(O_{i,a}\sim q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,a}\), the target orbits are the two lifts of the one common \(T\sim q_{\mathrm{tar}}\), and the calibration draws use \(\texttt{ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization}\), including their within-replicate cross-arm coupling. The functional is restricted to bounded orbit-constant score maps. Under \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), set \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)=\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)\), recovering the symmetric frontier.
defs.def:finite-rank-error:
For \(N=M+1\), prespecified \(\boldsymbol\varepsilon=(\varepsilon_0,\varepsilon_1)\), and \(k_a=\lceil N(1-\varepsilon_a)\rceil\), define
\[
\rho_{M,\varepsilon_a}=\frac{N-k_a}{N}=\frac{\lfloor N\varepsilon_a\rfloor}{N},\qquad a\in\{0,1\}.
\]
Under \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), write their common value as
\[
\rho_{M,\alpha}=\frac{M+1-k}{M+1}=\frac{\lfloor(M+1)\alpha/2\rfloor}{M+1}.
\]
defs.def:rank-inversion: Prespecify \(\boldsymbol\varepsilon=(\varepsilon_0,\varepsilon_1)\) with \(\varepsilon_a>0\) and \(\varepsilon_0+\varepsilon_1\le\alpha\), and set \(k_a=\lceil(M+1)(1-\varepsilon_a)\rceil\). For each \(a\in\{0,1\}\), compute \(S_{i,a}=|Y^{\mathrm{obs}}_{iJ_{i,a}}-\widehat m_a(X_{iJ_{i,a}})|\), take \(r_{a,(k_a)}\), set \(I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}=[\widehat m_a(X_{0T})-r_{a,(k_a)},\widehat m_a(X_{0T})+r_{a,(k_a)}]\cap[-K,K]\), and return \(\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w=I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{1,w}-I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{0,w}\). If \(k_a=M+1\), set \(r_{a,(k_a)}=+\infty\), so \(I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}=[-K,K]\). For proof ranks only, append independent continuous uniforms and use lexicographic ranks; reported closed intervals use weak inclusion. Under \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), define \(k=k_0=k_1\), \(r_{a,(k)}=r_{a,(k_a)}\), \(I_{a,w}=I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}\), and \(\mathcal C_w=\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w\), recovering the symmetric construction.

assumptions.ass:observable-selector-kernel: \(\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\ge0,\quad\sum_{v\in\mathcal V}\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=1,\quad z_v\ne a\Rightarrow\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=0\)
assumptions.ass:selector-equivariance: \(\kappa_{h,a}(\pi_gv\mid gz)=\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\quad\forall g\in\mathcal G,\ h\in\mathcal H,\ a\in\{0,1\}\)
assumptions.ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization: \(\mathcal L((J_{i,a})_{1\le i\le M,\ a\in\{0,1\}}\mid(L_i)_{0\le i\le M},(Z_i)_{0\le i\le M},T,\mathcal T)=\prod_{i=1}^M\prod_{a=0}^1\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\)

snapshot.stmt:
Suppose \(k_0,k_1\le M\). Write \(\mathcal O_a=\{O\in\mathcal O:O\subseteq\mathcal U_a\}\), \(m_a=|\mathcal O_a|\), and let \(F_m\) be the number of total preorders on an \(m\)-element labelled set. Define
\[
\kappa_{w,a}(O\mid z)=\sum_{v:(v,a)\in O}\kappa_{w,a}(v\mid z),\qquad R_w(O_0,O_1)=\sum_z\nu(z)\kappa_{w,0}(O_0\mid z)\kappa_{w,1}(O_1\mid z).
\]
For \(v\in\mathcal V\) and \(a\in\{0,1\}\), let \(O_a(v)\) be the orbit containing \((v,a)\), and define the coherent target-orbit-pair law and its support size by
\[
P_T(O_0,O_1)=\sum_{v\in\mathcal V}q_{\mathrm{tar}}(v)\mathbf 1\{O_0(v)=O_0,\ O_1(v)=O_1\},\qquad L=|\operatorname{supp}(P_T)|.
\]
Then \(L\le\min\{|\mathcal V|,m_0m_1\}\). For a pair of total preorders with ordered nonempty tie classes \(C_{a,1}\prec_a\cdots\prec_a C_{a,d_a}\), let \(c_a:\mathcal O_a\to\{1,\ldots,d_a\}\) be the class map and push \(P_T\) forward to
\[
P_T^C(j,\ell)=\sum_{\substack{O_0,O_1\\c_0(O_0)=j,\ c_1(O_1)=\ell}}P_T(O_0,O_1),\qquad
L_C(c_0,c_1)=|\operatorname{supp}(P_T^C)|.
\]
Then \(L_C(c_0,c_1)\le\min\{L,d_0d_1\}\). One two-dimensional prefix table of the full joint calibration-orbit law \(R_w\) yields in constant time the four Bernoulli-cell probabilities for every supported class pair. All supported coherent target-orbit pairs with the same class pair \((j,\ell)\) have those same four probabilities and hence the same coupled two-count dynamic-program value. Thus the dynamic program is run once per supported class pair and weighted by \(P_T^C(j,\ell)\), preserving the one common target and the full within-replicate cross-arm calibration dependence.

Let \(\mathfrak C\) be the \(F_{m_0}F_{m_1}\) preorder pairs and put \(L_C=\max_{(c_0,c_1)\in\mathfrak C}L_C(c_0,c_1)\). After \(R_w\) is tabulated, \(P_T\) is tabulated in \(O(|\mathcal V|)\) operations, and exact enumeration uses the instance-adaptive bound
\[
O\!\left(
|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}(m_0m_1+L)
+\{1+Mk_0k_1\}\sum_{(c_0,c_1)\in\mathfrak C}L_C(c_0,c_1)
\right).
\]
In particular, this is
\[
O\!\left(|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}\left[m_0m_1+L+L_C\{1+Mk_0k_1\}\right]\right)
\]
unit-cost arithmetic operations and \(O(m_0m_1+L+k_0k_1)\) working memory.

snapshot.stmt: The functional \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)\) in \(\texttt{def:coverage-frontier}\) is the exact finite worst-coverage deficit over the stated class of bounded orbit-constant score maps; no equality with a supremum over all \(P\in\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\) is asserted. Writing \(\mathcal O_a=\{O\in\mathcal O:O\subseteq\mathcal U_a\}\) and \(m_a=|\mathcal O_a|\), its maximum is attained by enumerating pairs of total preorders on \(\mathcal O_0\) and \(\mathcal O_1\). Every preorder has a representative in \([0,K]\) with at most \(m_a\) levels, and the exact objective is a finite sum over coherent target members and calibration orbit-pair configurations. Let \(F_m\) be the number of total preorders on an \(m\)-element labelled set. Under disjoint target and calibration orbit support in one arm and \(k\le M\), \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)=1-\alpha\).

snapshot.stmt:
The functional \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)\) in \(\texttt{def:coverage-frontier}\) is the exact finite worst-coverage deficit over the stated class of bounded orbit-constant score maps for armwise ranks \(k_0,k_1\); no equality with a supremum over all \(P\in\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\) is asserted. Write \(\mathcal O_a=\{O\in\mathcal O:O\subseteq\mathcal U_a\}\), \(m_a=|\mathcal O_a|\), and let \(F_m\) denote the number of total preorders on an \(m\)-element labelled set. If \(k_0,k_1\le M\), the maximum is attained by enumerating preorder pairs, and every preorder has a representative in \([0,K]\) with at most \(m_a\) levels. Let \(P_T\), \(L\), and \(R_w\) be as in \(\texttt{lem:coherent-two-finite-rank-frontier-evaluator}\). For preorder pair \(C\), let \(P_T^C\) be the coherent target pushforward, let \(L_C=|\operatorname{supp}(P_T^C)|\), and let \(U_C\) be the number of distinct supported exact prefix-table vectors \(\pi^C(j,\ell)=(\pi_{00}^C,\pi_{01}^C,\pi_{10}^C,\pi_{11}^C)\). Then \(U_C\le L_C\le\min\{L,d_0d_1\}\). Class pairs with the same exact vector have the same coupled two-count recurrence value, so their coherent target masses are aggregated and the recurrence is run once per distinct vector. Deterministic balanced-tree grouping keyed by three independent exact coordinates costs \(O(L_C\log(1+L_C))\) exact comparisons and \(O(L_C)\) aggregation arithmetic. If \(\mathfrak C\) contains all \(F_{m_0}F_{m_1}\) preorder pairs, exact enumeration uses
\[
O\!\left(
|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}(m_0m_1+L)+\sum_{C\in\mathfrak C}\left[L_C\log(1+L_C)+L_C+U_C\{1+Mk_0k_1\}\right]
\right).
\]
With \(L_{\max}=\max_C L_C\) and \(U_{\max}=\max_C U_C\), a uniform bound is
\[
O\!\left(|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}\left[m_0m_1+L+L_{\max}\log(1+L_{\max})+L_{\max}+U_{\max}\{1+Mk_0k_1\}\right]\right),
\]
and working memory, including the current exact-key table, is \(O(m_0m_1+L+k_0k_1+U_{\max})\). The grouping preserves the one common target and the full within-replicate calibration-arm dependence.

If exactly one rank \(k_b=M+1\), put \(c=1-b\), so \(k_c\le M\). For a fixed nonboundary score map \(s_c\), define \(\theta(O_t)=\sum_{O\in\mathcal O_c:s_c(O)<s_c(O_t)}q_{c,O}\). The exact rank-event probability is
\[
\sum_{O_t\in\mathcal O_c}p_{c,O_t}\Pr\!\left\{\operatorname{Bin}\bigl(M,\theta(O_t)\bigr)<k_c\right\}.
\]
For ordered nonempty tie classes \(C_1\prec\cdots\prec C_d\), define \(q_j=q_c(C_j)\), \(p_j=p_c(C_j)\), and \(\theta_j=\sum_{\ell<j}q_\ell\); then the probability is \(\sum_{j=1}^d p_j\Pr\{\operatorname{Bin}(M,\theta_j)<k_c\}\). These masses are formed by one cumulative ordered-class scan. For \(0<\theta<1\), exact lower-tail terms start at \(t_0=(1-\theta)^M\) and obey
\[
t_{r+1}=t_r\frac{M-r}{r+1}\frac{\theta}{1-\theta},\qquad 0\le r<k_c-1,
\]
whereas exact upper-tail terms start at \(u_M=\theta^M\) and obey
\[
u_{r-1}=u_r\frac{r}{M-r+1}\frac{1-\theta}{\theta},\qquad k_c<r\le M.
\]
Exponentiation by squaring initializes either endpoint in \(O(\log M)\) operations, and the shorter tail uses \(O(\min\{k_c,M-k_c+1\})\) further operations. At \(\theta=0\) and \(\theta=1\), the probabilities are \(1\) and \(0\), respectively, without division. After \(q_c\) and \(p_c\) are tabulated, exact maximization enumerates only the \(F_{m_c}\) nonboundary preorders and uses
\[
O\!\left(F_{m_c}m_c\left[1+\log M+\min\{k_c,M-k_c+1\}\right]\right)
\]
arithmetic operations and \(O(1)\) auxiliary CDF memory, with no factor \(F_{m_{1-c}}\), \(|\mathcal V|\), or per-case \(m_0m_1\) scan.

If both ranks equal \(M+1\), evaluation is constant and the frontier is zero. Binary attainment is false: in the admissible symmetric instance \(M=3\), \(\alpha=1/2\), and \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=1/4\), a four-level objective equals \(24957/160000\), whereas every binary pair has objective at most \(759109/5000000\), a gap of \(83189/20000000\). If target and calibration orbit supports are disjoint in arm \(a\) and \(k_a\le M\), then \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)=1-\alpha\). If \(k_a=M+1\), the frontier reduces exactly to the other arm's rank event through the preceding one-arm formula. On the diagonal \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), this is \(\texttt{thm:sharp-orbit-coverage-frontier}\).

defs.def:pair-action: For pair block \(b\), \(s_b(v_{b,r},a)=(v_{b,1-r},a)\) for \(r,a\in\{0,1\}\); \(s_b\) fixes members outside block \(b\) and relabels \(D_i\) by \(\texttt{def:group-action}\). Thus \(O_{b,0}=\{(v_{b,0},0),(v_{b,1},0)\}\) and \(O_{b,1}=\{(v_{b,0},1),(v_{b,1},1)\}\).
defs.def:observable-selector-mixture: For every \(i\in\{1,\ldots,M\}\) and \(a\in\{0,1\}\), set \(\kappa_{w,a}=\sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_h\kappa_{h,a}\), draw \(J_{i,a}\sim\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\), set \(U_{i,a}=(J_{i,a},a)\), and set \(q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,a}=\sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_hq^{\mathrm{obs}}_{h,a}\).
defs.def:coverage-frontier:
For prespecified \(\boldsymbol\varepsilon=(\varepsilon_0,\varepsilon_1)\), define
\[
\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)=\max_{\substack{s_a:\mathcal O\to[0,K]\\a\in\{0,1\}}}\left[(1-\alpha)-\Pr\left\{s_1(O_{T,1})\le Q_{k_1}(s_1(O_{1,1}),\ldots,s_1(O_{M,1}))\ \text{and}\ s_0(O_{T,0})\le Q_{k_0}(s_0(O_{1,0}),\ldots,s_0(O_{M,0}))\right\}\right]_+.
\]
Here \(O_{i,a}\sim q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,a}\), the target orbits are the two lifts of the one common \(T\sim q_{\mathrm{tar}}\), and the calibration draws use \(\texttt{ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization}\), including their within-replicate cross-arm coupling. The functional is restricted to bounded orbit-constant score maps. Under \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), set \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)=\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)\), recovering the symmetric frontier.
defs.def:finite-rank-error:
For \(N=M+1\), prespecified \(\boldsymbol\varepsilon=(\varepsilon_0,\varepsilon_1)\), and \(k_a=\lceil N(1-\varepsilon_a)\rceil\), define
\[
\rho_{M,\varepsilon_a}=\frac{N-k_a}{N}=\frac{\lfloor N\varepsilon_a\rfloor}{N},\qquad a\in\{0,1\}.
\]
Under \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), write their common value as
\[
\rho_{M,\alpha}=\frac{M+1-k}{M+1}=\frac{\lfloor(M+1)\alpha/2\rfloor}{M+1}.
\]
defs.def:rank-inversion: Prespecify \(\boldsymbol\varepsilon=(\varepsilon_0,\varepsilon_1)\) with \(\varepsilon_a>0\) and \(\varepsilon_0+\varepsilon_1\le\alpha\), and set \(k_a=\lceil(M+1)(1-\varepsilon_a)\rceil\). For each \(a\in\{0,1\}\), compute \(S_{i,a}=|Y^{\mathrm{obs}}_{iJ_{i,a}}-\widehat m_a(X_{iJ_{i,a}})|\), take \(r_{a,(k_a)}\), set \(I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}=[\widehat m_a(X_{0T})-r_{a,(k_a)},\widehat m_a(X_{0T})+r_{a,(k_a)}]\cap[-K,K]\), and return \(\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w=I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{1,w}-I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{0,w}\). If \(k_a=M+1\), set \(r_{a,(k_a)}=+\infty\), so \(I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}=[-K,K]\). For proof ranks only, append independent continuous uniforms and use lexicographic ranks; reported closed intervals use weak inclusion. Under \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), define \(k=k_0=k_1\), \(r_{a,(k)}=r_{a,(k_a)}\), \(I_{a,w}=I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}\), and \(\mathcal C_w=\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w\), recovering the symmetric construction.

assumptions.ass:observable-selector-kernel: \(\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\ge0,\quad\sum_{v\in\mathcal V}\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=1,\quad z_v\ne a\Rightarrow\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=0\)
assumptions.ass:selector-equivariance: \(\kappa_{h,a}(\pi_gv\mid gz)=\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\quad\forall g\in\mathcal G,\ h\in\mathcal H,\ a\in\{0,1\}\)
assumptions.ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization: \(\mathcal L((J_{i,a})_{1\le i\le M,\ a\in\{0,1\}}\mid(L_i)_{0\le i\le M},(Z_i)_{0\le i\le M},T,\mathcal T)=\prod_{i=1}^M\prod_{a=0}^1\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\)

snapshot.stmt:
Suppose \(k_0,k_1\le M\). Write \(\mathcal O_a=\{O\in\mathcal O:O\subseteq\mathcal U_a\}\), \(m_a=|\mathcal O_a|\), and let \(F_m\) be the number of total preorders on an \(m\)-element labelled set. Define
\[
\kappa_{w,a}(O\mid z)=\sum_{v:(v,a)\in O}\kappa_{w,a}(v\mid z),\qquad R_w(O_0,O_1)=\sum_z\nu(z)\kappa_{w,0}(O_0\mid z)\kappa_{w,1}(O_1\mid z).
\]
For \(v\in\mathcal V\) and \(a\in\{0,1\}\), let \(O_a(v)\) be the orbit containing \((v,a)\), and define
\[
P_T(O_0,O_1)=\sum_{v\in\mathcal V}q_{\mathrm{tar}}(v)\mathbf 1\{O_0(v)=O_0,\ O_1(v)=O_1\},\qquad L=|\operatorname{supp}(P_T)|.
\]
Then \(L\le\min\{|\mathcal V|,m_0m_1\}\). For a preorder pair \(C=(c_0,c_1)\) with ordered nonempty tie classes \(C_{a,1}\prec_a\cdots\prec_a C_{a,d_a}\), let
\[
P_T^C(j,\ell)=\sum_{\substack{O_0,O_1\\c_0(O_0)=j,\ c_1(O_1)=\ell}}P_T(O_0,O_1),\qquad L_C=|\operatorname{supp}(P_T^C)|.
\]
Then \(L_C\le\min\{L,d_0d_1\}\). One two-dimensional prefix table of the full joint calibration-orbit law \(R_w\) yields the exact four-cell vector \(\pi^C(j,\ell)=(\pi_{00}^C,\pi_{01}^C,\pi_{10}^C,\pi_{11}^C)\) in constant time for every supported target class pair. Define
\[
\Pi_C=\{\pi^C(j,\ell):(j,\ell)\in\operatorname{supp}(P_T^C)\},\qquad U_C=|\Pi_C|\le L_C,
\]
and aggregate \(P_T^C\) mass over identical exact vectors. The coupled two-count recurrence depends only on \(\pi\) and the fixed ranks, so it is run once per \(\pi\in\Pi_C\), weighted by the aggregated coherent target mass. This preserves the one common target and the full within-replicate cross-arm calibration dependence.

Let \(\mathfrak C\) be the \(F_{m_0}F_{m_1}\) preorder pairs. A deterministic balanced-tree implementation keyed by three independent exact coordinates uses \(O(L_C\log(1+L_C))\) exact comparisons and \(O(L_C)\) aggregation arithmetic for preorder pair \(C\). After \(R_w\) is tabulated, exact enumeration uses
\[
O\!\left(
|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}(m_0m_1+L)+\sum_{C\in\mathfrak C}\left[L_C\log(1+L_C)+L_C+U_C\{1+Mk_0k_1\}\right]
\right)
\]
unit-cost exact comparisons and arithmetic operations. If \(L_{\max}=\max_C L_C\) and \(U_{\max}=\max_C U_C\), a uniform bound is
\[
O\!\left(|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}\left[m_0m_1+L+L_{\max}\log(1+L_{\max})+L_{\max}+U_{\max}\{1+Mk_0k_1\}\right]\right).
\]
The working-memory bound, including the current exact-key table, is \(O(m_0m_1+L+k_0k_1+U_{\max})\).

defs.def:observable-selector-mixture: For every \(i\in\{1,\ldots,M\}\) and \(a\in\{0,1\}\), set \(\kappa_{w,a}=\sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_h\kappa_{h,a}\), draw \(J_{i,a}\sim\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\), set \(U_{i,a}=(J_{i,a},a)\), and set \(q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,a}=\sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_hq^{\mathrm{obs}}_{h,a}\).

\]

The functional is restricted to bounded orbit-constant score maps. Under \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), set \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)=\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)\), recovering the symmetric frontier.

For each \(a\in\{0,1\}\), compute \(S_{i,a}=|Y^{\mathrm{obs}}_{iJ_{i,a}}-\widehat m_a(X_{iJ_{i,a}})|\), take \(r_{a,(k_a)}\), set \(I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}=[\widehat m_a(X_{0T})-r_{a,(k_a)},\widehat m_a(X_{0T})+r_{a,(k_a)}]\cap[-K,K]\), and return \(\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w=I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{1,w}-I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{0,w}\). assumptions.ass:observable-selector-kernel: \(\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\ge0,\quad\sum_{v\in\mathcal V}\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=1,\quad z_v\ne a\Rightarrow\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=0\)
assumptions.ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization: \(\mathcal L((J_{i,a})_{1\le i\le M,\ a\in\{0,1\}}\mid(L_i)_{0\le i\le M},(Z_i)_{0\le i\le M},T,\mathcal T)=\prod_{i=1}^M\prod_{a=0}^1\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\)

snapshot.stmt:
The functional \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)\) in \(\texttt{def:coverage-frontier}\) is the exact finite worst-coverage deficit over the stated class of bounded orbit-constant score maps; no equality with a supremum over all \(P\in\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\) is asserted. Write \(\mathcal O_a=\{O\in\mathcal O:O\subseteq\mathcal U_a\}\), \(m_a=|\mathcal O_a|\), and let \(F_m\) be the number of total preorders on an \(m\)-element labelled set. Its maximum is attained by enumerating the \(F_{m_0}F_{m_1}\) pairs of total preorders on \(\mathcal O_0\) and \(\mathcal O_1\); every preorder has a representative in \([0,K]\) with at most \(m_a\) levels. For \(v\in\mathcal V\), let \(O_a(v)\) be the orbit containing \((v,a)\), and define
\[
\kappa_{w,a}(O\mid z)=\sum_{v:(v,a)\in O}\kappa_{w,a}(v\mid z),\qquad R_w(O_0,O_1)=\sum_z\nu(z)\kappa_{w,0}(O_0\mid z)\kappa_{w,1}(O_1\mid z),
\]
\[
P_T(O_0,O_1)=\sum_{v\in\mathcal V}q_{\mathrm{tar}}(v)\mathbf 1\{O_0(v)=O_0,\ O_1(v)=O_1\},\qquad L=|\operatorname{supp}(P_T)|.
\]
Thus \(P_T\) preserves the one common target and \(R_w\) preserves the selectors' shared-assignment dependence. For a preorder pair \(C=(c_0,c_1)\), let \(P_T^C\) be the pushforward of \(P_T\) to its ordered class pairs, put \(L_C=|\operatorname{supp}(P_T^C)|\), and let \(U_C\) be the number of distinct supported exact four-cell vectors \(\pi^C(j,\ell)\) obtained from the two-dimensional prefix table of \(R_w\). Then \(U_C\le L_C\le\min\{L,d_0d_1\}\), where \(d_a\) is the number of nonempty classes of \(c_a\). Class pairs with the same exact vector have the same coupled two-count recurrence value, so their coherent target masses are aggregated and the recurrence is run once per distinct vector. Let \(\mathfrak C\) be the set of all preorder pairs. After \(R_w\) is tabulated, if \(k\le M\), deterministic balanced-tree grouping keyed by three independent exact coordinates gives the instance-adaptive evaluation bound
\[
O\!\left(
|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}(m_0m_1+L)+\sum_{C\in\mathfrak C}\left[L_C\log(1+L_C)+L_C+U_C\{1+Mk^2\}\right]
\right)
\]
unit-cost exact comparisons and arithmetic operations. With \(L_{\max}=\max_C L_C\) and \(U_{\max}=\max_C U_C\), a uniform bound is
\[
O\!\left(|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}\left[m_0m_1+L+L_{\max}\log(1+L_{\max})+L_{\max}+U_{\max}\{1+Mk^2\}\right]\right),
\]
and the working-memory bound, including the current exact-key table, is \(O(m_0m_1+L+k^2+U_{\max})\). If \(k=M+1\), evaluation is constant and \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)=0\). Binary attainment is false: in an admissible four-pair instance with \(M=3\) and \(\alpha=1/2\), a four-level ordering has frontier objective \(24957/160000\), whereas every binary score pair has objective at most \(759109/5000000\), a strict gap of \(83189/20000000\). Under disjoint target and calibration orbit support in one arm and \(k\le M\), \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)=1-\alpha\).
