Here \(O_{i,a}\sim q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,a}\), the target orbits are the two lifts of the one common \(T\sim q_{\mathrm{tar}}\), and the calibration draws use \(\texttt{ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization}\), including their within-replicate cross-arm coupling. The functional is restricted to bounded orbit-constant score maps. Under \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), set \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)=\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)\), recovering the symmetric frontier.
defs.def:rank-inversion: Prespecify \(\boldsymbol\varepsilon=(\varepsilon_0,\varepsilon_1)\) with \(\varepsilon_a>0\) and \(\varepsilon_0+\varepsilon_1\le\alpha\), and set \(k_a=\lceil(M+1)(1-\varepsilon_a)\rceil\). For each \(a\in\{0,1\}\), compute \(S_{i,a}=|Y^{\mathrm{obs}}_{iJ_{i,a}}-\widehat m_a(X_{iJ_{i,a}})|\), take \(r_{a,(k_a)}\), set \(I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}=[\widehat m_a(X_{0T})-r_{a,(k_a)},\widehat m_a(X_{0T})+r_{a,(k_a)}]\cap[-K,K]\), and return \(\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w=I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{1,w}-I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{0,w}\). If \(k_a=M+1\), set \(r_{a,(k_a)}=+\infty\), so \(I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}=[-K,K]\). For proof ranks only, append independent continuous uniforms and use lexicographic ranks; reported closed intervals use weak inclusion. Under \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), define \(k=k_0=k_1\), \(r_{a,(k)}=r_{a,(k_a)}\), \(I_{a,w}=I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}\), and \(\mathcal C_w=\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w\), recovering the symmetric construction.

assumptions.ass:observable-selector-kernel: \(\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\ge0,\quad\sum_{v\in\mathcal V}\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=1,\quad z_v\ne a\Rightarrow\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=0\)
assumptions.ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization: \(\mathcal L((J_{i,a})_{1\le i\le M,\ a\in\{0,1\}}\mid(L_i)_{0\le i\le M},(Z_i)_{0\le i\le M},T,\mathcal T)=\prod_{i=1}^M\prod_{a=0}^1\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\)

snapshot.stmt:
The functional \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)\) in \(\texttt{def:coverage-frontier}\) is the exact finite worst-coverage deficit over the stated class of bounded orbit-constant score maps; no equality with a supremum over all \(P\in\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\) is asserted. Write \(\mathcal O_a=\{O\in\mathcal O:O\subseteq\mathcal U_a\}\), \(m_a=|\mathcal O_a|\), and let \(F_m\) be the number of total preorders on an \(m\)-element labelled set. Its maximum is attained by enumerating the \(F_{m_0}F_{m_1}\) pairs of total preorders on \(\mathcal O_0\) and \(\mathcal O_1\); every preorder has a representative in \([0,K]\) with at most \(m_a\) levels. For \(v\in\mathcal V\), let \(O_a(v)\) be the orbit containing \((v,a)\), and define
\[
\kappa_{w,a}(O\mid z)=\sum_{v:(v,a)\in O}\kappa_{w,a}(v\mid z),\qquad R_w(O_0,O_1)=\sum_z\nu(z)\kappa_{w,0}(O_0\mid z)\kappa_{w,1}(O_1\mid z),
\]
\[
P_T(O_0,O_1)=\sum_{v\in\mathcal V}q_{\mathrm{tar}}(v)\mathbf 1\{O_0(v)=O_0,\ O_1(v)=O_1\},\qquad L=|\operatorname{supp}(P_T)|.
\]
Thus \(P_T\) preserves the one common target and \(R_w\) preserves the selectors' shared-assignment dependence. For a preorder pair \(C=(c_0,c_1)\), let \(P_T^C\) be the pushforward of \(P_T\) to its ordered class pairs, put \(L_C=|\operatorname{supp}(P_T^C)|\), let \(\Pi_C\) be its set of distinct supported exact four-cell vectors, and put \(U_C=|\Pi_C|\le L_C\le\min\{L,d_0d_1\}\). Retain the local coherent weights \(\omega_C(\pi)\), let \(\mathfrak C\) be the set of all preorder pairs, and define
\[
\Pi_\star=\bigcup_{C\in\mathfrak C}\Pi_C,\qquad G=|\Pi_\star|.
\]
If \(k\le M\), a global memo keyed exactly by \((M,k,k,\pi_{00},\pi_{01},\pi_{10})\), with \(\pi_{11}=1-\pi_{00}-\pi_{01}-\pi_{10}\), computes each coupled recurrence value once. Differing \(\omega_C(\pi)\) are multiplied only when each preorder objective is formed. Deterministic local grouping and global balanced-tree lookup give the instance-adaptive evaluation bound
\[
O\!\left(
|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}(m_0m_1+L)+\sum_{C\in\mathfrak C}\left[L_C\log(1+L_C)+L_C+U_C\right]+\left\{\sum_{C\in\mathfrak C}U_C\right\}\log(1+G)+GMk^2
\right)
\]
unit-cost exact comparisons and arithmetic operations. With \(L_{\max}=\max_C L_C\) and \(U_{\max}=\max_C U_C\), a uniform bound is
\[
O\!\left(|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}\left[m_0m_1+L+L_{\max}\log(1+L_{\max})+L_{\max}+U_{\max}\{1+\log(1+G)\}\right]+GMk^2\right),
\]
and working memory is \(O(m_0m_1+L+k^2+U_{\max}+G)\). Every \(\pi\) is constructed from the unchanged full \(R_w\), and every local weight is derived from the unchanged coherent \(P_T^C\), so the memo changes computation but neither probability law. If \(k=M+1\), evaluation is constant and \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)=0\). Binary attainment is false: in an admissible four-pair instance with \(M=3\) and \(\alpha=1/2\), a four-level ordering has frontier objective \(24957/160000\), whereas every binary score pair has objective at most \(759109/5000000\), a strict gap of \(83189/20000000\). Under disjoint target and calibration orbit support in one arm and \(k\le M\), \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)=1-\alpha\).

defs.def:pair-action: For pair block \(b\), \(s_b(v_{b,r},a)=(v_{b,1-r},a)\) for \(r,a\in\{0,1\}\); \(s_b\) fixes members outside block \(b\) and relabels \(D_i\) by \(\texttt{def:group-action}\). Thus \(O_{b,0}=\{(v_{b,0},0),(v_{b,1},0)\}\) and \(O_{b,1}=\{(v_{b,0},1),(v_{b,1},1)\}\).
defs.def:observable-selector-mixture: For every \(i\in\{1,\ldots,M\}\) and \(a\in\{0,1\}\), set \(\kappa_{w,a}=\sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_h\kappa_{h,a}\), draw \(J_{i,a}\sim\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\), set \(U_{i,a}=(J_{i,a},a)\), and set \(q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,a}=\sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_hq^{\mathrm{obs}}_{h,a}\).
defs.def:coverage-frontier:
For prespecified \(\boldsymbol\varepsilon=(\varepsilon_0,\varepsilon_1)\), define
\[
\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)=\max_{\substack{s_a:\mathcal O\to[0,K]\\a\in\{0,1\}}}\left[(1-\alpha)-\Pr\left\{s_1(O_{T,1})\le Q_{k_1}(s_1(O_{1,1}),\ldots,s_1(O_{M,1}))\ \text{and}\ s_0(O_{T,0})\le Q_{k_0}(s_0(O_{1,0}),\ldots,s_0(O_{M,0}))\right\}\right]_+.
\]
Here \(O_{i,a}\sim q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,a}\), the target orbits are the two lifts of the one common \(T\sim q_{\mathrm{tar}}\), and the calibration draws use \(\texttt{ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization}\), including their within-replicate cross-arm coupling. The functional is restricted to bounded orbit-constant score maps. Under \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), set \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)=\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)\), recovering the symmetric frontier.
defs.def:finite-rank-error:
For \(N=M+1\), prespecified \(\boldsymbol\varepsilon=(\varepsilon_0,\varepsilon_1)\), and \(k_a=\lceil N(1-\varepsilon_a)\rceil\), define
\[
\rho_{M,\varepsilon_a}=\frac{N-k_a}{N}=\frac{\lfloor N\varepsilon_a\rfloor}{N},\qquad a\in\{0,1\}.
\]
Under \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), write their common value as
\[
\rho_{M,\alpha}=\frac{M+1-k}{M+1}=\frac{\lfloor(M+1)\alpha/2\rfloor}{M+1}.
\]
defs.def:rank-inversion: Prespecify \(\boldsymbol\varepsilon=(\varepsilon_0,\varepsilon_1)\) with \(\varepsilon_a>0\) and \(\varepsilon_0+\varepsilon_1\le\alpha\), and set \(k_a=\lceil(M+1)(1-\varepsilon_a)\rceil\). For each \(a\in\{0,1\}\), compute \(S_{i,a}=|Y^{\mathrm{obs}}_{iJ_{i,a}}-\widehat m_a(X_{iJ_{i,a}})|\), take \(r_{a,(k_a)}\), set \(I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}=[\widehat m_a(X_{0T})-r_{a,(k_a)},\widehat m_a(X_{0T})+r_{a,(k_a)}]\cap[-K,K]\), and return \(\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w=I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{1,w}-I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{0,w}\). If \(k_a=M+1\), set \(r_{a,(k_a)}=+\infty\), so \(I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}=[-K,K]\). For proof ranks only, append independent continuous uniforms and use lexicographic ranks; reported closed intervals use weak inclusion. Under \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), define \(k=k_0=k_1\), \(r_{a,(k)}=r_{a,(k_a)}\), \(I_{a,w}=I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}\), and \(\mathcal C_w=\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w\), recovering the symmetric construction.

assumptions.ass:observable-selector-kernel: \(\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\ge0,\quad\sum_{v\in\mathcal V}\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=1,\quad z_v\ne a\Rightarrow\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=0\)
assumptions.ass:selector-equivariance: \(\kappa_{h,a}(\pi_gv\mid gz)=\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\quad\forall g\in\mathcal G,\ h\in\mathcal H,\ a\in\{0,1\}\)
assumptions.ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization: \(\mathcal L((J_{i,a})_{1\le i\le M,\ a\in\{0,1\}}\mid(L_i)_{0\le i\le M},(Z_i)_{0\le i\le M},T,\mathcal T)=\prod_{i=1}^M\prod_{a=0}^1\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\)

snapshot.stmt: Conditional on \(\mathcal T\), joint orbit balance is equivalent to equality in law of \(S_{i,a}\) and \(S_{0,a}\), for each \(a\in\{0,1\}\), uniformly over \(P\in\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\) and arbitrary fixed measurable trained predictor maps \(\widehat m_a:\mathsf X\to\mathbb R\). Under balance, conditional on \(\mathcal T\), \(S_{1,a},\ldots,S_{M,a},S_{0,a}\) are iid for each arm. The assertion is armwise and makes no joint-exchangeability claim across arms. If an orbit mass differs, \(\texttt{def:orbit-separating-law}\) gives a bounded invariant law and fixed predictor maps with unequal score laws.

defs.def:observable-replicated-experiment: For each \(i\in\{0,\ldots,M\}\), draw \(L_i\), draw \(Z_i\sim\nu\), set \(Y^{\mathrm{obs}}_{iv}=Y_{iv}(Z_{iv})\), and form \(\mathsf O_i=\{(X_{iv},Z_{iv},Y^{\mathrm{obs}}_{iv}):v\in\mathcal V\}\). Draw the one common \(T\sim q_{\mathrm{tar}}\), observe \(X_{0T}\) after selection, and use neither \(Y_{0T}(0)\) nor \(Y_{0T}(1)\) in fitting or calibration.
defs.def:observable-selector-mixture: For every \(i\in\{1,\ldots,M\}\) and \(a\in\{0,1\}\), set \(\kappa_{w,a}=\sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_h\kappa_{h,a}\), draw \(J_{i,a}\sim\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\), set \(U_{i,a}=(J_{i,a},a)\), and set \(q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,a}=\sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_hq^{\mathrm{obs}}_{h,a}\).
defs.def:invariant-laws: \(\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}=\{P:P\circ(gL_i)^{-1}=P\quad\forall g\in\mathcal G,\quad\max_{v\in\mathcal V,\ a\in\{0,1\}}|Y_v(a)|\le K\}\).
defs.def:orbit-separating-law: For \(a\in\{0,1\}\) and \(O\in\mathcal O\), set \(\widehat m_0=\widehat m_1=0\), set \(Y_v(a)=K\mathbf 1\{\iota_a(v)\in O\}\), set \(Y_v(1-a)=0\), and randomize the orbit-constant prototype by a uniform \(g\in\mathcal G\) before drawing \(Z_i\sim\nu\).

assumptions.ass:observable-selector-kernel: \(\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\ge0,\quad\sum_{v\in\mathcal V}\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=1,\quad z_v\ne a\Rightarrow\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=0\)
assumptions.ass:selector-equivariance: \(\kappa_{h,a}(\pi_gv\mid gz)=\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\quad\forall g\in\mathcal G,\ h\in\mathcal H,\ a\in\{0,1\}\)
assumptions.ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization: \(\mathcal L((J_{i,a})_{1\le i\le M,\ a\in\{0,1\}}\mid(L_i)_{0\le i\le M},(Z_i)_{0\le i\le M},T,\mathcal T)=\prod_{i=1}^M\prod_{a=0}^1\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\)

snapshot.stmt: There exists \(w\in\Delta(\mathcal H)\) jointly orbit-balancing both arms if and only if \(b_{\mathrm{LP}}\in\operatorname{conv}\{A_{\cdot h}:h\in\mathcal H\}\). Equivalently, the stacked LP in \(\texttt{def:common-selector-feasibility-lp}\) is feasible. If the condition fails, a Farkas separating hyperplane separates \(b_{\mathrm{LP}}\) from that convex hull. Fix a prespecified \(\boldsymbol\varepsilon=(\varepsilon_0,\varepsilon_1)\) with \(\varepsilon_a>0\) and \(\varepsilon_0+\varepsilon_1\le\alpha\). Conditional on \(\mathcal T\), a fixed feasible common \(w\), iid arrays, the independent common target \(T\), and selector auxiliary draws imply, for each arm, \[\Pr\!\left(S_{0,a}>r_{a,(k_a)}\mid\mathcal T\right)\le\frac{M+1-k_a}{M+1}=\rho_{M,\varepsilon_a}\le\varepsilon_a.\] Clipping to \([-K,K]\) and Bonferroni therefore give \[\Pr\!\left(\tau_T\in\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w\mid\mathcal T\right)\ge1-\rho_{M,\varepsilon_0}-\rho_{M,\varepsilon_1}\ge1-\varepsilon_0-\varepsilon_1\ge1-\alpha,\] without arm independence. Under \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), one has \(k_0=k_1=k\), \(I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}=I_{a,w}\), and \(\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w=\mathcal C_w\), so the symmetric rank and coverage formulas follow as a corollary.

defs.def:rank-inversion: Prespecify \(\boldsymbol\varepsilon=(\varepsilon_0,\varepsilon_1)\) with \(\varepsilon_a>0\) and \(\varepsilon_0+\varepsilon_1\le\alpha\), and set \(k_a=\lceil(M+1)(1-\varepsilon_a)\rceil\). For each \(a\in\{0,1\}\), compute \(S_{i,a}=|Y^{\mathrm{obs}}_{iJ_{i,a}}-\widehat m_a(X_{iJ_{i,a}})|\), take \(r_{a,(k_a)}\), set \(I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}=[\widehat m_a(X_{0T})-r_{a,(k_a)},\widehat m_a(X_{0T})+r_{a,(k_a)}]\cap[-K,K]\), and return \(\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w=I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{1,w}-I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{0,w}\). If \(k_a=M+1\), set \(r_{a,(k_a)}=+\infty\), so \(I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}=[-K,K]\). For proof ranks only, append independent continuous uniforms and use lexicographic ranks; reported closed intervals use weak inclusion. Under \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), define \(k=k_0=k_1\), \(r_{a,(k)}=r_{a,(k_a)}\), \(I_{a,w}=I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}\), and \(\mathcal C_w=\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w\), recovering the symmetric construction.
defs.def:finite-rank-error:
For \(N=M+1\), prespecified \(\boldsymbol\varepsilon=(\varepsilon_0,\varepsilon_1)\), and \(k_a=\lceil N(1-\varepsilon_a)\rceil\), define
\[
\rho_{M,\varepsilon_a}=\frac{N-k_a}{N}=\frac{\lfloor N\varepsilon_a\rfloor}{N},\qquad a\in\{0,1\}.
\]
Under \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), write their common value as
\[
\rho_{M,\alpha}=\frac{M+1-k}{M+1}=\frac{\lfloor(M+1)\alpha/2\rfloor}{M+1}.
\]

assumptions.ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization: \(\mathcal L((J_{i,a})_{1\le i\le M,\ a\in\{0,1\}}\mid(L_i)_{0\le i\le M},(Z_i)_{0\le i\le M},T,\mathcal T)=\prod_{i=1}^M\prod_{a=0}^1\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\)

snapshot.stmt: Let \(\epsilon_0,\epsilon_1>0\) be prespecified and satisfy \(\epsilon_0+\epsilon_1\le\alpha\), and let arm \(a\)'s weighted threshold in \(\texttt{def:weighted-rank-inversion}\) use its left-continuous \((1-\epsilon_a)\)-quantile. Conditional on \(\mathcal T\), orbit overlap implies \[\Pr_P(\tau_T\in\mathcal C^{\mathrm W}_w\mid\mathcal T)\ge1-\epsilon_0-\epsilon_1\ge1-\alpha\] for every fixed or prespecified \(w\), every \(P\in\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\), and arbitrary fixed measurable trained predictor maps. Invariance makes score laws coincide within an orbit, so the orbit likelihood ratios suffice even with member-level selector singularity. Under joint balance, every relevant \(\lambda_{a,O}=1\), and \(r^{\mathrm W}_{a,w}=r_{a,(k_a)}\) for \(k_a=\lceil(M+1)(1-\epsilon_a)\rceil\), including the \(+\infty\) clause when \(k_a=M+1\); hence weighted inversion recovers ordinary arm-specific rank inversion. Taking \(\epsilon_0=\epsilon_1=\alpha/2\) gives \(k_0=k_1=\lceil(M+1)(1-\alpha/2)\rceil\) and recovers the original symmetric statement and formulas.

defs.def:weighted-rank-inversion: Prespecify \(\boldsymbol\varepsilon=(\varepsilon_0,\varepsilon_1)\) with \(\varepsilon_a>0\) and \(\varepsilon_0+\varepsilon_1\le\alpha\). For each arm \(a\), place mass \(\lambda_{a,O_{i,a}}\) at each \(S_{i,a}\), place mass \(\lambda_{a,O_{T,a}}\) at \(+\infty\), normalize the total mass, and take its left-continuous \((1-\varepsilon_a)\)-quantile \(r^{\mathrm W}_{a,w}\). Set \(I^{\mathrm W}_{a,w}=[\widehat m_a(X_{0T})-r^{\mathrm W}_{a,w},\widehat m_a(X_{0T})+r^{\mathrm W}_{a,w}]\cap[-K,K]\), and return \(\mathcal C^{\mathrm W}_w=I^{\mathrm W}_{1,w}-I^{\mathrm W}_{0,w}\). Under \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), this is the previous symmetric weighted construction. Thus the declared estimand functional \(\mathcal C^{\mathrm W}_w\) now denotes the arm-specific weighted procedure while retaining its equal-split specialization.
defs.def:rank-inversion: Prespecify \(\boldsymbol\varepsilon=(\varepsilon_0,\varepsilon_1)\) with \(\varepsilon_a>0\) and \(\varepsilon_0+\varepsilon_1\le\alpha\), and set \(k_a=\lceil(M+1)(1-\varepsilon_a)\rceil\). For each \(a\in\{0,1\}\), compute \(S_{i,a}=|Y^{\mathrm{obs}}_{iJ_{i,a}}-\widehat m_a(X_{iJ_{i,a}})|\), take \(r_{a,(k_a)}\), set \(I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}=[\widehat m_a(X_{0T})-r_{a,(k_a)},\widehat m_a(X_{0T})+r_{a,(k_a)}]\cap[-K,K]\), and return \(\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w=I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{1,w}-I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{0,w}\). If \(k_a=M+1\), set \(r_{a,(k_a)}=+\infty\), so \(I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}=[-K,K]\). For proof ranks only, append independent continuous uniforms and use lexicographic ranks; reported closed intervals use weak inclusion. Under \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), define \(k=k_0=k_1\), \(r_{a,(k)}=r_{a,(k_a)}\), \(I_{a,w}=I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}\), and \(\mathcal C_w=\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w\), recovering the symmetric construction.

assumptions.ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization: \(\mathcal L((J_{i,a})_{1\le i\le M,\ a\in\{0,1\}}\mid(L_i)_{0\le i\le M},(Z_i)_{0\le i\le M},T,\mathcal T)=\prod_{i=1}^M\prod_{a=0}^1\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\)

snapshot.stmt: Suppose \(k\le M\), equivalently \(\alpha\ge2/(M+1)\), and, for one arm \(a\), every target-supported orbit has zero \(q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,a}\) mass. Then \(\texttt{def:disjoint-orbit-law}\) belongs to \(\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\), all adverse-arm calibration scores equal \(0\), and \(r_{a,(k)}=0\). The other arm's scores and threshold equal \(0\), so \(\mathcal C_w=\{0\}\). The common target effect equals \(K\) for \(a=1\) and \(-K\) for \(a=0\), hence \(\Pr_P(\tau_T\in\mathcal C_w\mid\mathcal T)=0\).

defs.def:disjoint-orbit-law: Fix \(a\in\{0,1\}\). Set \(\widehat m_0=\widehat m_1=0\), set \(Y_v(a)=K\) on target-supported arm-\(a\) orbits, set \(Y_v(a)=0\) on every state with positive calibration mass and on remaining states, set \(Y_v(1-a)=0\), and randomize the orbit-constant prototype by a uniform \(g\in\mathcal G\) before drawing \(Z_i\sim\nu\).
defs.def:invariant-laws: \(\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}=\{P:P\circ(gL_i)^{-1}=P\quad\forall g\in\mathcal G,\quad\max_{v\in\mathcal V,\ a\in\{0,1\}}|Y_v(a)|\le K\}\).
defs.def:observable-selector-mixture: For every \(i\in\{1,\ldots,M\}\) and \(a\in\{0,1\}\), set \(\kappa_{w,a}=\sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_h\kappa_{h,a}\), draw \(J_{i,a}\sim\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\), set \(U_{i,a}=(J_{i,a},a)\), and set \(q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,a}=\sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_hq^{\mathrm{obs}}_{h,a}\).
defs.def:rank-inversion: Prespecify \(\boldsymbol\varepsilon=(\varepsilon_0,\varepsilon_1)\) with \(\varepsilon_a>0\) and \(\varepsilon_0+\varepsilon_1\le\alpha\), and set \(k_a=\lceil(M+1)(1-\varepsilon_a)\rceil\). For each \(a\in\{0,1\}\), compute \(S_{i,a}=|Y^{\mathrm{obs}}_{iJ_{i,a}}-\widehat m_a(X_{iJ_{i,a}})|\), take \(r_{a,(k_a)}\), set \(I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}=[\widehat m_a(X_{0T})-r_{a,(k_a)},\widehat m_a(X_{0T})+r_{a,(k_a)}]\cap[-K,K]\), and return \(\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w=I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{1,w}-I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{0,w}\). If \(k_a=M+1\), set \(r_{a,(k_a)}=+\infty\), so \(I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}=[-K,K]\). For proof ranks only, append independent continuous uniforms and use lexicographic ranks; reported closed intervals use weak inclusion. Under \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), define \(k=k_0=k_1\), \(r_{a,(k)}=r_{a,(k_a)}\), \(I_{a,w}=I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}\), and \(\mathcal C_w=\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w\), recovering the symmetric construction.

assumptions.ass:observable-selector-kernel: \(\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\ge0,\quad\sum_{v\in\mathcal V}\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=1,\quad z_v\ne a\Rightarrow\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=0\)
assumptions.ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization: \(\mathcal L((J_{i,a})_{1\le i\le M,\ a\in\{0,1\}}\mid(L_i)_{0\le i\le M},(Z_i)_{0\le i\le M},T,\mathcal T)=\prod_{i=1}^M\prod_{a=0}^1\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\)

snapshot.stmt: For a pair block \(b\), \(s_b(v_{b,r},a)=(v_{b,1-r},a)\) for \(r,a\in\{0,1\}\), with \(s_b\) fixing all outside members and relabelling complete records as in \(\texttt{def:group-action}\). Its arm-specific orbits are \(O_{b,0}=\{(v_{b,0},0),(v_{b,1},0)\}\) and \(O_{b,1}=\{(v_{b,0},1),(v_{b,1},1)\}\). If the target arm-\(1\) lifted law is supported on \(O_{b,1}\), an exclusive mate-control analogue supported on the realized mate's control state in \(O_{b,0}\) has a different orbit-mass vector, cannot satisfy exact joint balance, and is not a legal arm-\(1\) selector. Separately, if a legal implemented arm-\(1\) selector satisfies \(q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,1}(O_{b,1})=0\), then arm \(1\) satisfies the disjoint-orbit premise. Its zero-coverage conclusion applies when \(k\le M\); for \(k=M+1\), coverage is one by \(\texttt{prop:rank-boundary}\).

defs.def:pair-action: For pair block \(b\), \(s_b(v_{b,r},a)=(v_{b,1-r},a)\) for \(r,a\in\{0,1\}\); \(s_b\) fixes members outside block \(b\) and relabels \(D_i\) by \(\texttt{def:group-action}\). Thus \(O_{b,0}=\{(v_{b,0},0),(v_{b,1},0)\}\) and \(O_{b,1}=\{(v_{b,0},1),(v_{b,1},1)\}\).

assumptions.ass:observable-selector-kernel: \(\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\ge0,\quad\sum_{v\in\mathcal V}\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=1,\quad z_v\ne a\Rightarrow\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=0\)

snapshot.stmt: Let \(\mathcal V=\{v_{1,0},v_{1,1},v_{2,0},v_{2,1}\}\) and \(\mathcal G=S_2\times S_2\), with the generators in \(\texttt{def:pair-action}\). The four arm-specific orbits are \(O_{1,0},O_{1,1},O_{2,0},O_{2,1}\). If \(q_{\mathrm{tar}}\) concentrates on \(v_{1,0}\), its arm \(1\) orbit vector has mass \(1\) on \(O_{1,1}\). An exclusive mate-control analogue concentrated on \((v_{1,1},0)\) has mass \(1\) on \(O_{1,0}\), so the relevant stacked LP equations cannot both hold and the analogue is not a legal arm \(1\) selector. If a legal implemented selector has \(q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,1}(O_{1,1})=0\), then for \(k\le M\) the bounded witness has \(\mathcal C_w=\{0\}\) and coverage \(0\); for \(k=M+1\), it has \(I_{0,w}=I_{1,w}=[-K,K]\), \(\mathcal C_w=[-2K,2K]\), and coverage \(1\).

defs.def:pair-action: For pair block \(b\), \(s_b(v_{b,r},a)=(v_{b,1-r},a)\) for \(r,a\in\{0,1\}\); \(s_b\) fixes members outside block \(b\) and relabels \(D_i\) by \(\texttt{def:group-action}\). Thus \(O_{b,0}=\{(v_{b,0},0),(v_{b,1},0)\}\) and \(O_{b,1}=\{(v_{b,0},1),(v_{b,1},1)\}\).

assumptions.ass:observable-selector-kernel: \(\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\ge0,\quad\sum_{v\in\mathcal V}\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=1,\quad z_v\ne a\Rightarrow\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=0\)

snapshot.stmt: Let \(\mathscr O\) be finite, let \(F_O\) be probability laws on \([0,\infty)\), and let \(q=(q_O)\) and \(p=(p_O)\) be probability mass functions satisfying \(p_O>0\Rightarrow q_O>0\). Define \(\mathsf Q(\{O\}\times C)=q_OF_O(C)\), \(\mathsf P(\{O\}\times C)=p_OF_O(C)\), and \(\lambda_O=p_O/q_O\) when \(q_O>0\), with value zero when \(p_O=q_O=0\). Let \((O_i,R_i)\), \(1\le i\le M\), be iid from \(\mathsf Q\), independently of \((O_0,R_0)\sim\mathsf P\). For \(0<\epsilon<1\), let \(r^{\mathrm W}\) be the left-continuous \((1-\epsilon)\)-quantile of the normalized measure placing mass \(\lambda_{O_i}\) at \(R_i\) for \(1\le i\le M\) and mass \(\lambda_{O_0}\) at \(+\infty\). Then \(\Pr(R_0>r^{\mathrm W})\le\epsilon\).

Fix an arm \(a\) and suppose every target-supported arm-\(a\) orbit has zero \(q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,a}\) mass. Under \(\texttt{def:disjoint-orbit-law}\), the latent law belongs to \(\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\), every selected calibration score in both arms is zero, and the common target effect is \(K\) if \(a=1\) and \(-K\) if \(a=0\). If \(k_0\le M\) and \(k_1\le M\), then \(I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{0,w}=I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{1,w}=\{0\}\), \(\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w=\{0\}\), and coverage is zero. If exactly one \(k_c=M+1\), then the boundary arm interval is \([-K,K]\), the other is \(\{0\}\), \(\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w=[-K,K]\), and this witness has coverage one. If both \(k_0=k_1=M+1\), then \(\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w=[-2K,2K]\) and this witness again has coverage one. Moreover \(k_c\le M\) is equivalent to \(\varepsilon_c\ge1/(M+1)\). The symmetric choice recovers \(\texttt{prop:disjoint-orbit-witness}\).

defs.def:disjoint-orbit-law: Fix \(a\in\{0,1\}\). Set \(\widehat m_0=\widehat m_1=0\), set \(Y_v(a)=K\) on target-supported arm-\(a\) orbits, set \(Y_v(a)=0\) on every state with positive calibration mass and on remaining states, set \(Y_v(1-a)=0\), and randomize the orbit-constant prototype by a uniform \(g\in\mathcal G\) before drawing \(Z_i\sim\nu\).
defs.def:invariant-laws: \(\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}=\{P:P\circ(gL_i)^{-1}=P\quad\forall g\in\mathcal G,\quad\max_{v\in\mathcal V,\ a\in\{0,1\}}|Y_v(a)|\le K\}\).
defs.def:observable-selector-mixture: For every \(i\in\{1,\ldots,M\}\) and \(a\in\{0,1\}\), set \(\kappa_{w,a}=\sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_h\kappa_{h,a}\), draw \(J_{i,a}\sim\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\), set \(U_{i,a}=(J_{i,a},a)\), and set \(q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,a}=\sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_hq^{\mathrm{obs}}_{h,a}\).
defs.def:rank-inversion: Prespecify \(\boldsymbol\varepsilon=(\varepsilon_0,\varepsilon_1)\) with \(\varepsilon_a>0\) and \(\varepsilon_0+\varepsilon_1\le\alpha\), and set \(k_a=\lceil(M+1)(1-\varepsilon_a)\rceil\). For each \(a\in\{0,1\}\), compute \(S_{i,a}=|Y^{\mathrm{obs}}_{iJ_{i,a}}-\widehat m_a(X_{iJ_{i,a}})|\), take \(r_{a,(k_a)}\), set \(I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}=[\widehat m_a(X_{0T})-r_{a,(k_a)},\widehat m_a(X_{0T})+r_{a,(k_a)}]\cap[-K,K]\), and return \(\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w=I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{1,w}-I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{0,w}\). If \(k_a=M+1\), set \(r_{a,(k_a)}=+\infty\), so \(I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}=[-K,K]\). For proof ranks only, append independent continuous uniforms and use lexicographic ranks; reported closed intervals use weak inclusion. Under \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), define \(k=k_0=k_1\), \(r_{a,(k)}=r_{a,(k_a)}\), \(I_{a,w}=I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}\), and \(\mathcal C_w=\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w\), recovering the symmetric construction.

assumptions.ass:observable-selector-kernel: \(\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\ge0,\quad\sum_{v\in\mathcal V}\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=1,\quad z_v\ne a\Rightarrow\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=0\)
assumptions.ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization: \(\mathcal L((J_{i,a})_{1\le i\le M,\ a\in\{0,1\}}\mid(L_i)_{0\le i\le M},(Z_i)_{0\le i\le M},T,\mathcal T)=\prod_{i=1}^M\prod_{a=0}^1\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\)

snapshot.stmt: The pair-swap and mate-control legality conclusions of \(\texttt{prop:s2-reduction}\) remain unchanged under the asymmetric budget. If the target arm-\(1\) lift is supported on \(O_{b,1}\) and a legal implemented arm-\(1\) selector has \(q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,1}(O_{b,1})=0\), the asymmetric disjoint-orbit witness has zero coverage when both \(k_0,k_1\le M\), has effect set \([-K,K]\) and coverage one when exactly one rank equals \(M+1\), and has effect set \([-2K,2K]\) and coverage one when both ranks equal \(M+1\). The symmetric choice recovers \(\texttt{prop:s2-reduction}\).

defs.def:pair-action: Fix a pair block \(b\) with \(\mathcal V_b=\{v_{b,0},v_{b,1}\}\). The pair notation and its global-orbit consequences are invoked only under the local condition \(\langle s_b\rangle=\{e,s_b\}\le\mathcal G\). Under that condition, \(s_b(v_{b,r},a)=(v_{b,1-r},a)\) for \(r,a\in\{0,1\}\), \(s_b\) fixes members outside block \(b\), and it relabels complete records by \(\texttt{def:group-action}\). Because every group element preserves blocks and block \(b\) has exactly two members, the global \(\mathcal G\)-orbits in that block are \(O_{b,0}=\{(v_{b,0},0),(v_{b,1},0)\}\) and \(O_{b,1}=\{(v_{b,0},1),(v_{b,1},1)\}\). For a collection of pair blocks, every invoked local subgroup must be contained in \(\mathcal G\); the standard \(S_2^B\) specialization contains the product of all relevant swaps.

assumptions.ass:observable-selector-kernel: \(\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\ge0,\quad\sum_{v\in\mathcal V}\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=1,\quad z_v\ne a\Rightarrow\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=0\)

snapshot.stmt: In the two-pair instance of \(\texttt{prop:two-pair-sanity-check}\), the four arm-specific orbits and the mate-control LP and legality failures are unchanged under the asymmetric budget. If a legal selector has \(q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,1}(O_{1,1})=0\), the bounded witness has \(\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w=\{0\}\) and coverage zero when both ranks are finite, \(\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w=[-K,K]\) and coverage one when exactly one rank is at the boundary, and \(\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w=[-2K,2K]\) and coverage one when both ranks are at the boundary. The symmetric choice recovers the frozen two-pair calculation.

defs.def:pair-action: Fix a pair block \(b\) with \(\mathcal V_b=\{v_{b,0},v_{b,1}\}\). The pair notation and its global-orbit consequences are invoked only under the local condition \(\langle s_b\rangle=\{e,s_b\}\le\mathcal G\). Under that condition, \(s_b(v_{b,r},a)=(v_{b,1-r},a)\) for \(r,a\in\{0,1\}\), \(s_b\) fixes members outside block \(b\), and it relabels complete records by \(\texttt{def:group-action}\). Because every group element preserves blocks and block \(b\) has exactly two members, the global \(\mathcal G\)-orbits in that block are \(O_{b,0}=\{(v_{b,0},0),(v_{b,1},0)\}\) and \(O_{b,1}=\{(v_{b,0},1),(v_{b,1},1)\}\). For a collection of pair blocks, every invoked local subgroup must be contained in \(\mathcal G\); the standard \(S_2^B\) specialization contains the product of all relevant swaps.

assumptions.ass:observable-selector-kernel: \(\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\ge0,\quad\sum_{v\in\mathcal V}\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=1,\quad z_v\ne a\Rightarrow\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=0\)

snapshot.stmt:
The functional \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)\) in \(\texttt{def:coverage-frontier}\) is the exact finite worst-coverage deficit over the stated class of bounded orbit-constant score maps for armwise ranks \(k_0,k_1\); no equality with a supremum over all \(P\in\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\) is asserted. Write \(\mathcal O_a=\{O\in\mathcal O:O\subseteq\mathcal U_a\}\), \(m_a=|\mathcal O_a|\), and let \(F_m\) denote the number of total preorders on an \(m\)-element labelled set. If \(k_0,k_1\le M\), the maximum is attained by enumerating preorder pairs, and every preorder has a representative in \([0,K]\) with at most \(m_a\) levels. Let \(P_T\), \(L\), and \(R_w\) be as in \(\texttt{lem:coherent-two-finite-rank-frontier-evaluator}\). For preorder pair \(C\), let \(P_T^C\) be the coherent target pushforward, let \(L_C=|\operatorname{supp}(P_T^C)|\), let \(\Pi_C\) be the set of distinct supported exact prefix-table vectors \(\pi^C(j,\ell)=(\pi_{00}^C,\pi_{01}^C,\pi_{10}^C,\pi_{11}^C)\), and put \(U_C=|\Pi_C|\le L_C\le\min\{L,d_0d_1\}\). Retain the local coherent weights \(\omega_C(\pi)\) obtained by aggregating \(P_T^C\) mass over identical vectors. Let \(\mathfrak C\) contain all \(F_{m_0}F_{m_1}\) preorder pairs and define
\[
\Pi_\star=\bigcup_{C\in\mathfrak C}\Pi_C,\qquad G=|\Pi_\star|.
\]
The global recurrence memo is keyed exactly by \((M,k_0,k_1,\pi_{00},\pi_{01},\pi_{10})\), with \(\pi_{11}=1-\pi_{00}-\pi_{01}-\pi_{10}\). Each recurrence value \(D_{M,k_0,k_1}(\pi)\) is computed once globally, while the differing weights \(\omega_C(\pi)\) never enter the recurrence and are multiplied only when each preorder objective is formed. Deterministic local grouping costs \(O(L_C\log(1+L_C))\) exact comparisons and \(O(L_C)\) aggregation arithmetic, and global balanced-tree lookup costs \(O((\sum_CU_C)\log(1+G))\). After \(R_w\) is tabulated, exact enumeration uses
\[
O\!\left(
|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}(m_0m_1+L)+\sum_{C\in\mathfrak C}\left[L_C\log(1+L_C)+L_C+U_C\right]+\left\{\sum_{C\in\mathfrak C}U_C\right\}\log(1+G)+GMk_0k_1
\right).
\]
With \(L_{\max}=\max_C L_C\) and \(U_{\max}=\max_C U_C\), a uniform bound is
\[
O\!\left(|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}\left[m_0m_1+L+L_{\max}\log(1+L_{\max})+L_{\max}+U_{\max}\{1+\log(1+G)\}\right]+GMk_0k_1\right),
\]
and working memory is \(O(m_0m_1+L+k_0k_1+U_{\max}+G)\). Every \(\pi\) is constructed from the unchanged full \(R_w\), while every \(\omega_C\) is derived from the unchanged coherent \(P_T^C\); hence global memoization preserves both calibration-arm dependence and the one common target.

If exactly one rank \(k_b=M+1\), put \(c=1-b\), so \(k_c\le M\). For a fixed nonboundary score map \(s_c\), define \(\theta(O_t)=\sum_{O\in\mathcal O_c:s_c(O)<s_c(O_t)}q_{c,O}\). The exact rank-event probability is
\[
\sum_{O_t\in\mathcal O_c}p_{c,O_t}\Pr\!\left\{\operatorname{Bin}\bigl(M,\theta(O_t)\bigr)<k_c\right\}.
\]
For ordered nonempty tie classes \(C_1\prec\cdots\prec C_d\), define \(q_j=q_c(C_j)\), \(p_j=p_c(C_j)\), and \(\theta_j=\sum_{\ell<j}q_\ell\); then the probability is \(\sum_{j=1}^d p_j\Pr\{\operatorname{Bin}(M,\theta_j)<k_c\}\). These masses are formed by one cumulative ordered-class scan. For \(0<\theta<1\), exact lower-tail terms start at \(t_0=(1-\theta)^M\) and obey
\[
t_{r+1}=t_r\frac{M-r}{r+1}\frac{\theta}{1-\theta},\qquad 0\le r<k_c-1,
\]
whereas exact upper-tail terms start at \(u_M=\theta^M\) and obey
\[
u_{r-1}=u_r\frac{r}{M-r+1}\frac{1-\theta}{\theta},\qquad k_c<r\le M.
\]
Exponentiation by squaring initializes either endpoint in \(O(\log M)\) operations, and the shorter tail uses \(O(\min\{k_c,M-k_c+1\})\) further operations. At \(\theta=0\) and \(\theta=1\), the probabilities are \(1\) and \(0\), respectively, without division. After \(q_c\) and \(p_c\) are tabulated, exact maximization enumerates only the \(F_{m_c}\) nonboundary preorders and uses
\[
O\!\left(F_{m_c}m_c\left[1+\log M+\min\{k_c,M-k_c+1\}\right]\right)
\]
arithmetic operations and \(O(1)\) auxiliary CDF memory, with no factor \(F_{m_{1-c}}\), \(|\mathcal V|\), or per-case \(m_0m_1\) scan.

If both ranks equal \(M+1\), evaluation is constant and the frontier is zero. Binary attainment is false: in the admissible symmetric instance \(M=3\), \(\alpha=1/2\), and \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=1/4\), a four-level objective equals \(24957/160000\), whereas every binary pair has objective at most \(759109/5000000\), a gap of \(83189/20000000\). If target and calibration orbit supports are disjoint in arm \(a\) and \(k_a\le M\), then \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)=1-\alpha\). If \(k_a=M+1\), the frontier reduces exactly to the other arm's rank event through the preceding one-arm formula. On the diagonal \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), this is \(\texttt{thm:sharp-orbit-coverage-frontier}\).

defs.def:pair-action: Fix a pair block \(b\) with \(\mathcal V_b=\{v_{b,0},v_{b,1}\}\). The pair notation and its global-orbit consequences are invoked only under the local condition \(\langle s_b\rangle=\{e,s_b\}\le\mathcal G\). Under that condition, \(s_b(v_{b,r},a)=(v_{b,1-r},a)\) for \(r,a\in\{0,1\}\), \(s_b\) fixes members outside block \(b\), and it relabels complete records by \(\texttt{def:group-action}\). Because every group element preserves blocks and block \(b\) has exactly two members, the global \(\mathcal G\)-orbits in that block are \(O_{b,0}=\{(v_{b,0},0),(v_{b,1},0)\}\) and \(O_{b,1}=\{(v_{b,0},1),(v_{b,1},1)\}\). For a collection of pair blocks, every invoked local subgroup must be contained in \(\mathcal G\); the standard \(S_2^B\) specialization contains the product of all relevant swaps.
defs.def:observable-selector-mixture: For every \(i\in\{1,\ldots,M\}\) and \(a\in\{0,1\}\), set \(\kappa_{w,a}=\sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_h\kappa_{h,a}\), draw \(J_{i,a}\sim\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\), set \(U_{i,a}=(J_{i,a},a)\), and set \(q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,a}=\sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_hq^{\mathrm{obs}}_{h,a}\).
defs.def:coverage-frontier:
For prespecified \(\boldsymbol\varepsilon=(\varepsilon_0,\varepsilon_1)\), define
\[
\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)=\max_{\substack{s_a:\mathcal O\to[0,K]\\a\in\{0,1\}}}\left[(1-\alpha)-\Pr\left\{s_1(O_{T,1})\le Q_{k_1}(s_1(O_{1,1}),\ldots,s_1(O_{M,1}))\ \text{and}\ s_0(O_{T,0})\le Q_{k_0}(s_0(O_{1,0}),\ldots,s_0(O_{M,0}))\right\}\right]_+.
\]
Here \(O_{i,a}\sim q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,a}\), the target orbits are the two lifts of the one common \(T\sim q_{\mathrm{tar}}\), and the calibration draws use \(\texttt{ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization}\), including their within-replicate cross-arm coupling. The functional is restricted to bounded orbit-constant score maps. Under \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), set \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)=\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)\), recovering the symmetric frontier.
defs.def:finite-rank-error:
For \(N=M+1\), prespecified \(\boldsymbol\varepsilon=(\varepsilon_0,\varepsilon_1)\), and \(k_a=\lceil N(1-\varepsilon_a)\rceil\), define
\[
\rho_{M,\varepsilon_a}=\frac{N-k_a}{N}=\frac{\lfloor N\varepsilon_a\rfloor}{N},\qquad a\in\{0,1\}.
\]
Under \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), write their common value as
\[
\rho_{M,\alpha}=\frac{M+1-k}{M+1}=\frac{\lfloor(M+1)\alpha/2\rfloor}{M+1}.
\]
defs.def:rank-inversion: Prespecify \(\boldsymbol\varepsilon=(\varepsilon_0,\varepsilon_1)\) with \(\varepsilon_a>0\) and \(\varepsilon_0+\varepsilon_1\le\alpha\), and set \(k_a=\lceil(M+1)(1-\varepsilon_a)\rceil\). For each \(a\in\{0,1\}\), compute \(S_{i,a}=|Y^{\mathrm{obs}}_{iJ_{i,a}}-\widehat m_a(X_{iJ_{i,a}})|\), take \(r_{a,(k_a)}\), set \(I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}=[\widehat m_a(X_{0T})-r_{a,(k_a)},\widehat m_a(X_{0T})+r_{a,(k_a)}]\cap[-K,K]\), and return \(\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w=I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{1,w}-I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{0,w}\). If \(k_a=M+1\), set \(r_{a,(k_a)}=+\infty\), so \(I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}=[-K,K]\). For proof ranks only, append independent continuous uniforms and use lexicographic ranks; reported closed intervals use weak inclusion. Under \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), define \(k=k_0=k_1\), \(r_{a,(k)}=r_{a,(k_a)}\), \(I_{a,w}=I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}\), and \(\mathcal C_w=\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w\), recovering the symmetric construction.

assumptions.ass:observable-selector-kernel: \(\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\ge0,\quad\sum_{v\in\mathcal V}\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=1,\quad z_v\ne a\Rightarrow\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=0\)
assumptions.ass:selector-equivariance: \(\kappa_{h,a}(\pi_gv\mid gz)=\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\quad\forall g\in\mathcal G,\ h\in\mathcal H,\ a\in\{0,1\}\)
assumptions.ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization: \(\mathcal L((J_{i,a})_{1\le i\le M,\ a\in\{0,1\}}\mid(L_i)_{0\le i\le M},(Z_i)_{0\le i\le M},T,\mathcal T)=\prod_{i=1}^M\prod_{a=0}^1\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\)

snapshot.stmt:
Assume that \(\mathcal H\) is finite and nonempty. The minimum \(D_*\) in \(\texttt{def:minimum-imbalance-common-selector}\) is attained, so \(\mathcal W_*\) is nonempty, and the displayed absolute-value linear program has value \(D_*\). Its quantitative dual is attained and satisfies the strong-duality identity
\[
D_*=\max_{\lVert y\rVert_\infty\le1/2}\left\{\min_{h\in\mathcal H}y^\top A_{\cdot h}-y^\top b_{\mathrm{LP}}\right\}.
\]
If \(D_*>0\), the negative of any maximizing dual vector is a Farkas separating direction with margin \(D_*\). Every \(w_*\in\mathcal W_*\) is computable solely from the known randomization law, target rule, and selector library before scores are observed, and hence is prespecified. Moreover, \(D_*=0\) if and only if the exact joint-balance linear program in \(\texttt{def:common-selector-feasibility-lp}\) is feasible. For every prespecified \(\boldsymbol\varepsilon=(\varepsilon_0,\varepsilon_1)\) with \(\varepsilon_a>0\) and \(\varepsilon_0+\varepsilon_1\le\alpha\), every \(P\in\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\), and arbitrary fixed measurable trained predictor maps, conditional on \(\mathcal T\),
\[
\Pr_P\!\left(\tau_T\in\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{w_*}\mid\mathcal T\right)\ge\max\!\left\{0,1-\rho_{M,\varepsilon_0}-\rho_{M,\varepsilon_1}-D_*\right\}.
\]
This numerical certificate weakly dominates the corresponding certificate for every fixed common \(w\in\Delta(\mathcal H)\), and the dominance is strict whenever \(D_*<\Delta_0(w)+\Delta_1(w)\) and \(1-\rho_{M,\varepsilon_0}-\rho_{M,\varepsilon_1}-D_*>0\). On the equal-split diagonal \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\),
\[
\Pr_P(\tau_T\in\mathcal C_{w_*}\mid\mathcal T)\ge\max\{0,1-2\rho_{M,\alpha}-D_*\}.
\]

defs.def:observable-selector-mixture: For every \(i\in\{1,\ldots,M\}\) and \(a\in\{0,1\}\), set \(\kappa_{w,a}=\sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_h\kappa_{h,a}\), draw \(J_{i,a}\sim\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\), set \(U_{i,a}=(J_{i,a},a)\), and set \(q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,a}=\sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_hq^{\mathrm{obs}}_{h,a}\).
defs.def:minimum-imbalance-common-selector: For every finite selector library define \(\Delta(\mathcal H)=\{w\in\mathbb R^{|\mathcal H|}:w_h\ge0\ \forall h,\ \sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_h=1\}\); in particular \(\Delta(\varnothing)=\varnothing\), because the empty sum is zero. If \(\mathcal H=\varnothing\), no selector mixture, implemented kernel, weighted procedure, minimum \(D_*\), optimizer set \(\mathcal W_*\), or choice \(w_*\) exists. If \(\mathcal H\) is finite and nonempty, define \[D_*=\min_{w\in\Delta(\mathcal H)}\{\Delta_0(w)+\Delta_1(w)\}=\min_{w\in\Delta(\mathcal H)}\frac12\sum_{a=0}^1\sum_{O\in\mathcal O}|(Aw-b_{\mathrm{LP}})_{a,O}|,\qquad \mathcal W_*=\operatorname*{arg\,min}_{w\in\Delta(\mathcal H)}\{\Delta_0(w)+\Delta_1(w)\}.\] The finite absolute-value linear program is \[\begin{aligned}\operatorname*{minimize}_{w,(t_{a,O})}\quad&\frac12\sum_{a=0}^1\sum_{O\in\mathcal O}t_{a,O}\\\text{subject to}\quad&-t_{a,O}\le(Aw-b_{\mathrm{LP}})_{a,O}\le t_{a,O},\quad t_{a,O}\ge0,\\&w_h\ge0,\quad\sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_h=1.\end{aligned}\] Its quantitative dual is \[\operatorname*{maximize}_{\lVert y\rVert_\infty\le1/2}\left\{\min_{h\in\mathcal H}y^\top A_{\cdot h}-y^\top b_{\mathrm{LP}}\right\}.\] These primal and dual objects are defined only in the finite nonempty case; their equality and attainment are certified by \(\texttt{thm:minimum-imbalance-selector-certificate}\).
defs.def:invariant-laws: \(\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}=\{P:P\circ(gL_i)^{-1}=P\quad\forall g\in\mathcal G,\quad\max_{v\in\mathcal V,\ a\in\{0,1\}}|Y_v(a)|\le K\}\).
defs.def:rank-inversion: Prespecify \(\boldsymbol\varepsilon=(\varepsilon_0,\varepsilon_1)\) with \(\varepsilon_a>0\) and \(\varepsilon_0+\varepsilon_1\le\alpha\), and set \(k_a=\lceil(M+1)(1-\varepsilon_a)\rceil\). For each \(a\in\{0,1\}\), compute \(S_{i,a}=|Y^{\mathrm{obs}}_{iJ_{i,a}}-\widehat m_a(X_{iJ_{i,a}})|\), take \(r_{a,(k_a)}\), set \(I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}=[\widehat m_a(X_{0T})-r_{a,(k_a)},\widehat m_a(X_{0T})+r_{a,(k_a)}]\cap[-K,K]\), and return \(\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w=I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{1,w}-I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{0,w}\). If \(k_a=M+1\), set \(r_{a,(k_a)}=+\infty\), so \(I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}=[-K,K]\). For proof ranks only, append independent continuous uniforms and use lexicographic ranks; reported closed intervals use weak inclusion. Under \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), define \(k=k_0=k_1\), \(r_{a,(k)}=r_{a,(k_a)}\), \(I_{a,w}=I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}\), and \(\mathcal C_w=\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w\), recovering the symmetric construction.
defs.def:finite-rank-error:
For \(N=M+1\), prespecified \(\boldsymbol\varepsilon=(\varepsilon_0,\varepsilon_1)\), and \(k_a=\lceil N(1-\varepsilon_a)\rceil\), define
\[
\rho_{M,\varepsilon_a}=\frac{N-k_a}{N}=\frac{\lfloor N\varepsilon_a\rfloor}{N},\qquad a\in\{0,1\}.
\]
Under \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), write their common value as
\[
\rho_{M,\alpha}=\frac{M+1-k}{M+1}=\frac{\lfloor(M+1)\alpha/2\rfloor}{M+1}.
\]

assumptions.ass:observable-selector-kernel: \(\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\ge0,\quad\sum_{v\in\mathcal V}\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=1,\quad z_v\ne a\Rightarrow\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=0\)

snapshot.stmt:
Suppose \(k_0,k_1\le M\). Write \(\mathcal O_a=\{O\in\mathcal O:O\subseteq\mathcal U_a\}\), \(m_a=|\mathcal O_a|\), and let \(F_m\) be the number of total preorders on an \(m\)-element labelled set. Define
\[
\kappa_{w,a}(O\mid z)=\sum_{v:(v,a)\in O}\kappa_{w,a}(v\mid z),\qquad R_w(O_0,O_1)=\sum_z\nu(z)\kappa_{w,0}(O_0\mid z)\kappa_{w,1}(O_1\mid z).
\]
For \(v\in\mathcal V\) and \(a\in\{0,1\}\), let \(O_a(v)\) be the orbit containing \((v,a)\), and define
\[
P_T(O_0,O_1)=\sum_{v\in\mathcal V}q_{\mathrm{tar}}(v)\mathbf 1\{O_0(v)=O_0,\ O_1(v)=O_1\},\qquad L=|\operatorname{supp}(P_T)|.
\]
Then \(L\le\min\{|\mathcal V|,m_0m_1\}\). For a preorder pair \(C=(c_0,c_1)\) with ordered nonempty tie classes \(C_{a,1}\prec_a\cdots\prec_a C_{a,d_a}\), let
\[
P_T^C(j,\ell)=\sum_{\substack{O_0,O_1\\c_0(O_0)=j,\ c_1(O_1)=\ell}}P_T(O_0,O_1),\qquad L_C=|\operatorname{supp}(P_T^C)|.
\]
Then \(L_C\le\min\{L,d_0d_1\}\). One two-dimensional prefix table of the unchanged full joint calibration-orbit law \(R_w\) yields the exact four-cell vector \(\pi^C(j,\ell)=(\pi_{00}^C,\pi_{01}^C,\pi_{10}^C,\pi_{11}^C)\) in constant time for every supported target class pair. Define
\[
\Pi_C=\{\pi^C(j,\ell):(j,\ell)\in\operatorname{supp}(P_T^C)\},\qquad U_C=|\Pi_C|\le L_C,
\]
and retain the local coherent weights
\[
\omega_C(\pi)=\sum_{\substack{(j,\ell)\in\operatorname{supp}(P_T^C)\\\pi^C(j,\ell)=\pi}}P_T^C(j,\ell).
\]
The preorder objective is \(\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_C}\omega_C(\pi)D_{M,k_0,k_1}(\pi)\), where the coupled two-count recurrence value depends only on \(M\), \(k_0\), \(k_1\), and \(\pi\). Let \(\mathfrak C\) be the \(F_{m_0}F_{m_1}\) preorder pairs and define
\[
\Pi_\star=\bigcup_{C\in\mathfrak C}\Pi_C,\qquad G=|\Pi_\star|.
\]
A global cross-preorder memo is keyed exactly by \((M,k_0,k_1,\pi_{00},\pi_{01},\pi_{10})\), with \(\pi_{11}=1-\pi_{00}-\pi_{01}-\pi_{10}\). Differing local weights \(\omega_C(\pi)\) never enter the recurrence and are multiplied only when each preorder objective is formed. Hence the memo preserves the one common target through the unchanged coherent \(P_T^C\) and the full within-replicate cross-arm calibration dependence through the unchanged \(R_w\).

A deterministic local balanced tree uses \(O(L_C\log(1+L_C))\) exact comparisons and \(O(L_C)\) aggregation arithmetic for preorder \(C\). The global balanced-tree memo adds \(O((\sum_CU_C)\log(1+G))\) exact comparisons and runs the recurrence only \(G\) times. After \(R_w\) is tabulated, exact enumeration uses
\[
O\!\left(
|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}(m_0m_1+L)+\sum_{C\in\mathfrak C}\left[L_C\log(1+L_C)+L_C+U_C\right]+\left\{\sum_{C\in\mathfrak C}U_C\right\}\log(1+G)+GMk_0k_1
\right)
\]
unit-cost exact comparisons and arithmetic operations. If \(L_{\max}=\max_C L_C\) and \(U_{\max}=\max_C U_C\), a uniform bound is
\[
O\!\left(|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}\left[m_0m_1+L+L_{\max}\log(1+L_{\max})+L_{\max}+U_{\max}\{1+\log(1+G)\}\right]+GMk_0k_1\right).
\]
The working-memory bound, including the current local exact-key table and the global memo, is \(O(m_0m_1+L+k_0k_1+U_{\max}+G)\).

defs.def:observable-selector-mixture: For every \(i\in\{1,\ldots,M\}\) and \(a\in\{0,1\}\), set \(\kappa_{w,a}=\sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_h\kappa_{h,a}\), draw \(J_{i,a}\sim\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\), set \(U_{i,a}=(J_{i,a},a)\), and set \(q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,a}=\sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_hq^{\mathrm{obs}}_{h,a}\).
defs.def:coverage-frontier:
For prespecified \(\boldsymbol\varepsilon=(\varepsilon_0,\varepsilon_1)\), define
\[
\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)=\max_{\substack{s_a:\mathcal O\to[0,K]\\a\in\{0,1\}}}\left[(1-\alpha)-\Pr\left\{s_1(O_{T,1})\le Q_{k_1}(s_1(O_{1,1}),\ldots,s_1(O_{M,1}))\ \text{and}\ s_0(O_{T,0})\le Q_{k_0}(s_0(O_{1,0}),\ldots,s_0(O_{M,0}))\right\}\right]_+.
\]
Here \(O_{i,a}\sim q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,a}\), the target orbits are the two lifts of the one common \(T\sim q_{\mathrm{tar}}\), and the calibration draws use \(\texttt{ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization}\), including their within-replicate cross-arm coupling. The functional is restricted to bounded orbit-constant score maps. Under \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), set \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)=\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)\), recovering the symmetric frontier.
defs.def:rank-inversion: Prespecify \(\boldsymbol\varepsilon=(\varepsilon_0,\varepsilon_1)\) with \(\varepsilon_a>0\) and \(\varepsilon_0+\varepsilon_1\le\alpha\), and set \(k_a=\lceil(M+1)(1-\varepsilon_a)\rceil\). For each \(a\in\{0,1\}\), compute \(S_{i,a}=|Y^{\mathrm{obs}}_{iJ_{i,a}}-\widehat m_a(X_{iJ_{i,a}})|\), take \(r_{a,(k_a)}\), set \(I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}=[\widehat m_a(X_{0T})-r_{a,(k_a)},\widehat m_a(X_{0T})+r_{a,(k_a)}]\cap[-K,K]\), and return \(\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w=I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{1,w}-I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{0,w}\). If \(k_a=M+1\), set \(r_{a,(k_a)}=+\infty\), so \(I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}=[-K,K]\). For proof ranks only, append independent continuous uniforms and use lexicographic ranks; reported closed intervals use weak inclusion. Under \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), define \(k=k_0=k_1\), \(r_{a,(k)}=r_{a,(k_a)}\), \(I_{a,w}=I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}\), and \(\mathcal C_w=\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w\), recovering the symmetric construction.

assumptions.ass:observable-selector-kernel: \(\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\ge0,\quad\sum_{v\in\mathcal V}\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=1,\quad z_v\ne a\Rightarrow\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=0\)
assumptions.ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization: \(\mathcal L((J_{i,a})_{1\le i\le M,\ a\in\{0,1\}}\mid(L_i)_{0\le i\le M},(Z_i)_{0\le i\le M},T,\mathcal T)=\prod_{i=1}^M\prod_{a=0}^1\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\)

snapshot.stmt:
The functional \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)\) in \(\texttt{def:coverage-frontier}\) is the exact finite worst-coverage deficit over the stated class of bounded orbit-constant score maps; no equality with a supremum over all \(P\in\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\) is asserted. Write \(\mathcal O_a=\{O\in\mathcal O:O\subseteq\mathcal U_a\}\), \(m_a=|\mathcal O_a|\), and let \(F_m\) be the number of total preorders on an \(m\)-element labelled set. Its maximum is attained by enumerating the \(F_{m_0}F_{m_1}\) pairs of total preorders on \(\mathcal O_0\) and \(\mathcal O_1\); every preorder has a representative in \([0,K]\) with at most \(m_a\) levels. For \(v\in\mathcal V\), let \(O_a(v)\) be the orbit containing \((v,a)\), and define
\[
\kappa_{w,a}(O\mid z)=\sum_{v:(v,a)\in O}\kappa_{w,a}(v\mid z),\qquad R_w(O_0,O_1)=\sum_z\nu(z)\kappa_{w,0}(O_0\mid z)\kappa_{w,1}(O_1\mid z),
\]
\[
P_T(O_0,O_1)=\sum_{v\in\mathcal V}q_{\mathrm{tar}}(v)\mathbf 1\{O_0(v)=O_0,\ O_1(v)=O_1\},\qquad L=|\operatorname{supp}(P_T)|.
\]
Thus \(P_T\) preserves the one common target and \(R_w\) preserves the selectors' shared-assignment dependence. For a preorder pair \(C=(c_0,c_1)\), let \(P_T^C\) be the pushforward of \(P_T\) to its ordered class pairs, put \(L_C=|\operatorname{supp}(P_T^C)|\), let \(\Pi_C\) be its set of distinct supported exact four-cell vectors, and put \(U_C=|\Pi_C|\le L_C\le\min\{L,d_0d_1\}\). Retain the local coherent weights \(\omega_C(\pi)\), let \(\mathfrak C\) be the set of all preorder pairs, and define
\[
\Pi_\star=\bigcup_{C\in\mathfrak C}\Pi_C,\qquad G=|\Pi_\star|.
\]
If \(k\le M\), a global memo keyed exactly by \((M,k,k,\pi_{00},\pi_{01},\pi_{10})\), with \(\pi_{11}=1-\pi_{00}-\pi_{01}-\pi_{10}\), computes each coupled recurrence value once. Differing \(\omega_C(\pi)\) are multiplied only when each preorder objective is formed. Deterministic local grouping and global balanced-tree lookup give the instance-adaptive evaluation bound
\[
O\!\left(
|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}(m_0m_1+L)+\sum_{C\in\mathfrak C}\left[L_C\log(1+L_C)+L_C+U_C\right]+\left\{\sum_{C\in\mathfrak C}U_C\right\}\log(1+G)+GMk^2
\right)
\]
unit-cost exact comparisons and arithmetic operations. With \(L_{\max}=\max_C L_C\) and \(U_{\max}=\max_C U_C\), a uniform bound is
\[
O\!\left(|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}\left[m_0m_1+L+L_{\max}\log(1+L_{\max})+L_{\max}+U_{\max}\{1+\log(1+G)\}\right]+GMk^2\right),
\]
and working memory is \(O(m_0m_1+L+k^2+U_{\max}+G)\). Every \(\pi\) is constructed from the unchanged full \(R_w\), and every local weight is derived from the unchanged coherent \(P_T^C\), so the memo changes computation but neither probability law. If \(k=M+1\), evaluation is constant and \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)=0\). Binary attainment is false: in an admissible four-pair instance with \(M=3\) and \(\alpha=1/2\), a four-level ordering has frontier objective \(24957/160000\), whereas every binary score pair has objective at most \(759109/5000000\), a strict gap of \(83189/20000000\). Under disjoint target and calibration orbit support in one arm and \(k\le M\), \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)=1-\alpha\).

defs.def:rank-inversion: Prespecify \(\boldsymbol\varepsilon=(\varepsilon_0,\varepsilon_1)\) with \(\varepsilon_a>0\) and \(\varepsilon_0+\varepsilon_1\le\alpha\), and set \(k_a=\lceil(M+1)(1-\varepsilon_a)\rceil\). For each \(a\in\{0,1\}\), compute \(S_{i,a}=|Y^{\mathrm{obs}}_{iJ_{i,a}}-\widehat m_a(X_{iJ_{i,a}})|\), take \(r_{a,(k_a)}\), set \(I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}=[\widehat m_a(X_{0T})-r_{a,(k_a)},\widehat m_a(X_{0T})+r_{a,(k_a)}]\cap[-K,K]\), and return \(\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w=I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{1,w}-I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{0,w}\). If \(k_a=M+1\), set \(r_{a,(k_a)}=+\infty\), so \(I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}=[-K,K]\). For proof ranks only, append independent continuous uniforms and use lexicographic ranks; reported closed intervals use weak inclusion. Under \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), define \(k=k_0=k_1\), \(r_{a,(k)}=r_{a,(k_a)}\), \(I_{a,w}=I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}\), and \(\mathcal C_w=\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w\), recovering the symmetric construction.

assumptions.ass:observable-selector-kernel: \(\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\ge0,\quad\sum_{v\in\mathcal V}\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=1,\quad z_v\ne a\Rightarrow\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=0\)
assumptions.ass:selector-equivariance: \(\kappa_{h,a}(\pi_gv\mid gz)=\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\quad\forall g\in\mathcal G,\ h\in\mathcal H,\ a\in\{0,1\}\)
assumptions.ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization: \(\mathcal L((J_{i,a})_{1\le i\le M,\ a\in\{0,1\}}\mid(L_i)_{0\le i\le M},(Z_i)_{0\le i\le M},T,\mathcal T)=\prod_{i=1}^M\prod_{a=0}^1\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\)

snapshot.stmt: Conditional on \(\mathcal T\), joint orbit balance is equivalent to equality in law of \(S_{i,a}\) and \(S_{0,a}\), for each \(a\in\{0,1\}\), uniformly over \(P\in\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\) and arbitrary fixed measurable trained predictor maps \(\widehat m_a:\mathsf X\to\mathbb R\). Under balance, conditional on \(\mathcal T\), \(S_{1,a},\ldots,S_{M,a},S_{0,a}\) are iid for each arm. The assertion is armwise and makes no joint-exchangeability claim across arms. If an orbit mass differs, \(\texttt{def:orbit-separating-law}\) gives a bounded invariant law and fixed predictor maps with unequal score laws.

defs.def:observable-replicated-experiment: For each \(i\in\{0,\ldots,M\}\), draw \(L_i\), draw \(Z_i\sim\nu\), set \(Y^{\mathrm{obs}}_{iv}=Y_{iv}(Z_{iv})\), and form \(\mathsf O_i=\{(X_{iv},Z_{iv},Y^{\mathrm{obs}}_{iv}):v\in\mathcal V\}\). Draw the one common \(T\sim q_{\mathrm{tar}}\), observe \(X_{0T}\) after selection, and use neither \(Y_{0T}(0)\) nor \(Y_{0T}(1)\) in fitting or calibration.
defs.def:observable-selector-mixture: For every \(i\in\{1,\ldots,M\}\) and \(a\in\{0,1\}\), set \(\kappa_{w,a}=\sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_h\kappa_{h,a}\), draw \(J_{i,a}\sim\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\), set \(U_{i,a}=(J_{i,a},a)\), and set \(q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,a}=\sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_hq^{\mathrm{obs}}_{h,a}\).
defs.def:invariant-laws: \(\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}=\{P:P\circ(gL_i)^{-1}=P\quad\forall g\in\mathcal G,\quad\max_{v\in\mathcal V,\ a\in\{0,1\}}|Y_v(a)|\le K\}\).
defs.def:orbit-separating-law: For \(a\in\{0,1\}\) and \(O\in\mathcal O\), set \(\widehat m_0=\widehat m_1=0\), set \(Y_v(a)=K\mathbf 1\{\iota_a(v)\in O\}\), set \(Y_v(1-a)=0\), and randomize the orbit-constant prototype by a uniform \(g\in\mathcal G\) before drawing \(Z_i\sim\nu\).

assumptions.ass:observable-selector-kernel: \(\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\ge0,\quad\sum_{v\in\mathcal V}\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=1,\quad z_v\ne a\Rightarrow\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=0\)
assumptions.ass:selector-equivariance: \(\kappa_{h,a}(\pi_gv\mid gz)=\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\quad\forall g\in\mathcal G,\ h\in\mathcal H,\ a\in\{0,1\}\)
assumptions.ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization: \(\mathcal L((J_{i,a})_{1\le i\le M,\ a\in\{0,1\}}\mid(L_i)_{0\le i\le M},(Z_i)_{0\le i\le M},T,\mathcal T)=\prod_{i=1}^M\prod_{a=0}^1\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\)

snapshot.stmt: Adopt \(\Delta(\varnothing)=\varnothing\) and \(\operatorname{conv}(\varnothing)=\varnothing\). There exists \(w\in\Delta(\mathcal H)\) jointly orbit-balancing both arms if and only if \(b_{\mathrm{LP}}\in\operatorname{conv}\{A_{\cdot h}:h\in\mathcal H\}\). Equivalently, the stacked LP in \(\texttt{def:common-selector-feasibility-lp}\) is feasible. If \(\mathcal H=\varnothing\), infeasibility is certified by \(\sum_{h\in\varnothing}w_h=0\ne1\); no selector mixture, implemented kernel, selector-indexed ordinary or weighted procedure, \(D_*\), \(\mathcal W_*\), or \(w_*\) exists. If \(\mathcal H\ne\varnothing\) and the condition fails, a strict Farkas hyperplane separates \(b_{\mathrm{LP}}\) from the nonempty convex hull. Fix a prespecified \(\boldsymbol\varepsilon=(\varepsilon_0,\varepsilon_1)\) with \(\varepsilon_a>0\) and \(\varepsilon_0+\varepsilon_1\le\alpha\). Conditional on \(\mathcal T\), a fixed feasible common \(w\), iid arrays, the independent common target \(T\), and selector auxiliary draws imply, for each arm, \[\Pr\!\left(S_{0,a}>r_{a,(k_a)}\mid\mathcal T\right)\le\frac{M+1-k_a}{M+1}=\rho_{M,\varepsilon_a}\le\varepsilon_a.\] Clipping to \([-K,K]\) and Bonferroni therefore give \[\Pr\!\left(\tau_T\in\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w\mid\mathcal T\right)\ge1-\rho_{M,\varepsilon_0}-\rho_{M,\varepsilon_1}\ge1-\varepsilon_0-\varepsilon_1\ge1-\alpha,\] without arm independence. Under \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), one has \(k_0=k_1=k\), \(I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}=I_{a,w}\), and \(\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w=\mathcal C_w\), so the symmetric rank and coverage formulas follow as a corollary.

defs.def:rank-inversion: Prespecify \(\boldsymbol\varepsilon=(\varepsilon_0,\varepsilon_1)\) with \(\varepsilon_a>0\) and \(\varepsilon_0+\varepsilon_1\le\alpha\), and set \(k_a=\lceil(M+1)(1-\varepsilon_a)\rceil\). For each \(a\in\{0,1\}\), compute \(S_{i,a}=|Y^{\mathrm{obs}}_{iJ_{i,a}}-\widehat m_a(X_{iJ_{i,a}})|\), take \(r_{a,(k_a)}\), set \(I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}=[\widehat m_a(X_{0T})-r_{a,(k_a)},\widehat m_a(X_{0T})+r_{a,(k_a)}]\cap[-K,K]\), and return \(\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w=I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{1,w}-I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{0,w}\). If \(k_a=M+1\), set \(r_{a,(k_a)}=+\infty\), so \(I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}=[-K,K]\). For proof ranks only, append independent continuous uniforms and use lexicographic ranks; reported closed intervals use weak inclusion. Under \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), define \(k=k_0=k_1\), \(r_{a,(k)}=r_{a,(k_a)}\), \(I_{a,w}=I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}\), and \(\mathcal C_w=\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w\), recovering the symmetric construction.
defs.def:finite-rank-error:
For \(N=M+1\), prespecified \(\boldsymbol\varepsilon=(\varepsilon_0,\varepsilon_1)\), and \(k_a=\lceil N(1-\varepsilon_a)\rceil\), define
\[
\rho_{M,\varepsilon_a}=\frac{N-k_a}{N}=\frac{\lfloor N\varepsilon_a\rfloor}{N},\qquad a\in\{0,1\}.
\]
Under \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), write their common value as
\[
\rho_{M,\alpha}=\frac{M+1-k}{M+1}=\frac{\lfloor(M+1)\alpha/2\rfloor}{M+1}.
\]

assumptions.ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization: \(\mathcal L((J_{i,a})_{1\le i\le M,\ a\in\{0,1\}}\mid(L_i)_{0\le i\le M},(Z_i)_{0\le i\le M},T,\mathcal T)=\prod_{i=1}^M\prod_{a=0}^1\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\)

snapshot.stmt: Suppose \(k\le M\), equivalently \(\alpha\ge2/(M+1)\), and, for one arm \(a\), every target-supported orbit has zero \(q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,a}\) mass. Then \(\texttt{def:disjoint-orbit-law}\) belongs to \(\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\), all adverse-arm calibration scores equal \(0\), and \(r_{a,(k)}=0\). The other arm's scores and threshold equal \(0\), so \(\mathcal C_w=\{0\}\). The common target effect equals \(K\) for \(a=1\) and \(-K\) for \(a=0\), hence \(\Pr_P(\tau_T\in\mathcal C_w\mid\mathcal T)=0\).

defs.def:disjoint-orbit-law: Fix \(a\in\{0,1\}\). Set \(\widehat m_0=\widehat m_1=0\), set \(Y_v(a)=K\) on target-supported arm-\(a\) orbits, set \(Y_v(a)=0\) on every state with positive calibration mass and on remaining states, set \(Y_v(1-a)=0\), and randomize the orbit-constant prototype by a uniform \(g\in\mathcal G\) before drawing \(Z_i\sim\nu\).
defs.def:invariant-laws: \(\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}=\{P:P\circ(gL_i)^{-1}=P\quad\forall g\in\mathcal G,\quad\max_{v\in\mathcal V,\ a\in\{0,1\}}|Y_v(a)|\le K\}\).
defs.def:observable-selector-mixture: For every \(i\in\{1,\ldots,M\}\) and \(a\in\{0,1\}\), set \(\kappa_{w,a}=\sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_h\kappa_{h,a}\), draw \(J_{i,a}\sim\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\), set \(U_{i,a}=(J_{i,a},a)\), and set \(q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,a}=\sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_hq^{\mathrm{obs}}_{h,a}\).
defs.def:rank-inversion: Prespecify \(\boldsymbol\varepsilon=(\varepsilon_0,\varepsilon_1)\) with \(\varepsilon_a>0\) and \(\varepsilon_0+\varepsilon_1\le\alpha\), and set \(k_a=\lceil(M+1)(1-\varepsilon_a)\rceil\). For each \(a\in\{0,1\}\), compute \(S_{i,a}=|Y^{\mathrm{obs}}_{iJ_{i,a}}-\widehat m_a(X_{iJ_{i,a}})|\), take \(r_{a,(k_a)}\), set \(I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}=[\widehat m_a(X_{0T})-r_{a,(k_a)},\widehat m_a(X_{0T})+r_{a,(k_a)}]\cap[-K,K]\), and return \(\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w=I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{1,w}-I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{0,w}\). If \(k_a=M+1\), set \(r_{a,(k_a)}=+\infty\), so \(I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}=[-K,K]\). For proof ranks only, append independent continuous uniforms and use lexicographic ranks; reported closed intervals use weak inclusion. Under \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), define \(k=k_0=k_1\), \(r_{a,(k)}=r_{a,(k_a)}\), \(I_{a,w}=I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}\), and \(\mathcal C_w=\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w\), recovering the symmetric construction.

assumptions.ass:observable-selector-kernel: \(\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\ge0,\quad\sum_{v\in\mathcal V}\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=1,\quad z_v\ne a\Rightarrow\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=0\)
assumptions.ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization: \(\mathcal L((J_{i,a})_{1\le i\le M,\ a\in\{0,1\}}\mid(L_i)_{0\le i\le M},(Z_i)_{0\le i\le M},T,\mathcal T)=\prod_{i=1}^M\prod_{a=0}^1\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\)

snapshot.stmt: Fix a pair block \(b\) with \(\mathcal V_b=\{v_{b,0},v_{b,1}\}\) and suppose the local swap subgroup satisfies \(\langle s_b\rangle\le\mathcal G\). Then \(s_b(v_{b,r},a)=(v_{b,1-r},a)\) for \(r,a\in\{0,1\}\), with \(s_b\) fixing all outside members and relabelling complete records as in \(\texttt{def:group-action}\), and the global \(\mathcal G\)-orbits in block \(b\) are \(O_{b,0}=\{(v_{b,0},0),(v_{b,1},0)\}\) and \(O_{b,1}=\{(v_{b,0},1),(v_{b,1},1)\}\). If the target arm-\(1\) lifted law is supported on \(O_{b,1}\), an exclusive mate-control analogue supported on the realized mate's control state in \(O_{b,0}\) has a different orbit-mass vector, cannot satisfy exact joint balance, and is not a legal arm-\(1\) selector. Separately, if a legal implemented arm-\(1\) selector satisfies \(q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,1}(O_{b,1})=0\), then arm \(1\) satisfies the disjoint-orbit premise. Its zero-coverage conclusion applies when \(k\le M\); for \(k=M+1\), coverage is one by \(\texttt{prop:rank-boundary}\).

defs.def:pair-action: Fix a pair block \(b\) with \(\mathcal V_b=\{v_{b,0},v_{b,1}\}\). The pair notation and its global-orbit consequences are invoked only under the local condition \(\langle s_b\rangle=\{e,s_b\}\le\mathcal G\). Under that condition, \(s_b(v_{b,r},a)=(v_{b,1-r},a)\) for \(r,a\in\{0,1\}\), \(s_b\) fixes members outside block \(b\), and it relabels complete records by \(\texttt{def:group-action}\). Because every group element preserves blocks and block \(b\) has exactly two members, the global \(\mathcal G\)-orbits in that block are \(O_{b,0}=\{(v_{b,0},0),(v_{b,1},0)\}\) and \(O_{b,1}=\{(v_{b,0},1),(v_{b,1},1)\}\). For a collection of pair blocks, every invoked local subgroup must be contained in \(\mathcal G\); the standard \(S_2^B\) specialization contains the product of all relevant swaps.

assumptions.ass:observable-selector-kernel: \(\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\ge0,\quad\sum_{v\in\mathcal V}\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=1,\quad z_v\ne a\Rightarrow\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=0\)

snapshot.stmt: Let \(\mathcal V=\{v_{1,0},v_{1,1},v_{2,0},v_{2,1}\}\) and \(\mathcal G=S_2\times S_2\), with the generators in \(\texttt{def:pair-action}\). The four arm-specific orbits are \(O_{1,0},O_{1,1},O_{2,0},O_{2,1}\). If \(q_{\mathrm{tar}}\) concentrates on \(v_{1,0}\), its arm \(1\) orbit vector has mass \(1\) on \(O_{1,1}\). An exclusive mate-control analogue concentrated on \((v_{1,1},0)\) has mass \(1\) on \(O_{1,0}\), so the relevant stacked LP equations cannot both hold and the analogue is not a legal arm \(1\) selector. If a legal implemented selector has \(q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,1}(O_{1,1})=0\), then for \(k\le M\) the bounded witness has \(\mathcal C_w=\{0\}\) and coverage \(0\); for \(k=M+1\), it has \(I_{0,w}=I_{1,w}=[-K,K]\), \(\mathcal C_w=[-2K,2K]\), and coverage \(1\).

defs.def:pair-action: Fix a pair block \(b\) with \(\mathcal V_b=\{v_{b,0},v_{b,1}\}\). The pair notation and its global-orbit consequences are invoked only under the local condition \(\langle s_b\rangle=\{e,s_b\}\le\mathcal G\). Under that condition, \(s_b(v_{b,r},a)=(v_{b,1-r},a)\) for \(r,a\in\{0,1\}\), \(s_b\) fixes members outside block \(b\), and it relabels complete records by \(\texttt{def:group-action}\). Because every group element preserves blocks and block \(b\) has exactly two members, the global \(\mathcal G\)-orbits in that block are \(O_{b,0}=\{(v_{b,0},0),(v_{b,1},0)\}\) and \(O_{b,1}=\{(v_{b,0},1),(v_{b,1},1)\}\). For a collection of pair blocks, every invoked local subgroup must be contained in \(\mathcal G\); the standard \(S_2^B\) specialization contains the product of all relevant swaps.

assumptions.ass:observable-selector-kernel: \(\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\ge0,\quad\sum_{v\in\mathcal V}\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=1,\quad z_v\ne a\Rightarrow\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=0\)

snapshot.stmt: Let \(\mathsf E\) be finite, let \(Q\) and \(P\) be probability laws on \(\mathsf E\times[0,\infty)\) with \(P\ll Q\), and suppose their likelihood ratio has the form \(dP/dQ(e,r)=\lambda(e)\). Let \(X_i=(E_i,R_i)\), \(1\le i\le M\), be independent and identically distributed from \(Q\), and let the independent target \(X_0=(E_0,R_0)\) have law \(P\). Put \(W_i=\lambda(E_i)\). For \(\epsilon\in(0,1)\), form the normalized measure placing mass \(W_i\) at \(R_i\), \(1\le i\le M\), and mass \(W_0\) at \(+\infty\), and let \(q_{1-\epsilon}\) be its left-continuous \((1-\epsilon)\)-quantile. Then \(\Pr_P\{R_0>q_{1-\epsilon}\}\le\epsilon\).

Fix an arm \(a\) and suppose every target-supported arm-\(a\) orbit has zero \(q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,a}\) mass. Under \(\texttt{def:disjoint-orbit-law}\), the latent law belongs to \(\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\), every selected calibration score in both arms is zero, and the common target effect is \(K\) if \(a=1\) and \(-K\) if \(a=0\). If \(k_0\le M\) and \(k_1\le M\), then \(I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{0,w}=I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{1,w}=\{0\}\), \(\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w=\{0\}\), and coverage is zero. If exactly one \(k_c=M+1\), then the boundary arm interval is \([-K,K]\), the other is \(\{0\}\), \(\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w=[-K,K]\), and this witness has coverage one. If both \(k_0=k_1=M+1\), then \(\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w=[-2K,2K]\) and this witness again has coverage one. Moreover \(k_c\le M\) is equivalent to \(\varepsilon_c\ge1/(M+1)\). The symmetric choice recovers \(\texttt{prop:disjoint-orbit-witness}\).

defs.def:disjoint-orbit-law: Fix \(a\in\{0,1\}\). Set \(\widehat m_0=\widehat m_1=0\), set \(Y_v(a)=K\) on target-supported arm-\(a\) orbits, set \(Y_v(a)=0\) on every state with positive calibration mass and on remaining states, set \(Y_v(1-a)=0\), and randomize the orbit-constant prototype by a uniform \(g\in\mathcal G\) before drawing \(Z_i\sim\nu\).
