For each \(a\in\{0,1\}\), compute \(S_{i,a}=|Y^{\mathrm{obs}}_{iJ_{i,a}}-\widehat m_a(X_{iJ_{i,a}})|\), take \(r_{a,(k_a)}\), set \(I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}=[\widehat m_a(X_{0T})-r_{a,(k_a)},\widehat m_a(X_{0T})+r_{a,(k_a)}]\cap[-K,K]\), and return \(\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w=I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{1,w}-I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{0,w}\). If \(k_a=M+1\), set \(r_{a,(k_a)}=+\infty\), so \(I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}=[-K,K]\). For proof ranks only, append independent continuous uniforms and use lexicographic ranks; reported closed intervals use weak inclusion. Under \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), define \(k=k_0=k_1\), \(r_{a,(k)}=r_{a,(k_a)}\), \(I_{a,w}=I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}\), and \(\mathcal C_w=\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w\), recovering the symmetric construction.

assumptions.ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization: \(\mathcal L((J_{i,a})_{1\le i\le M,\ a\in\{0,1\}}\mid(L_i)_{0\le i\le M},(Z_i)_{0\le i\le M},T,\mathcal T)=\prod_{i=1}^M\prod_{a=0}^1\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\)

snapshot.stmt:
Assume that \(\mathcal H\) is finite and nonempty. The unchanged joint two-arm objects \(D_*\) and \(\mathcal W_*\) are attained, the full absolute-value linear program has value \(D_*\), and
\[
D_*=\max_{\lVert y\rVert_\infty\le1/2}
\left\{\min_{h\in\mathcal H}y^\top A_{\cdot h}-y^\top b_{\mathrm{LP}}\right\}.
\]
If \(D_*>0\), the negative of every maximizing vector is a Farkas separator with margin \(D_*\), and \(D_*=0\) if and only if the exact joint-balance program is feasible. For a prespecified \(\boldsymbol\varepsilon\), let \(\mathcal A_{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}=\{a:k_a\le M\}\). The active-row minimum \(D_{*,\mathcal A}\) and optimizer set \(\mathcal W_{*,\mathcal A}\) in \(\texttt{def:minimum-imbalance-common-selector}\) are attained, and the active-row absolute-value program has value \(D_{*,\mathcal A}\). Its attained dual is
\[
D_{*,\mathcal A}=\max_{\substack{\lVert y\rVert_\infty\le1/2\\y_{a,O}=0\ \mathrm{for}\ a\notin\mathcal A_{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}}}
\left\{\min_{h\in\mathcal H}y^\top A_{\cdot h}-y^\top b_{\mathrm{LP}}\right\}.
\]
Moreover, \(D_{*,\mathcal A}=0\) if and only if one selector balances every active-arm row; this characterizes joint two-arm balance only when \(\mathcal A_{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}=\{0,1\}\). Every prespecified \(w_{*,\mathcal A}\in\mathcal W_{*,\mathcal A}\) is computable before scores are observed and satisfies
\[
\Pr_P\!\left\{\tau_T\in\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{w_{*,\mathcal A}}\mid\mathcal T\right\}
\ge\max\!\left\{0,1-\sum_{a\in\mathcal A_{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}}\rho_{M,\varepsilon_a}-D_{*,\mathcal A}\right\}.
\]
This active-row certificate weakly dominates the corresponding certificate for every fixed common \(w\), with strict dominance whenever its active imbalance is strictly smaller and the optimized untruncated lower bound is positive. If exactly one arm is at the boundary, the optimization contains only the other arm's rows. If \(\mathcal A_{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}=\varnothing\), then \(D_{*,\mathcal A}=0\), \(\mathcal W_{*,\mathcal A}=\Delta(\mathcal H)\), no optimization is needed, and coverage is one. On the equal-split finite-rank diagonal, \(D_{*,\mathcal A}=D_*\) and \(\mathcal W_{*,\mathcal A}=\mathcal W_*\); at the two-boundary elbow, coverage is one. The original \(w_*\)-certificate and its strict-dominance claim remain valid as consequences of the full two-arm optimum. No selector-indexed object exists when \(\mathcal H=\varnothing\).

defs.def:observable-selector-mixture: For every \(i\in\{1,\ldots,M\}\) and \(a\in\{0,1\}\), set \(\kappa_{w,a}=\sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_h\kappa_{h,a}\), draw \(J_{i,a}\sim\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\), set \(U_{i,a}=(J_{i,a},a)\), and set \(q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,a}=\sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_hq^{\mathrm{obs}}_{h,a}\).
defs.def:minimum-imbalance-common-selector: For every finite selector library define \(\Delta(\mathcal H)=\{w\in\mathbb R^{|\mathcal H|}:w_h\ge0\ \forall h,\ \sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_h=1\}\); in particular \(\Delta(\varnothing)=\varnothing\), because the empty sum is zero. If \(\mathcal H=\varnothing\), no selector mixture, implemented kernel, weighted procedure, minimum \(D_*\), optimizer set \(\mathcal W_*\), or choice \(w_*\) exists. If \(\mathcal H\) is finite and nonempty, define \[D_*=\min_{w\in\Delta(\mathcal H)}\{\Delta_0(w)+\Delta_1(w)\}=\min_{w\in\Delta(\mathcal H)}\frac12\sum_{a=0}^1\sum_{O\in\mathcal O}|(Aw-b_{\mathrm{LP}})_{a,O}|,\qquad \mathcal W_*=\operatorname*{arg\,min}_{w\in\Delta(\mathcal H)}\{\Delta_0(w)+\Delta_1(w)\}.\] The finite absolute-value linear program is \[\begin{aligned}\operatorname*{minimize}_{w,(t_{a,O})}\quad&\frac12\sum_{a=0}^1\sum_{O\in\mathcal O}t_{a,O}\\\text{subject to}\quad&-t_{a,O}\le(Aw-b_{\mathrm{LP}})_{a,O}\le t_{a,O},\quad t_{a,O}\ge0,\\&w_h\ge0,\quad\sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_h=1.\end{aligned}\] Its quantitative dual is \[\operatorname*{maximize}_{\lVert y\rVert_\infty\le1/2}\left\{\min_{h\in\mathcal H}y^\top A_{\cdot h}-y^\top b_{\mathrm{LP}}\right\}.\] These primal and dual objects are defined only in the finite nonempty case; their equality and attainment are certified by \(\texttt{thm:minimum-imbalance-selector-certificate}\).
defs.def:invariant-laws: \(\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}=\{P:P\circ(gL_i)^{-1}=P\quad\forall g\in\mathcal G,\quad\max_{v\in\mathcal V,\ a\in\{0,1\}}|Y_v(a)|\le K\}\).
defs.def:rank-inversion: Prespecify \(\boldsymbol\varepsilon=(\varepsilon_0,\varepsilon_1)\) with \(\varepsilon_a>0\) and \(\varepsilon_0+\varepsilon_1\le\alpha\), and set \(k_a=\lceil(M+1)(1-\varepsilon_a)\rceil\). For each \(a\in\{0,1\}\), compute \(S_{i,a}=|Y^{\mathrm{obs}}_{iJ_{i,a}}-\widehat m_a(X_{iJ_{i,a}})|\), take \(r_{a,(k_a)}\), set \(I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}=[\widehat m_a(X_{0T})-r_{a,(k_a)},\widehat m_a(X_{0T})+r_{a,(k_a)}]\cap[-K,K]\), and return \(\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w=I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{1,w}-I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{0,w}\). If \(k_a=M+1\), set \(r_{a,(k_a)}=+\infty\), so \(I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}=[-K,K]\). For proof ranks only, append independent continuous uniforms and use lexicographic ranks; reported closed intervals use weak inclusion. Under \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), define \(k=k_0=k_1\), \(r_{a,(k)}=r_{a,(k_a)}\), \(I_{a,w}=I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}\), and \(\mathcal C_w=\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w\), recovering the symmetric construction.
defs.def:finite-rank-error:
For \(N=M+1\), prespecified \(\boldsymbol\varepsilon=(\varepsilon_0,\varepsilon_1)\), and \(k_a=\lceil N(1-\varepsilon_a)\rceil\), define
\[
\rho_{M,\varepsilon_a}=\frac{N-k_a}{N}=\frac{\lfloor N\varepsilon_a\rfloor}{N},\qquad a\in\{0,1\}.
\]
Under \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), write their common value as
\[
\rho_{M,\alpha}=\frac{M+1-k}{M+1}=\frac{\lfloor(M+1)\alpha/2\rfloor}{M+1}.
\]

assumptions.ass:observable-selector-kernel: \(\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\ge0,\quad\sum_{v\in\mathcal V}\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=1,\quad z_v\ne a\Rightarrow\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=0\)

snapshot.stmt:
For every fixed or prespecified \(w\), conditional on \(\mathcal T\), the asymmetric ordinary rank construction satisfies
\[
\Pr_P\!\left\{\tau_T\in\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w\mid\mathcal T\right\}
\ge
\max\!\left\{0,1-\rho_{M,\varepsilon_0}-\rho_{M,\varepsilon_1}-\Delta_0(w)-\Delta_1(w)\right\}
\]
for every \(P\in\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\) and arbitrary fixed measurable trained predictor maps. The coordinate elbows are \(\varepsilon_a=j/(M+1)\), and \(|\delta_{a,O}(w)|\le\Delta_a(w)\) for every arm and orbit. On the diagonal \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), this becomes the symmetric bound \(\max\{0,1-2\rho_{M,\alpha}-\Delta_0(w)-\Delta_1(w)\}\).

defs.def:finite-rank-error:
For \(N=M+1\), prespecified \(\boldsymbol\varepsilon=(\varepsilon_0,\varepsilon_1)\), and \(k_a=\lceil N(1-\varepsilon_a)\rceil\), define
\[
\rho_{M,\varepsilon_a}=\frac{N-k_a}{N}=\frac{\lfloor N\varepsilon_a\rfloor}{N},\qquad a\in\{0,1\}.
\]
Under \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), write their common value as
\[
\rho_{M,\alpha}=\frac{M+1-k}{M+1}=\frac{\lfloor(M+1)\alpha/2\rfloor}{M+1}.
\]
defs.def:rank-inversion: Prespecify \(\boldsymbol\varepsilon=(\varepsilon_0,\varepsilon_1)\) with \(\varepsilon_a>0\) and \(\varepsilon_0+\varepsilon_1\le\alpha\), and set \(k_a=\lceil(M+1)(1-\varepsilon_a)\rceil\). For each \(a\in\{0,1\}\), compute \(S_{i,a}=|Y^{\mathrm{obs}}_{iJ_{i,a}}-\widehat m_a(X_{iJ_{i,a}})|\), take \(r_{a,(k_a)}\), set \(I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}=[\widehat m_a(X_{0T})-r_{a,(k_a)},\widehat m_a(X_{0T})+r_{a,(k_a)}]\cap[-K,K]\), and return \(\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w=I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{1,w}-I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{0,w}\). If \(k_a=M+1\), set \(r_{a,(k_a)}=+\infty\), so \(I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}=[-K,K]\). For proof ranks only, append independent continuous uniforms and use lexicographic ranks; reported closed intervals use weak inclusion. Under \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), define \(k=k_0=k_1\), \(r_{a,(k)}=r_{a,(k_a)}\), \(I_{a,w}=I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}\), and \(\mathcal C_w=\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w\), recovering the symmetric construction.

assumptions.ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization: \(\mathcal L((J_{i,a})_{1\le i\le M,\ a\in\{0,1\}}\mid(L_i)_{0\le i\le M},(Z_i)_{0\le i\le M},T,\mathcal T)=\prod_{i=1}^M\prod_{a=0}^1\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\)

snapshot.stmt:
Assume that \(\mathcal H\) is finite and nonempty. The minimum \(D_*\) in \(\texttt{def:minimum-imbalance-common-selector}\) is attained, so \(\mathcal W_*\) is nonempty, and the displayed absolute-value linear program has value \(D_*\). Its quantitative dual is attained and satisfies the strong-duality identity
\[
D_*=\max_{\lVert y\rVert_\infty\le1/2}\left\{\min_{h\in\mathcal H}y^\top A_{\cdot h}-y^\top b_{\mathrm{LP}}\right\}.
\]
If \(D_*>0\), the negative of any maximizing dual vector is a Farkas separating direction with margin \(D_*\). Every \(w_*\in\mathcal W_*\) is computable solely from the known randomization law, target rule, and selector library before scores are observed, and hence is prespecified. Moreover, \(D_*=0\) if and only if the exact joint-balance linear program in \(\texttt{def:common-selector-feasibility-lp}\) is feasible. For every prespecified \(\boldsymbol\varepsilon=(\varepsilon_0,\varepsilon_1)\) with \(\varepsilon_a>0\) and \(\varepsilon_0+\varepsilon_1\le\alpha\), every \(P\in\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\), and arbitrary fixed measurable trained predictor maps, conditional on \(\mathcal T\),
\[
\Pr_P\!\left(\tau_T\in\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{w_*}\mid\mathcal T\right)\ge\max\!\left\{0,1-\rho_{M,\varepsilon_0}-\rho_{M,\varepsilon_1}-D_*\right\}.
\]
This numerical certificate weakly dominates the corresponding certificate for every fixed common \(w\in\Delta(\mathcal H)\), and the dominance is strict whenever \(D_*<\Delta_0(w)+\Delta_1(w)\) and \(1-\rho_{M,\varepsilon_0}-\rho_{M,\varepsilon_1}-D_*>0\). On the equal-split diagonal \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\),
\[
\Pr_P(\tau_T\in\mathcal C_{w_*}\mid\mathcal T)\ge\max\{0,1-2\rho_{M,\alpha}-D_*\}.
\]

defs.def:observable-selector-mixture: For every \(i\in\{1,\ldots,M\}\) and \(a\in\{0,1\}\), set \(\kappa_{w,a}=\sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_h\kappa_{h,a}\), draw \(J_{i,a}\sim\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\), set \(U_{i,a}=(J_{i,a},a)\), and set \(q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,a}=\sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_hq^{\mathrm{obs}}_{h,a}\).
defs.def:minimum-imbalance-common-selector:
For every finite selector library define \(\Delta(\mathcal H)=\{w\in\mathbb R^{|\mathcal H|}:w_h\ge0\ \forall h,\ \sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_h=1\}\); in particular \(\Delta(\varnothing)=\varnothing\). If \(\mathcal H=\varnothing\), no selector mixture, implemented kernel, selector-indexed ordinary or weighted procedure, \(D_*\), \(D_{*,\mathcal A}\), \(\mathcal W_*\), \(\mathcal W_{*,\mathcal A}\), \(w_*\), or \(w_{*,\mathcal A}\) exists. If \(\mathcal H\) is finite and nonempty, retain the joint two-arm diagnostics
\[
D_*=\min_{w\in\Delta(\mathcal H)}\frac12\sum_{a=0}^1\sum_{O\in\mathcal O}|(Aw-b_{\mathrm{LP}})_{a,O}|,\qquad
\mathcal W_*=\operatorname*{arg\,min}_{w\in\Delta(\mathcal H)}\frac12\sum_{a=0}^1\sum_{O\in\mathcal O}|(Aw-b_{\mathrm{LP}})_{a,O}|.
\]
Their absolute-value linear program minimizes \(\frac12\sum_{a=0}^1\sum_Ot_{a,O}\) subject to \(-t_{a,O}\le(Aw-b_{\mathrm{LP}})_{a,O}\le t_{a,O}\), \(t_{a,O}\ge0\), \(w\in\Delta(\mathcal H)\), and their dual is
\[
\max_{\lVert y\rVert_\infty\le1/2}\left\{\min_{h\in\mathcal H}y^\top A_{\cdot h}-y^\top b_{\mathrm{LP}}\right\}.
\]
For the prespecified armwise ranks define \(\mathcal A_{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}=\{a\in\{0,1\}:k_a\le M\}\) and
\[
D_{*,\mathcal A}=\min_{w\in\Delta(\mathcal H)}\frac12\sum_{a\in\mathcal A_{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}}\sum_{O\in\mathcal O}|(Aw-b_{\mathrm{LP}})_{a,O}|,\qquad
\mathcal W_{*,\mathcal A}=\operatorname*{arg\,min}_{w\in\Delta(\mathcal H)}\frac12\sum_{a\in\mathcal A_{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}}\sum_{O\in\mathcal O}|(Aw-b_{\mathrm{LP}})_{a,O}|.
\]
The active-row linear program minimizes \(\frac12\sum_{a\in\mathcal A_{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}}\sum_Ot_{a,O}\) subject to the same two-sided constraints only for \(a\in\mathcal A_{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}\), together with \(t_{a,O}\ge0\) and \(w\in\Delta(\mathcal H)\). Its dual is
\[
\max_{\substack{\lVert y\rVert_\infty\le1/2\\y_{a,O}=0\ \mathrm{for}\ a\notin\mathcal A_{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}}}\left\{\min_{h\in\mathcal H}y^\top A_{\cdot h}-y^\top b_{\mathrm{LP}}\right\}.
\]
If \(\mathcal A_{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}=\varnothing\), define \(D_{*,\mathcal A}=0\) and \(\mathcal W_{*,\mathcal A}=\Delta(\mathcal H)\), and choose \(w_{*,\mathcal A}\) without solving an LP. The full objects \(D_*\) and \(\mathcal W_*\) remain the joint two-arm balance diagnostics; \(D_{*,\mathcal A}=0\) characterizes only active-row balance unless both arms are active. Primal and dual equality, attainment, and the coverage certificates are proved in \(\texttt{thm:active-arm-minimum-imbalance-selector-certificate}\).
defs.def:invariant-laws: \(\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}=\{P:P\circ(gL_i)^{-1}=P\quad\forall g\in\mathcal G,\quad\max_{v\in\mathcal V,\ a\in\{0,1\}}|Y_v(a)|\le K\}\).
defs.def:rank-inversion: Prespecify \(\boldsymbol\varepsilon=(\varepsilon_0,\varepsilon_1)\) with \(\varepsilon_a>0\) and \(\varepsilon_0+\varepsilon_1\le\alpha\), and set \(k_a=\lceil(M+1)(1-\varepsilon_a)\rceil\). For each \(a\in\{0,1\}\), compute \(S_{i,a}=|Y^{\mathrm{obs}}_{iJ_{i,a}}-\widehat m_a(X_{iJ_{i,a}})|\), take \(r_{a,(k_a)}\), set \(I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}=[\widehat m_a(X_{0T})-r_{a,(k_a)},\widehat m_a(X_{0T})+r_{a,(k_a)}]\cap[-K,K]\), and return \(\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w=I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{1,w}-I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{0,w}\). If \(k_a=M+1\), set \(r_{a,(k_a)}=+\infty\), so \(I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}=[-K,K]\). For proof ranks only, append independent continuous uniforms and use lexicographic ranks; reported closed intervals use weak inclusion. Under \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), define \(k=k_0=k_1\), \(r_{a,(k)}=r_{a,(k_a)}\), \(I_{a,w}=I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}\), and \(\mathcal C_w=\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w\), recovering the symmetric construction.
defs.def:finite-rank-error:
For \(N=M+1\), prespecified \(\boldsymbol\varepsilon=(\varepsilon_0,\varepsilon_1)\), and \(k_a=\lceil N(1-\varepsilon_a)\rceil\), define
\[
\rho_{M,\varepsilon_a}=\frac{N-k_a}{N}=\frac{\lfloor N\varepsilon_a\rfloor}{N},\qquad a\in\{0,1\}.
\]
Under \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), write their common value as
\[
\rho_{M,\alpha}=\frac{M+1-k}{M+1}=\frac{\lfloor(M+1)\alpha/2\rfloor}{M+1}.
\]

assumptions.ass:observable-selector-kernel: \(\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\ge0,\quad\sum_{v\in\mathcal V}\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=1,\quad z_v\ne a\Rightarrow\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=0\)

snapshot.stmt:
Suppose \(k_0,k_1\le M\). Write \(\mathcal O_a=\{O\in\mathcal O:O\subseteq\mathcal U_a\}\), \(m_a=|\mathcal O_a|\), and let \(F_m\) be the number of total preorders on an \(m\)-element labelled set. Define
\[
\kappa_{w,a}(O\mid z)=\sum_{v:(v,a)\in O}\kappa_{w,a}(v\mid z),\qquad R_w(O_0,O_1)=\sum_z\nu(z)\kappa_{w,0}(O_0\mid z)\kappa_{w,1}(O_1\mid z).
\]
For \(v\in\mathcal V\) and \(a\in\{0,1\}\), let \(O_a(v)\) be the orbit containing \((v,a)\), and define
\[
P_T(O_0,O_1)=\sum_{v\in\mathcal V}q_{\mathrm{tar}}(v)\mathbf 1\{O_0(v)=O_0,\ O_1(v)=O_1\},\qquad L=|\operatorname{supp}(P_T)|.
\]
Then \(L\le\min\{|\mathcal V|,m_0m_1\}\). For a preorder pair \(C=(c_0,c_1)\) with ordered nonempty tie classes \(C_{a,1}\prec_a\cdots\prec_a C_{a,d_a}\), let
\[
P_T^C(j,\ell)=\sum_{\substack{O_0,O_1\\c_0(O_0)=j,\ c_1(O_1)=\ell}}P_T(O_0,O_1),\qquad L_C=|\operatorname{supp}(P_T^C)|.
\]
Then \(L_C\le\min\{L,d_0d_1\}\). One two-dimensional prefix table of the unchanged full joint calibration-orbit law \(R_w\) yields the exact four-cell vector \(\pi^C(j,\ell)=(\pi_{00}^C,\pi_{01}^C,\pi_{10}^C,\pi_{11}^C)\) in constant time for every supported target class pair. Define
\[
\Pi_C=\{\pi^C(j,\ell):(j,\ell)\in\operatorname{supp}(P_T^C)\},\qquad U_C=|\Pi_C|\le L_C,
\]
and retain the local coherent weights
\[
\omega_C(\pi)=\sum_{\substack{(j,\ell)\in\operatorname{supp}(P_T^C)\\\pi^C(j,\ell)=\pi}}P_T^C(j,\ell).
\]
The preorder objective is \(\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_C}\omega_C(\pi)D_{M,k_0,k_1}(\pi)\), where the coupled two-count recurrence value depends only on \(M\), \(k_0\), \(k_1\), and \(\pi\). Let \(\mathfrak C\) be the \(F_{m_0}F_{m_1}\) preorder pairs and define
\[
\Pi_\star=\bigcup_{C\in\mathfrak C}\Pi_C,\qquad G=|\Pi_\star|.
\]
A global cross-preorder memo is keyed exactly by \((M,k_0,k_1,\pi_{00},\pi_{01},\pi_{10})\), with \(\pi_{11}=1-\pi_{00}-\pi_{01}-\pi_{10}\). Differing local weights \(\omega_C(\pi)\) never enter the recurrence and are multiplied only when each preorder objective is formed. Hence the memo preserves the one common target through the unchanged coherent \(P_T^C\) and the full within-replicate cross-arm calibration dependence through the unchanged \(R_w\).

A deterministic local balanced tree uses \(O(L_C\log(1+L_C))\) exact comparisons and \(O(L_C)\) aggregation arithmetic for preorder \(C\). The global balanced-tree memo adds \(O((\sum_CU_C)\log(1+G))\) exact comparisons and runs the recurrence only \(G\) times. After \(R_w\) is tabulated, exact enumeration uses
\[
O\!\left(
|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}(m_0m_1+L)+\sum_{C\in\mathfrak C}\left[L_C\log(1+L_C)+L_C+U_C\right]+\left\{\sum_{C\in\mathfrak C}U_C\right\}\log(1+G)+GMk_0k_1
\right)
\]
unit-cost exact comparisons and arithmetic operations. If \(L_{\max}=\max_C L_C\) and \(U_{\max}=\max_C U_C\), a uniform bound is
\[
O\!\left(|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}\left[m_0m_1+L+L_{\max}\log(1+L_{\max})+L_{\max}+U_{\max}\{1+\log(1+G)\}\right]+GMk_0k_1\right).
\]
The working-memory bound, including the current local exact-key table and the global memo, is \(O(m_0m_1+L+k_0k_1+U_{\max}+G)\).

defs.def:observable-selector-mixture: For every \(i\in\{1,\ldots,M\}\) and \(a\in\{0,1\}\), set \(\kappa_{w,a}=\sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_h\kappa_{h,a}\), draw \(J_{i,a}\sim\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\), set \(U_{i,a}=(J_{i,a},a)\), and set \(q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,a}=\sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_hq^{\mathrm{obs}}_{h,a}\).
defs.def:coverage-frontier:
For prespecified \(\boldsymbol\varepsilon=(\varepsilon_0,\varepsilon_1)\), define
\[
\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)=\max_{\substack{s_a:\mathcal O\to[0,K]\\a\in\{0,1\}}}\left[(1-\alpha)-\Pr\left\{s_1(O_{T,1})\le Q_{k_1}(s_1(O_{1,1}),\ldots,s_1(O_{M,1}))\ \text{and}\ s_0(O_{T,0})\le Q_{k_0}(s_0(O_{1,0}),\ldots,s_0(O_{M,0}))\right\}\right]_+.
\]
Here \(O_{i,a}\sim q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,a}\), the target orbits are the two lifts of the one common \(T\sim q_{\mathrm{tar}}\), and the calibration draws use \(\texttt{ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization}\), including their within-replicate cross-arm coupling. The functional is restricted to bounded orbit-constant score maps. Under \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), set \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)=\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)\), recovering the symmetric frontier.
defs.def:rank-inversion: Prespecify \(\boldsymbol\varepsilon=(\varepsilon_0,\varepsilon_1)\) with \(\varepsilon_a>0\) and \(\varepsilon_0+\varepsilon_1\le\alpha\), and set \(k_a=\lceil(M+1)(1-\varepsilon_a)\rceil\). For each \(a\in\{0,1\}\), compute \(S_{i,a}=|Y^{\mathrm{obs}}_{iJ_{i,a}}-\widehat m_a(X_{iJ_{i,a}})|\), take \(r_{a,(k_a)}\), set \(I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}=[\widehat m_a(X_{0T})-r_{a,(k_a)},\widehat m_a(X_{0T})+r_{a,(k_a)}]\cap[-K,K]\), and return \(\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w=I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{1,w}-I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{0,w}\). If \(k_a=M+1\), set \(r_{a,(k_a)}=+\infty\), so \(I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}=[-K,K]\). For proof ranks only, append independent continuous uniforms and use lexicographic ranks; reported closed intervals use weak inclusion. Under \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), define \(k=k_0=k_1\), \(r_{a,(k)}=r_{a,(k_a)}\), \(I_{a,w}=I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}\), and \(\mathcal C_w=\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w\), recovering the symmetric construction.

assumptions.ass:observable-selector-kernel: \(\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\ge0,\quad\sum_{v\in\mathcal V}\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=1,\quad z_v\ne a\Rightarrow\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=0\)
assumptions.ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization: \(\mathcal L((J_{i,a})_{1\le i\le M,\ a\in\{0,1\}}\mid(L_i)_{0\le i\le M},(Z_i)_{0\le i\le M},T,\mathcal T)=\prod_{i=1}^M\prod_{a=0}^1\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\)

snapshot.stmt:
The functional \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)\) in \(\texttt{def:coverage-frontier}\) is the exact finite worst-coverage deficit over the stated class of bounded orbit-constant score maps for armwise ranks \(k_0,k_1\); no equality with a supremum over all \(P\in\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\) is asserted. Write \(\mathcal O_a=\{O\in\mathcal O:O\subseteq\mathcal U_a\}\), \(m_a=|\mathcal O_a|\), and let \(F_m\) denote the number of total preorders on an \(m\)-element labelled set. If \(k_0,k_1\le M\), the maximum is attained by enumerating preorder pairs, and every preorder has a representative in \([0,K]\) with at most \(m_a\) levels. Let \(P_T\), \(L\), and \(R_w\) be as in \(\texttt{lem:coherent-two-finite-rank-frontier-evaluator}\). For preorder pair \(C\), let \(P_T^C\) be the coherent target pushforward, let \(L_C=|\operatorname{supp}(P_T^C)|\), let \(\Pi_C\) be the set of distinct supported exact prefix-table vectors \(\pi^C(j,\ell)=(\pi_{00}^C,\pi_{01}^C,\pi_{10}^C,\pi_{11}^C)\), and put \(U_C=|\Pi_C|\le L_C\le\min\{L,d_0d_1\}\). Retain the local coherent weights \(\omega_C(\pi)\) obtained by aggregating \(P_T^C\) mass over identical vectors. Let \(\mathfrak C\) contain all \(F_{m_0}F_{m_1}\) preorder pairs and define
\[
\Pi_\star=\bigcup_{C\in\mathfrak C}\Pi_C,\qquad G=|\Pi_\star|.
\]
The global recurrence memo is keyed exactly by \((M,k_0,k_1,\pi_{00},\pi_{01},\pi_{10})\), with \(\pi_{11}=1-\pi_{00}-\pi_{01}-\pi_{10}\). Each recurrence value \(D_{M,k_0,k_1}(\pi)\) is computed once globally, while the differing weights \(\omega_C(\pi)\) never enter the recurrence and are multiplied only when each preorder objective is formed. Deterministic local grouping costs \(O(L_C\log(1+L_C))\) exact comparisons and \(O(L_C)\) aggregation arithmetic, and global balanced-tree lookup costs \(O((\sum_CU_C)\log(1+G))\). After \(R_w\) is tabulated, exact enumeration uses
\[
O\!\left(
|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}(m_0m_1+L)+\sum_{C\in\mathfrak C}\left[L_C\log(1+L_C)+L_C+U_C\right]+\left\{\sum_{C\in\mathfrak C}U_C\right\}\log(1+G)+GMk_0k_1
\right).
\]
With \(L_{\max}=\max_C L_C\) and \(U_{\max}=\max_C U_C\), a uniform bound is
\[
O\!\left(|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}\left[m_0m_1+L+L_{\max}\log(1+L_{\max})+L_{\max}+U_{\max}\{1+\log(1+G)\}\right]+GMk_0k_1\right),
\]
and working memory is \(O(m_0m_1+L+k_0k_1+U_{\max}+G)\). Every \(\pi\) is constructed from the unchanged full \(R_w\), while every \(\omega_C\) is derived from the unchanged coherent \(P_T^C\); hence global memoization preserves both calibration-arm dependence and the one common target.

If exactly one rank \(k_b=M+1\), put \(c=1-b\), so \(k_c\le M\). For a fixed nonboundary score map \(s_c\), define \(\theta(O_t)=\sum_{O\in\mathcal O_c:s_c(O)<s_c(O_t)}q_{c,O}\). The exact rank-event probability is
\[
\sum_{O_t\in\mathcal O_c}p_{c,O_t}\Pr\!\left\{\operatorname{Bin}\bigl(M,\theta(O_t)\bigr)<k_c\right\}.
\]
For ordered nonempty tie classes \(C_1\prec\cdots\prec C_d\), define \(q_j=q_c(C_j)\), \(p_j=p_c(C_j)\), and \(\theta_j=\sum_{\ell<j}q_\ell\); then the probability is \(\sum_{j=1}^d p_j\Pr\{\operatorname{Bin}(M,\theta_j)<k_c\}\). These masses are formed by one cumulative ordered-class scan. For \(0<\theta<1\), exact lower-tail terms start at \(t_0=(1-\theta)^M\) and obey
\[
t_{r+1}=t_r\frac{M-r}{r+1}\frac{\theta}{1-\theta},\qquad 0\le r<k_c-1,
\]
whereas exact upper-tail terms start at \(u_M=\theta^M\) and obey
\[
u_{r-1}=u_r\frac{r}{M-r+1}\frac{1-\theta}{\theta},\qquad k_c<r\le M.
\]
Exponentiation by squaring initializes either endpoint in \(O(\log M)\) operations, and the shorter tail uses \(O(\min\{k_c,M-k_c+1\})\) further operations. At \(\theta=0\) and \(\theta=1\), the probabilities are \(1\) and \(0\), respectively, without division. After \(q_c\) and \(p_c\) are tabulated, exact maximization enumerates only the \(F_{m_c}\) nonboundary preorders and uses
\[
O\!\left(F_{m_c}m_c\left[1+\log M+\min\{k_c,M-k_c+1\}\right]\right)
\]
arithmetic operations and \(O(1)\) auxiliary CDF memory, with no factor \(F_{m_{1-c}}\), \(|\mathcal V|\), or per-case \(m_0m_1\) scan.

If both ranks equal \(M+1\), evaluation is constant and the frontier is zero. Binary attainment is false: in the admissible symmetric instance \(M=3\), \(\alpha=1/2\), and \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=1/4\), a four-level objective equals \(24957/160000\), whereas every binary pair has objective at most \(759109/5000000\), a gap of \(83189/20000000\). If target and calibration orbit supports are disjoint in arm \(a\) and \(k_a\le M\), then \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)=1-\alpha\). If \(k_a=M+1\), the frontier reduces exactly to the other arm's rank event through the preceding one-arm formula. On the diagonal \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), this is \(\texttt{thm:sharp-orbit-coverage-frontier}\).

defs.def:pair-action: For named pair members define \(O_{b,a}=\{(v_{b,0},a),(v_{b,1},a)\}\) as a two-state pair cell, without thereby asserting that it is a global orbit. If \(\mathcal V_b=\{v_{b,0},v_{b,1}\}\) and the pure local swap subgroup \(\langle s_b\rangle=\{e,s_b\}\) is contained in \(\mathcal G\), then \(s_b(v_{b,r},a)=(v_{b,1-r},a)\) for \(r,a\in\{0,1\}\), \(s_b\) fixes members outside block \(b\), and it relabels complete records by \(\texttt{def:group-action}\). Because every group element preserves blocks and arms and block \(b\) has exactly two members, this sufficient audit implies that \(O_{b,0}\) and \(O_{b,1}\) are global \(\mathcal G\)-orbits. The audit is not necessary: a coupled-swap subgroup may also generate the same orbit. Absent the audit, the pair-cell notation alone makes no global-orbit claim. Pair-specific propositions and examples are invoked after this sufficient audit; a product specialization such as \(S_2^B\) verifies every required pure local swap separately.
defs.def:observable-selector-mixture: For every \(i\in\{1,\ldots,M\}\) and \(a\in\{0,1\}\), set \(\kappa_{w,a}=\sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_h\kappa_{h,a}\), draw \(J_{i,a}\sim\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\), set \(U_{i,a}=(J_{i,a},a)\), and set \(q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,a}=\sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_hq^{\mathrm{obs}}_{h,a}\).
defs.def:coverage-frontier:
For prespecified \(\boldsymbol\varepsilon=(\varepsilon_0,\varepsilon_1)\), define
\[
\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)=\max_{\substack{s_a:\mathcal O\to[0,K]\\a\in\{0,1\}}}\left[(1-\alpha)-\Pr\left\{s_1(O_{T,1})\le Q_{k_1}(s_1(O_{1,1}),\ldots,s_1(O_{M,1}))\ \text{and}\ s_0(O_{T,0})\le Q_{k_0}(s_0(O_{1,0}),\ldots,s_0(O_{M,0}))\right\}\right]_+.
\]
Here \(O_{i,a}\sim q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,a}\), the target orbits are the two lifts of the one common \(T\sim q_{\mathrm{tar}}\), and the calibration draws use \(\texttt{ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization}\), including their within-replicate cross-arm coupling. The functional is restricted to bounded orbit-constant score maps. Under \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), set \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)=\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)\), recovering the symmetric frontier.
defs.def:finite-rank-error:
For \(N=M+1\), prespecified \(\boldsymbol\varepsilon=(\varepsilon_0,\varepsilon_1)\), and \(k_a=\lceil N(1-\varepsilon_a)\rceil\), define
\[
\rho_{M,\varepsilon_a}=\frac{N-k_a}{N}=\frac{\lfloor N\varepsilon_a\rfloor}{N},\qquad a\in\{0,1\}.
\]
Under \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), write their common value as
\[
\rho_{M,\alpha}=\frac{M+1-k}{M+1}=\frac{\lfloor(M+1)\alpha/2\rfloor}{M+1}.
\]
defs.def:rank-inversion: Prespecify \(\boldsymbol\varepsilon=(\varepsilon_0,\varepsilon_1)\) with \(\varepsilon_a>0\) and \(\varepsilon_0+\varepsilon_1\le\alpha\), and set \(k_a=\lceil(M+1)(1-\varepsilon_a)\rceil\). For each \(a\in\{0,1\}\), compute \(S_{i,a}=|Y^{\mathrm{obs}}_{iJ_{i,a}}-\widehat m_a(X_{iJ_{i,a}})|\), take \(r_{a,(k_a)}\), set \(I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}=[\widehat m_a(X_{0T})-r_{a,(k_a)},\widehat m_a(X_{0T})+r_{a,(k_a)}]\cap[-K,K]\), and return \(\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w=I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{1,w}-I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{0,w}\). If \(k_a=M+1\), set \(r_{a,(k_a)}=+\infty\), so \(I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}=[-K,K]\). For proof ranks only, append independent continuous uniforms and use lexicographic ranks; reported closed intervals use weak inclusion. Under \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), define \(k=k_0=k_1\), \(r_{a,(k)}=r_{a,(k_a)}\), \(I_{a,w}=I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}\), and \(\mathcal C_w=\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w\), recovering the symmetric construction.

assumptions.ass:observable-selector-kernel: \(\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\ge0,\quad\sum_{v\in\mathcal V}\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=1,\quad z_v\ne a\Rightarrow\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=0\)
assumptions.ass:selector-equivariance: \(\kappa_{h,a}(\pi_gv\mid gz)=\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\quad\forall g\in\mathcal G,\ h\in\mathcal H,\ a\in\{0,1\}\)
assumptions.ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization: \(\mathcal L((J_{i,a})_{1\le i\le M,\ a\in\{0,1\}}\mid(L_i)_{0\le i\le M},(Z_i)_{0\le i\le M},T,\mathcal T)=\prod_{i=1}^M\prod_{a=0}^1\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\)

snapshot.stmt:
The functional \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)\) in \(\texttt{def:coverage-frontier}\) is the exact finite worst-coverage deficit over the stated class of bounded orbit-constant score maps; no equality with a supremum over all \(P\in\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\) is asserted. Write \(\mathcal O_a=\{O\in\mathcal O:O\subseteq\mathcal U_a\}\), \(m_a=|\mathcal O_a|\), and let \(F_m\) be the number of total preorders on an \(m\)-element labelled set. Its maximum is attained by enumerating the \(F_{m_0}F_{m_1}\) pairs of total preorders on \(\mathcal O_0\) and \(\mathcal O_1\); every preorder has a representative in \([0,K]\) with at most \(m_a\) levels. For \(v\in\mathcal V\), let \(O_a(v)\) be the orbit containing \((v,a)\), and define
\[
\kappa_{w,a}(O\mid z)=\sum_{v:(v,a)\in O}\kappa_{w,a}(v\mid z),\qquad R_w(O_0,O_1)=\sum_z\nu(z)\kappa_{w,0}(O_0\mid z)\kappa_{w,1}(O_1\mid z),
\]
\[
P_T(O_0,O_1)=\sum_{v\in\mathcal V}q_{\mathrm{tar}}(v)\mathbf 1\{O_0(v)=O_0,\ O_1(v)=O_1\},\qquad L=|\operatorname{supp}(P_T)|.
\]
Thus \(P_T\) preserves the one common target and \(R_w\) preserves the selectors' shared-assignment dependence. For a preorder pair \(C=(c_0,c_1)\), let \(P_T^C\) be the pushforward of \(P_T\) to its ordered class pairs, put \(L_C=|\operatorname{supp}(P_T^C)|\), let \(\Pi_C\) be its set of distinct supported exact four-cell vectors, and put \(U_C=|\Pi_C|\le L_C\le\min\{L,d_0d_1\}\). Retain the local coherent weights \(\omega_C(\pi)\), let \(\mathfrak C\) be the set of all preorder pairs, and define
\[
\Pi_\star=\bigcup_{C\in\mathfrak C}\Pi_C,\qquad G=|\Pi_\star|.
\]
If \(k\le M\), a global memo keyed exactly by \((M,k,k,\pi_{00},\pi_{01},\pi_{10})\), with \(\pi_{11}=1-\pi_{00}-\pi_{01}-\pi_{10}\), computes each coupled recurrence value once. Differing \(\omega_C(\pi)\) are multiplied only when each preorder objective is formed. Deterministic local grouping and global balanced-tree lookup give the instance-adaptive evaluation bound
\[
O\!\left(
|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}(m_0m_1+L)+\sum_{C\in\mathfrak C}\left[L_C\log(1+L_C)+L_C+U_C\right]+\left\{\sum_{C\in\mathfrak C}U_C\right\}\log(1+G)+GMk^2
\right)
\]
unit-cost exact comparisons and arithmetic operations. With \(L_{\max}=\max_C L_C\) and \(U_{\max}=\max_C U_C\), a uniform bound is
\[
O\!\left(|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}\left[m_0m_1+L+L_{\max}\log(1+L_{\max})+L_{\max}+U_{\max}\{1+\log(1+G)\}\right]+GMk^2\right),
\]
and working memory is \(O(m_0m_1+L+k^2+U_{\max}+G)\). Every \(\pi\) is constructed from the unchanged full \(R_w\), and every local weight is derived from the unchanged coherent \(P_T^C\), so the memo changes computation but neither probability law. If \(k=M+1\), evaluation is constant and \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)=0\). Binary attainment is false: in an admissible four-pair instance with \(M=3\) and \(\alpha=1/2\), a four-level ordering has frontier objective \(24957/160000\), whereas every binary score pair has objective at most \(759109/5000000\), a strict gap of \(83189/20000000\). Under disjoint target and calibration orbit support in one arm and \(k\le M\), \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)=1-\alpha\).

The audit is not necessary: a coupled-swap subgroup may also generate the same orbit. Absent the audit, the pair-cell notation alone makes no global-orbit claim. Pair-specific propositions and examples are invoked after this sufficient audit; a product specialization such as \(S_2^B\) verifies every required pure local swap separately.

assumptions.ass:observable-selector-kernel: \(\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\ge0,\quad\sum_{v\in\mathcal V}\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=1,\quad z_v\ne a\Rightarrow\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=0\)
assumptions.ass:selector-equivariance: \(\kappa_{h,a}(\pi_gv\mid gz)=\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\quad\forall g\in\mathcal G,\ h\in\mathcal H,\ a\in\{0,1\}\)
assumptions.ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization: \(\mathcal L((J_{i,a})_{1\le i\le M,\ a\in\{0,1\}}\mid(L_i)_{0\le i\le M},(Z_i)_{0\le i\le M},T,\mathcal T)=\prod_{i=1}^M\prod_{a=0}^1\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\)

snapshot.stmt:
The functional \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)\) in \(\texttt{def:coverage-frontier}\) is the exact finite worst-coverage deficit over the stated class of bounded orbit-constant score maps for armwise ranks \(k_0,k_1\); no equality with a supremum over all \(P\in\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\) is asserted. Write \(\mathcal O_a=\{O\in\mathcal O:O\subseteq\mathcal U_a\}\), \(m_a=|\mathcal O_a|\), and let \(F_m\) denote the number of total preorders on an \(m\)-element labelled set. If \(k_0,k_1\le M\), the maximum is attained by enumerating preorder pairs, and every preorder has a representative in \([0,K]\) with at most \(m_a\) levels. Let \(P_T\), \(L\), and \(R_w\) be as in \(\texttt{lem:coherent-two-finite-rank-frontier-evaluator}\). For preorder pair \(C\), let \(P_T^C\) be the coherent target pushforward, let \(L_C=|\operatorname{supp}(P_T^C)|\), let \(\Pi_C\) be the set of distinct supported exact prefix-table vectors \(\pi^C(j,\ell)=(\pi_{00}^C,\pi_{01}^C,\pi_{10}^C,\pi_{11}^C)\), and put \(U_C=|\Pi_C|\le L_C\le\min\{L,d_0d_1\}\). Retain the local coherent weights \(\omega_C(\pi)\) obtained by aggregating \(P_T^C\) mass over identical vectors. Let \(\mathfrak C\) contain all \(F_{m_0}F_{m_1}\) preorder pairs and define
\[
\Pi_\star=\bigcup_{C\in\mathfrak C}\Pi_C,\qquad G=|\Pi_\star|.
\]
The global recurrence memo is keyed exactly by \((M,k_0,k_1,\pi_{00},\pi_{01},\pi_{10})\), with \(\pi_{11}=1-\pi_{00}-\pi_{01}-\pi_{10}\). Each recurrence value \(D_{M,k_0,k_1}(\pi)\) is computed once globally, while the differing weights \(\omega_C(\pi)\) never enter the recurrence and are multiplied only when each preorder objective is formed. Deterministic local grouping costs \(O(L_C\log(1+L_C))\) exact comparisons and \(O(L_C)\) aggregation arithmetic, and global balanced-tree lookup costs \(O((\sum_CU_C)\log(1+G))\). After \(R_w\) is tabulated, exact enumeration uses
\[
O\!\left(
|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}(m_0m_1+L)+\sum_{C\in\mathfrak C}\left[L_C\log(1+L_C)+L_C+U_C\right]+\left\{\sum_{C\in\mathfrak C}U_C\right\}\log(1+G)+GMk_0k_1
\right).
\]
With \(L_{\max}=\max_C L_C\) and \(U_{\max}=\max_C U_C\), a uniform bound is
\[
O\!\left(|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}\left[m_0m_1+L+L_{\max}\log(1+L_{\max})+L_{\max}+U_{\max}\{1+\log(1+G)\}\right]+GMk_0k_1\right),
\]
and working memory is \(O(m_0m_1+L+k_0k_1+U_{\max}+G)\). Every \(\pi\) is constructed from the unchanged full \(R_w\), while every \(\omega_C\) is derived from the unchanged coherent \(P_T^C\); hence global memoization preserves both calibration-arm dependence and the one common target.

If exactly one rank \(k_b=M+1\), put \(c=1-b\), so \(k_c\le M\). For a fixed nonboundary score map \(s_c\), define \(\theta(O_t)=\sum_{O\in\mathcal O_c:s_c(O)<s_c(O_t)}q_{c,O}\). The exact rank-event probability is
\[
\sum_{O_t\in\mathcal O_c}p_{c,O_t}\Pr\!\left\{\operatorname{Bin}\bigl(M,\theta(O_t)\bigr)<k_c\right\}.
\]
For ordered nonempty tie classes \(C_1\prec\cdots\prec C_d\), define \(q_j=q_c(C_j)\), \(p_j=p_c(C_j)\), and \(\theta_j=\sum_{\ell<j}q_\ell\); then the probability is \(\sum_{j=1}^d p_j\Pr\{\operatorname{Bin}(M,\theta_j)<k_c\}\). These masses are formed by one cumulative ordered-class scan. For \(0<\theta<1\), exact lower-tail terms start at \(t_0=(1-\theta)^M\) and obey
\[
t_{r+1}=t_r\frac{M-r}{r+1}\frac{\theta}{1-\theta},\qquad 0\le r<k_c-1,
\]
whereas exact upper-tail terms start at \(u_M=\theta^M\) and obey
\[
u_{r-1}=u_r\frac{r}{M-r+1}\frac{1-\theta}{\theta},\qquad k_c<r\le M.
\]
Exponentiation by squaring initializes either endpoint in \(O(\log M)\) operations, and the shorter tail uses \(O(\min\{k_c,M-k_c+1\})\) further operations. At \(\theta=0\) and \(\theta=1\), the probabilities are \(1\) and \(0\), respectively, without division. After \(q_c\) and \(p_c\) are tabulated, exact maximization enumerates only the \(F_{m_c}\) nonboundary preorders and uses
\[
O\!\left(F_{m_c}m_c\left[1+\log M+\min\{k_c,M-k_c+1\}\right]\right)
\]
arithmetic operations and \(O(1)\) auxiliary CDF memory, with no factor \(F_{m_{1-c}}\), \(|\mathcal V|\), or per-case \(m_0m_1\) scan.

If both ranks equal \(M+1\), evaluation is constant and the frontier is zero. Binary attainment is false: in the admissible symmetric instance \(M=3\), \(\alpha=1/2\), and \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=1/4\), a four-level objective equals \(24957/160000\), whereas every binary pair has objective at most \(759109/5000000\), a gap of \(83189/20000000\). If target and calibration orbit supports are disjoint in arm \(a\) and \(k_a\le M\), then \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)=1-\alpha\). If \(k_a=M+1\), the frontier reduces exactly to the other arm's rank event through the preceding one-arm formula. On the diagonal \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), this is \(\texttt{thm:sharp-orbit-coverage-frontier}\).

Under \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), define \(k=k_0=k_1\), \(r_{a,(k)}=r_{a,(k_a)}\), \(I_{a,w}=I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}\), and \(\mathcal C_w=\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w\), recovering the symmetric construction.

assumptions.ass:observable-selector-kernel: \(\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\ge0,\quad\sum_{v\in\mathcal V}\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=1,\quad z_v\ne a\Rightarrow\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=0\)
assumptions.ass:selector-equivariance: \(\kappa_{h,a}(\pi_gv\mid gz)=\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\quad\forall g\in\mathcal G,\ h\in\mathcal H,\ a\in\{0,1\}\)
assumptions.ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization: \(\mathcal L((J_{i,a})_{1\le i\le M,\ a\in\{0,1\}}\mid(L_i)_{0\le i\le M},(Z_i)_{0\le i\le M},T,\mathcal T)=\prod_{i=1}^M\prod_{a=0}^1\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\)

snapshot.stmt:
The functional \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)\) in \(\texttt{def:coverage-frontier}\) is the exact finite worst-coverage deficit over the stated class of bounded orbit-constant score maps; no equality with a supremum over all \(P\in\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\) is asserted. Write \(\mathcal O_a=\{O\in\mathcal O:O\subseteq\mathcal U_a\}\), \(m_a=|\mathcal O_a|\), and let \(F_m\) be the number of total preorders on an \(m\)-element labelled set. Its maximum is attained by enumerating the \(F_{m_0}F_{m_1}\) pairs of total preorders on \(\mathcal O_0\) and \(\mathcal O_1\); every preorder has a representative in \([0,K]\) with at most \(m_a\) levels. For \(v\in\mathcal V\), let \(O_a(v)\) be the orbit containing \((v,a)\), and define
\[
\kappa_{w,a}(O\mid z)=\sum_{v:(v,a)\in O}\kappa_{w,a}(v\mid z),\qquad R_w(O_0,O_1)=\sum_z\nu(z)\kappa_{w,0}(O_0\mid z)\kappa_{w,1}(O_1\mid z),
\]
\[
P_T(O_0,O_1)=\sum_{v\in\mathcal V}q_{\mathrm{tar}}(v)\mathbf 1\{O_0(v)=O_0,\ O_1(v)=O_1\},\qquad L=|\operatorname{supp}(P_T)|.
\]
Thus \(P_T\) preserves the one common target and \(R_w\) preserves the selectors' shared-assignment dependence. For a preorder pair \(C=(c_0,c_1)\), let \(P_T^C\) be the pushforward of \(P_T\) to its ordered class pairs, put \(L_C=|\operatorname{supp}(P_T^C)|\), let \(\Pi_C\) be its set of distinct supported exact four-cell vectors, and put \(U_C=|\Pi_C|\le L_C\le\min\{L,d_0d_1\}\). Retain the local coherent weights \(\omega_C(\pi)\), let \(\mathfrak C\) be the set of all preorder pairs, and define
\[
\Pi_\star=\bigcup_{C\in\mathfrak C}\Pi_C,\qquad G=|\Pi_\star|.
\]
If \(k\le M\), a global memo keyed exactly by \((M,k,k,\pi_{00},\pi_{01},\pi_{10})\), with \(\pi_{11}=1-\pi_{00}-\pi_{01}-\pi_{10}\), computes each coupled recurrence value once. Differing \(\omega_C(\pi)\) are multiplied only when each preorder objective is formed. Deterministic local grouping and global balanced-tree lookup give the instance-adaptive evaluation bound
\[
O\!\left(
|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}(m_0m_1+L)+\sum_{C\in\mathfrak C}\left[L_C\log(1+L_C)+L_C+U_C\right]+\left\{\sum_{C\in\mathfrak C}U_C\right\}\log(1+G)+GMk^2
\right)
\]
unit-cost exact comparisons and arithmetic operations. With \(L_{\max}=\max_C L_C\) and \(U_{\max}=\max_C U_C\), a uniform bound is
\[
O\!\left(|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}\left[m_0m_1+L+L_{\max}\log(1+L_{\max})+L_{\max}+U_{\max}\{1+\log(1+G)\}\right]+GMk^2\right),
\]
and working memory is \(O(m_0m_1+L+k^2+U_{\max}+G)\). Every \(\pi\) is constructed from the unchanged full \(R_w\), and every local weight is derived from the unchanged coherent \(P_T^C\), so the memo changes computation but neither probability law. If \(k=M+1\), evaluation is constant and \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)=0\). Binary attainment is false: in an admissible four-pair instance with \(M=3\) and \(\alpha=1/2\), a four-level ordering has frontier objective \(24957/160000\), whereas every binary score pair has objective at most \(759109/5000000\), a strict gap of \(83189/20000000\). Under disjoint target and calibration orbit support in one arm and \(k\le M\), \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)=1-\alpha\).

defs.def:pair-action: For named pair members define \(O_{b,a}=\{(v_{b,0},a),(v_{b,1},a)\}\) as a two-state pair cell, without thereby asserting that it is a global orbit. If \(\mathcal V_b=\{v_{b,0},v_{b,1}\}\) and the pure local swap subgroup \(\langle s_b\rangle=\{e,s_b\}\) is contained in \(\mathcal G\), then \(s_b(v_{b,r},a)=(v_{b,1-r},a)\) for \(r,a\in\{0,1\}\), \(s_b\) fixes members outside block \(b\), and it relabels complete records by \(\texttt{def:group-action}\). Because every group element preserves blocks and arms and block \(b\) has exactly two members, this sufficient audit implies that \(O_{b,0}\) and \(O_{b,1}\) are global \(\mathcal G\)-orbits. The audit is not necessary: a coupled-swap subgroup may also generate the same orbit. Absent the audit, the pair-cell notation alone makes no global-orbit claim. Pair-specific propositions and examples are invoked after this sufficient audit; a product specialization such as \(S_2^B\) verifies every required pure local swap separately.
defs.def:observable-selector-mixture: For every \(i\in\{1,\ldots,M\}\) and \(a\in\{0,1\}\), set \(\kappa_{w,a}=\sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_h\kappa_{h,a}\), draw \(J_{i,a}\sim\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\), set \(U_{i,a}=(J_{i,a},a)\), and set \(q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,a}=\sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_hq^{\mathrm{obs}}_{h,a}\).
defs.def:coverage-frontier:
For prespecified \(\boldsymbol\varepsilon=(\varepsilon_0,\varepsilon_1)\), define
\[
\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)=\max_{\substack{s_a:\mathcal O\to[0,K]\\a\in\{0,1\}}}\left[(1-\alpha)-\Pr\left\{s_1(O_{T,1})\le Q_{k_1}(s_1(O_{1,1}),\ldots,s_1(O_{M,1}))\ \text{and}\ s_0(O_{T,0})\le Q_{k_0}(s_0(O_{1,0}),\ldots,s_0(O_{M,0}))\right\}\right]_+.
\]
Here \(O_{i,a}\sim q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,a}\), the target orbits are the two lifts of the one common \(T\sim q_{\mathrm{tar}}\), and the calibration draws use \(\texttt{ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization}\), including their within-replicate cross-arm coupling. The functional is restricted to bounded orbit-constant score maps. Under \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), set \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)=\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)\), recovering the symmetric frontier.
defs.def:finite-rank-error:
For \(N=M+1\), prespecified \(\boldsymbol\varepsilon=(\varepsilon_0,\varepsilon_1)\), and \(k_a=\lceil N(1-\varepsilon_a)\rceil\), define
\[
\rho_{M,\varepsilon_a}=\frac{N-k_a}{N}=\frac{\lfloor N\varepsilon_a\rfloor}{N},\qquad a\in\{0,1\}.
\]
Under \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), write their common value as
\[
\rho_{M,\alpha}=\frac{M+1-k}{M+1}=\frac{\lfloor(M+1)\alpha/2\rfloor}{M+1}.
\]
defs.def:rank-inversion: Prespecify \(\boldsymbol\varepsilon=(\varepsilon_0,\varepsilon_1)\) with \(\varepsilon_a>0\) and \(\varepsilon_0+\varepsilon_1\le\alpha\), and set \(k_a=\lceil(M+1)(1-\varepsilon_a)\rceil\). For each \(a\in\{0,1\}\), compute \(S_{i,a}=|Y^{\mathrm{obs}}_{iJ_{i,a}}-\widehat m_a(X_{iJ_{i,a}})|\), take \(r_{a,(k_a)}\), set \(I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}=[\widehat m_a(X_{0T})-r_{a,(k_a)},\widehat m_a(X_{0T})+r_{a,(k_a)}]\cap[-K,K]\), and return \(\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w=I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{1,w}-I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{0,w}\). If \(k_a=M+1\), set \(r_{a,(k_a)}=+\infty\), so \(I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}=[-K,K]\). For proof ranks only, append independent continuous uniforms and use lexicographic ranks; reported closed intervals use weak inclusion. Under \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), define \(k=k_0=k_1\), \(r_{a,(k)}=r_{a,(k_a)}\), \(I_{a,w}=I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}\), and \(\mathcal C_w=\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w\), recovering the symmetric construction.

assumptions.ass:observable-selector-kernel: \(\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\ge0,\quad\sum_{v\in\mathcal V}\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=1,\quad z_v\ne a\Rightarrow\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=0\)
assumptions.ass:selector-equivariance: \(\kappa_{h,a}(\pi_gv\mid gz)=\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\quad\forall g\in\mathcal G,\ h\in\mathcal H,\ a\in\{0,1\}\)
assumptions.ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization: \(\mathcal L((J_{i,a})_{1\le i\le M,\ a\in\{0,1\}}\mid(L_i)_{0\le i\le M},(Z_i)_{0\le i\le M},T,\mathcal T)=\prod_{i=1}^M\prod_{a=0}^1\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\)

snapshot.stmt: Let \(\mathscr E_0\) and \(\mathscr E_1\) be finite sets, let \(R\) be a probability mass function on \(\mathscr E_0\times\mathscr E_1\), and let \(b_a:\mathscr E_a\to\{0,1\}\). For \((x,y)\in\{0,1\}^2\), define \(\pi_{xy}=\sum_{e_0,e_1}R(e_0,e_1)\mathbf 1\{b_0(e_0)=x,b_1(e_1)=y\}\). Let \(1\le k_0,k_1\le M+1\). If both ranks are at most \(M\), then the recurrence \(d_0(0,0)=1\) and \(d_{i+1}(r,s)=\sum_{x,y\in\{0,1\}}\pi_{xy}d_i(r-x,s-y)\), with out-of-range entries zero and only \(0\le r<k_0\), \(0\le s<k_1\) retained, satisfies \[\sum_{r<k_0,s<k_1}d_M(r,s)=\Pr\{\sum_i b_0(E_{i,0})<k_0,\quad \sum_i b_1(E_{i,1})<k_1\}.\] After \(R\) is tabulated, the four cells and recurrence require \(O(|\mathscr E_0||\mathscr E_1|+Mk_0k_1)\) arithmetic operations and \(O(k_0k_1)\) memory. If exactly one rank equals \(M+1\), the corresponding event is certain and a one-dimensional recurrence for the other count requires \(O(|\mathscr E_0||\mathscr E_1|+Mk_c)\) operations and \(O(k_c)\) memory, where \(k_c\le M\) is the other rank. If both ranks equal \(M+1\), the joint event has probability one and requires no recurrence.

Let \(M\ge1\), let \(1\le k_0,k_1\le M\), and let \(\pi=(\pi_{00},\pi_{01},\pi_{10},\pi_{11})\) be a probability mass function on \(\{0,1\}^2\). For \(M\) independent draws with this law, the retained-rectangle probability is
\[
\begin{aligned}
Q_{M,k_0,k_1}(\pi)
={}&\sum_{r=0}^{k_0-1}\sum_{s=0}^{k_1-1}
\sum_{t=\max\{0,r+s-M\}}^{\min\{r,s\}}
\frac{M!}{(M-r-s+t)!(s-t)!(r-t)!t!}\\
&\qquad\qquad{}\times
\pi_{00}^{M-r-s+t}\pi_{01}^{s-t}\pi_{10}^{r-t}\pi_{11}^{t}.
\end{aligned}
\]
An empty inner range contributes zero, \(0^0=1\), and \(0^j=0\) for every positive integer \(j\). If
\[
\mathsf T_{M,k_0,k_1}
=\sum_{r=0}^{k_0-1}\sum_{s=0}^{k_1-1}
\left[\min\{r,s\}-\max\{0,r+s-M\}+1\right]_+,
\]
then \(\mathsf T_{M,k_0,k_1}\le k_0k_1\min\{k_0,k_1\}\). After probability-independent factorials through \(M!\) are precomputed once, one key \(\pi\) is evaluated by rebuilding its four power tables in \(O(M)\) operations and then summing the feasible terms in \(O(\mathsf T_{M,k_0,k_1})\) operations, using \(O(M)\) auxiliary memory. The rolling two-count recurrence is an exact alternative using \(O(Mk_0k_1)\) operations and \(O(k_0k_1)\) auxiliary memory. A time-selecting hybrid therefore uses \(O(\min\{M+\mathsf T_{M,k_0,k_1},Mk_0k_1\})\) operations per key, conditional memory \(O(M)\) on the multinomial branch and \(O(k_0k_1)\) on the recurrence branch, and unconditional auxiliary memory \(O(\max\{M,k_0k_1\})\). For a finite set \(\Pi\) of keys processed sequentially, if \(\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\) and \(\Pi_{\mathrm{DP}}\) are the keys assigned to the two branches, the exact aggregate resource charge is
\[
O\!\left(M+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}}(M+\mathsf T_{M,k_0,k_1})+|\Pi_{\mathrm{DP}}|Mk_0k_1\right)
\]
operations, with the \(O(M)\) factorial preprocessing shared once, four probability-specific power tables rebuilt for each \(\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\), sequential key processing, and \(O(|\Pi|+\max\{M,k_0k_1\})\) memory when one scalar answer per key is retained.

Suppose \(k_0,k_1\le M\). Write \(\mathcal O_a=\{O\in\mathcal O:O\subseteq\mathcal U_a\}\), \(m_a=|\mathcal O_a|\), and let \(F_m\) be the number of total preorders on an \(m\)-element labelled set. Define
\[
\kappa_{w,a}(O\mid z)=\sum_{v:(v,a)\in O}\kappa_{w,a}(v\mid z),\qquad R_w(O_0,O_1)=\sum_z\nu(z)\kappa_{w,0}(O_0\mid z)\kappa_{w,1}(O_1\mid z).
\]
For \(v\in\mathcal V\) and \(a\in\{0,1\}\), let \(O_a(v)\) be the orbit containing \((v,a)\), and define
\[
P_T(O_0,O_1)=\sum_{v\in\mathcal V}q_{\mathrm{tar}}(v)\mathbf 1\{O_0(v)=O_0,\ O_1(v)=O_1\},\qquad L=|\operatorname{supp}(P_T)|.
\]
Then \(L\le\min\{|\mathcal V|,m_0m_1\}\). For a preorder pair \(C=(c_0,c_1)\) with ordered nonempty tie classes \(C_{a,1}\prec_a\cdots\prec_a C_{a,d_a}\), let
\[
P_T^C(j,\ell)=\sum_{\substack{O_0,O_1\\c_0(O_0)=j,\ c_1(O_1)=\ell}}P_T(O_0,O_1),\qquad L_C=|\operatorname{supp}(P_T^C)|.
\]
Then \(L_C\le\min\{L,d_0d_1\}\). One two-dimensional prefix table of the unchanged full joint calibration-orbit law \(R_w\) yields the exact four-cell vector \(\pi^C(j,\ell)=(\pi_{00}^C,\pi_{01}^C,\pi_{10}^C,\pi_{11}^C)\) in constant time for every supported target class pair. Define
\[
\Pi_C=\{\pi^C(j,\ell):(j,\ell)\in\operatorname{supp}(P_T^C)\},\qquad U_C=|\Pi_C|\le L_C,
\]
and retain the local coherent weights
\[
\omega_C(\pi)=\sum_{\substack{(j,\ell)\in\operatorname{supp}(P_T^C)\\\pi^C(j,\ell)=\pi}}P_T^C(j,\ell).
\]
The preorder objective is \(\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_C}\omega_C(\pi)D_{M,k_0,k_1}(\pi)\), where the coupled two-count recurrence value depends only on \(M\), \(k_0\), \(k_1\), and \(\pi\). Let \(\mathfrak C\) be the \(F_{m_0}F_{m_1}\) preorder pairs and define
\[
\Pi_\star=\bigcup_{C\in\mathfrak C}\Pi_C,\qquad G=|\Pi_\star|.
\]
A global cross-preorder memo is keyed exactly by \((M,k_0,k_1,\pi_{00},\pi_{01},\pi_{10})\), with \(\pi_{11}=1-\pi_{00}-\pi_{01}-\pi_{10}\). Differing local weights \(\omega_C(\pi)\) never enter the recurrence and are multiplied only when each preorder objective is formed. Hence the memo preserves the one common target through the unchanged coherent \(P_T^C\), the full within-replicate cross-arm calibration dependence through the unchanged \(R_w\), and the common selector \(w\). Put
\[
h_a=M-k_a+1,\qquad \bar k_a=\min\{k_a,h_a\},\qquad \mathsf C_{M,k_0,k_1}=\sum_{i=0}^{M-1}\min\{\bar k_0,i+1\}\min\{\bar k_1,i+1\}.
\]
For each \(\pi\in\Pi_\star\), deterministically select a least-charge exact evaluator among: the corner-oriented rolling branch with charge \(\mathsf C_{M,k_0,k_1}\); the zero-cell-safe multinomial-sum branch with charge \(M+\mathsf T_{M,\bar k_0,\bar k_1}\); and the positive-corner coefficient branch with charge \(\mathsf C_+(\pi)\). Break ties prespecifically, and let
\[
\Pi_\star=\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_+
\]
be the resulting exact-key partition. For \(\pi\in\Pi_+\), let \(\sigma(\pi)\) be a minimizing positive-base orientation in \(\mathsf C_+(\pi)\), with oriented thresholds \(t_0^{\sigma(\pi)},t_1^{\sigma(\pi)}\). Define
\[
\begin{aligned}
\mathsf B={}&M\mathbf 1\{\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\}+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}}\mathsf C_{M,k_0,k_1}\\
&+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}}\left(M+\mathsf T_{M,\bar k_0,\bar k_1}\right)+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_+}\mathsf C_+(\pi),
\end{aligned}
\]
where every empty sum is zero, and
\[
\mathsf W=\max\!\left(\{0\}\cup\{\bar k_0\bar k_1:\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}\ne\varnothing\}\cup\{M:\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\}\cup\left\{\min\{t_0^{\sigma(\pi)},t_1^{\sigma(\pi)}\}:\pi\in\Pi_+\right\}\right).
\]
Thus empty-branch maxima are harmless. A deterministic local balanced tree uses \(O(L_C\log(1+L_C))\) exact comparisons and \(O(L_C)\) aggregation arithmetic for preorder \(C\). The global balanced-tree memo adds \(O((\sum_CU_C)\log(1+G))\) exact comparisons and evaluates only the \(G\) distinct keys. After \(R_w\) is tabulated, exact enumeration uses
\[
O\!\left(
|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}(m_0m_1+L)+\sum_{C\in\mathfrak C}\left[L_C\log(1+L_C)+L_C+U_C\right]+\left\{\sum_{C\in\mathfrak C}U_C\right\}\log(1+G)+\mathsf B
\right).
\]
The term \(M\mathbf 1\{\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\}\) is exactly one shared factorial-preprocessing charge and is absent when \(\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}=\varnothing\). If \(L_{\max}=\max_C L_C\) and \(U_{\max}=\max_C U_C\), a uniform bound is
\[
O\!\left(|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}\left[m_0m_1+L+L_{\max}\log(1+L_{\max})+L_{\max}+U_{\max}\{1+\log(1+G)\}\right]+\mathsf B\right).
\]
The working-memory bound, including all non-recurrence tables, the current local exact-key table, and the \(G\) memoized scalar answers, is \(O(m_0m_1+L+U_{\max}+G+\mathsf W)\). In particular, if \(k_0=k_1=M\), then \(\bar k_0=\bar k_1=1\) and \(\mathsf C_{M,M,M}=M\), so the rolling per-key charge improves from \(\Theta(M^3)\) to \(\Theta(M)\), with the positive-corner orientation potentially smaller.

defs.def:observable-selector-mixture: For every \(i\in\{1,\ldots,M\}\) and \(a\in\{0,1\}\), set \(\kappa_{w,a}=\sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_h\kappa_{h,a}\), draw \(J_{i,a}\sim\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\), set \(U_{i,a}=(J_{i,a},a)\), and set \(q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,a}=\sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_hq^{\mathrm{obs}}_{h,a}\).
defs.def:coverage-frontier:
For prespecified \(\boldsymbol\varepsilon=(\varepsilon_0,\varepsilon_1)\), define
\[
\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)=\max_{\substack{s_a:\mathcal O\to[0,K]\\a\in\{0,1\}}}\left[(1-\alpha)-\Pr\left\{s_1(O_{T,1})\le Q_{k_1}(s_1(O_{1,1}),\ldots,s_1(O_{M,1}))\ \text{and}\ s_0(O_{T,0})\le Q_{k_0}(s_0(O_{1,0}),\ldots,s_0(O_{M,0}))\right\}\right]_+.
\]
Here \(O_{i,a}\sim q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,a}\), the target orbits are the two lifts of the one common \(T\sim q_{\mathrm{tar}}\), and the calibration draws use \(\texttt{ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization}\), including their within-replicate cross-arm coupling. The functional is restricted to bounded orbit-constant score maps. Under \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), set \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)=\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)\), recovering the symmetric frontier.
defs.def:rank-inversion: Prespecify \(\boldsymbol\varepsilon=(\varepsilon_0,\varepsilon_1)\) with \(\varepsilon_a>0\) and \(\varepsilon_0+\varepsilon_1\le\alpha\), and set \(k_a=\lceil(M+1)(1-\varepsilon_a)\rceil\). For each \(a\in\{0,1\}\), compute \(S_{i,a}=|Y^{\mathrm{obs}}_{iJ_{i,a}}-\widehat m_a(X_{iJ_{i,a}})|\), take \(r_{a,(k_a)}\), set \(I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}=[\widehat m_a(X_{0T})-r_{a,(k_a)},\widehat m_a(X_{0T})+r_{a,(k_a)}]\cap[-K,K]\), and return \(\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w=I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{1,w}-I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{0,w}\). If \(k_a=M+1\), set \(r_{a,(k_a)}=+\infty\), so \(I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}=[-K,K]\). For proof ranks only, append independent continuous uniforms and use lexicographic ranks; reported closed intervals use weak inclusion. Under \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), define \(k=k_0=k_1\), \(r_{a,(k)}=r_{a,(k_a)}\), \(I_{a,w}=I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}\), and \(\mathcal C_w=\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w\), recovering the symmetric construction.

assumptions.ass:observable-selector-kernel: \(\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\ge0,\quad\sum_{v\in\mathcal V}\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=1,\quad z_v\ne a\Rightarrow\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=0\)
assumptions.ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization: \(\mathcal L((J_{i,a})_{1\le i\le M,\ a\in\{0,1\}}\mid(L_i)_{0\le i\le M},(Z_i)_{0\le i\le M},T,\mathcal T)=\prod_{i=1}^M\prod_{a=0}^1\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\)

snapshot.stmt:
The functional \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)\) in \(\texttt{def:coverage-frontier}\) is the exact finite worst-coverage deficit over the stated class of bounded orbit-constant score maps for armwise ranks \(k_0,k_1\); no equality with a supremum over all \(P\in\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\) is asserted. Write \(\mathcal O_a=\{O\in\mathcal O:O\subseteq\mathcal U_a\}\), \(m_a=|\mathcal O_a|\), and let \(F_m\) denote the number of total preorders on an \(m\)-element labelled set. If \(k_0,k_1\le M\), the maximum is attained by enumerating preorder pairs, and every preorder has a representative in \([0,K]\) with at most \(m_a\) levels. Let \(P_T\), \(L\), \(R_w\), \(P_T^C\), \(L_C\), \(\Pi_C\), \(U_C\), \(\omega_C\), \(\Pi_\star\), and \(G\) be as in \(\texttt{lem:coherent-two-finite-rank-frontier-evaluator}\). Thus \(U_C\le L_C\le\min\{L,d_0d_1\}\), the global memo is keyed exactly by \((M,k_0,k_1,\pi_{00},\pi_{01},\pi_{10})\), and \(\pi_{11}=1-\pi_{00}-\pi_{01}-\pi_{10}\). Each exact four-cell probability is computed once globally, while the differing local coherent weights are multiplied only when each preorder objective is formed. Put
\[
h_a=M-k_a+1,\qquad \bar k_a=\min\{k_a,h_a\},\qquad \mathsf C_{M,k_0,k_1}=\sum_{i=0}^{M-1}\min\{\bar k_0,i+1\}\min\{\bar k_1,i+1\}.
\]
For each \(\pi\in\Pi_\star\), deterministically select a least-charge exact evaluator among the corner-oriented rolling charge \(\mathsf C_{M,k_0,k_1}\), the zero-cell-safe multinomial-sum charge \(M+\mathsf T_{M,\bar k_0,\bar k_1}\), and the positive-corner coefficient charge \(\mathsf C_+(\pi)\). With prespecified tie-breaking, write
\[
\Pi_\star=\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_+.
\]
For \(\pi\in\Pi_+\), let \(\sigma(\pi)\) attain \(\mathsf C_+(\pi)\). Define
\[
\begin{aligned}
\mathsf B={}&M\mathbf 1\{\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\}+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}}\mathsf C_{M,k_0,k_1}\\
&+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}}\left(M+\mathsf T_{M,\bar k_0,\bar k_1}\right)+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_+}\mathsf C_+(\pi),
\end{aligned}
\]
where empty sums are zero, and
\[
\mathsf W=\max\!\left(\{0\}\cup\{\bar k_0\bar k_1:\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}\ne\varnothing\}\cup\{M:\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\}\cup\left\{\min\{t_0^{\sigma(\pi)},t_1^{\sigma(\pi)}\}:\pi\in\Pi_+\right\}\right).
\]
Deterministic local grouping costs \(O(L_C\log(1+L_C))\) exact comparisons and \(O(L_C)\) aggregation arithmetic, and global lookup costs \(O((\sum_CU_C)\log(1+G))\). After \(R_w\) is tabulated, exact enumeration uses
\[
O\!\left(
|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}(m_0m_1+L)+\sum_{C\in\mathfrak C}\left[L_C\log(1+L_C)+L_C+U_C\right]+\left\{\sum_{C\in\mathfrak C}U_C\right\}\log(1+G)+\mathsf B
\right).
\]
The leading \(M\mathbf 1\{\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\}\) is exactly one shared factorial-preprocessing term. With \(L_{\max}=\max_C L_C\) and \(U_{\max}=\max_C U_C\), a uniform bound is
\[
O\!\left(|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}\left[m_0m_1+L+L_{\max}\log(1+L_{\max})+L_{\max}+U_{\max}\{1+\log(1+G)\}\right]+\mathsf B\right),
\]
and working memory is \(O(m_0m_1+L+U_{\max}+G+\mathsf W)\). Every \(\pi\) is constructed from the unchanged full \(R_w\), while every \(\omega_C\) is derived from the unchanged coherent \(P_T^C\); hence memoization and branch selection preserve calibration-arm dependence, the one common target, and the common selector. If \(k_0=k_1=M\), then \(\bar k_0=\bar k_1=1\) and the rolling per-key charge is \(\Theta(M)\), rather than the coarse \(\Theta(M^3)\), with the positive-corner orientation potentially smaller.

If exactly one rank \(k_b=M+1\), put \(c=1-b\), so \(k_c\le M\). For a fixed nonboundary score map \(s_c\), define \(\theta(O_t)=\sum_{O\in\mathcal O_c:s_c(O)<s_c(O_t)}q_{c,O}\). The exact rank-event probability is
\[
\sum_{O_t\in\mathcal O_c}p_{c,O_t}\Pr\!\left\{\operatorname{Bin}\bigl(M,\theta(O_t)\bigr)<k_c\right\}.
\]
For ordered nonempty tie classes \(C_1\prec\cdots\prec C_d\), define \(q_j=q_c(C_j)\), \(p_j=p_c(C_j)\), and \(\theta_j=\sum_{\ell<j}q_\ell\); then the probability is \(\sum_{j=1}^d p_j\Pr\{\operatorname{Bin}(M,\theta_j)<k_c\}\). These masses are formed by one cumulative ordered-class scan. For \(0<\theta<1\), exact lower-tail terms start at \(t_0=(1-\theta)^M\) and obey
\[
t_{r+1}=t_r\frac{M-r}{r+1}\frac{\theta}{1-\theta},\qquad 0\le r<k_c-1,
\]
whereas exact upper-tail terms start at \(u_M=\theta^M\) and obey
\[
u_{r-1}=u_r\frac{r}{M-r+1}\frac{1-\theta}{\theta},\qquad k_c<r\le M.
\]
Exponentiation by squaring initializes either endpoint in \(O(\log M)\) operations, and the shorter tail uses \(O(\min\{k_c,M-k_c+1\})\) further operations. At \(\theta=0\) and \(\theta=1\), the probabilities are \(1\) and \(0\), respectively, without division. After \(q_c\) and \(p_c\) are tabulated, exact maximization enumerates only the \(F_{m_c}\) nonboundary preorders and uses
\[
O\!\left(F_{m_c}m_c\left[1+\log M+\min\{k_c,M-k_c+1\}\right]\right)
\]
arithmetic operations and \(O(1)\) auxiliary CDF memory, with no factor \(F_{m_{1-c}}\), \(|\mathcal V|\), or per-case \(m_0m_1\) scan. If both ranks equal \(M+1\), evaluation is constant and the frontier is zero. Binary attainment is false: in the admissible symmetric instance \(M=3\), \(\alpha=1/2\), and \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=1/4\), a four-level objective equals \(24957/160000\), whereas every binary pair has objective at most \(759109/5000000\), a gap of \(83189/20000000\). If target and calibration orbit supports are disjoint in arm \(a\) and \(k_a\le M\), then \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)=1-\alpha\). If \(k_a=M+1\), the frontier reduces exactly to the other arm's rank event through the preceding one-arm formula. On the diagonal \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), this is \(\texttt{thm:sharp-orbit-coverage-frontier}\).

defs.def:pair-action: For named pair members define \(O_{b,a}=\{(v_{b,0},a),(v_{b,1},a)\}\) as a two-state pair cell, without thereby asserting that it is a global orbit. If \(\mathcal V_b=\{v_{b,0},v_{b,1}\}\) and the pure local swap subgroup \(\langle s_b\rangle=\{e,s_b\}\) is contained in \(\mathcal G\), then \(s_b(v_{b,r},a)=(v_{b,1-r},a)\) for \(r,a\in\{0,1\}\), \(s_b\) fixes members outside block \(b\), and it relabels complete records by \(\texttt{def:group-action}\). Because every group element preserves blocks and arms and block \(b\) has exactly two members, this sufficient audit implies that \(O_{b,0}\) and \(O_{b,1}\) are global \(\mathcal G\)-orbits. The audit is not necessary: a coupled-swap subgroup may also generate the same orbit. Absent the audit, the pair-cell notation alone makes no global-orbit claim. Pair-specific propositions and examples are invoked after this sufficient audit; a product specialization such as \(S_2^B\) verifies every required pure local swap separately.
defs.def:observable-selector-mixture: For every \(i\in\{1,\ldots,M\}\) and \(a\in\{0,1\}\), set \(\kappa_{w,a}=\sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_h\kappa_{h,a}\), draw \(J_{i,a}\sim\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\), set \(U_{i,a}=(J_{i,a},a)\), and set \(q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,a}=\sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_hq^{\mathrm{obs}}_{h,a}\).
defs.def:coverage-frontier:
For prespecified \(\boldsymbol\varepsilon=(\varepsilon_0,\varepsilon_1)\), define
\[
\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)=\max_{\substack{s_a:\mathcal O\to[0,K]\\a\in\{0,1\}}}\left[(1-\alpha)-\Pr\left\{s_1(O_{T,1})\le Q_{k_1}(s_1(O_{1,1}),\ldots,s_1(O_{M,1}))\ \text{and}\ s_0(O_{T,0})\le Q_{k_0}(s_0(O_{1,0}),\ldots,s_0(O_{M,0}))\right\}\right]_+.
\]
Here \(O_{i,a}\sim q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,a}\), the target orbits are the two lifts of the one common \(T\sim q_{\mathrm{tar}}\), and the calibration draws use \(\texttt{ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization}\), including their within-replicate cross-arm coupling. The functional is restricted to bounded orbit-constant score maps. Under \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), set \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)=\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)\), recovering the symmetric frontier.
defs.def:finite-rank-error:
For \(N=M+1\), prespecified \(\boldsymbol\varepsilon=(\varepsilon_0,\varepsilon_1)\), and \(k_a=\lceil N(1-\varepsilon_a)\rceil\), define
\[
\rho_{M,\varepsilon_a}=\frac{N-k_a}{N}=\frac{\lfloor N\varepsilon_a\rfloor}{N},\qquad a\in\{0,1\}.
\]
Under \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), write their common value as
\[
\rho_{M,\alpha}=\frac{M+1-k}{M+1}=\frac{\lfloor(M+1)\alpha/2\rfloor}{M+1}.
\]
defs.def:rank-inversion: Prespecify \(\boldsymbol\varepsilon=(\varepsilon_0,\varepsilon_1)\) with \(\varepsilon_a>0\) and \(\varepsilon_0+\varepsilon_1\le\alpha\), and set \(k_a=\lceil(M+1)(1-\varepsilon_a)\rceil\). For each \(a\in\{0,1\}\), compute \(S_{i,a}=|Y^{\mathrm{obs}}_{iJ_{i,a}}-\widehat m_a(X_{iJ_{i,a}})|\), take \(r_{a,(k_a)}\), set \(I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}=[\widehat m_a(X_{0T})-r_{a,(k_a)},\widehat m_a(X_{0T})+r_{a,(k_a)}]\cap[-K,K]\), and return \(\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w=I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{1,w}-I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{0,w}\). If \(k_a=M+1\), set \(r_{a,(k_a)}=+\infty\), so \(I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}=[-K,K]\). For proof ranks only, append independent continuous uniforms and use lexicographic ranks; reported closed intervals use weak inclusion. Under \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), define \(k=k_0=k_1\), \(r_{a,(k)}=r_{a,(k_a)}\), \(I_{a,w}=I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}\), and \(\mathcal C_w=\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w\), recovering the symmetric construction.

assumptions.ass:observable-selector-kernel: \(\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\ge0,\quad\sum_{v\in\mathcal V}\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=1,\quad z_v\ne a\Rightarrow\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=0\)
assumptions.ass:selector-equivariance: \(\kappa_{h,a}(\pi_gv\mid gz)=\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\quad\forall g\in\mathcal G,\ h\in\mathcal H,\ a\in\{0,1\}\)
assumptions.ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization: \(\mathcal L((J_{i,a})_{1\le i\le M,\ a\in\{0,1\}}\mid(L_i)_{0\le i\le M},(Z_i)_{0\le i\le M},T,\mathcal T)=\prod_{i=1}^M\prod_{a=0}^1\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\)

snapshot.stmt:
The functional \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)\) in \(\texttt{def:coverage-frontier}\) is the exact finite worst-coverage deficit over the stated class of bounded orbit-constant score maps; no equality with a supremum over all \(P\in\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\) is asserted. Write \(\mathcal O_a=\{O\in\mathcal O:O\subseteq\mathcal U_a\}\), \(m_a=|\mathcal O_a|\), and let \(F_m\) be the number of total preorders on an \(m\)-element labelled set. Its maximum is attained by enumerating the \(F_{m_0}F_{m_1}\) pairs of total preorders; every preorder has a representative in \([0,K]\) with at most \(m_a\) levels. Retain the unchanged coherent \(P_T^C\), full joint calibration-orbit law \(R_w\), local weights \(\omega_C\), exact vectors \(\Pi_C\), union \(\Pi_\star\), and \(G=|\Pi_\star|\) from \(\texttt{lem:coherent-two-finite-rank-frontier-evaluator}\). If \(k\le M\), put
\[
\bar k=\min\{k,M-k+1\},\qquad \mathsf C_{M,k,k}=\sum_{i=0}^{M-1}\min\{\bar k,i+1\}^2.
\]
For every exact key \(\pi\), deterministically select a least-charge evaluator among the rolling charge \(\mathsf C_{M,k,k}\), the multinomial-sum charge \(M+\mathsf T_{M,\bar k,\bar k}\), and the positive-corner charge \(\mathsf C_+(\pi)\), and write
\[
\Pi_\star=\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_+.
\]
For \(\pi\in\Pi_+\), let \(\sigma(\pi)\) attain \(\mathsf C_+(\pi)\). Define
\[
\begin{aligned}
\mathsf B_{\mathrm{sym}}={}&M\mathbf 1\{\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\}+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}}\mathsf C_{M,k,k}\\
&+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}}\left(M+\mathsf T_{M,\bar k,\bar k}\right)+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_+}\mathsf C_+(\pi),
\end{aligned}
\]
where empty sums are zero, and
\[
\mathsf W_{\mathrm{sym}}=\max\!\left(\{0\}\cup\{\bar k^2:\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}\ne\varnothing\}\cup\{M:\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\}\cup\left\{\min\{t_0^{\sigma(\pi)},t_1^{\sigma(\pi)}\}:\pi\in\Pi_+\right\}\right).
\]
A global memo keyed exactly by \((M,k,k,\pi_{00},\pi_{01},\pi_{10})\), with \(\pi_{11}=1-\pi_{00}-\pi_{01}-\pi_{10}\), computes each exact probability once, while differing local weights are applied only to their own preorder objectives. Exact enumeration uses
\[
O\!\left(
|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}(m_0m_1+L)+\sum_{C\in\mathfrak C}\left[L_C\log(1+L_C)+L_C+U_C\right]+\left\{\sum_{C\in\mathfrak C}U_C\right\}\log(1+G)+\mathsf B_{\mathrm{sym}}
\right).
\]
With \(L_{\max}=\max_C L_C\) and \(U_{\max}=\max_C U_C\), the uniform bound replaces the two middle sums by
\[
F_{m_0}F_{m_1}\left[m_0m_1+L+L_{\max}\log(1+L_{\max})+L_{\max}+U_{\max}\{1+\log(1+G)\}\right]
\]
and retains the terminal \(\mathsf B_{\mathrm{sym}}\). Working memory is \(O(m_0m_1+L+U_{\max}+G+\mathsf W_{\mathrm{sym}})\). The term \(M\mathbf 1\{\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\}\) is exactly one shared factorial-preprocessing term. Every exact key is constructed from unchanged \(R_w\), and every local weight from unchanged coherent \(P_T^C\), so the common target, common selector, and cross-arm dependence are preserved. At \(k=M\), \(\bar k=1\) and the rolling per-key charge improves from \(\Theta(M^3)\) to \(\Theta(M)\), with the positive-corner orientation potentially smaller. If \(k=M+1\), evaluation is constant and \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)=0\). Binary attainment is false: in an admissible four-pair instance with \(M=3\) and \(\alpha=1/2\), a four-level ordering has frontier objective \(24957/160000\), whereas every binary score pair has objective at most \(759109/5000000\), a strict gap of \(83189/20000000\). Under disjoint target and calibration orbit support in one arm and \(k\le M\), \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)=1-\alpha\).

defs.def:pair-action: For named pair members define \(O_{b,a}=\{(v_{b,0},a),(v_{b,1},a)\}\) as a two-state pair cell, without thereby asserting that it is a global orbit. If \(\mathcal V_b=\{v_{b,0},v_{b,1}\}\) and the pure local swap subgroup \(\langle s_b\rangle=\{e,s_b\}\) is contained in \(\mathcal G\), then \(s_b(v_{b,r},a)=(v_{b,1-r},a)\) for \(r,a\in\{0,1\}\), \(s_b\) fixes members outside block \(b\), and it relabels complete records by \(\texttt{def:group-action}\). Because every group element preserves blocks and arms and block \(b\) has exactly two members, this sufficient audit implies that \(O_{b,0}\) and \(O_{b,1}\) are global \(\mathcal G\)-orbits. The audit is not necessary: a coupled-swap subgroup may also generate the same orbit. Absent the audit, the pair-cell notation alone makes no global-orbit claim. Pair-specific propositions and examples are invoked after this sufficient audit; a product specialization such as \(S_2^B\) verifies every required pure local swap separately.
defs.def:observable-selector-mixture: For every \(i\in\{1,\ldots,M\}\) and \(a\in\{0,1\}\), set \(\kappa_{w,a}=\sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_h\kappa_{h,a}\), draw \(J_{i,a}\sim\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\), set \(U_{i,a}=(J_{i,a},a)\), and set \(q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,a}=\sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_hq^{\mathrm{obs}}_{h,a}\).
defs.def:coverage-frontier:
For prespecified \(\boldsymbol\varepsilon=(\varepsilon_0,\varepsilon_1)\), define
\[
\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)=\max_{\substack{s_a:\mathcal O\to[0,K]\\a\in\{0,1\}}}\left[(1-\alpha)-\Pr\left\{s_1(O_{T,1})\le Q_{k_1}(s_1(O_{1,1}),\ldots,s_1(O_{M,1}))\ \text{and}\ s_0(O_{T,0})\le Q_{k_0}(s_0(O_{1,0}),\ldots,s_0(O_{M,0}))\right\}\right]_+.
\]
Here \(O_{i,a}\sim q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,a}\), the target orbits are the two lifts of the one common \(T\sim q_{\mathrm{tar}}\), and the calibration draws use \(\texttt{ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization}\), including their within-replicate cross-arm coupling. The functional is restricted to bounded orbit-constant score maps. Under \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), set \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)=\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)\), recovering the symmetric frontier.
defs.def:finite-rank-error:
For \(N=M+1\), prespecified \(\boldsymbol\varepsilon=(\varepsilon_0,\varepsilon_1)\), and \(k_a=\lceil N(1-\varepsilon_a)\rceil\), define
\[
\rho_{M,\varepsilon_a}=\frac{N-k_a}{N}=\frac{\lfloor N\varepsilon_a\rfloor}{N},\qquad a\in\{0,1\}.
\]
Under \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), write their common value as
\[
\rho_{M,\alpha}=\frac{M+1-k}{M+1}=\frac{\lfloor(M+1)\alpha/2\rfloor}{M+1}.
\]
defs.def:rank-inversion: Prespecify \(\boldsymbol\varepsilon=(\varepsilon_0,\varepsilon_1)\) with \(\varepsilon_a>0\) and \(\varepsilon_0+\varepsilon_1\le\alpha\), and set \(k_a=\lceil(M+1)(1-\varepsilon_a)\rceil\). For each \(a\in\{0,1\}\), compute \(S_{i,a}=|Y^{\mathrm{obs}}_{iJ_{i,a}}-\widehat m_a(X_{iJ_{i,a}})|\), take \(r_{a,(k_a)}\), set \(I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}=[\widehat m_a(X_{0T})-r_{a,(k_a)},\widehat m_a(X_{0T})+r_{a,(k_a)}]\cap[-K,K]\), and return \(\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w=I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{1,w}-I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{0,w}\). If \(k_a=M+1\), set \(r_{a,(k_a)}=+\infty\), so \(I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}=[-K,K]\). For proof ranks only, append independent continuous uniforms and use lexicographic ranks; reported closed intervals use weak inclusion. Under \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), define \(k=k_0=k_1\), \(r_{a,(k)}=r_{a,(k_a)}\), \(I_{a,w}=I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}\), and \(\mathcal C_w=\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w\), recovering the symmetric construction.

assumptions.ass:observable-selector-kernel: \(\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\ge0,\quad\sum_{v\in\mathcal V}\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=1,\quad z_v\ne a\Rightarrow\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=0\)
assumptions.ass:selector-equivariance: \(\kappa_{h,a}(\pi_gv\mid gz)=\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\quad\forall g\in\mathcal G,\ h\in\mathcal H,\ a\in\{0,1\}\)
assumptions.ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization: \(\mathcal L((J_{i,a})_{1\le i\le M,\ a\in\{0,1\}}\mid(L_i)_{0\le i\le M},(Z_i)_{0\le i\le M},T,\mathcal T)=\prod_{i=1}^M\prod_{a=0}^1\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\)

snapshot.stmt:
Let \(\mathscr E_0\) and \(\mathscr E_1\) be finite sets, let \(R\) be a probability mass function on \(\mathscr E_0\times\mathscr E_1\), and let \(b_a:\mathscr E_a\to\{0,1\}\). For \((x,y)\in\{0,1\}^2\), define \(\pi_{xy}=\sum_{e_0,e_1}R(e_0,e_1)\mathbf 1\{b_0(e_0)=x,b_1(e_1)=y\}\). Let \(1\le k_0,k_1\le M+1\). If both ranks are at most \(M\), then the recurrence \(d_0(0,0)=1\) and \(d_{i+1}(r,s)=\sum_{x,y\in\{0,1\}}\pi_{xy}d_i(r-x,s-y)\), with out-of-range entries zero and only \(0\le r<k_0\), \(0\le s<k_1\) retained, satisfies \[\sum_{r<k_0,s<k_1}d_M(r,s)=\Pr\{\sum_i b_0(E_{i,0})<k_0,\quad \sum_i b_1(E_{i,1})<k_1\}.\] After \(R\) is tabulated, the four cells and recurrence require \(O(|\mathscr E_0||\mathscr E_1|+Mk_0k_1)\) arithmetic operations and \(O(k_0k_1)\) memory. If exactly one rank equals \(M+1\), the corresponding event is certain and a one-dimensional recurrence for the other count requires \(O(|\mathscr E_0||\mathscr E_1|+Mk_c)\) operations and \(O(k_c)\) memory, where \(k_c\le M\) is the other rank. If both ranks equal \(M+1\), the joint event has probability one and requires no recurrence. For any finite collection \(\Pi\) of four-cell keys, define
\[
\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}=\{\pi\in\Pi:\max_{x,y\in\{0,1\}}\pi_{xy}=1\}.
\]
If \(\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\) and \(\pi_{xy}=1\), then the two terminal counts are \(Mx\) and \(My\) surely, so
\[
D_{M,k_0,k_1}(\pi)=\mathbf 1\{Mx<k_0\ \text{and}\ My<k_1\}.
\]
Thus the simplex-vertex prepass evaluates each such key in \(O(1)\) exact time and \(O(1)\) workspace before any recurrence; only nonvertex two-finite-rank keys require a generic exact evaluator.

defs.def:simplex-vertex-key-prepass:
For a finite set \(\Pi_\star\) of probability mass functions on \(\{0,1\}^2\), set
\[
\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}=\{\pi\in\Pi_\star:\max_{x,y\in\{0,1\}}\pi_{xy}=1\}.
\]
For \(\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\), let \((x_\pi,y_\pi)\) be its unique unit-mass cell and return \(\mathbf 1\{Mx_\pi<k_0,\ My_\pi<k_1\}\). Only keys in \(\Pi_\star\setminus\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\) enter the remaining exact dispatcher.

snapshot.stmt:
Let \(M\ge1\), let \(1\le k_0,k_1\le M\), and let \(\pi=(\pi_{00},\pi_{01},\pi_{10},\pi_{11})\) be a probability mass function on \(\{0,1\}^2\). For \(M\) independent draws with this law, the retained-rectangle probability is
\[
\begin{aligned}
Q_{M,k_0,k_1}(\pi)
={}&\sum_{r=0}^{k_0-1}\sum_{s=0}^{k_1-1}
\sum_{t=\max\{0,r+s-M\}}^{\min\{r,s\}}
\frac{M!}{(M-r-s+t)!(s-t)!(r-t)!t!}\\
&\qquad\qquad{}\times
\pi_{00}^{M-r-s+t}\pi_{01}^{s-t}\pi_{10}^{r-t}\pi_{11}^{t}.
\end{aligned}
\]
An empty inner range contributes zero, \(0^0=1\), and \(0^j=0\) for every positive integer \(j\). If
\[
\mathsf T_{M,k_0,k_1}
=\sum_{r=0}^{k_0-1}\sum_{s=0}^{k_1-1}
\left[\min\{r,s\}-\max\{0,r+s-M\}+1\right]_+,
\]
then \(\mathsf T_{M,k_0,k_1}\le k_0k_1\min\{k_0,k_1\}\). After probability-independent factorials through \(M!\) are precomputed once, one key \(\pi\) is evaluated by rebuilding its four power tables in \(O(M)\) operations and then summing the feasible terms in \(O(\mathsf T_{M,k_0,k_1})\) operations, using \(O(M)\) auxiliary memory. The rolling two-count recurrence is an exact alternative using \(O(Mk_0k_1)\) operations and \(O(k_0k_1)\) auxiliary memory. A time-selecting hybrid therefore uses \(O(\min\{M+\mathsf T_{M,k_0,k_1},Mk_0k_1\})\) operations per key, conditional memory \(O(M)\) on the multinomial branch and \(O(k_0k_1)\) on the recurrence branch, and unconditional auxiliary memory \(O(\max\{M,k_0k_1\})\). For a finite set \(\Pi\) of keys processed sequentially, define
\[
\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}=\{\pi\in\Pi:\max_{x,y\in\{0,1\}}\pi_{xy}=1\}.
\]
If \(\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\) and \(\pi_{xy}=1\), then both counts are deterministic and
\[
D_{M,k_0,k_1}(\pi)=\mathbf 1\{Mx<k_0\ \text{and}\ My<k_1\},
\]
