assumptions.ass:observable-selector-kernel: \(\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\ge0,\quad\sum_{v\in\mathcal V}\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=1,\quad z_v\ne a\Rightarrow\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=0\)
assumptions.ass:selector-equivariance: \(\kappa_{h,a}(\pi_gv\mid gz)=\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\quad\forall g\in\mathcal G,\ h\in\mathcal H,\ a\in\{0,1\}\)
assumptions.ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization: \(\mathcal L((J_{i,a})_{1\le i\le M,\ a\in\{0,1\}}\mid(L_i)_{0\le i\le M},(Z_i)_{0\le i\le M},T,\mathcal T)=\prod_{i=1}^M\prod_{a=0}^1\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\)

snapshot.stmt:
Let \(M\ge1\), let \(1\le k_0,k_1\le M\), and let \(\pi\) be a support-four probability mass function on \(\{0,1\}^2\), written in \(00,01,10,11\) order. Suppose that the exact determinant identity
\[
\pi_{00}\pi_{11}=\pi_{01}\pi_{10}
\]
holds, and define
\[
\theta_0=\pi_{10}+\pi_{11},\qquad
\theta_1=\pi_{01}+\pi_{11},\qquad
\bar k_a=\min\{k_a,M-k_a+1\}.
\]
Then the one-draw probability-generating function factors exactly as
\[
\pi_{00}+\pi_{10}x+\pi_{01}y+\pi_{11}xy
=\{(1-\theta_0)+\theta_0x\}\{(1-\theta_1)+\theta_1y\}.
\]
Consequently, for \(M\) iid draws, the terminal counts \(R\) and \(S\) are independent with \(R\sim\operatorname{Bin}(M,\theta_0)\) and \(S\sim\operatorname{Bin}(M,\theta_1)\), and
\[
D_{M,k_0,k_1}(\pi)=H_{M,k_0}(\theta_0)H_{M,k_1}(\theta_1),
\qquad
H_{M,k}(\theta)=\Pr\{\operatorname{Bin}(M,\theta)<k\}.
\]
The determinant test uses exact arithmetic on the memoized cells. With
\[
\mathsf R_{M,k_0,k_1}=1+2\lceil\log_2M\rceil+\bar k_0+\bar k_1,
\]
the two factors are evaluated in \(O(\mathsf R_{M,k_0,k_1})\) exact arithmetic operations and \(O(1)\) auxiliary workspace.

This prepass has a strict admissible matched-block witness. Let \(M=2n\) with \(n\ge3\), set \(k_0=n\) and \(k_1=n+2\), use two pair blocks with the trivial group and independent fair one-treated--one-control assignment in each block, and use a singleton common selector whose arm-\(0\) kernel chooses the unique control in block \(1\) and whose arm-\(1\) kernel chooses the unique treated member in block \(2\). Put the common target at \(v_{2,0}\). Choose the arm-\(0\) preorder so that exactly one block-\(1\) control singleton orbit is strictly below the target orbit, and choose the arm-\(1\) preorder so that the block-\(2\) mate-treatment singleton orbit is strictly below the target orbit. The resulting strict-prefix key is
\[
\pi=(1/4,1/4,1/4,1/4),
\qquad
D_{2n,n,n+2}(\pi)=H_{2n,n}(1/2)H_{2n,n+2}(1/2).
\]
The levels
\[
\varepsilon_0=\frac{n+1}{2n+1},\qquad
\varepsilon_1=\frac{n-1}{2n+1},\qquad
\alpha=\frac{2n}{2n+1}
\]
are admissible and yield these ranks. On this key the rank-one evaluator uses \(\Theta(M)\) exact arithmetic and \(O(1)\) workspace, whereas every previously eligible bivariate branch uses \(\Omega(M^2)\) arithmetic; the least-charge former branch uses \(\Theta(M^2)\) arithmetic and \(\Theta(M)\) workspace.

defs.def:observable-selector-mixture: For every \(i\in\{1,\ldots,M\}\) and \(a\in\{0,1\}\), set \(\kappa_{w,a}=\sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_h\kappa_{h,a}\), draw \(J_{i,a}\sim\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\), set \(U_{i,a}=(J_{i,a},a)\), and set \(q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,a}=\sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_hq^{\mathrm{obs}}_{h,a}\).
defs.def:coverage-frontier:
For prespecified \(\boldsymbol\varepsilon=(\varepsilon_0,\varepsilon_1)\), define
\[
\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)=\max_{\substack{s_a:\mathcal O\to[0,K]\\a\in\{0,1\}}}\left[(1-\alpha)-\Pr\left\{s_1(O_{T,1})\le Q_{k_1}(s_1(O_{1,1}),\ldots,s_1(O_{M,1}))\ \text{and}\ s_0(O_{T,0})\le Q_{k_0}(s_0(O_{1,0}),\ldots,s_0(O_{M,0}))\right\}\right]_+.
\]
Here \(O_{i,a}\sim q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,a}\), the target orbits are the two lifts of the one common \(T\sim q_{\mathrm{tar}}\), and the calibration draws use \(\texttt{ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization}\), including their within-replicate cross-arm coupling. The functional is restricted to bounded orbit-constant score maps. Under \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), set \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)=\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)\), recovering the symmetric frontier.
defs.def:rank-inversion: Prespecify \(\boldsymbol\varepsilon=(\varepsilon_0,\varepsilon_1)\) with \(\varepsilon_a>0\) and \(\varepsilon_0+\varepsilon_1\le\alpha\), and set \(k_a=\lceil(M+1)(1-\varepsilon_a)\rceil\). For each \(a\in\{0,1\}\), compute \(S_{i,a}=|Y^{\mathrm{obs}}_{iJ_{i,a}}-\widehat m_a(X_{iJ_{i,a}})|\), take \(r_{a,(k_a)}\), set \(I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}=[\widehat m_a(X_{0T})-r_{a,(k_a)},\widehat m_a(X_{0T})+r_{a,(k_a)}]\cap[-K,K]\), and return \(\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w=I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{1,w}-I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{0,w}\). If \(k_a=M+1\), set \(r_{a,(k_a)}=+\infty\), so \(I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}=[-K,K]\). For proof ranks only, append independent continuous uniforms and use lexicographic ranks; reported closed intervals use weak inclusion. Under \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), define \(k=k_0=k_1\), \(r_{a,(k)}=r_{a,(k_a)}\), \(I_{a,w}=I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}\), and \(\mathcal C_w=\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w\), recovering the symmetric construction.

assumptions.ass:observable-selector-kernel: \(\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\ge0,\quad\sum_{v\in\mathcal V}\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=1,\quad z_v\ne a\Rightarrow\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=0\)
assumptions.ass:selector-equivariance: \(\kappa_{h,a}(\pi_gv\mid gz)=\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\quad\forall g\in\mathcal G,\ h\in\mathcal H,\ a\in\{0,1\}\)
assumptions.ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization: \(\mathcal L((J_{i,a})_{1\le i\le M,\ a\in\{0,1\}}\mid(L_i)_{0\le i\le M},(Z_i)_{0\le i\le M},T,\mathcal T)=\prod_{i=1}^M\prod_{a=0}^1\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\)

snapshot.stmt:
Suppose \(k_0,k_1\le M\). Define the arm-orbit sets \(\mathcal O_a\), their sizes \(m_a\), the joint calibration-orbit law
\[
R_w(O_0,O_1)=\sum_z\nu(z)\kappa_{w,0}(O_0\mid z)\kappa_{w,1}(O_1\mid z),
\]
and the coherent common-target law
\[
P_T(O_0,O_1)=\sum_{v\in\mathcal V}q_{\mathrm{tar}}(v)
\mathbf 1\{O_0(v)=O_0,\ O_1(v)=O_1\}.
\]
Let \(L=|\operatorname{supp}(P_T)|\le\min\{|\mathcal V|,m_0m_1\}\). For every preorder pair \(C=(c_0,c_1)\), let \(P_T^C\), \(L_C\), the strict-prefix keys \(\Pi_C\), \(U_C\), and the local weights \(\omega_C\) be the corresponding pushforwards. Then \(U_C\le L_C\le\min\{L,d_0d_1\}\), and
\[
\Pr\{\text{both rank events under }C\}
=\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_C}\omega_C(\pi)D_{M,k_0,k_1}(\pi).
\]
A global memo is keyed exactly by \((M,k_0,k_1,\pi_{00},\pi_{01},\pi_{10})\); the fourth cell is determined by normalization, and local weights never enter the key. Thus the unchanged \(R_w\), unchanged coherent \(P_T^C\), common selector, common target, and within-replicate cross-arm dependence are preserved.

Let \(\Pi_\star=\bigcup_C\Pi_C\), \(G=|\Pi_\star|\), and partition it first into the support-one stratum \(\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\), then the support-two stratum \(\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}\), then the eligible support-three face stratum \(\Pi_{\mathrm{face}}\), then the remaining support-four determinant-zero stratum \(\Pi_{\mathrm{rank1}}\), and finally the residual strata \(\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}},\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}},\Pi_+\) chosen by deterministic least-charge comparison. Put
\[
h_a=M-k_a+1,\qquad \bar k_a=\min\{k_a,h_a\},\qquad
\mathsf C_{M,k_0,k_1}=\sum_{i=0}^{M-1}
\min\{\bar k_0,i+1\}\min\{\bar k_1,i+1\}.
\]
Vertices use the deterministic formula; edges use the exact binomial interval charge \(\mathsf E_M(\pi)\); eligible support-three face keys use \(\mathsf F_M(\pi)\); rank-one support-four keys satisfying \(\pi_{00}\pi_{11}=\pi_{01}\pi_{10}\) use \(\mathsf R_{M,k_0,k_1}=1+2\lceil\log_2M\rceil+\bar k_0+\bar k_1\); and only residual keys enter the rolling, zero-cell-safe multinomial, or positive-corner branches. With prespecified tie-breaking,
\[
\Pi_\star=\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}
\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{face}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}
\Pi_{\mathrm{rank1}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}
\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}
\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_+,
\]
and
\[
\begin{aligned}
\mathsf B={}&|\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}|
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}}\mathsf E_M(\pi)
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{face}}}\mathsf F_M(\pi)
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{rank1}}}\mathsf R_{M,k_0,k_1}
+M\mathbf 1\{\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\}
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}}\mathsf C_{M,k_0,k_1}\\
&+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}}
\left(M+\mathsf T_{M,\bar k_0,\bar k_1}\right)
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_+}\mathsf C_+(\pi).
\end{aligned}
\]
If \(\sigma(\pi)\) minimizes the positive-corner charge, define
\[
\mathsf W=\max\!\left(
\{0\}\cup\{\bar k_0\bar k_1:\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}\ne\varnothing\}
\cup\{M:\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\}
\cup\{\min(t_0^{\sigma(\pi)},t_1^{\sigma(\pi)}):\pi\in\Pi_+\}
\right).
\]
Vertices, edges, face keys, and rank-one keys contribute only \(O(1)\) workspace, and the factorial preprocessing occurs if and only if \(\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\). The face and rank-one strata contribute no nonconstant term to \(\mathsf W\). With \(\mathfrak C\) the \(F_{m_0}F_{m_1}\) preorder pairs, exact enumeration after \(R_w\) is tabulated costs
\[
O\!\left(
|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}(m_0m_1+L)
+\sum_{C\in\mathfrak C}[L_C\log(1+L_C)+L_C+U_C]
+\Bigl(\sum_CU_C\Bigr)\log(1+G)+\mathsf B
\right),
\]
and its uniform version replaces the middle preorder-specific terms by
\[
F_{m_0}F_{m_1}
[m_0m_1+L+L_{\max}\log(1+L_{\max})+L_{\max}
+U_{\max}\{1+\log(1+G)\}].
\]
Working memory is \(O(m_0m_1+L+U_{\max}+G+\mathsf W)\).

There is a strict admissible edge improvement. Let \(M=2n\ge4\), \(k_0=n\), \(k_1=M\), and \(\pi=(1/2,1/2,0,0)\) in \(00,01,10,11\) order. Then
\[
D_{M,n,M}(\pi)=1-2^{-M}.
\]
The edge evaluator uses \(\Theta(\log M)\) exact arithmetic and \(O(1)\) workspace, whereas the least-charge former nonvertex dispatcher uses \(\Theta(M)\) arithmetic. This key is realized by two audited pair blocks under \(S_2^2\), independent fair one-treated--one-control assignment in each block, a singleton selector library whose legal equivariant arm-\(1\) rule selects either block equiprobably and then its unique observed arm-\(1\) member, a target supported in block \(2\), an arm-\(0\) preorder with block \(2\) lowest, and an arm-\(1\) preorder with block \(1\) below block \(2\). Taking
\[
\varepsilon_0=\frac{n+1}{2n+1},\qquad
\varepsilon_1=\frac1{2n+1},\qquad
\alpha=\frac{n+2}{2n+1}
\]
gives the stated ranks and satisfies the error-budget assumptions.

There is also a strict admissible zero-corner face improvement for every \(M\ge2\). Let \(k_0=k_1=M\) and \(\pi=(1/3,1/3,1/3,0)\) in \(00,01,10,11\) order. Then
\[
D_{M,M,M}(\pi)=1-2\cdot3^{-M}.
\]
The \(LL\) face orientation has \(\pi_{11}=0\) and \(t_0+t_1=2M>M\), so the face evaluator costs \(\Theta(\log M)\) exact arithmetic and \(O(1)\) workspace. The rolling, multinomial, and every allowed positive-base coefficient orientation each cost \(\Theta(M)\). The key is realized by two pair blocks with the trivial finite group, a known law uniform on three assignment vectors having one treated member per block, deterministic legal observable selector lookups whose strict-prefix pairs are \(00\), \(01\), and \(10\), a singleton common selector library, and a fixed common target in block \(2\). In each arm, place both block-\(1\) singleton orbits strictly below the target orbit and tie the block-\(2\) singleton orbits with the target. Taking
\[
\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\frac1{M+1},
\qquad
\alpha=\frac2{M+1}
\]
gives \(k_0=k_1=M\) and satisfies the error-budget assumptions.

There is a strict admissible rank-one support-four improvement. Let \(M=2n\) with \(n\ge3\), \(k_0=n\), \(k_1=n+2\), and \(\pi=(1/4,1/4,1/4,1/4)\). Then
\[
D_{2n,n,n+2}(\pi)=H_{2n,n}(1/2)H_{2n,n+2}(1/2).
\]
The rank-one prepass uses \(\Theta(M)\) exact arithmetic and \(O(1)\) workspace, while the least-charge former branch uses \(\Theta(M^2)\) arithmetic and \(\Theta(M)\) workspace. The key is realized by two pair blocks with the trivial group, independent fair one-treated--one-control assignments, a singleton selector choosing block \(1\)'s unique control in arm \(0\) and block \(2\)'s unique treated member in arm \(1\), the common target \(v_{2,0}\), and preorders making one block-\(1\) control singleton orbit and the block-\(2\) mate-treatment singleton orbit strictly below their target orbits. The admissible levels are
\[
\varepsilon_0=\frac{n+1}{2n+1},\qquad
\varepsilon_1=\frac{n-1}{2n+1},\qquad
\alpha=\frac{2n}{2n+1}.
\]

defs.def:observable-selector-mixture: For every \(i\in\{1,\ldots,M\}\) and \(a\in\{0,1\}\), set \(\kappa_{w,a}=\sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_h\kappa_{h,a}\), draw \(J_{i,a}\sim\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\), set \(U_{i,a}=(J_{i,a},a)\), and set \(q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,a}=\sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_hq^{\mathrm{obs}}_{h,a}\).
defs.def:coverage-frontier:
For prespecified \(\boldsymbol\varepsilon=(\varepsilon_0,\varepsilon_1)\), define
\[
\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)=\max_{\substack{s_a:\mathcal O\to[0,K]\\a\in\{0,1\}}}\left[(1-\alpha)-\Pr\left\{s_1(O_{T,1})\le Q_{k_1}(s_1(O_{1,1}),\ldots,s_1(O_{M,1}))\ \text{and}\ s_0(O_{T,0})\le Q_{k_0}(s_0(O_{1,0}),\ldots,s_0(O_{M,0}))\right\}\right]_+.
\]
Here \(O_{i,a}\sim q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,a}\), the target orbits are the two lifts of the one common \(T\sim q_{\mathrm{tar}}\), and the calibration draws use \(\texttt{ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization}\), including their within-replicate cross-arm coupling. The functional is restricted to bounded orbit-constant score maps. Under \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), set \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)=\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)\), recovering the symmetric frontier.
defs.def:rank-inversion: Prespecify \(\boldsymbol\varepsilon=(\varepsilon_0,\varepsilon_1)\) with \(\varepsilon_a>0\) and \(\varepsilon_0+\varepsilon_1\le\alpha\), and set \(k_a=\lceil(M+1)(1-\varepsilon_a)\rceil\). For each \(a\in\{0,1\}\), compute \(S_{i,a}=|Y^{\mathrm{obs}}_{iJ_{i,a}}-\widehat m_a(X_{iJ_{i,a}})|\), take \(r_{a,(k_a)}\), set \(I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}=[\widehat m_a(X_{0T})-r_{a,(k_a)},\widehat m_a(X_{0T})+r_{a,(k_a)}]\cap[-K,K]\), and return \(\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w=I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{1,w}-I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{0,w}\). If \(k_a=M+1\), set \(r_{a,(k_a)}=+\infty\), so \(I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}=[-K,K]\). For proof ranks only, append independent continuous uniforms and use lexicographic ranks; reported closed intervals use weak inclusion. Under \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), define \(k=k_0=k_1\), \(r_{a,(k)}=r_{a,(k_a)}\), \(I_{a,w}=I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}\), and \(\mathcal C_w=\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w\), recovering the symmetric construction.
defs.def:simplex-vertex-key-prepass:
For a finite set \(\Pi_\star\) of probability mass functions on \(\{0,1\}^2\), first set
\[
\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}=\{\pi\in\Pi_\star:|\operatorname{supp}(\pi)|=1\},
\qquad
\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}=\{\pi\in\Pi_\star\setminus\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}:|\operatorname{supp}(\pi)|=2\}.
\]
For \(\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\), let \(u=(x,y)\) be its unique support point and return \(\mathbf 1\{Mx<k_0,\ My<k_1\}\). For \(\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}\), order its two support points \(u=(u_0,u_1)\) and \(v=(v_0,v_1)\) deterministically; both possible orientations may be compared, with the smaller certified charge chosen and lexicographic tie-breaking. Put \(p=\pi_v\in(0,1)\), let \(N\sim\operatorname{Bin}(M,p)\), and define \(c_a=Mu_a\) and \(d_a=v_a-u_a\) for \(a\in\{0,1\}\). Set
\[
L_\pi=\max\!\left(\{0\}\cup\{c_a-k_a+1:d_a=-1\}\right),\qquad
U_\pi=\min\!\left(\{M\}\cup\{k_a-c_a-1:d_a=1\}\right).
\]
If \(d_a=0\) and \(c_a\ge k_a\) for some \(a\), declare \([L_\pi,U_\pi]\) empty. Otherwise return
\[
D_{M,k_0,k_1}(\pi)=\Pr\{L_\pi\le N\le U_\pi\}.
\]
For a nonempty interval define
\[
\eta_\pi=\min\!\left\{
\min\{L_\pi,M-L_\pi\}+U_\pi-L_\pi+1,\ 
L_\pi+M-U_\pi,\ 
\min\{U_\pi+1,M-U_\pi\}+\min\{L_\pi,M-L_\pi+1\}
\right\},
\]
and set \(\eta_\pi=0\) for an empty interval. Direct interval summation, complementary left-and-right tails, and a difference of two shorter binomial tails are three exact alternatives; choose the least charge deterministically and write \(\mathsf E_M(\pi)=1+\lceil\log_2 M\rceil+\eta_\pi\). The edge evaluation uses \(O(\mathsf E_M(\pi))\) exact arithmetic operations and \(O(1)\) auxiliary workspace.

For every remaining support-three key and every corner orientation \(\sigma\in\{L,U\}^2\), let \(\pi^\sigma\) be the transformed four-cell law and let \(t_a^\sigma=k_a\) under \(L\) and \(t_a^\sigma=M-k_a+1\) under \(U\). Define
\[
\Sigma_{\mathrm{face}}(\pi)=
\{\sigma:\pi^\sigma_{11}=0,\ t_0^\sigma+t_1^\sigma>M\},
\qquad
\Pi_{\mathrm{face}}=
\{\pi\in\Pi_\star\setminus(\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\cup\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}):
\Sigma_{\mathrm{face}}(\pi)\ne\varnothing\}.
\]
For \(\sigma\in\Sigma_{\mathrm{face}}(\pi)\), let \(T_a^\sigma\) be the transformed terminal count and set
\[
J_\sigma=
1-\Pr\{T_0^\sigma\ge t_0^\sigma\}
 -\Pr\{T_1^\sigma\ge t_1^\sigma\}.
\]
Reconstruct \(D_{M,k_0,k_1}(\pi)\) from \(J_\sigma\) by the \(LL\), \(LU\), \(UL\), or \(UU\) corner identity. Each required reconstruction marginal is the complement of one of the two tails already evaluated. With
\[
\ell_M(t)=1+\lceil\log_2 M\rceil+\min\{t,M-t+1\},
\qquad
\mathsf F_M(\pi)=1+
\min_{\sigma\in\Sigma_{\mathrm{face}}(\pi)}
\{\ell_M(t_0^\sigma)+\ell_M(t_1^\sigma)\},
\]
choose a minimizing orientation and break ties lexicographically. The face evaluation uses \(O(\mathsf F_M(\pi))\) exact arithmetic operations and \(O(1)\) auxiliary workspace.

For every remaining support-four key, test the exact memoized-cell identity
\[
\pi_{00}\pi_{11}=\pi_{01}\pi_{10}.
\]
Define
\[
\Pi_{\mathrm{rank1}}=
\{\pi\in\Pi_\star\setminus
(\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\cup\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}\cup\Pi_{\mathrm{face}}):
|\operatorname{supp}(\pi)|=4,\ 
\pi_{00}\pi_{11}=\pi_{01}\pi_{10}\}.
\]
For \(\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{rank1}}\), put
\[
\theta_0=\pi_{10}+\pi_{11},\qquad
\theta_1=\pi_{01}+\pi_{11},\qquad
\bar k_a=\min\{k_a,M-k_a+1\},
\]
and evaluate
\[
D_{M,k_0,k_1}(\pi)=
H_{M,k_0}(\theta_0)H_{M,k_1}(\theta_1),
\qquad
H_{M,k}(\theta)=\Pr\{\operatorname{Bin}(M,\theta)<k\}.
\]
With
\[
\mathsf R_{M,k_0,k_1}
=1+2\lceil\log_2M\rceil+\bar k_0+\bar k_1,
\]
the rank-one evaluation uses \(O(\mathsf R_{M,k_0,k_1})\) exact arithmetic operations and \(O(1)\) auxiliary workspace, with deterministic exact testing and no approximation. Only keys in
\(\Pi_\star\setminus(\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\cup\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}\cup\Pi_{\mathrm{face}}\cup\Pi_{\mathrm{rank1}})\)
enter the remaining rolling, multinomial-sum, or positive-corner dispatcher.

assumptions.ass:observable-selector-kernel: \(\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\ge0,\quad\sum_{v\in\mathcal V}\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=1,\quad z_v\ne a\Rightarrow\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=0\)
assumptions.ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization: \(\mathcal L((J_{i,a})_{1\le i\le M,\ a\in\{0,1\}}\mid(L_i)_{0\le i\le M},(Z_i)_{0\le i\le M},T,\mathcal T)=\prod_{i=1}^M\prod_{a=0}^1\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\)

snapshot.stmt:
The functional \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)\) is the exact finite worst-coverage deficit over bounded orbit-constant score maps for ranks \(k_0,k_1\); no equality with a supremum over all \(P\in\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\) is asserted. If both ranks are finite, the maximum is attained by enumerating the \(F_{m_0}F_{m_1}\) total-preorder pairs, every preorder has a representative in \([0,K]\) with at most \(m_a\) levels, and the coherent evaluator in \(\texttt{lem:coherent-two-finite-rank-frontier-evaluator}\) applies.

In that evaluator, retain the unchanged \(R_w\), coherent \(P_T^C\), local \(\omega_C\), exact-key sets \(\Pi_C\), union \(\Pi_\star\), and memo key \((M,k_0,k_1,\pi_{00},\pi_{01},\pi_{10})\). Partition
\[
\Pi_\star=\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}
\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{face}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}
\Pi_{\mathrm{rank1}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}
\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}
\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_+,
\]
where the first two strata have support sizes one and two, \(\Pi_{\mathrm{face}}\) contains eligible support-three zero-corner keys, \(\Pi_{\mathrm{rank1}}\) contains the remaining support-four keys satisfying \(\pi_{00}\pi_{11}=\pi_{01}\pi_{10}\), and the last three contain only residual keys. Vertices use their deterministic counts; edges use the exact affine-binomial interval evaluator; face keys use the incompatibility evaluator in \(\texttt{def:simplex-vertex-key-prepass}\); rank-one keys use the product of two exact strict binomial CDFs with charge \(\mathsf R_{M,k_0,k_1}\); the remaining keys use the prespecified least-charge rolling, multinomial-sum, or positive-corner branch. Put
\[
\begin{aligned}
\mathsf B={}&|\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}|
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}}\mathsf E_M(\pi)
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{face}}}\mathsf F_M(\pi)
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{rank1}}}\mathsf R_{M,k_0,k_1}
+M\mathbf 1\{\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\}
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}}\mathsf C_{M,k_0,k_1}\\
&+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}}
(M+\mathsf T_{M,\bar k_0,\bar k_1})
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_+}\mathsf C_+(\pi),
\end{aligned}
\]
and let \(\mathsf W\) be the maximum of \(0\), \(\bar k_0\bar k_1\) when the rolling branch is nonempty, \(M\) when the multinomial branch is nonempty, and the selected positive-corner rolling widths. There is no additional edge, face, or rank-one term in \(\mathsf W\). The exact enumeration and uniform time bounds are
\[
O\!\left(
|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}(m_0m_1+L)
+\sum_C[L_C\log(1+L_C)+L_C+U_C]
+\Bigl(\sum_CU_C\Bigr)\log(1+G)+\mathsf B
\right)
\]
and
\[
O\!\left(
|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}
[m_0m_1+L+L_{\max}\log(1+L_{\max})+L_{\max}
+U_{\max}\{1+\log(1+G)\}]
+\mathsf B
\right),
\]
with memory \(O(m_0m_1+L+U_{\max}+G+\mathsf W)\). The factorial table is built only when a remaining nonface support-at-least-three key is assigned to \(\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\); the face and rank-one strata neither trigger that table nor add nonconstant workspace.

For \(M=2n\ge4\), \(k_0=n\), \(k_1=M\), and \(\pi=(1/2,1/2,0,0)\),
\[
D_{M,n,M}(\pi)=1-2^{-M};
\]
the edge prepass costs \(\Theta(\log M)\) exact arithmetic and \(O(1)\) workspace, while the former least-charge nonvertex dispatcher costs \(\Theta(M)\). The audited two-pair realization, selector, target, preorders, and levels
\[
\varepsilon_0=\frac{n+1}{2n+1},\quad
\varepsilon_1=\frac1{2n+1},\quad
\alpha=\frac{n+2}{2n+1}
\]
are those stated in \(\texttt{lem:coherent-two-finite-rank-frontier-evaluator}\).

If exactly one rank \(k_b=M+1\), the boundary-arm event is certain and, for \(c=1-b\), the exact rank-event probability is
\[
\sum_{O_t\in\mathcal O_c}p_{c,O_t}
\Pr\!\left\{\operatorname{Bin}\!\left(M,
\sum_{O:s_c(O)<s_c(O_t)}q_{c,O}\right)<k_c\right\}.
\]
Enumeration uses only the \(F_{m_c}\) nonboundary preorders and costs
\[
O\!\left(F_{m_c}m_c[1+\log M+\min\{k_c,M-k_c+1\}]\right)
\]
arithmetic operations and \(O(1)\) auxiliary CDF memory after the two marginal vectors are tabulated. If both ranks equal \(M+1\), the frontier is zero. Binary attainment remains false: at \(M=3\), \(\alpha=1/2\), and \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=1/4\), a four-level objective is \(24957/160000\), every binary pair is at most \(759109/5000000\), and the gap is \(83189/20000000\). If target and calibration orbit supports are disjoint in a finite-rank arm, the frontier is \(1-\alpha\). On the equal-split diagonal this theorem yields \(\texttt{thm:sharp-orbit-coverage-frontier}\). The computational prepasses alter no active-arm, pair-audit, empty-library, ordinary-rank, or weighted-orbit semantics.

For every \(M\ge2\), the admissible face key \(k_0=k_1=M\) and \(\pi=(1/3,1/3,1/3,0)\) satisfies
\[
D_{M,M,M}(\pi)=1-2\cdot3^{-M}.
\]
The zero-corner face prepass costs \(\Theta(\log M)\) exact arithmetic and \(O(1)\) workspace, whereas rolling, multinomial summation, and every allowed positive-base coefficient orientation cost \(\Theta(M)\). The trivial-group two-pair assignment support, deterministic legal selector lookup, fixed block-\(2\) target, singleton-orbit preorders, and levels
\[
\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\frac1{M+1},
\qquad
\alpha=\frac2{M+1}
\]
are audited in \(\texttt{lem:coherent-two-finite-rank-frontier-evaluator}\).

For \(M=2n\) with \(n\ge3\), \(k_0=n\), \(k_1=n+2\), and \(\pi=(1/4,1/4,1/4,1/4)\), the exact determinant test selects the rank-one prepass and
\[
D_{2n,n,n+2}(\pi)=H_{2n,n}(1/2)H_{2n,n+2}(1/2).
\]
The new branch costs \(\Theta(M)\) exact arithmetic and \(O(1)\) workspace, while the least-charge former branch costs \(\Theta(M^2)\) arithmetic and \(\Theta(M)\) workspace. The legal two-block construction and levels
\[
\varepsilon_0=\frac{n+1}{2n+1},\qquad
\varepsilon_1=\frac{n-1}{2n+1},\qquad
\alpha=\frac{2n}{2n+1}
\]
are audited in the coherent evaluator.

defs.def:pair-action: For named pair members define \(O_{b,a}=\{(v_{b,0},a),(v_{b,1},a)\}\) as a two-state pair cell, without thereby asserting that it is a global orbit. If \(\mathcal V_b=\{v_{b,0},v_{b,1}\}\) and the pure local swap subgroup \(\langle s_b\rangle=\{e,s_b\}\) is contained in \(\mathcal G\), then \(s_b(v_{b,r},a)=(v_{b,1-r},a)\) for \(r,a\in\{0,1\}\), \(s_b\) fixes members outside block \(b\), and it relabels complete records by \(\texttt{def:group-action}\). Because every group element preserves blocks and arms and block \(b\) has exactly two members, this sufficient audit implies that \(O_{b,0}\) and \(O_{b,1}\) are global \(\mathcal G\)-orbits. The audit is not necessary: a coupled-swap subgroup may also generate the same orbit. Absent the audit, the pair-cell notation alone makes no global-orbit claim. Pair-specific propositions and examples are invoked after this sufficient audit; a product specialization such as \(S_2^B\) verifies every required pure local swap separately.
defs.def:observable-selector-mixture: For every \(i\in\{1,\ldots,M\}\) and \(a\in\{0,1\}\), set \(\kappa_{w,a}=\sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_h\kappa_{h,a}\), draw \(J_{i,a}\sim\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\), set \(U_{i,a}=(J_{i,a},a)\), and set \(q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,a}=\sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_hq^{\mathrm{obs}}_{h,a}\).
defs.def:coverage-frontier:
For prespecified \(\boldsymbol\varepsilon=(\varepsilon_0,\varepsilon_1)\), define
\[
\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)=\max_{\substack{s_a:\mathcal O\to[0,K]\\a\in\{0,1\}}}\left[(1-\alpha)-\Pr\left\{s_1(O_{T,1})\le Q_{k_1}(s_1(O_{1,1}),\ldots,s_1(O_{M,1}))\ \text{and}\ s_0(O_{T,0})\le Q_{k_0}(s_0(O_{1,0}),\ldots,s_0(O_{M,0}))\right\}\right]_+.
\]
Here \(O_{i,a}\sim q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,a}\), the target orbits are the two lifts of the one common \(T\sim q_{\mathrm{tar}}\), and the calibration draws use \(\texttt{ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization}\), including their within-replicate cross-arm coupling. The functional is restricted to bounded orbit-constant score maps. Under \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), set \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)=\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)\), recovering the symmetric frontier.
defs.def:finite-rank-error:
For \(N=M+1\), prespecified \(\boldsymbol\varepsilon=(\varepsilon_0,\varepsilon_1)\), and \(k_a=\lceil N(1-\varepsilon_a)\rceil\), define
\[
\rho_{M,\varepsilon_a}=\frac{N-k_a}{N}=\frac{\lfloor N\varepsilon_a\rfloor}{N},\qquad a\in\{0,1\}.
\]
Under \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), write their common value as
\[
\rho_{M,\alpha}=\frac{M+1-k}{M+1}=\frac{\lfloor(M+1)\alpha/2\rfloor}{M+1}.
\]
defs.def:rank-inversion: Prespecify \(\boldsymbol\varepsilon=(\varepsilon_0,\varepsilon_1)\) with \(\varepsilon_a>0\) and \(\varepsilon_0+\varepsilon_1\le\alpha\), and set \(k_a=\lceil(M+1)(1-\varepsilon_a)\rceil\). For each \(a\in\{0,1\}\), compute \(S_{i,a}=|Y^{\mathrm{obs}}_{iJ_{i,a}}-\widehat m_a(X_{iJ_{i,a}})|\), take \(r_{a,(k_a)}\), set \(I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}=[\widehat m_a(X_{0T})-r_{a,(k_a)},\widehat m_a(X_{0T})+r_{a,(k_a)}]\cap[-K,K]\), and return \(\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w=I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{1,w}-I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{0,w}\). If \(k_a=M+1\), set \(r_{a,(k_a)}=+\infty\), so \(I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}=[-K,K]\). For proof ranks only, append independent continuous uniforms and use lexicographic ranks; reported closed intervals use weak inclusion. Under \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), define \(k=k_0=k_1\), \(r_{a,(k)}=r_{a,(k_a)}\), \(I_{a,w}=I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}\), and \(\mathcal C_w=\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w\), recovering the symmetric construction.
defs.def:simplex-vertex-key-prepass:
For a finite set \(\Pi_\star\) of probability mass functions on \(\{0,1\}^2\), first set
\[
\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}=\{\pi\in\Pi_\star:|\operatorname{supp}(\pi)|=1\},
\qquad
\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}=\{\pi\in\Pi_\star\setminus\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}:|\operatorname{supp}(\pi)|=2\}.
\]
For \(\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\), let \(u=(x,y)\) be its unique support point and return \(\mathbf 1\{Mx<k_0,\ My<k_1\}\). For \(\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}\), order its two support points \(u=(u_0,u_1)\) and \(v=(v_0,v_1)\) deterministically; both possible orientations may be compared, with the smaller certified charge chosen and lexicographic tie-breaking. Put \(p=\pi_v\in(0,1)\), let \(N\sim\operatorname{Bin}(M,p)\), and define \(c_a=Mu_a\) and \(d_a=v_a-u_a\) for \(a\in\{0,1\}\). Set
\[
L_\pi=\max\!\left(\{0\}\cup\{c_a-k_a+1:d_a=-1\}\right),\qquad
U_\pi=\min\!\left(\{M\}\cup\{k_a-c_a-1:d_a=1\}\right).
\]
If \(d_a=0\) and \(c_a\ge k_a\) for some \(a\), declare \([L_\pi,U_\pi]\) empty. Otherwise return
\[
D_{M,k_0,k_1}(\pi)=\Pr\{L_\pi\le N\le U_\pi\}.
\]
For a nonempty interval define
\[
\eta_\pi=\min\!\left\{
\min\{L_\pi,M-L_\pi\}+U_\pi-L_\pi+1,\ 
L_\pi+M-U_\pi,\ 
\min\{U_\pi+1,M-U_\pi\}+\min\{L_\pi,M-L_\pi+1\}
\right\},
\]
and set \(\eta_\pi=0\) for an empty interval. Direct interval summation, complementary left-and-right tails, and a difference of two shorter binomial tails are three exact alternatives; choose the least charge deterministically and write \(\mathsf E_M(\pi)=1+\lceil\log_2 M\rceil+\eta_\pi\). The edge evaluation uses \(O(\mathsf E_M(\pi))\) exact arithmetic operations and \(O(1)\) auxiliary workspace.

For every remaining support-three key and every corner orientation \(\sigma\in\{L,U\}^2\), let \(\pi^\sigma\) be the transformed four-cell law and let \(t_a^\sigma=k_a\) under \(L\) and \(t_a^\sigma=M-k_a+1\) under \(U\). Define
\[
\Sigma_{\mathrm{face}}(\pi)=
\{\sigma:\pi^\sigma_{11}=0,\ t_0^\sigma+t_1^\sigma>M\},
\qquad
\Pi_{\mathrm{face}}=
\{\pi\in\Pi_\star\setminus(\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\cup\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}):
\Sigma_{\mathrm{face}}(\pi)\ne\varnothing\}.
\]
For \(\sigma\in\Sigma_{\mathrm{face}}(\pi)\), let \(T_a^\sigma\) be the transformed terminal count and set
\[
J_\sigma=
1-\Pr\{T_0^\sigma\ge t_0^\sigma\}
 -\Pr\{T_1^\sigma\ge t_1^\sigma\}.
\]
Reconstruct \(D_{M,k_0,k_1}(\pi)\) from \(J_\sigma\) by the \(LL\), \(LU\), \(UL\), or \(UU\) corner identity. Each required reconstruction marginal is the complement of one of the two tails already evaluated. With
\[
\ell_M(t)=1+\lceil\log_2 M\rceil+\min\{t,M-t+1\},
\qquad
\mathsf F_M(\pi)=1+
\min_{\sigma\in\Sigma_{\mathrm{face}}(\pi)}
\{\ell_M(t_0^\sigma)+\ell_M(t_1^\sigma)\},
\]
choose a minimizing orientation and break ties lexicographically. The face evaluation uses \(O(\mathsf F_M(\pi))\) exact arithmetic operations and \(O(1)\) auxiliary workspace.

For every remaining support-four key, test the exact memoized-cell identity
\[
\pi_{00}\pi_{11}=\pi_{01}\pi_{10}.
\]
Define
\[
\Pi_{\mathrm{rank1}}=
\{\pi\in\Pi_\star\setminus
(\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\cup\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}\cup\Pi_{\mathrm{face}}):
|\operatorname{supp}(\pi)|=4,\ 
\pi_{00}\pi_{11}=\pi_{01}\pi_{10}\}.
\]
For \(\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{rank1}}\), put
\[
\theta_0=\pi_{10}+\pi_{11},\qquad
\theta_1=\pi_{01}+\pi_{11},\qquad
\bar k_a=\min\{k_a,M-k_a+1\},
\]
and evaluate
\[
D_{M,k_0,k_1}(\pi)=
H_{M,k_0}(\theta_0)H_{M,k_1}(\theta_1),
\qquad
H_{M,k}(\theta)=\Pr\{\operatorname{Bin}(M,\theta)<k\}.
\]
With
\[
\mathsf R_{M,k_0,k_1}
=1+2\lceil\log_2M\rceil+\bar k_0+\bar k_1,
\]
the rank-one evaluation uses \(O(\mathsf R_{M,k_0,k_1})\) exact arithmetic operations and \(O(1)\) auxiliary workspace, with deterministic exact testing and no approximation. Only keys in
\(\Pi_\star\setminus(\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\cup\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}\cup\Pi_{\mathrm{face}}\cup\Pi_{\mathrm{rank1}})\)
enter the remaining rolling, multinomial-sum, or positive-corner dispatcher.

assumptions.ass:observable-selector-kernel: \(\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\ge0,\quad\sum_{v\in\mathcal V}\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=1,\quad z_v\ne a\Rightarrow\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=0\)
assumptions.ass:selector-equivariance: \(\kappa_{h,a}(\pi_gv\mid gz)=\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\quad\forall g\in\mathcal G,\ h\in\mathcal H,\ a\in\{0,1\}\)
assumptions.ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization: \(\mathcal L((J_{i,a})_{1\le i\le M,\ a\in\{0,1\}}\mid(L_i)_{0\le i\le M},(Z_i)_{0\le i\le M},T,\mathcal T)=\prod_{i=1}^M\prod_{a=0}^1\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\)

snapshot.stmt:
The functional \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)\) is the exact finite worst-coverage deficit over bounded orbit-constant score maps, with no asserted equality to a supremum over all invariant latent laws. It is the equal-split diagonal of \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)\). If \(k\le M\), its maximum is attained by the \(F_{m_0}F_{m_1}\) total-preorder pairs, with at most \(m_a\) score levels in arm \(a\), while retaining the unchanged \(R_w\), coherent \(P_T^C\), local weights, and exact memo keys.

Partition
\[
\Pi_\star=\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}
\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{face}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}
\Pi_{\mathrm{rank1}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}
\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}
\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_+,
\]
where \(\Pi_{\mathrm{face}}\) contains eligible support-three zero-corner keys, \(\Pi_{\mathrm{rank1}}\) contains remaining support-four determinant-zero keys, and the last three strata contain only residual keys. Put \(\bar k=\min\{k,M-k+1\}\) and
\[
\mathsf C_{M,k,k}=\sum_{i=0}^{M-1}\min\{\bar k,i+1\}^2.
\]
Vertices use deterministic counts, edges use the exact affine-binomial interval evaluator, face keys use the incompatibility evaluator, rank-one keys use the product-binomial evaluator, and only the remaining keys enter the three-way dispatcher. Define
\[
\begin{aligned}
\mathsf B_{\mathrm{sym}}={}&|\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}|
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}}\mathsf E_M(\pi)
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{face}}}\mathsf F_M(\pi)
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{rank1}}}\mathsf R_{M,k,k}
+M\mathbf 1\{\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\}
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}}\mathsf C_{M,k,k}\\
&+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}}
(M+\mathsf T_{M,\bar k,\bar k})
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_+}\mathsf C_+(\pi).
\end{aligned}
\]
Let \(\mathsf W_{\mathrm{sym}}\) be the maximum of \(0\), \(\bar k^2\) when rolling is used, \(M\) when the multinomial branch is used, and the selected positive-corner rolling widths. Vertices, edges, face keys, and rank-one keys add no nonconstant workspace. Exact enumeration costs
\[
O\!\left(
|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}(m_0m_1+L)
+\sum_C[L_C\log(1+L_C)+L_C+U_C]
+\Bigl(\sum_CU_C\Bigr)\log(1+G)+\mathsf B_{\mathrm{sym}}
\right),
\]
its uniform version replaces the preorder-specific terms by
\[
F_{m_0}F_{m_1}
[m_0m_1+L+L_{\max}\log(1+L_{\max})+L_{\max}
+U_{\max}\{1+\log(1+G)\}],
\]
and memory is \(O(m_0m_1+L+U_{\max}+G+\mathsf W_{\mathrm{sym}})\). Shared factorial preprocessing occurs if and only if a residual key enters \(\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\); rank-one keys do not trigger it.

For every \(M\ge2\), a diagonal strict edge witness is obtained from the audited two-pair realization by taking \(\alpha=2/(M+1)\), hence \(k=M\), and the same key \(\pi=(1/2,1/2,0,0)\): then \(D_{M,M,M}(\pi)=1-2^{-M}\), the edge branch costs \(\Theta(\log M)\), and the former dispatcher costs \(\Theta(M)\). The asymmetric audit witness \(M=2n\), \(k_0=n\), \(k_1=M\), together with its exact levels and legality checks, remains the strict off-diagonal certificate for the common evaluator. If \(k=M+1\), evaluation is constant and \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)=0\). Binary attainment is false with the exact four-level and binary values \(24957/160000\) and \(759109/5000000\) at \(M=3\), \(\alpha=1/2\). Under disjoint target and calibration orbit support in one arm and \(k\le M\), \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)=1-\alpha\). The prepasses change no common-target, active-arm, pair-audit, empty-library, ordinary-rank, or weighted-orbit semantics.

For every \(M\ge2\), the diagonal face key \(\pi=(1/3,1/3,1/3,0)\) at \(\alpha=2/(M+1)\), hence \(k=M\), satisfies
\[
D_{M,M,M}(\pi)=1-2\cdot3^{-M}.
\]
It has the trivial-group two-pair realization audited in \(\texttt{lem:coherent-two-finite-rank-frontier-evaluator}\). The face prepass costs \(\Theta(\log M)\) exact arithmetic and \(O(1)\) workspace, whereas each former branch costs \(\Theta(M)\).

The admissible off-diagonal audit \(M=2n\), \(n\ge3\), \(k_0=n\), \(k_1=n+2\), and \(\pi=(1/4,1/4,1/4,1/4)\) remains a strict certificate for the common evaluator: the rank-one branch costs \(\Theta(M)\) time and \(O(1)\) workspace, whereas the former least-charge branch costs \(\Theta(M^2)\) time and \(\Theta(M)\) workspace. The diagonal partition uses the same exact determinant test and the charge \(\mathsf R_{M,k,k}=1+2\lceil\log_2M\rceil+2\bar k\).

Because every group element preserves blocks and arms and block \(b\) has exactly two members, this sufficient audit implies that \(O_{b,0}\) and \(O_{b,1}\) are global \(\mathcal G\)-orbits. The audit is not necessary: a coupled-swap subgroup may also generate the same orbit. Absent the audit, the pair-cell notation alone makes no global-orbit claim. Pair-specific propositions and examples are invoked after this sufficient audit; a product specialization such as \(S_2^B\) verifies every required pure local swap separately.

\]

\[
\eta_\pi=\min\!\left\{
\min\{L_\pi,M-L_\pi\}+U_\pi-L_\pi+1,\ 
L_\pi+M-U_\pi,\

\[
\Sigma_{\mathrm{face}}(\pi)=
\{\sigma:\pi^\sigma_{11}=0,\ t_0^\sigma+t_1^\sigma>M\},
\qquad
\Pi_{\mathrm{face}}=
\{\pi\in\Pi_\star\setminus(\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\cup\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}):
\Sigma_{\mathrm{face}}(\pi)\ne\varnothing\}.
\]
For \(\sigma\in\Sigma_{\mathrm{face}}(\pi)\), let \(T_a^\sigma\) be the transformed terminal count and set
\[

1-\Pr\{T_0^\sigma\ge t_0^\sigma\}
 -\Pr\{T_1^\sigma\ge t_1^\sigma\}.
\]
Reconstruct \(D_{M,k_0,k_1}(\pi)\) from \(J_\sigma\) by the \(LL\), \(LU\), \(UL\), or \(UU\) corner identity. Each required reconstruction marginal is the complement of one of the two tails already evaluated. With
\[
\ell_M(t)=1+\lceil\log_2 M\rceil+\min\{t,M-t+1\},
\qquad
\mathsf F_M(\pi)=1+
\min_{\sigma\in\Sigma_{\mathrm{face}}(\pi)}
\{\ell_M(t_0^\sigma)+\ell_M(t_1^\sigma)\},
\]
choose a minimizing orientation and break ties lexicographically. The face evaluation uses \(O(\mathsf F_M(\pi))\) exact arithmetic operations and \(O(1)\) auxiliary workspace.

For every remaining support-four key, test the exact memoized-cell identity
\[
\pi_{00}\pi_{11}=\pi_{01}\pi_{10}.
\]
Define
\[
\Pi_{\mathrm{rank1}}=
\{\pi\in\Pi_\star\setminus
(\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\cup\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}\cup\Pi_{\mathrm{face}}):
|\operatorname{supp}(\pi)|=4,\ 
\pi_{00}\pi_{11}=\pi_{01}\pi_{10}\}.
\]
For \(\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{rank1}}\), put
\[
\theta_0=\pi_{10}+\pi_{11},\qquad
\theta_1=\pi_{01}+\pi_{11},\qquad
\bar k_a=\min\{k_a,M-k_a+1\},
\]
and evaluate
\[
D_{M,k_0,k_1}(\pi)=
H_{M,k_0}(\theta_0)H_{M,k_1}(\theta_1),
\qquad
H_{M,k}(\theta)=\Pr\{\operatorname{Bin}(M,\theta)<k\}.
\]
With
\[
\mathsf R_{M,k_0,k_1}
=1+2\lceil\log_2M\rceil+\bar k_0+\bar k_1,
\]
the rank-one evaluation uses \(O(\mathsf R_{M,k_0,k_1})\) exact arithmetic operations and \(O(1)\) auxiliary workspace, with deterministic exact testing and no approximation. Only keys in
\(\Pi_\star\setminus(\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\cup\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}\cup\Pi_{\mathrm{face}}\cup\Pi_{\mathrm{rank1}})\)
enter the remaining rolling, multinomial-sum, or positive-corner dispatcher.

assumptions.ass:observable-selector-kernel: \(\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\ge0,\quad\sum_{v\in\mathcal V}\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=1,\quad z_v\ne a\Rightarrow\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=0\)
assumptions.ass:selector-equivariance: \(\kappa_{h,a}(\pi_gv\mid gz)=\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\quad\forall g\in\mathcal G,\ h\in\mathcal H,\ a\in\{0,1\}\)
assumptions.ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization: \(\mathcal L((J_{i,a})_{1\le i\le M,\ a\in\{0,1\}}\mid(L_i)_{0\le i\le M},(Z_i)_{0\le i\le M},T,\mathcal T)=\prod_{i=1}^M\prod_{a=0}^1\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\)

snapshot.stmt: Conditional on \(\mathcal T\), for every arm \(a\) and every fixed measurable predictor \(\widehat m_a\), there are probability laws \(F_{a,O}\) on \([0,\infty)\), indexed by \(O\in\mathcal O\) with \(O\subseteq\mathcal U_a\), such that for every Borel set \(C\), \[\Pr(O_{i,a}=O,S_{i,a}\in C\mid\mathcal T)=q_{a,O}F_{a,O}(C),\qquad\Pr(O_{T,a}=O,S_{0,a}\in C\mid\mathcal T)=p_{a,O}F_{a,O}(C).\] The \(M\) calibration orbit-score pairs are iid and independent of the target pair, armwise.

defs.def:observable-selector-mixture: For every \(i\in\{1,\ldots,M\}\) and \(a\in\{0,1\}\), set \(\kappa_{w,a}=\sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_h\kappa_{h,a}\), draw \(J_{i,a}\sim\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\), set \(U_{i,a}=(J_{i,a},a)\), and set \(q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,a}=\sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_hq^{\mathrm{obs}}_{h,a}\).

assumptions.ass:observable-selector-kernel: \(\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\ge0,\quad\sum_{v\in\mathcal V}\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=1,\quad z_v\ne a\Rightarrow\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=0\)
assumptions.ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization: \(\mathcal L((J_{i,a})_{1\le i\le M,\ a\in\{0,1\}}\mid(L_i)_{0\le i\le M},(Z_i)_{0\le i\le M},T,\mathcal T)=\prod_{i=1}^M\prod_{a=0}^1\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\)

snapshot.stmt: For every fixed or prespecified \(w\), conditional on \(\mathcal T\), \[\Pr_P(\tau_T\in\mathcal C_w\mid\mathcal T)\ge\max\{0,1-2\rho_{M,\alpha}-\Delta_0(w)-\Delta_1(w)\}\] for every \(P\in\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\) and arbitrary fixed measurable trained predictor maps. Here \(\rho_{M,\alpha}=j/(M+1)\) on each staircase interval \(\alpha\in[2j/(M+1),2(j+1)/(M+1))\), so the elbows occur at \(\alpha=2j/(M+1)\). Consequently \(|\delta_{a,O}(w)|\le\Delta_a(w)\) for every \(a\in\{0,1\}\) and \(O\in\mathcal O\).

defs.def:finite-rank-error:
For \(N=M+1\), prespecified \(\boldsymbol\varepsilon=(\varepsilon_0,\varepsilon_1)\), and \(k_a=\lceil N(1-\varepsilon_a)\rceil\), define
\[
\rho_{M,\varepsilon_a}=\frac{N-k_a}{N}=\frac{\lfloor N\varepsilon_a\rfloor}{N},\qquad a\in\{0,1\}.
\]
Under \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), write their common value as
\[
\rho_{M,\alpha}=\frac{M+1-k}{M+1}=\frac{\lfloor(M+1)\alpha/2\rfloor}{M+1}.
\]
defs.def:rank-inversion: Prespecify \(\boldsymbol\varepsilon=(\varepsilon_0,\varepsilon_1)\) with \(\varepsilon_a>0\) and \(\varepsilon_0+\varepsilon_1\le\alpha\), and set \(k_a=\lceil(M+1)(1-\varepsilon_a)\rceil\). For each \(a\in\{0,1\}\), compute \(S_{i,a}=|Y^{\mathrm{obs}}_{iJ_{i,a}}-\widehat m_a(X_{iJ_{i,a}})|\), take \(r_{a,(k_a)}\), set \(I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}=[\widehat m_a(X_{0T})-r_{a,(k_a)},\widehat m_a(X_{0T})+r_{a,(k_a)}]\cap[-K,K]\), and return \(\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w=I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{1,w}-I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{0,w}\). If \(k_a=M+1\), set \(r_{a,(k_a)}=+\infty\), so \(I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}=[-K,K]\). For proof ranks only, append independent continuous uniforms and use lexicographic ranks; reported closed intervals use weak inclusion. Under \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), define \(k=k_0=k_1\), \(r_{a,(k)}=r_{a,(k_a)}\), \(I_{a,w}=I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}\), and \(\mathcal C_w=\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w\), recovering the symmetric construction.

assumptions.ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization: \(\mathcal L((J_{i,a})_{1\le i\le M,\ a\in\{0,1\}}\mid(L_i)_{0\le i\le M},(Z_i)_{0\le i\le M},T,\mathcal T)=\prod_{i=1}^M\prod_{a=0}^1\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\)

snapshot.stmt:
For every fixed or prespecified \(w\), conditional on \(\mathcal T\), the asymmetric ordinary rank construction satisfies
\[
\Pr_P\!\left\{\tau_T\in\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w\mid\mathcal T\right\}
\ge
\max\!\left\{0,1-\rho_{M,\varepsilon_0}-\rho_{M,\varepsilon_1}-\Delta_0(w)-\Delta_1(w)\right\}
\]
for every \(P\in\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\) and arbitrary fixed measurable trained predictor maps. The coordinate elbows are \(\varepsilon_a=j/(M+1)\), and \(|\delta_{a,O}(w)|\le\Delta_a(w)\) for every arm and orbit. On the diagonal \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), this becomes the symmetric bound \(\max\{0,1-2\rho_{M,\alpha}-\Delta_0(w)-\Delta_1(w)\}\).

snapshot.stmt: Fix an arm \(a\) and suppose every target-supported arm-\(a\) orbit has zero \(q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,a}\) mass. Under \(\texttt{def:disjoint-orbit-law}\), the latent law belongs to \(\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\), every selected calibration score in both arms is zero, and the common target effect is \(K\) if \(a=1\) and \(-K\) if \(a=0\). If \(k_0\le M\) and \(k_1\le M\), then \(I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{0,w}=I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{1,w}=\{0\}\), \(\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w=\{0\}\), and coverage is zero. If exactly one \(k_c=M+1\), then the boundary arm interval is \([-K,K]\), the other is \(\{0\}\), \(\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w=[-K,K]\), and this witness has coverage one. If both \(k_0=k_1=M+1\), then \(\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w=[-2K,2K]\) and this witness again has coverage one. Moreover \(k_c\le M\) is equivalent to \(\varepsilon_c\ge1/(M+1)\). The symmetric choice recovers \(\texttt{prop:disjoint-orbit-witness}\).

defs.def:disjoint-orbit-law: Fix \(a\in\{0,1\}\). Set \(\widehat m_0=\widehat m_1=0\), set \(Y_v(a)=K\) on target-supported arm-\(a\) orbits, set \(Y_v(a)=0\) on every state with positive calibration mass and on remaining states, set \(Y_v(1-a)=0\), and randomize the orbit-constant prototype by a uniform \(g\in\mathcal G\) before drawing \(Z_i\sim\nu\).
defs.def:invariant-laws: \(\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}=\{P:P\circ(gL_i)^{-1}=P\quad\forall g\in\mathcal G,\quad\max_{v\in\mathcal V,\ a\in\{0,1\}}|Y_v(a)|\le K\}\).
defs.def:observable-selector-mixture: For every \(i\in\{1,\ldots,M\}\) and \(a\in\{0,1\}\), set \(\kappa_{w,a}=\sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_h\kappa_{h,a}\), draw \(J_{i,a}\sim\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\), set \(U_{i,a}=(J_{i,a},a)\), and set \(q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,a}=\sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_hq^{\mathrm{obs}}_{h,a}\).

For each \(a\in\{0,1\}\), compute \(S_{i,a}=|Y^{\mathrm{obs}}_{iJ_{i,a}}-\widehat m_a(X_{iJ_{i,a}})|\), take \(r_{a,(k_a)}\), set \(I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}=[\widehat m_a(X_{0T})-r_{a,(k_a)},\widehat m_a(X_{0T})+r_{a,(k_a)}]\cap[-K,K]\), and return \(\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w=I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{1,w}-I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{0,w}\). If \(k_a=M+1\), set \(r_{a,(k_a)}=+\infty\), so \(I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}=[-K,K]\). For proof ranks only, append independent continuous uniforms and use lexicographic ranks; reported closed intervals use weak inclusion. Under \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), define \(k=k_0=k_1\), \(r_{a,(k)}=r_{a,(k_a)}\), \(I_{a,w}=I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}\), and \(\mathcal C_w=\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w\), recovering the symmetric construction.

assumptions.ass:observable-selector-kernel: \(\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\ge0,\quad\sum_{v\in\mathcal V}\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=1,\quad z_v\ne a\Rightarrow\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=0\)
assumptions.ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization: \(\mathcal L((J_{i,a})_{1\le i\le M,\ a\in\{0,1\}}\mid(L_i)_{0\le i\le M},(Z_i)_{0\le i\le M},T,\mathcal T)=\prod_{i=1}^M\prod_{a=0}^1\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\)

snapshot.stmt: The pair-swap and mate-control legality conclusions of \(\texttt{prop:s2-reduction}\) remain unchanged under the asymmetric budget. If the target arm-\(1\) lift is supported on \(O_{b,1}\) and a legal implemented arm-\(1\) selector has \(q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,1}(O_{b,1})=0\), the asymmetric disjoint-orbit witness has zero coverage when both \(k_0,k_1\le M\), has effect set \([-K,K]\) and coverage one when exactly one rank equals \(M+1\), and has effect set \([-2K,2K]\) and coverage one when both ranks equal \(M+1\). The symmetric choice recovers \(\texttt{prop:s2-reduction}\).

defs.def:pair-action: For named pair members define \(O_{b,a}=\{(v_{b,0},a),(v_{b,1},a)\}\) as a two-state pair cell, without thereby asserting that it is a global orbit. If \(\mathcal V_b=\{v_{b,0},v_{b,1}\}\) and the pure local swap subgroup \(\langle s_b\rangle=\{e,s_b\}\) is contained in \(\mathcal G\), then \(s_b(v_{b,r},a)=(v_{b,1-r},a)\) for \(r,a\in\{0,1\}\), \(s_b\) fixes members outside block \(b\), and it relabels complete records by \(\texttt{def:group-action}\). Because every group element preserves blocks and arms and block \(b\) has exactly two members, this sufficient audit implies that \(O_{b,0}\) and \(O_{b,1}\) are global \(\mathcal G\)-orbits. The audit is not necessary: a coupled-swap subgroup may also generate the same orbit. Absent the audit, the pair-cell notation alone makes no global-orbit claim. Pair-specific propositions and examples are invoked after this sufficient audit; a product specialization such as \(S_2^B\) verifies every required pure local swap separately.

assumptions.ass:observable-selector-kernel: \(\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\ge0,\quad\sum_{v\in\mathcal V}\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=1,\quad z_v\ne a\Rightarrow\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=0\)

snapshot.stmt: In the two-pair instance of \(\texttt{prop:two-pair-sanity-check}\), the four arm-specific orbits and the mate-control LP and legality failures are unchanged under the asymmetric budget. If a legal selector has \(q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,1}(O_{1,1})=0\), the bounded witness has \(\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w=\{0\}\) and coverage zero when both ranks are finite, \(\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w=[-K,K]\) and coverage one when exactly one rank is at the boundary, and \(\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w=[-2K,2K]\) and coverage one when both ranks are at the boundary. The symmetric choice recovers the frozen two-pair calculation.

defs.def:pair-action: For named pair members define \(O_{b,a}=\{(v_{b,0},a),(v_{b,1},a)\}\) as a two-state pair cell, without thereby asserting that it is a global orbit. If \(\mathcal V_b=\{v_{b,0},v_{b,1}\}\) and the pure local swap subgroup \(\langle s_b\rangle=\{e,s_b\}\) is contained in \(\mathcal G\), then \(s_b(v_{b,r},a)=(v_{b,1-r},a)\) for \(r,a\in\{0,1\}\), \(s_b\) fixes members outside block \(b\), and it relabels complete records by \(\texttt{def:group-action}\). Because every group element preserves blocks and arms and block \(b\) has exactly two members, this sufficient audit implies that \(O_{b,0}\) and \(O_{b,1}\) are global \(\mathcal G\)-orbits. The audit is not necessary: a coupled-swap subgroup may also generate the same orbit. Absent the audit, the pair-cell notation alone makes no global-orbit claim. Pair-specific propositions and examples are invoked after this sufficient audit; a product specialization such as \(S_2^B\) verifies every required pure local swap separately.

assumptions.ass:observable-selector-kernel: \(\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\ge0,\quad\sum_{v\in\mathcal V}\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=1,\quad z_v\ne a\Rightarrow\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=0\)

snapshot.stmt:
Assume that \(\mathcal H\) is finite and nonempty. The minimum \(D_*\) in \(\texttt{def:minimum-imbalance-common-selector}\) is attained, so \(\mathcal W_*\) is nonempty, and the displayed absolute-value linear program has value \(D_*\). Its quantitative dual is attained and satisfies the strong-duality identity
\[
D_*=\max_{\lVert y\rVert_\infty\le1/2}\left\{\min_{h\in\mathcal H}y^\top A_{\cdot h}-y^\top b_{\mathrm{LP}}\right\}.
\]
If \(D_*>0\), the negative of any maximizing dual vector is a Farkas separating direction with margin \(D_*\). Every \(w_*\in\mathcal W_*\) is computable solely from the known randomization law, target rule, and selector library before scores are observed, and hence is prespecified. Moreover, \(D_*=0\) if and only if the exact joint-balance linear program in \(\texttt{def:common-selector-feasibility-lp}\) is feasible. For every prespecified \(\boldsymbol\varepsilon=(\varepsilon_0,\varepsilon_1)\) with \(\varepsilon_a>0\) and \(\varepsilon_0+\varepsilon_1\le\alpha\), every \(P\in\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\), and arbitrary fixed measurable trained predictor maps, conditional on \(\mathcal T\),
\[
\Pr_P\!\left(\tau_T\in\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{w_*}\mid\mathcal T\right)\ge\max\!\left\{0,1-\rho_{M,\varepsilon_0}-\rho_{M,\varepsilon_1}-D_*\right\}.
\]
This numerical certificate weakly dominates the corresponding certificate for every fixed common \(w\in\Delta(\mathcal H)\), and the dominance is strict whenever \(D_*<\Delta_0(w)+\Delta_1(w)\) and \(1-\rho_{M,\varepsilon_0}-\rho_{M,\varepsilon_1}-D_*>0\). On the equal-split diagonal \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\),
\[
\Pr_P(\tau_T\in\mathcal C_{w_*}\mid\mathcal T)\ge\max\{0,1-2\rho_{M,\alpha}-D_*\}.
\]

defs.def:observable-selector-mixture: For every \(i\in\{1,\ldots,M\}\) and \(a\in\{0,1\}\), set \(\kappa_{w,a}=\sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_h\kappa_{h,a}\), draw \(J_{i,a}\sim\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\), set \(U_{i,a}=(J_{i,a},a)\), and set \(q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,a}=\sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_hq^{\mathrm{obs}}_{h,a}\).
defs.def:minimum-imbalance-common-selector:
For every finite selector library define \(\Delta(\mathcal H)=\{w\in\mathbb R^{|\mathcal H|}:w_h\ge0\ \forall h,\ \sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_h=1\}\); in particular \(\Delta(\varnothing)=\varnothing\). If \(\mathcal H=\varnothing\), no selector mixture, implemented kernel, selector-indexed ordinary or weighted procedure, \(D_*\), \(D_{*,\mathcal A}\), \(\mathcal W_*\), \(\mathcal W_{*,\mathcal A}\), \(w_*\), or \(w_{*,\mathcal A}\) exists. If \(\mathcal H\) is finite and nonempty, retain the joint two-arm diagnostics
\[
D_*=\min_{w\in\Delta(\mathcal H)}\frac12\sum_{a=0}^1\sum_{O\in\mathcal O}|(Aw-b_{\mathrm{LP}})_{a,O}|,\qquad
\mathcal W_*=\operatorname*{arg\,min}_{w\in\Delta(\mathcal H)}\frac12\sum_{a=0}^1\sum_{O\in\mathcal O}|(Aw-b_{\mathrm{LP}})_{a,O}|.
\]
Their absolute-value linear program minimizes \(\frac12\sum_{a=0}^1\sum_Ot_{a,O}\) subject to \(-t_{a,O}\le(Aw-b_{\mathrm{LP}})_{a,O}\le t_{a,O}\), \(t_{a,O}\ge0\), \(w\in\Delta(\mathcal H)\), and their dual is
\[
\max_{\lVert y\rVert_\infty\le1/2}\left\{\min_{h\in\mathcal H}y^\top A_{\cdot h}-y^\top b_{\mathrm{LP}}\right\}.
\]
For the prespecified armwise ranks define \(\mathcal A_{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}=\{a\in\{0,1\}:k_a\le M\}\) and
\[
D_{*,\mathcal A}=\min_{w\in\Delta(\mathcal H)}\frac12\sum_{a\in\mathcal A_{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}}\sum_{O\in\mathcal O}|(Aw-b_{\mathrm{LP}})_{a,O}|,\qquad
\mathcal W_{*,\mathcal A}=\operatorname*{arg\,min}_{w\in\Delta(\mathcal H)}\frac12\sum_{a\in\mathcal A_{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}}\sum_{O\in\mathcal O}|(Aw-b_{\mathrm{LP}})_{a,O}|.
\]
The active-row linear program minimizes \(\frac12\sum_{a\in\mathcal A_{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}}\sum_Ot_{a,O}\) subject to the same two-sided constraints only for \(a\in\mathcal A_{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}\), together with \(t_{a,O}\ge0\) and \(w\in\Delta(\mathcal H)\). Its dual is
\[
\max_{\substack{\lVert y\rVert_\infty\le1/2\\y_{a,O}=0\ \mathrm{for}\ a\notin\mathcal A_{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}}}\left\{\min_{h\in\mathcal H}y^\top A_{\cdot h}-y^\top b_{\mathrm{LP}}\right\}.
\]
If \(\mathcal A_{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}=\varnothing\), define \(D_{*,\mathcal A}=0\) and \(\mathcal W_{*,\mathcal A}=\Delta(\mathcal H)\), and choose \(w_{*,\mathcal A}\) without solving an LP. The full objects \(D_*\) and \(\mathcal W_*\) remain the joint two-arm balance diagnostics; \(D_{*,\mathcal A}=0\) characterizes only active-row balance unless both arms are active. Primal and dual equality, attainment, and the coverage certificates are proved in \(\texttt{thm:active-arm-minimum-imbalance-selector-certificate}\).

defs.def:rank-inversion: Prespecify \(\boldsymbol\varepsilon=(\varepsilon_0,\varepsilon_1)\) with \(\varepsilon_a>0\) and \(\varepsilon_0+\varepsilon_1\le\alpha\), and set \(k_a=\lceil(M+1)(1-\varepsilon_a)\rceil\). For each \(a\in\{0,1\}\), compute \(S_{i,a}=|Y^{\mathrm{obs}}_{iJ_{i,a}}-\widehat m_a(X_{iJ_{i,a}})|\), take \(r_{a,(k_a)}\), set \(I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}=[\widehat m_a(X_{0T})-r_{a,(k_a)},\widehat m_a(X_{0T})+r_{a,(k_a)}]\cap[-K,K]\), and return \(\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w=I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{1,w}-I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{0,w}\). If \(k_a=M+1\), set \(r_{a,(k_a)}=+\infty\), so \(I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}=[-K,K]\). For proof ranks only, append independent continuous uniforms and use lexicographic ranks; reported closed intervals use weak inclusion. Under \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), define \(k=k_0=k_1\), \(r_{a,(k)}=r_{a,(k_a)}\), \(I_{a,w}=I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}\), and \(\mathcal C_w=\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w\), recovering the symmetric construction.
defs.def:finite-rank-error:
For \(N=M+1\), prespecified \(\boldsymbol\varepsilon=(\varepsilon_0,\varepsilon_1)\), and \(k_a=\lceil N(1-\varepsilon_a)\rceil\), define
\[
\rho_{M,\varepsilon_a}=\frac{N-k_a}{N}=\frac{\lfloor N\varepsilon_a\rfloor}{N},\qquad a\in\{0,1\}.
\]
Under \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), write their common value as
\[
\rho_{M,\alpha}=\frac{M+1-k}{M+1}=\frac{\lfloor(M+1)\alpha/2\rfloor}{M+1}.
\]

assumptions.ass:observable-selector-kernel: \(\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\ge0,\quad\sum_{v\in\mathcal V}\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=1,\quad z_v\ne a\Rightarrow\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=0\)

snapshot.stmt:
For a prespecified \(\boldsymbol\varepsilon=(\varepsilon_0,\varepsilon_1)\), define the active-arm set \(\mathcal A_{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}=\{a\in\{0,1\}:k_a\le M\}\). For every fixed or prespecified \(w\), conditional on \(\mathcal T\),
\[
\Pr_P\!\left\{\tau_T\in\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w\mid\mathcal T\right\}
\ge
\max\!\left\{0,1-\sum_{a\in\mathcal A_{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}}\bigl(\rho_{M,\varepsilon_a}+\Delta_a(w)\bigr)\right\}
\]
for every \(P\in\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\) and arbitrary fixed measurable trained predictor maps. If \(a\notin\mathcal A_{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}\), then \(I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}=[-K,K]\) contains \(Y_{0T}(a)\) surely, so arm \(a\) contributes neither a rank penalty nor an orbit-gap penalty. The coordinate elbows are \(\varepsilon_a=j/(M+1)\), and \(|\delta_{a,O}(w)|\le\Delta_a(w)\) for every arm and orbit. If exactly one arm is at the boundary, only the other arm appears in the sum. If both arms are at the boundary, the effect set is \([-2K,2K]\) and coverage is one. On the equal-split diagonal with \(k\le M\), the bound is \(\max\{0,1-2\rho_{M,\alpha}-\Delta_0(w)-\Delta_1(w)\}\); with \(k=M+1\), coverage is one.

defs.def:finite-rank-error:
For \(N=M+1\), prespecified \(\boldsymbol\varepsilon=(\varepsilon_0,\varepsilon_1)\), and \(k_a=\lceil N(1-\varepsilon_a)\rceil\), define
\[
\rho_{M,\varepsilon_a}=\frac{N-k_a}{N}=\frac{\lfloor N\varepsilon_a\rfloor}{N},\qquad a\in\{0,1\}.
\]
Under \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), write their common value as
\[
\rho_{M,\alpha}=\frac{M+1-k}{M+1}=\frac{\lfloor(M+1)\alpha/2\rfloor}{M+1}.
\]
defs.def:rank-inversion: Prespecify \(\boldsymbol\varepsilon=(\varepsilon_0,\varepsilon_1)\) with \(\varepsilon_a>0\) and \(\varepsilon_0+\varepsilon_1\le\alpha\), and set \(k_a=\lceil(M+1)(1-\varepsilon_a)\rceil\). For each \(a\in\{0,1\}\), compute \(S_{i,a}=|Y^{\mathrm{obs}}_{iJ_{i,a}}-\widehat m_a(X_{iJ_{i,a}})|\), take \(r_{a,(k_a)}\), set \(I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}=[\widehat m_a(X_{0T})-r_{a,(k_a)},\widehat m_a(X_{0T})+r_{a,(k_a)}]\cap[-K,K]\), and return \(\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w=I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{1,w}-I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{0,w}\). If \(k_a=M+1\), set \(r_{a,(k_a)}=+\infty\), so \(I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}=[-K,K]\). For proof ranks only, append independent continuous uniforms and use lexicographic ranks; reported closed intervals use weak inclusion. Under \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), define \(k=k_0=k_1\), \(r_{a,(k)}=r_{a,(k_a)}\), \(I_{a,w}=I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}\), and \(\mathcal C_w=\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w\), recovering the symmetric construction.

assumptions.ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization: \(\mathcal L((J_{i,a})_{1\le i\le M,\ a\in\{0,1\}}\mid(L_i)_{0\le i\le M},(Z_i)_{0\le i\le M},T,\mathcal T)=\prod_{i=1}^M\prod_{a=0}^1\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\)

snapshot.stmt:
Assume that \(\mathcal H\) is finite and nonempty. The unchanged joint two-arm objects \(D_*\) and \(\mathcal W_*\) are attained, the full absolute-value linear program has value \(D_*\), and
\[
D_*=\max_{\lVert y\rVert_\infty\le1/2}
\left\{\min_{h\in\mathcal H}y^\top A_{\cdot h}-y^\top b_{\mathrm{LP}}\right\}.
\]
If \(D_*>0\), the negative of every maximizing vector is a Farkas separator with margin \(D_*\), and \(D_*=0\) if and only if the exact joint-balance program is feasible. For a prespecified \(\boldsymbol\varepsilon\), let \(\mathcal A_{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}=\{a:k_a\le M\}\). The active-row minimum \(D_{*,\mathcal A}\) and optimizer set \(\mathcal W_{*,\mathcal A}\) in \(\texttt{def:minimum-imbalance-common-selector}\) are attained, and the active-row absolute-value program has value \(D_{*,\mathcal A}\). Its attained dual is
\[
D_{*,\mathcal A}=\max_{\substack{\lVert y\rVert_\infty\le1/2\\y_{a,O}=0\ \mathrm{for}\ a\notin\mathcal A_{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}}}
\left\{\min_{h\in\mathcal H}y^\top A_{\cdot h}-y^\top b_{\mathrm{LP}}\right\}.
\]
Moreover, \(D_{*,\mathcal A}=0\) if and only if one selector balances every active-arm row; this characterizes joint two-arm balance only when \(\mathcal A_{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}=\{0,1\}\). Every prespecified \(w_{*,\mathcal A}\in\mathcal W_{*,\mathcal A}\) is computable before scores are observed and satisfies
\[
\Pr_P\!\left\{\tau_T\in\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{w_{*,\mathcal A}}\mid\mathcal T\right\}
\ge\max\!\left\{0,1-\sum_{a\in\mathcal A_{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}}\rho_{M,\varepsilon_a}-D_{*,\mathcal A}\right\}.
\]
This active-row certificate weakly dominates the corresponding certificate for every fixed common \(w\), with strict dominance whenever its active imbalance is strictly smaller and the optimized untruncated lower bound is positive. If exactly one arm is at the boundary, the optimization contains only the other arm's rows. If \(\mathcal A_{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}=\varnothing\), then \(D_{*,\mathcal A}=0\), \(\mathcal W_{*,\mathcal A}=\Delta(\mathcal H)\), no optimization is needed, and coverage is one. On the equal-split finite-rank diagonal, \(D_{*,\mathcal A}=D_*\) and \(\mathcal W_{*,\mathcal A}=\mathcal W_*\); at the two-boundary elbow, coverage is one. The original \(w_*\)-certificate and its strict-dominance claim remain valid as consequences of the full two-arm optimum. No selector-indexed object exists when \(\mathcal H=\varnothing\).

defs.def:observable-selector-mixture: For every \(i\in\{1,\ldots,M\}\) and \(a\in\{0,1\}\), set \(\kappa_{w,a}=\sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_h\kappa_{h,a}\), draw \(J_{i,a}\sim\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\), set \(U_{i,a}=(J_{i,a},a)\), and set \(q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,a}=\sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_hq^{\mathrm{obs}}_{h,a}\).
defs.def:minimum-imbalance-common-selector:
For every finite selector library define \(\Delta(\mathcal H)=\{w\in\mathbb R^{|\mathcal H|}:w_h\ge0\ \forall h,\ \sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_h=1\}\); in particular \(\Delta(\varnothing)=\varnothing\). If \(\mathcal H=\varnothing\), no selector mixture, implemented kernel, selector-indexed ordinary or weighted procedure, \(D_*\), \(D_{*,\mathcal A}\), \(\mathcal W_*\), \(\mathcal W_{*,\mathcal A}\), \(w_*\), or \(w_{*,\mathcal A}\) exists. If \(\mathcal H\) is finite and nonempty, retain the joint two-arm diagnostics
\[
D_*=\min_{w\in\Delta(\mathcal H)}\frac12\sum_{a=0}^1\sum_{O\in\mathcal O}|(Aw-b_{\mathrm{LP}})_{a,O}|,\qquad
\mathcal W_*=\operatorname*{arg\,min}_{w\in\Delta(\mathcal H)}\frac12\sum_{a=0}^1\sum_{O\in\mathcal O}|(Aw-b_{\mathrm{LP}})_{a,O}|.
\]
Their absolute-value linear program minimizes \(\frac12\sum_{a=0}^1\sum_Ot_{a,O}\) subject to \(-t_{a,O}\le(Aw-b_{\mathrm{LP}})_{a,O}\le t_{a,O}\), \(t_{a,O}\ge0\), \(w\in\Delta(\mathcal H)\), and their dual is
\[
\max_{\lVert y\rVert_\infty\le1/2}\left\{\min_{h\in\mathcal H}y^\top A_{\cdot h}-y^\top b_{\mathrm{LP}}\right\}.
\]
For the prespecified armwise ranks define \(\mathcal A_{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}=\{a\in\{0,1\}:k_a\le M\}\) and
\[
D_{*,\mathcal A}=\min_{w\in\Delta(\mathcal H)}\frac12\sum_{a\in\mathcal A_{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}}\sum_{O\in\mathcal O}|(Aw-b_{\mathrm{LP}})_{a,O}|,\qquad
\mathcal W_{*,\mathcal A}=\operatorname*{arg\,min}_{w\in\Delta(\mathcal H)}\frac12\sum_{a\in\mathcal A_{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}}\sum_{O\in\mathcal O}|(Aw-b_{\mathrm{LP}})_{a,O}|.
\]
The active-row linear program minimizes \(\frac12\sum_{a\in\mathcal A_{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}}\sum_Ot_{a,O}\) subject to the same two-sided constraints only for \(a\in\mathcal A_{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}\), together with \(t_{a,O}\ge0\) and \(w\in\Delta(\mathcal H)\). Its dual is
\[
\max_{\substack{\lVert y\rVert_\infty\le1/2\\y_{a,O}=0\ \mathrm{for}\ a\notin\mathcal A_{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}}}\left\{\min_{h\in\mathcal H}y^\top A_{\cdot h}-y^\top b_{\mathrm{LP}}\right\}.
\]
If \(\mathcal A_{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}=\varnothing\), define \(D_{*,\mathcal A}=0\) and \(\mathcal W_{*,\mathcal A}=\Delta(\mathcal H)\), and choose \(w_{*,\mathcal A}\) without solving an LP. The full objects \(D_*\) and \(\mathcal W_*\) remain the joint two-arm balance diagnostics; \(D_{*,\mathcal A}=0\) characterizes only active-row balance unless both arms are active. Primal and dual equality, attainment, and the coverage certificates are proved in \(\texttt{thm:active-arm-minimum-imbalance-selector-certificate}\).
defs.def:invariant-laws: \(\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}=\{P:P\circ(gL_i)^{-1}=P\quad\forall g\in\mathcal G,\quad\max_{v\in\mathcal V,\ a\in\{0,1\}}|Y_v(a)|\le K\}\).
defs.def:rank-inversion: Prespecify \(\boldsymbol\varepsilon=(\varepsilon_0,\varepsilon_1)\) with \(\varepsilon_a>0\) and \(\varepsilon_0+\varepsilon_1\le\alpha\), and set \(k_a=\lceil(M+1)(1-\varepsilon_a)\rceil\). For each \(a\in\{0,1\}\), compute \(S_{i,a}=|Y^{\mathrm{obs}}_{iJ_{i,a}}-\widehat m_a(X_{iJ_{i,a}})|\), take \(r_{a,(k_a)}\), set \(I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}=[\widehat m_a(X_{0T})-r_{a,(k_a)},\widehat m_a(X_{0T})+r_{a,(k_a)}]\cap[-K,K]\), and return \(\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w=I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{1,w}-I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{0,w}\). If \(k_a=M+1\), set \(r_{a,(k_a)}=+\infty\), so \(I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}=[-K,K]\). For proof ranks only, append independent continuous uniforms and use lexicographic ranks; reported closed intervals use weak inclusion. Under \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), define \(k=k_0=k_1\), \(r_{a,(k)}=r_{a,(k_a)}\), \(I_{a,w}=I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}\), and \(\mathcal C_w=\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w\), recovering the symmetric construction.
defs.def:finite-rank-error:
For \(N=M+1\), prespecified \(\boldsymbol\varepsilon=(\varepsilon_0,\varepsilon_1)\), and \(k_a=\lceil N(1-\varepsilon_a)\rceil\), define
\[
\rho_{M,\varepsilon_a}=\frac{N-k_a}{N}=\frac{\lfloor N\varepsilon_a\rfloor}{N},\qquad a\in\{0,1\}.
\]
Under \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), write their common value as
\[
\rho_{M,\alpha}=\frac{M+1-k}{M+1}=\frac{\lfloor(M+1)\alpha/2\rfloor}{M+1}.
\]

assumptions.ass:observable-selector-kernel: \(\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\ge0,\quad\sum_{v\in\mathcal V}\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=1,\quad z_v\ne a\Rightarrow\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=0\)

snapshot.stmt:
Suppose \(k_0,k_1\le M\). Define the arm-orbit sets \(\mathcal O_a\), their sizes \(m_a\), the joint calibration-orbit law
\[
R_w(O_0,O_1)=\sum_z\nu(z)\kappa_{w,0}(O_0\mid z)\kappa_{w,1}(O_1\mid z),
\]
and the coherent common-target law
\[
P_T(O_0,O_1)=\sum_{v\in\mathcal V}q_{\mathrm{tar}}(v)
\mathbf 1\{O_0(v)=O_0,\ O_1(v)=O_1\}.
\]
Let \(L=|\operatorname{supp}(P_T)|\le\min\{|\mathcal V|,m_0m_1\}\). For every preorder pair \(C=(c_0,c_1)\), let \(P_T^C\), \(L_C\), the strict-prefix keys \(\Pi_C\), \(U_C\), and the local weights \(\omega_C\) be the corresponding pushforwards. Then \(U_C\le L_C\le\min\{L,d_0d_1\}\), and
\[
\Pr\{\text{both rank events under }C\}
=\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_C}\omega_C(\pi)D_{M,k_0,k_1}(\pi).
\]
A global memo is keyed exactly by \((M,k_0,k_1,\pi_{00},\pi_{01},\pi_{10})\); the fourth cell is determined by normalization, and local weights never enter the key. Thus the unchanged \(R_w\), unchanged coherent \(P_T^C\), common selector, common target, and within-replicate cross-arm dependence are preserved.

Let \(\Pi_\star=\bigcup_C\Pi_C\), \(G=|\Pi_\star|\), and partition it first into the support-one stratum \(\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\), then the support-two stratum \(\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}\), then the eligible support-three face stratum \(\Pi_{\mathrm{face}}\), then the remaining support-four determinant-zero stratum \(\Pi_{\mathrm{rank1}}\), and finally the residual strata \(\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}},\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}},\Pi_+\) chosen by deterministic least-charge comparison. Put
\[
h_a=M-k_a+1,\qquad \bar k_a=\min\{k_a,h_a\},\qquad
\mathsf C_{M,k_0,k_1}=\sum_{i=0}^{M-1}
\min\{\bar k_0,i+1\}\min\{\bar k_1,i+1\}.
\]
Vertices use the deterministic formula; edges use the exact binomial interval charge \(\mathsf E_M(\pi)\); eligible support-three face keys use \(\mathsf F_M(\pi)\); rank-one support-four keys satisfying \(\pi_{00}\pi_{11}=\pi_{01}\pi_{10}\) use \(\mathsf R_{M,k_0,k_1}=1+2\lceil\log_2M\rceil+\bar k_0+\bar k_1\); and only residual keys enter the rolling, zero-cell-safe multinomial, or positive-corner branches. With prespecified tie-breaking,
\[
\Pi_\star=\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}
\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{face}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}
\Pi_{\mathrm{rank1}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}
\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}
\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_+,
\]
and
\[
\begin{aligned}
\mathsf B={}&|\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}|
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}}\mathsf E_M(\pi)
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{face}}}\mathsf F_M(\pi)
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{rank1}}}\mathsf R_{M,k_0,k_1}
+M\mathbf 1\{\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\}
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}}\mathsf C_{M,k_0,k_1}\\
&+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}}
\left(M+\mathsf T_{M,\bar k_0,\bar k_1}\right)
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_+}\mathsf C_+(\pi).
\end{aligned}
\]
If \(\sigma(\pi)\) minimizes the positive-corner charge, define
\[
\mathsf W=\max\!\left(
\{0\}\cup\{\bar k_0\bar k_1:\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}\ne\varnothing\}
\cup\{M:\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\}
\cup\{\min(t_0^{\sigma(\pi)},t_1^{\sigma(\pi)}):\pi\in\Pi_+\}
\right).
\]
Vertices, edges, face keys, and rank-one keys contribute only \(O(1)\) workspace, and the factorial preprocessing occurs if and only if \(\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\). The face and rank-one strata contribute no nonconstant term to \(\mathsf W\). With \(\mathfrak C\) the \(F_{m_0}F_{m_1}\) preorder pairs, exact enumeration after \(R_w\) is tabulated costs
\[
O\!\left(
|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}(m_0m_1+L)
+\sum_{C\in\mathfrak C}[L_C\log(1+L_C)+L_C+U_C]
+\Bigl(\sum_CU_C\Bigr)\log(1+G)+\mathsf B
\right),
\]
and its uniform version replaces the middle preorder-specific terms by
\[
F_{m_0}F_{m_1}
[m_0m_1+L+L_{\max}\log(1+L_{\max})+L_{\max}
+U_{\max}\{1+\log(1+G)\}].
\]
Working memory is \(O(m_0m_1+L+U_{\max}+G+\mathsf W)\).

There is a strict admissible edge improvement. Let \(M=2n\ge4\), \(k_0=n\), \(k_1=M\), and \(\pi=(1/2,1/2,0,0)\) in \(00,01,10,11\) order. Then
\[
D_{M,n,M}(\pi)=1-2^{-M}.
\]
The edge evaluator uses \(\Theta(\log M)\) exact arithmetic and \(O(1)\) workspace, whereas the least-charge former nonvertex dispatcher uses \(\Theta(M)\) arithmetic. This key is realized by two audited pair blocks under \(S_2^2\), independent fair one-treated--one-control assignment in each block, a singleton selector library whose legal equivariant arm-\(1\) rule selects either block equiprobably and then its unique observed arm-\(1\) member, a target supported in block \(2\), an arm-\(0\) preorder with block \(2\) lowest, and an arm-\(1\) preorder with block \(1\) below block \(2\). Taking
\[
\varepsilon_0=\frac{n+1}{2n+1},\qquad
\varepsilon_1=\frac1{2n+1},\qquad
\alpha=\frac{n+2}{2n+1}
\]
gives the stated ranks and satisfies the error-budget assumptions.

There is also a strict admissible zero-corner face improvement for every \(M\ge2\). Let \(k_0=k_1=M\) and \(\pi=(1/3,1/3,1/3,0)\) in \(00,01,10,11\) order. Then
\[
D_{M,M,M}(\pi)=1-2\cdot3^{-M}.
\]
The \(LL\) face orientation has \(\pi_{11}=0\) and \(t_0+t_1=2M>M\), so the face evaluator costs \(\Theta(\log M)\) exact arithmetic and \(O(1)\) workspace. The rolling, multinomial, and every allowed positive-base coefficient orientation each cost \(\Theta(M)\). The key is realized by two pair blocks with the trivial finite group, a known law uniform on three assignment vectors having one treated member per block, deterministic legal observable selector lookups whose strict-prefix pairs are \(00\), \(01\), and \(10\), a singleton common selector library, and a fixed common target in block \(2\). In each arm, place both block-\(1\) singleton orbits strictly below the target orbit and tie the block-\(2\) singleton orbits with the target. Taking
\[
\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\frac1{M+1},
\qquad
\alpha=\frac2{M+1}
\]
gives \(k_0=k_1=M\) and satisfies the error-budget assumptions.

There is a strict admissible rank-one support-four improvement. Let \(M=2n\) with \(n\ge3\), \(k_0=n\), \(k_1=n+2\), and \(\pi=(1/4,1/4,1/4,1/4)\). Then
\[
D_{2n,n,n+2}(\pi)=H_{2n,n}(1/2)H_{2n,n+2}(1/2).
\]
The rank-one prepass uses \(\Theta(M)\) exact arithmetic and \(O(1)\) workspace, while the least-charge former branch uses \(\Theta(M^2)\) arithmetic and \(\Theta(M)\) workspace. The key is realized by two pair blocks with the trivial group, independent fair one-treated--one-control assignments, a singleton selector choosing block \(1\)'s unique control in arm \(0\) and block \(2\)'s unique treated member in arm \(1\), the common target \(v_{2,0}\), and preorders making one block-\(1\) control singleton orbit and the block-\(2\) mate-treatment singleton orbit strictly below their target orbits. The admissible levels are
\[
\varepsilon_0=\frac{n+1}{2n+1},\qquad
\varepsilon_1=\frac{n-1}{2n+1},\qquad
\alpha=\frac{2n}{2n+1}.
\]

defs.def:observable-selector-mixture: For every \(i\in\{1,\ldots,M\}\) and \(a\in\{0,1\}\), set \(\kappa_{w,a}=\sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_h\kappa_{h,a}\), draw \(J_{i,a}\sim\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\), set \(U_{i,a}=(J_{i,a},a)\), and set \(q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,a}=\sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_hq^{\mathrm{obs}}_{h,a}\).
defs.def:coverage-frontier:
For prespecified \(\boldsymbol\varepsilon=(\varepsilon_0,\varepsilon_1)\), define
\[
\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)=\max_{\substack{s_a:\mathcal O\to[0,K]\\a\in\{0,1\}}}\left[(1-\alpha)-\Pr\left\{s_1(O_{T,1})\le Q_{k_1}(s_1(O_{1,1}),\ldots,s_1(O_{M,1}))\ \text{and}\ s_0(O_{T,0})\le Q_{k_0}(s_0(O_{1,0}),\ldots,s_0(O_{M,0}))\right\}\right]_+.
\]
Here \(O_{i,a}\sim q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,a}\), the target orbits are the two lifts of the one common \(T\sim q_{\mathrm{tar}}\), and the calibration draws use \(\texttt{ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization}\), including their within-replicate cross-arm coupling. The functional is restricted to bounded orbit-constant score maps. Under \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), set \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)=\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)\), recovering the symmetric frontier.
defs.def:rank-inversion: Prespecify \(\boldsymbol\varepsilon=(\varepsilon_0,\varepsilon_1)\) with \(\varepsilon_a>0\) and \(\varepsilon_0+\varepsilon_1\le\alpha\), and set \(k_a=\lceil(M+1)(1-\varepsilon_a)\rceil\). For each \(a\in\{0,1\}\), compute \(S_{i,a}=|Y^{\mathrm{obs}}_{iJ_{i,a}}-\widehat m_a(X_{iJ_{i,a}})|\), take \(r_{a,(k_a)}\), set \(I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}=[\widehat m_a(X_{0T})-r_{a,(k_a)},\widehat m_a(X_{0T})+r_{a,(k_a)}]\cap[-K,K]\), and return \(\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w=I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{1,w}-I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{0,w}\). If \(k_a=M+1\), set \(r_{a,(k_a)}=+\infty\), so \(I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}=[-K,K]\). For proof ranks only, append independent continuous uniforms and use lexicographic ranks; reported closed intervals use weak inclusion. Under \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), define \(k=k_0=k_1\), \(r_{a,(k)}=r_{a,(k_a)}\), \(I_{a,w}=I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}\), and \(\mathcal C_w=\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w\), recovering the symmetric construction.
defs.def:simplex-vertex-key-prepass:
For a finite set \(\Pi_\star\) of probability mass functions on \(\{0,1\}^2\), first set
\[
\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}=\{\pi\in\Pi_\star:|\operatorname{supp}(\pi)|=1\},
\qquad
\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}=\{\pi\in\Pi_\star\setminus\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}:|\operatorname{supp}(\pi)|=2\}.
\]
For \(\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\), let \(u=(x,y)\) be its unique support point and return \(\mathbf 1\{Mx<k_0,\ My<k_1\}\). For \(\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}\), order its two support points \(u=(u_0,u_1)\) and \(v=(v_0,v_1)\) deterministically; both possible orientations may be compared, with the smaller certified charge chosen and lexicographic tie-breaking. Put \(p=\pi_v\in(0,1)\), let \(N\sim\operatorname{Bin}(M,p)\), and define \(c_a=Mu_a\) and \(d_a=v_a-u_a\) for \(a\in\{0,1\}\). Set
\[
L_\pi=\max\!\left(\{0\}\cup\{c_a-k_a+1:d_a=-1\}\right),\qquad
U_\pi=\min\!\left(\{M\}\cup\{k_a-c_a-1:d_a=1\}\right).
\]
If \(d_a=0\) and \(c_a\ge k_a\) for some \(a\), declare \([L_\pi,U_\pi]\) empty. Otherwise return
\[
D_{M,k_0,k_1}(\pi)=\Pr\{L_\pi\le N\le U_\pi\}.
\]
For a nonempty interval define
\[
\eta_\pi=\min\!\left\{
\min\{L_\pi,M-L_\pi\}+U_\pi-L_\pi+1,\ 
L_\pi+M-U_\pi,\ 
\min\{U_\pi+1,M-U_\pi\}+\min\{L_\pi,M-L_\pi+1\}
\right\},
\]
and set \(\eta_\pi=0\) for an empty interval. Direct interval summation, complementary left-and-right tails, and a difference of two shorter binomial tails are three exact alternatives; choose the least charge deterministically and write \(\mathsf E_M(\pi)=1+\lceil\log_2 M\rceil+\eta_\pi\). The edge evaluation uses \(O(\mathsf E_M(\pi))\) exact arithmetic operations and \(O(1)\) auxiliary workspace.

For every remaining support-three key and every corner orientation \(\sigma\in\{L,U\}^2\), let \(\pi^\sigma\) be the transformed four-cell law and let \(t_a^\sigma=k_a\) under \(L\) and \(t_a^\sigma=M-k_a+1\) under \(U\). Define
\[
\Sigma_{\mathrm{face}}(\pi)=
\{\sigma:\pi^\sigma_{11}=0,\ t_0^\sigma+t_1^\sigma>M\},
\qquad
\Pi_{\mathrm{face}}=
\{\pi\in\Pi_\star\setminus(\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\cup\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}):
\Sigma_{\mathrm{face}}(\pi)\ne\varnothing\}.
\]
For \(\sigma\in\Sigma_{\mathrm{face}}(\pi)\), let \(T_a^\sigma\) be the transformed terminal count and set
\[
J_\sigma=
1-\Pr\{T_0^\sigma\ge t_0^\sigma\}
 -\Pr\{T_1^\sigma\ge t_1^\sigma\}.
\]
Reconstruct \(D_{M,k_0,k_1}(\pi)\) from \(J_\sigma\) by the \(LL\), \(LU\), \(UL\), or \(UU\) corner identity. Each required reconstruction marginal is the complement of one of the two tails already evaluated. With
\[
\ell_M(t)=1+\lceil\log_2 M\rceil+\min\{t,M-t+1\},
\qquad
\mathsf F_M(\pi)=1+
\min_{\sigma\in\Sigma_{\mathrm{face}}(\pi)}
\{\ell_M(t_0^\sigma)+\ell_M(t_1^\sigma)\},
\]
choose a minimizing orientation and break ties lexicographically. The face evaluation uses \(O(\mathsf F_M(\pi))\) exact arithmetic operations and \(O(1)\) auxiliary workspace.

For every remaining support-four key, test the exact memoized-cell identity
\[
\pi_{00}\pi_{11}=\pi_{01}\pi_{10}.
\]
Define
\[
\Pi_{\mathrm{rank1}}=
\{\pi\in\Pi_\star\setminus
(\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\cup\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}\cup\Pi_{\mathrm{face}}):
|\operatorname{supp}(\pi)|=4,\ 
\pi_{00}\pi_{11}=\pi_{01}\pi_{10}\}.
\]
For \(\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{rank1}}\), put
\[
